TI Serving robot for restaurant e.g. family            restaurant, has drink pouring unit that is injected            into container placed in container placing unit, and            control unit which is provided for electrically            controlling alcoholic drink in drink storing unit.
AU SUZUKI K
AE SHERPA CO LTD (SHER-Non-standard)
GA 2022516808
AB    NOVELTY - The robot has a container mounting unit that                holds an empty container for liquor drinks                containing carbon dioxide while holding. A drink                storage unit stores the liquor drink. A drink                injection unit injected into the container placed                in the container mounting unit. A control unit                electrical controls the alcoholic drink in the                drink storing unit. The driving unit moves the                container mounting unit, and the posture of the                container mounting unit carries the container is                placed between a horizontal state and an inclined                state by the driving unit.
   USE - Waiter robot for restaurants such as family                restaurant, rolled sushi shop, grilled meat shop,                Chinese restaurant and food and drink store.
   ADVANTAGE - The liquor drink such as draft beer is poured                into the container in a preferable mode for a                person to be drunk and can be provided to a                customer like beer with beer poured on a jug by a                salesclerk. The customer who has ordered an order                can be satisfied, and the liquor drink can be                served at a designated place and the user has an                excellent effect.

AU LU B
AE JIANGXI HUICE EXPLOSION PROOF ELECTRIC (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022156813
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a long-life explosion-proof explosion-proof emergency lamp, relating to the technical field of novel automatic instrument and instrument, comprising a shell and an explosion-proof box, two sides of the top end of the shell is provided with a lamp head, and the middle position of the shell is provided with an explosion-proof box, two sides of the explosion-proof box are provided with explosion-proof structure, the middle position of the two sides of the shell is provided with an isolation structure, one side of the shell is provided with a sealing structure. The utility model through the explosion, the buffer block for primary buffer damping, at the same time, the impact of the explosion drives the piston plate to push the push plate to extrude the inflatable bag to buffer, the final inflatable bag is top needle top breaking, the carbon dioxide gas in the inner part is discharged, avoiding the fire, ensuring good explosion-proof performance, so as to solve the problem that the explosion-proof performance is not high.

   USE - Mixed matrix membrane used in material science                field and gas separation field for separating gas                from a mixture of gas and water, preferably                hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, carbon dioxide,                methane, sulfur dioxide, hydrogen sulfide,                ethylene, ethane, propylene and propane                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The porous metal-organic cage complex is a                zero-dimensional functional complex, has good                dispersibility and characteristic of a simple                structure. The prepared mixed matrix film has                better separation performance. The method is                simple, easy for industrial application, and                overcomes the interface defect problem existing in                the mixed matrix films.

TI Preparing perovskite thin film useful in solar            cell, comprises dissolving perovskite precursor            material in liquid substance of high saturated steam            pressure under pressure to obtain perovskite precursor            solution and forming film to obtain perovskite thin            film.
AU XIONG J

   QIN X

GA 202225171A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing perovskite thin film comprises (i)                dissolving the perovskite precursor material in the                liquid substance of high saturated steam pressure                under the pressurizing condition to obtain                perovskite precursor solution; and (ii) forming a                film from the precursor solution of the perovskite                to obtain a perovskite thin film. The perovskite                precursor material has the general formula shown in                formula of ABX3 formula, where A is CH3NH3,                NH2CHNH2, C8H9NH3 or caesium, B is lead or tin and                X comprising chlorine, bromine and/or iodine. The                liquid substance with high saturated vapor pressure                is chosen from liquid methylamine, liquid ammonia                or supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing perovskite                thin film is useful in solar cell e.g. Third                generation solar cell and fresh concept solar                cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not need to use anti-solvent                and curing treatment; has simple preparation                process, simple technique and mild condition and                wide application prospect; is easy to control and                for large-scale production; and produces perovskite                film with good uniformity and high stability.

PN CN113750332-A
TI Elastic wave positive pressure oscillation ventilation method involves obtaining ventilation pressure information, and triggering oscillation when and only when pressure reaches pressure baseline platform.
AU SU Z
   WANG X
   ZHANG X
   ZHENG H
   WANG T
   WU Z
   ZHAO Y
   WEI R
   DIAO J
AE BEIJING AEROSPACE CHANGFENG CO LTD (CAES-C)
GA 202223486M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing elastic wave positive pressure oscillation ventilation mode based on a first elastic medium and a second elastic medium. The first elastic material comprises an elastic oscillation film of a ventilation system. The second elastic material includes a lung bubble and a preset pressure baseline platform. Ventilation pressure information is obtained. An elastic wave is triggered to oscillate when the pressure reaches the pressure level of the pressure base platform.
   USE - Elastic wave positive pressure oscillation ventilation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The elastic wave positive pressure oscillation ventilation system is set to effectively realize lung multiple, prevent airway collapse, at the same time, better realizing oxygen exchange, discharging carbon dioxide. The preset low voltage baseline platform, and the high pressure baseline platform and the low pressure baseline platforms have pressure difference, so as to move the sputum of the foaming by pressure difference to realize lung complex, and thus, prevent the airway from collapsing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   1. An elastic wave positive pressure oscillation ventilation system;
   2. a computer device comprising processor and memory; and
   3. a computer readable storage medium.

PT P
PN DE102020114494-A1
TI Fuel mixture used as fuel for e.g. vehicles, comprises hydrogenated vegetable oil, fuel produced from fatty acid methyl esters, and fossil fuel pretreated by contacting with alkane-containing fluid on metal at preset elevated temperature.
AU WITTENBECHER L
   HENDLER M
AE VNG AG (VNGV-Non-standard)
GA 2021D94394
AB    NOVELTY - A fuel mixture comprises (a) fossil fuel, (b) hydrogenated vegetable oil, and (c) fuel produced from fatty acid methyl esters. The fossil fuel is pretreated by contacting with an alkane-containing fluid on a metal at an elevated temperature of more 60 degrees C. The fuel mixture comprises more than 33 %mass mixture of treated fossil fuel, hydrogenated vegetable oil and fatty acid methyl esters in the produced fuel in regenerative proportion.
   USE - Fuel mixture used as fuel for vehicles, ships and airplane and as substitute for heating oil (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel mixture has an improved carbon dioxide balance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of fuel mixture involves pretreating fossil fuel by contacting with an alkane-containing fluid on a metal at an elevated temperature of more than 60 degrees C, and generating the fuel mixture comprising more than 33 %mass mixture of treated fossil fuel, hydrogenated vegetable oil and fuel produced from fatty acid methyl esters.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal on which the fossil fuel is in contact with an alkane-containing fluid comprises cobalt, nickel, tungsten, chromium, aluminum or transition metal, and their alloy. The transition metal or alloy is formed by windings on an iron framework.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The fuel mixture has minimum density of 780 kg/m3, preferably 820 kg/m3 at 15 degrees C. The fossil fuel is liquid at a temperature of 15 degrees C and a pressure of 1 bar. Preferred Components: The fossil fuel comprises diesel, kerosene or gas oil. The fuel produced from fatty acid methyl ester is produced at least partially from used cooking oil, cooking oil, rapeseed oil, technical oils or from oils or fats of animal origin. The alkane comprises methane. Preferred Composition: The alkane in the alkane-containing fluid comprises at least 30 vol.%, preferably 50 vol.% non-fossil origin from fermentation or fermentation of biomass or other organic origin, and at least 50 vol.% methane of non-fossil origin. Preferred Conditions: The pretreatment of the fossil fuel takes place at a temperature of more than 80 degrees C and below the boiling point of the fossil fuel component.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)
MC H06-B; H06-P
IP C10L-001/19
PD DE102020114494-A1   02 Dec 2021   C10L-001/19   202203   Pages: 6   German

AB    NOVELTY - Solid solution photocatalyst, is claimed. The catalyst is a Sr1-xBaxTiO3 solid solution photocatalyst whose molar ratio of strontium element to barium element in the catalyst is Sr:Ba=1-x:x (x less than or equal to 0.2).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of the strontium element and the barium element is 2:1, 5:1 or 8:1, and the total amount of the strontium element and the barium element is 12 mmol. In the step (i), the compound of strontium element is strontium nitrate or strontium chloride, the compound of barium element is barium acetate or barium chloride, and the compound of titanium element is titanium butoxide or titanium tetrachloride; the water is distilled water, and the amount of water is 15-20 ml; and the organic solution is an ethylene glycol solution, and the amount of the ethylene glycol solution is 15-20 ml.Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the pH in is 9-12. The step (iii) comprises after stirring the mixture, hydrothermally reacting at 160-180degrees Celsius for 12-48 hours. In the step (iv), the reactant is washed and dried at 60-80degrees Celsius for 3-24 hours.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The diaphragm further comprises an adhesive film layer; in a 180 degrees peel test, the adhesive force between the adhesive film layer and the foamed thermoplastic polyester elastomer film layer is greater than 50 g/25 mm. The elastic recovery rate of the foamed thermoplastic polyester elastomer film layer after 10% strain is less than or equal to 80%. The diaphragm is a single-layer diaphragm, and the single-layer diaphragm is made of a layer of foamed thermoplastic polyester elastomer film; or the diaphragm is a composite diaphragm, and the composite diaphragm includes two layers, three layers, four layers, or five layers. The composite diaphragm includes at least one layer of foamed thermoplastic polyester elastomer film. The thickness of the foaming thermoplastic polyester elastomer film layer is 50-2000 mu m. Preferred Method: The thermal plasticity temperature of the foaming thermoplastic polyester elastomer is 80-200 degrees C. The elongation at break of the foamed thermoplastic polyester elastomer is not less than 150%. The tensile strength of the foamed thermoplastic polyester elastomer is 0.1-50 MPa. The density of the foaming thermoplastic polyester elastomer is 0.1- 1 g/cm3, and the porosity is 10-90%.

PT P
PN WO2021186132-A1; FR3108329-A1
TI Polymer complex obtained by reverse emulsion polymerization of water-soluble monomers in presence of cationic water-soluble host polymer comprising amine functions used to manufacture paper and cardboard.
AU HUND R
   FAUCHER G
   FOUGEROUSE D
AE SPCM SA (SPCM-C)
   SNF SA (SPCM-C)
GA 2021B6030G
AB    NOVELTY - Polymer complex obtained by reverse emulsion polymerization of water-soluble monomers in presence of a cationic water-soluble host polymer comprising amine functions.
   USE - The complex is useful for manufacturing paper and cardboard (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The complex: which is stable and is satisfactory in drainage properties when used in the manufacture of paper and cardboard; improves product performance; reduces the quantity of product necessary for the application therefore implicitly participates in the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions e.g. carbon dioxide; and allows energy savings during the drying step of the sheet of paper which requires less steam.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing (M1) the polymer complex comprising preparation of an aqueous phase comprising at least one host polymer and water-soluble monomers, emulsification of the aqueous solution in an oil phase and obtaining the polymer complex by polymerization of water-soluble monomers; and
   (2) manufacturing (M2) paper, cardboard, according to which, before forming the sheet, the polymer complex is added to a suspension of fibers, at injection points.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The host polymer comprises poly-(dimethylamine(co)epichlorohydrin) and poly(dimethylamine-co-epichlorohydrin-co-ethylenediamine). The host polymer is poly(dimethylamine-co-epichlorohydrin-co-ethylenediamine). The mass ratio between the water-soluble monomers and the host polymer is 99/1 and 1/99, preferably 95/5-40/60. The water-soluble monomers comprise quaternary ammonium salts of dimethylaminoethyl acrylate, quaternary ammonium salts of dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate, dimethyldiallylammonium chloride, acrylamido propyltrimethyl ammonium chloride, methacrylamido propyltrimethyl ammonium chloride, acrylamide, N-isopropylacrylamide, N,N-dimethylacrylamide, N-vinylformamide, N-vinylpyrrolidone, acrylic acid, methacrylic acid, itaconic acid, crotonic acid, maleic acid, fumaric acid, 2-acrylamido-2-methylpropanesulfonic acid, vinylsulfonic acid, vinylphosphonic acid, allylsulfonic acid, allylphosphonic acid, styrene sulfonic acid, the water-soluble salts of an alkali metal, of an alkaline earth metal, or of ammonium of these monomers. Preferred Method: The polymerization is carried out in the absence of branching or crosslinking agent of polyfunctional ethylenic type. The method (M2) comprises on a paper machine, placing fibers in aqueous suspension, preferably cellulosic fibers, adding the polymer complex object, forming a sheet of paper, cardboard or the like on the fabric of the paper machine and drying of the sheet. The method (M2) comprises 3-10000 g/tonne of fibers i.e. cellulose, preferably 30-3000 g/tonne of fibers the polymer complex. The method (M2) comprises adding, before the formation of the sheet, the addition of at least one additive, distinct from the complex comprising coagulants, retention agents, flocculants and starch.
   EXAMPLE - Aqueous phase was prepared by adding 287.8 g acrylamide (50 wt.% solution in water), 210.1 g dimethylaminoethyl acrylate, methyl chloride (80 wt.% solution in water), 0.7 g water and 237.5 g polyamine H-1. The pH of the solution was adjusted to 4-5 with adipic acid. 100-250 ppm/MS of potassium bromate and 800-1500 ppm/MS sodium diethylenetriaminepentaacetate were then added as initiators. The organic phase was prepared by adding to a reactor 214.2 g Exxsol D100S (RTM: Hydrocarbon fluid) oil, 4.7 g of sorbitan monooleate, 8.2 g polyethylene oxide sorbitan monooleate (oxyethylene group), 11.1 g polyoxyethylene sorbitan monooleate (oxyethylene group) and 4.8 g surfactant polymer (Rhodibloc RS (RTM: Amphiphilic block copolymer)). The aqueous phase was then transferred to the organic phase and then emulsified at 8000 rpm for 1 minute in order to obtain a uniform inverse emulsion. The reverse emulsion was deoxygenated with nitrogen bubbling for 30 minutes. The polymerization was initiated by adding sodium bisulfite and the temperature was maintained at 55 degrees C for 1 hour 30 minutes. The reaction medium was finally treated with an excess of sodium bisulfite to reduce the free monomers. Once the inverse emulsion was produced, the Brookfield viscosity was measured.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).)

AU ZHANG Z
AE ZHANG Z (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2021B0861V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of virus-free sponge iron-hydrogen carbon steel industry chain comprises using water containing alkali metal compounds or alkaline earth metal compounds with concentration of 30-100 gmol/L, putting in container set by helicopter, unmanned aerial vehicle and other aircraft, spraying into air before early morning, neutralizing alkaline solution with highly acidic nitrogen oxide, similarly to exhaust gas emitted by automobile engines, especially diesel engines, and industry that both use air as oxidant and produce and emit nitrogen oxide/carbon particles, using pH test paper to check that pH value is less than 8, replacing with new alkaline solution, so that there will be no virus/carbon particles generated and discharged in industry and city.
   USE - The method is used for preparing virus-free sponge iron-hydrogen carbon steel industry chain.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables producing virus-free sponge iron-hydrogen carbon steel industry chain with high value-added product of national development strategy, forming industrial chain, GDP 327.09 billion Yuan, pure profit 315.57 billion Yuan, and no greenhouse gas generated by carbon dioxide.

TI Inorganic casting mold and inner core molding method involves mixing molding sand, sodium silicate or potassium silicate, kaolin and magnesium salt, reacting, forming magnesium silicate on surface of sand, filling sand in mold, and hardening.
AU KOBAYASHI Y
AE OSAKA KEISO KK (OSAK-Non-standard)
GA 2021A74023
AB    NOVELTY - Molding of inorganic casting mold and inner core involves mixing molding sand, sodium silicate or potassium silicate, kaolin and a magnesium salt, reacting to form magnesium silicate on the surface of the molding sand, filling the molding mold with the molding sand on which the magnesium silicate is formed, and curing the mold and the inner core by heating the molding mold or blowing hot air or carbon dioxide gas into the molding mold. At 700-1000 degrees C of the mold and core, the hot strength is maintained up to an arbitrary temperature. The mold and core at a temperature of 50 degrees C directly above after the loss of hot strength are completely disintegrated. The mold and core do not absorb moisture in the molding sand after disintegration and maintain fluidity even during long-term storage.
   USE - Molding of inorganic casting mold and inner core.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables molding of inorganic casting mold and inner ring having excellent fluidity, without hygroscopicity in molding sand after disintegration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molding sand contains siliceous sand as a main component. The kaolin contains at least one of water-containing kaolin or calcined kaolin. The magnesium salt contains at least one of magnesium hydroxide or magnesium carbonate. Preferred Composition: The mol ratio of sodium silicate and potassium silicate is 0.4-4.3. Preferred Process: The method further involves adding water to the mixture.

AU WANG P
AE WANG P (WANG-Individual)
GA 202198455C

TI Pyrometric gas analyzer for determining            concentration of particular gas in measuring cell has            pyrometric sensor that converts infra-red radiation            into electrical signal which is amplified by interface            unit.
AU ZAKHARYAN R A
   OSTAPCHUK E E
   SARASEKO V V
   GARELINA S A
   KHNYCHENKO K E
   KUTSOVSKY L YU
AE FEDERAL STATE BUDGETARY MILITARY EDUCATI (BUDG-Non-standard)
GA 202289275R
AB    NOVELTY - The analyzer has coarse and fine filters that                continuously pumps the test gas sample into a                measuring cuvette. An infra-red (IR) source                generates the optical radiation and is made in the                form of a filament. A cylindrical modulator mirror                rotates around the filament at a certain frequency.                An IR transparent window partially absorbs the                optical radiation by the test gas. The difference                in the signal from IR radiation passed through the                measuring cuvette and directed directly at the                pyroreceiver window is the value of the IR                radiation absorbed in a measuring chamber. A                pyrometric sensor converts IR radiation into an                electrical signal which is amplified by an                interface unit. A measuring microprocessor                determines the concentration of a specific gas in a                measuring cell.
   USE - Pyrometric gas analyzer for determining                concentration of particular gas such as carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide, nitric oxide, nitrogen                dioxide, sulfur monoxide, methane, and propane in                measuring cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The concentrations of gas can be determined.                Number of optical components can be made minimum                due to the use of modulator mirror and one                measuring cell with spherical reflector and one                optical window.

PN CN112850722-A; CN112850722-B
TI Preparing white carbon black and cryolite by using            fluorine-containing silicon slag comprises e.g.            dissolving fluorine-containing silicon slag in sodium            hydroxide solution to obtain first solution and            separating the liquid and solid.
AU CHEN C
   WANG G
   LI J
   XIA F
   LAN Y
   YANG F
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 202161804X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing white carbon black and cryolite by                using fluorine-containing silicon slag insteps                comprises (i) dissolving fluorine-containing                silicon slag in sodium hydroxide solution to obtain                solution A; (ii) introducing carbon dioxide into                the solution A, controlling the pH of the reaction                end point, separating the liquid and solid to                obtain the silicon dioxide wet cake and solution B;                (iii) washing the silicon dioxide wet cake, drying                to obtain white carbon black product; and (iv)                adjusting the pH of the solution B, and adding                aluminum source to the solution B to adjust the mol                ratio of aluminum and fluorine, then introducing                CO2 and controlling the reaction end point pH,                liquid-solid separating to obtain cryolite                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing white                carbon black and cryolite by using                fluorine-containing silicon slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low energy consumption in the                treatment process of fluorine-containing silicon                slag, low equipment requirements, environmental                protection, separation of fluorine and silicon                dioxide, high product added value, and favorable                large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  concentration of sodium hydroxide solution is                  25-300 g/l. In the step (iv), the aluminum source                  is sodium aluminate or mixed solution of aluminum                  salt and sodium hydroxide. Preferred Method: The                  liquid-solid ratio of sodium hydroxide solution                  and fluorine-containing silicon slag is 4-50:1,                  the dissolving temperature is 40-150 degrees C                  and dissolving time is 30-150 minutes. In the                  step (ii), the inlet rate of carbon dioxide is                  5-30 ml/minute, the pH of the reaction end point                  of the solution A is 7.5-10. In the step (iv),                  adjusting he pH of the solution B = 12-14 and the                  molar ratio of aluminum and fluorine is 1:6-8. In                  the step (iv), introducing CO2 speed is 5-30                  ml/min, solution B reaction end pH = 8.5-10.5. In                  the step (iv), subjecting solution B liquid-solid                  separation to obtain solution C, solution C can                  realize the regeneration of sodium hydroxide,                  carbon dioxide and calcium oxide by                  processing.

PN CN112574022-A
TI Preparing alkyl salicylic acid useful for preparing alkyl salicylate-type lubricating oil detergent, comprises contacting salicylic acid and Fischer-Tropsch synthetic oil to carry out alkylation reaction in presence of catalyst.
AU YUAN H
   JIN Z
   ZHANG A
   YUAN W
   LIU S
   AN L
   SHAO G
   LIANG X
   YU Y
   WANG L
AE CHN ENERGY NINGXIA COAL IND CO LTD (CHNE-Non-standard)
GA 202137214Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alkyl salicylic acid comprises contacting salicylic acid and Fischer-Tropsch synthetic oil to carry out an alkylation reaction in the presence of a catalyst, where the Fischer-Tropsch synthetic oil contains 30-70 wt.% alpha -olefin.
   USE - The alkyl salicylic acid is useful for preparing alkyl salicylate-type lubricating oil detergent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes easy to obtain raw materials, and has a higher olefin conversion rate, and the obtains alkyl salicylic acid and alkyl salicylate-type lubricating oil detergent with relatively high quality and has better prospects for industrial applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) alkyl salicylic acid;
   (2) a method for preparing alkyl salicylate-type lubricating oil detergent, comprising (i) preparing alkyl salicylic acid, (ii) in the presence of an organic solvent and an accelerator, contacting the alkyl salicylic acid with excess alkali for neutralization reaction to obtain a material containing alkyl salicylate, and (iii) contacting the material with carbon dioxide and allowing the unreacted base to undergo carbonation reaction; and
   (3) an alkyl salicylate-type lubricating oil detergent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Fischer-Tropsch synthetic oil comprises 35-65 wt.% alpha -olefin, 30-60 wt.% normal alkane, and 3-5 wt.% organic oxygen-containing compound. The number of carbon atoms of the alpha -olefin is 6-32, preferably 10-20. The content of linear alpha -olefin in the alpha -olefin is not less than 95 wt.% and the alpha -olefin contains at least 2 kinds of olefin. The amount of the Fischer-Tropsch synthetic oil based on alpha -olefin is 75-780 pts. wt., preferably 110-425 pts. wt.; the amount of the catalyst is 2-60 pts. wt., preferably 10-30 pts. wt. The molar ratio of the salicylic acid and alpha -olefin is 1.2-1.5:1. The catalyst is an acid catalyst, preferably an organic acid catalyst. The organic acid catalyst is an aryl sulfonic acid with 6-18 carbon atoms, preferably, the organic acid catalyst benzenesulfonic acid, p-toluenesulfonic acid and/or dodecylbenzenesulfonic acid. In the lubricating oil detergent, the amount of the organic solvent is 30-100 pts. wt. , and the amount of the accelerator is 10-40 pts. wt.; The organic solvent is a mixed aromatic hydrocarbon obtained in a process of methanol to propylene, preferably a fraction in the mixed aromatic hydrocarbon with a distillation range of 60-175 degrees C; the accelerator is alcohols having 1-8 carbon atoms, preferably, methanol, ethanol or isopropanol. In the step (ii), the amount of the alkali is 20-120 pts. wt.; the alkali is oxides of alkaline earth metals and/or hydroxides of alkaline earth metals, preferably calcium hydroxide and/or magnesium hydroxide. In the step (iii), the amount of carbon dioxide is 15-65 pts. wt. Preferred Method: The alkylation reaction is carried out in a nitrogen atmosphere and/or an inert gas atmosphere, at 100-150 degrees C, preferably 110-130 degrees C for 2-24 hours, preferably 8-16 hours; and the water generated by the alkylation reaction is removed during the alkylation reaction. The method further comprises removing unreacted raw materials by distillation under reduced pressure at 180-220 degrees C and 0.25-3.5 kPa after removing the catalyst. The method of the neutralization reaction includes heating the organic solvent and the accelerator to 40-60 degrees C, and mixing with 5-20 pts. wt. water, alkali and alkyl salicylic acid and carrying out neutralization reaction at 40-60 degrees C for 1-3 hours.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); H07 (Lubricants and lubrication - this excludes self-lubricating surfaces e.g. PTFE coated surfaces and lubrication systems in general. TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extraction is done for 1.5-2 hours at extracting kettle temperature of 45-55oC and extracting kettle pressure of 25-35 MPa. The supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extraction further involves setting carbon dioxide flow rate to 20-26 kg/hour. High pressure high temperature extracting tank extracting is done by pressurizing to 200 MPa-300, and keeping pressure for 10-15 minutes. Extracting 2 times involves adding 10 times water when first extracting, decocting for 2 hours, adding 8 times water when second extracting, and decocting for 1.5 hours. Vacuum concentrating is done to relative density of 1.2-1.25.
   ACTIVITY - Antianemic.

AB    NOVELTY - An acidic-gas absorber comprises amino acid salt compound and amine compound.
   USE - An acidic-gas absorber for apparatus used for removing acidic-gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Diffusion of absorber component is prevented. The absorbed quantity of acidic gas such as carbon dioxide can be heightened. The absorber requires less energy required when separating acidic gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An acidic-gas absorber comprises amino acid salt compound of formula (2) and amine compound of formula R1-N(R2)-R3 (1).
   R1,R2=2-6C linear alkyl group which has OH;
   R3=3-6C branched alkyl group or cycloalkyl group;
   R4=H or -COOM;
   M=alkali-metal atom; and
   n=2-5.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) removing acidic gas which involves making gas containing acidic gas and acidic-gas absorber to contact and removing acidic gas from gas containing the acidic gas; and
   (2) acidic-gas removal apparatus comprising absorber which removes acidic gas from the gas which contains acidic gas by making the acidic-gas absorber absorb acidic gas by contact with the gas and acidic-gas absorber, regenerator which regenerates the acidic-gas absorber and recycles the acidic-gas absorber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine compound (1) is chosen from N-isopropyldiethanolamine, 3-((2-hydroxyethyl)isopropyl amino)-1-propanol, and N-isopropyl dipropanol amine, N-sec.-butyldiethanolamine, N-sec.-butyldipropanolamine, N-sec.-butyldibutanolamine, N-cyclopentyl-diethanolamine, and N-cyclopentyl-dipropanolamine. The cyclic amino acid salt compound (2) is alpha -amino-acid salt compound. The cyclic amino acid salt compound (2) is chosen from pyrrolidine-2-carboxylic acid sodium salt and piperidine-2-carboxylic acid sodium salt. The acidic-gas absorber further contains amino alcohol compound chosen from monoethanolamine, 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, 2-amino-2-methyl-1,3-dipropanol, diethanolamine, bis(2-hydroxy-1-methyl ethyl)amine, methyldiethanolamine, dimethylethanolamine, diethylethanolamine, triethanolamine, dimethylamino-1-methylethanol, 2-methylamino ethanol, 2-ethylamino ethanol, 2-propylamino ethanol, n-butylamino ethanol, 2-(isopropylamino)ethanol, 3-ethylaminopropanol, triethanolamine, and diethanolamine. The content of amino compound is 10-60 mass% and of cyclic amino compound is 1-40 mass% with respect to 100 mass% acidic-gas absorber.

TI Brewing fruit and western beating by using Kluyveromyces marxii yeast Fim-1 as fermentation strain, by crushing, fruit, removing slag, stirring and dissolving sugar and potassium metabisulfite, inoculating, fermenting, and pasteurizing.
AU LV H
   ZHOU J
   LAN Q
   YU Y
AE UNIV FUDAN (UYFU-C)
GA 2021113046
AB    NOVELTY - Brewing fruit and western beating by using Kluyveromyces marxii yeast Fim-1 as fermentation strain, comprises (1) removing fruit core, crushing, removing slag, and collecting juice; (2) adding suitable amount of sugar, adjusting the sugar degree of the juice is 13-16 degrees Bx; (3) adding 20-100 partspermillion (ppm) potassium metabisulfite, stirring and dissolving for 12-24 hours; (4) inoculating the Kluyveromyces marxii Fim-1 strain; performing liquid fermentation of the juice; the fermentation temperature is 20-30 degrees C; and the fermentation time is 5-14 days; (5) after finishing fermentation, filtering and clarifying the fermentation liquid, then filling carbon dioxide, and canning; and (6) pasteurizing to obtain the fruit wine product.
   USE - The method is useful for brewing fruit and western beating by using Kluyveromyces marxii yeast Fim-1 as fermentation strain.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has short fermentation time, is green and natural, and does not use additives. The fermented product: has bright color, high clarity, and refreshing taste, higher amino acid content, lower alcohol content; and is a kind of nutrient-rich, fruity flavor, and soft feel fruit custard.

The tail gas obtained from preparation of melamine is absorbed by water and/or stripping liquid obtained by stripping to prepare aqueous ammonium carbamate solution. The water content of the stripping gas is less than 4%.

PN CN111747844-A
TI Continuously and quickly preparing propioic acid and derivative by using micro-reactor comprises e.g. adding metallic magnesium, iodine and solvent into reactor under anhydrous and oxygen-free conditions, and uniformly stirring.
AU DENG Q
   HUANG X
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 2020A11441
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously and quickly preparing propioic acid and derivative by using micro-reactor comprises (1) adding metallic magnesium, iodine and solvent into the reactor under anhydrous and oxygen-free conditions, uniformly stirring, dripping halogenated hydrocarbons dropwise, and continuously refluxing and reacting at a certain temperature for 1-3 hours to generate the alkyl magnesium halide Grignard reagent after the dripping is completed, (2) pumping the terminal alkyne and the prepared alkyl magnesium halide into the micro-reactor via an injection pump, reacting for 10 seconds to 10 minutes at a certain temperature, and obtaining alkynyl magnesium halide after Grignard exchange reaction, and (3) injecting the alkynyl magnesium halide and carbon dioxide into the micro-reactor through the injection pump to undergo a nucleophilic addition reaction, and hydrolyzing the product after reacting at a certain temperature for 10 second to 10 minutes.
   USE - The method is useful for continuously and quickly preparing propioic acid and derivative by using micro-reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has rapid and continuous preparation of propioic acid, continuously controllable process, and mild conditions; and realizes industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound of the following formula is used as the terminal alkyne i.e. R-C=C-H where R hydrogen and/or substituted or unsubstituted phenyl, thienyl, alkyl (i.e. cyclohexyl or cyclopropyl). The substituent is alkyl (i.e. methyl), halogen (fluorine, chlorine, bromine), and/or substituted or unsubstituted phenyl. The compound of the following formula is used as halogenated hydrocarbon i.e. R-X, where R is alkyl, alkenyl and/or phenyl, X is chlorine, bromine and/or iodine. The solvent is anhydrous ether and/or tetrahydrofuran. The micro-reactor comprises a micro-mixer and a micro-channel reactor. The micro-mixer and the micro-channel reactor are connected together. The inner diameter of the micro-mixer used is 0.021-10 mm, and the inner diameter of the micro-channel reactor is 0.1-10 mm. In the step (2), the molar ratio of halogenated hydrocarbon to terminal alkyne is 1:(0.8-1.5). In the step (3), the molar ratio of alkynyl magnesium halide to carbon dioxide is 1:(0.8-1.5). Preferred Method: In the step (1), the reflux temperature is 60-100 degrees C. In the step (2), the reaction temperature is -20-40 degrees C. In the step (3), the reaction temperature is 20-40 degrees C.

TI Recycling valuable metal in lithium batteries of energy vehicles comprises e.g. using lithium battery disassembly, disassembling discharged lithium battery, separating anode and cathode sheet, and precipitating lithium.
AU SONG W
AE LIUZHOU YONGANXING AUTOMOBILE TECHNOLOGY (LIUZ-Non-standard)
GA 202091481M
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling valuable metal in lithium batteries of energy vehicles comprises (i) using lithium battery disassembly, disassembling the discharged lithium battery, separating the anode and cathode sheet, then separating the anode material in the anode sheet and the cathode material in the cathode sheet from the aluminum foil, (ii) mixing the separated anode material and cathode material with the calcining agent, and calcinating at low temperature to obtain the calcined material, where the calcining agent includes ammonium bisulfate, (iii) placing the calcined material in a container, adding specific amount of chemical reagents and stirring, allowing to stand for 15 minutes, (iv) adding ammonium carbonate, ammonium bicarbonate or blowing carbon dioxide, precipitating lithium, performing solid-liquid separation to obtain lithium carbonate, and (v) heating and melting the lithium carbonate obtained in the step (iv), removing impurities, adding additives and then melting.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling valuable metal in lithium batteries of energy vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: requires a short time; does not need to consume lot of acid and alkali; saves energy; has no pollution, high degree of enrichment and high product purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The precipitation agent is ammonium oxalate or ammonium hydrogen oxalate. The anode material of the positive plate is titanium-based lead dioxide or titanium coated with precious metals. The cathode material of the negative plate is stainless steel or titanium. In the step (iv), the alkaline solution is ammonium carbonate or ammonium bicarbonate. The alkaline solution is sodium carbonate solution or liquid caustic soda with mass concentration of 10-20%. The additive is alpha -tocopherol.

TI COMPOSITION OF A REGENERATIVE PRODUCT FOR REBREATHERS AND A METHOD FOR PRODUCTION THEREOF.
AU RYLOV YU B
   PLOTNIKOV M YU
   CHAPLYGINA E V
AE ROSKHIMZASHITA CORP STOCK CO (ROSK-Non-standard)
GA 202094639N
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the technology of producing chemical substances used in rebreathers on chemically bound oxygen, and can be used in production of products for regeneration of air based on potassium superoxide. Regenerative product for rebreathers includes potassium superoxide and magnesium silicate, obtained from chrysotile asbestos, with ratio of components in the composition of the regenerative product, wt%: chamber potassium superoxide KO2 9095; magnesium silicate MgSiO3 510. Method of producing a regenerative product for rebreathers involves mixing potassium superoxide with magnesium silicate, followed by molding a mixture, wherein magnesium silicate is obtained from chrysotile asbestos by exposure to temperature of 80020C for 2-3 hours with subsequent grinding in a drum mill. After molding, the regenerative product can be thermally stabilized according to: heating to 20020C at rate of 15C/min; exposure at 20020C for 12 hours. Regenerative product of the presented composition provides, during operation in the composition of the rebreather, a low average and maximum volume fraction of carbon dioxide on inhalation, a low temperature of the regenerated air on inhalation and a longer time of protective action.
   USE - Chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - This provides more comfortable conditions for the user and allows to significantly expand the range of persons who can use these rebreathers.

PT P
PN CN111592018-A
TI Separating and extracting lithium from salt lake            brine involves filling brine chamber with salt lake            brine, and filling the electrolyte chamber with            electrolyte solution, and coating the ion sieve with            selective adsorption of lithium ion.
AU MENG Y
   BA T
AE MENG Y (MENG-Individual)
GA 202085781S
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and extracting lithium from salt                lake brine involves filling brine chamber with salt                lake brine, and filling the electrolyte chamber                with electrolyte solution, and coating the ion                sieve with selective adsorption of lithium ion                (Li+) on the conductive substrate and placing in                the brine chamber of the electrodialysis device,                with the ion sieve as the cathode. The conductive                substrate is taken out from the brine chamber, and                passed carbon dioxide (CO2) and pure water into the                lithium ion sieve for deintercalation, and the                hydrogen ion (H+) generated by CO2 and pure water                replaces Li+ in the ion sieve to obtain lithium                bicarbonate (LiHCO3). The lithium bicarbonate                aqueous solution is heated, and controlled the                temperature and system pressure, and stirred to                precipitate the lithium bicarbonate and decomposed                to obtain the lithium carbonate precipitation. The                precipitate is separated, washed and dried to                obtain lithium carbonate crystals.
   USE - Method for separating and extracting lithium                from salt lake brine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to separated and extracted                lithium from salt lake brine, that has simple                operation, good continuity, low energy consumption,                and is easy to scale.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ion sieve is one                  or a mixture of iron phosphate, lithium titanate,                  and manganese oxide. The iron phosphate is                  Fe1-xMexPO4, where Me is one or a mixture of                  manganese, cobalt, molybdenum, nickel and                  niobium, and the range of x is 0-0.1. The lithium                  titanate is one or a mixture of lithium titanium                  oxide (Li4Ti5O12), LixMeyTi5O12, and                  Li4MemTinO12, where Me is one or a mixture of                  vanadium, iron, cobalt , manganese or fluorine, x                  is 0-4, y is 0-4, m is 0-5 and n is 0-5.                  Preferred Conditions: The CO2 and pure water are                  passed into the adsorbent, and the reaction is                  stirred at a temperature of 10-40 degrees C for                  1-30 minutes. The temperature is controlled at                  60-98 degrees C, the system pressure is                  controlled at -0.01-0.1 megapascal, the stirring                  rate is 200-800 revolutions/minute, and the                  heating is 0.5-1 hours.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)

PN WO2020156941-A1; FR3092334-A1; CN113366093-A; EP3918040-A1; IN202117033378-A; US2022098505-A1; FR3092334-B1
TI Use of fuel composition comprising cuts of            paraffinic hydrocarbons consisting of hydrotreated            vegetable oil(s) and having preset distillation            temperature, for reducing deposits present in internal            components of compression ignition engine.
AU DELORME G
   DAUPHIN R
   GERALDINE D
   ROLAND D
AE TOTAL MARKETING SERVICES (PETF-C)
   TOTAL MARKETING SERVICES (PETF-C)
   TOTAL MARKETING SERVICES SA (PETF-C)
   TOTAL MARKETING SERVICES (PETF-C)
   TOTALENERGIES MARKETING SERVICES (TOTA-Non-standard)
   TOTAL ENERGIES MARKETING SERVICES (PETF-C)
GA 202074012X
AB    NOVELTY - Use of fuel composition for reducing deposits                present in the internal components of a compression                ignition engine, is claimed. The fuel composition                comprises 85 wt.% or more one or more cuts of                paraffinic hydrocarbons consisting of hydrotreated                vegetable oil(s). The cut(s) has distillation                temperature of 100-400 degrees C and paraffin                content of 90 wt.% or more.
   USE - Use of fuel composition for reducing deposits                present in the internal components e.g. combustion                chamber and fuel injection system of compression                ignition engine e.g. diesel engine fitted to                stationary machine or vehicle chosen from light                vehicles, heavy goods vehicles including                medium-duty and heavy-duty vehicles, refuse                collection vehicles, buses, coaches and non-road                vehicles e.g. construction or public works                machinery, tractors, trains and boats (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel composition operated engine at                optimal condition, reduces emission of carbon                dioxide and other pollutants, and is                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of                cleaning deposits in the internal components of                compression ignition engine, which involves                carrying out the combustion of fuel composition in                engine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The dispersion of cut of                  paraffinic hydrocarbons is 130-350 degrees C,                  preferably 150-320 degrees C. Preferred                  Composition: The paraffin content of cut of                  paraffinic hydrocarbons is 95 wt.% or more,                  preferably 99.9 wt.% or more. The cut of                  paraffinic hydrocarbons contains 50 wt.% or more,                  preferably 90 wt.% or more isoparaffin. The fuel                  composition contains 90 wt.% or more, preferably                  99.5 wt.% or more paraffinic hydrocarbon cut(s).                  The fuel composition further contains 5-1000 ppm,                  preferably 50-200 ppm additive consisting of a                  quaternary ammonium salt, obtained by reaction                  with an agent for quaternizing a nitrogen                  compound comprising a tertiary amine functional                  group, preferably reaction product of an                  acylating agent substituted with a hydrocarbon                  group and a compound comprising at least one                  tertiary amine group and at least one group                  chosen from primary amines, secondary amines and                  alcohols. The acylating agent substituted with a                  hydrocarbon group is a polybutenyl succinic                  anhydride. The compound comprising at least one                  tertiary amine group and at least one group                  chosen from primary amines, secondary amines and                  alcohols is chosen from amines of formulae (I)                  and (II). The composition further contains 2-500                  ppm, preferably 10-150 ppm additive consisting of                  an antioxidant chosen from compounds comprising a                  phenol group, preferably alkylphenol, 0.5-25 ppm                  amine antioxidants chosen from aliphatic,                  cycloaliphatic and aromatic amines, preferably                  dicyclohexylamine, 0.5-25 ppm one or more metal                  passivators chosen from amines substituted by                  triazole groups, preferably                  N,N-bis(2-ethylhexyl)-((1,2,4-triazol-l-yl)methyl)                  amine and                  N,N'-bis-(2-ethylhexyl)-4-methyl-1H-benzotriazole                  amine, 0.2-50 ppm one or more chelating agents,                  preferably chosen from amines substituted by                  N,N'-disalicylidene groups, preferably                  N,N'-disalicylidene-1,2-diaminopropane.R6,R7 = 1-22C alkyl;X = 1-20C alkylene;m = 1-5;n = 0-20;andR8 = H or 1-22C alkyl.
DC A95 (Transport - including vehicle parts, tyres and armaments.); E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

TI Biotechnological filtering system for environmental pollution, comprises natural filter composed of varieties of bryophytes (moss), contained in panels installed in the structure of the system, with function of filtering polluting particles.
AU JIMENEZ VANO J
AE JIMENEZ VANO J (VANO-Individual)
GA 202086623L
AB    NOVELTY - The biotechnological filtering system, comprises a natural filter composed of varieties of bryophytes (moss), contained in panels installed in the structure of the system, with the function of filtering polluting particles. The fans (2) attached to the structure of the system, which recirculate the air flow through the filters. An irrigation system (3) distributed in the structure of the system that provides the required humidity to the filters. A solar panel (4) on the surface of the structure of the system, which provides energy to the whole, without being the only source of energy. The panel charges a battery (5) housed inside the system. A meteorological station (6) and a pollution measurement station (7), installed in the system itself. There is a digital monitor (8) that shows the information collected.
   USE - Biotechnological filtering system for environmental pollution.
   ADVANTAGE - Biotechnological filtering system reduces atmospheric pollution, absorbs carbon dioxide and produces oxygen independently, having high pollutant filtering capabilities.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a biotechnological filtering system.
   Fans (2)
   Irrigation system (3)
   Solar panel (4)
   Battery (5)
   Meteorological station (6)
   Pollution measurement station (7)
   Digital monitor (8)

TI Waste heat generating device, has slag cooler provided with discharging port, water outlet and water inlet that are connected with evaporative condenser through pipeline, and bypass heat exchanger connected with cooling tower through two branches.
AU CAO M
   ZHAO L
AE JIANGSU JUXIN ENERGY SAVING & ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHN (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202072461A
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a waste heat generating device, comprising a slag cooler, wherein the slag cooler is provided with a discharging port; the water outlet and the water inlet, the water outlet and the water inlet are connected with the evaporative condenser through the first pipeline; the evaporative condenser is connected with the ORC generating set; the evaporative condenser and the ORC generating set are connected with the cooling tower through the second pipeline; the first pipeline is provided with a first branch; the other side of the branch is provided with a bypass heat exchanger; the bypass heat exchanger is connected with the cooling tower through two second branches; compared with the existing technology, the technical solution is simple and reliable; it not only has economic benefit, but also has environment-friendly benefit, the economic benefit is that the electric power can be used and internet, environment-friendly benefit is reducing carbon dioxide emission, and with the national policy of one pulse.
DC Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P)); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23)); Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24))

PT P
PN CN111440773-A
TI Optimized culture method for transformation and expansion of chimeric antigen receptors, involves purifying T cells, mixing potassium ion medium, dripping magnetic bead suspension, culturing, performing lentivirus infection and testing.
AU WEI F
   ZHANG C
   CHANG H
   WANG H
   MA X
AE UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
GA 202071711W
AB    NOVELTY - Optimized culture method for transformation and expansion of chimeric antigen receptors, involves (i) isolating and purifying CD4+ and CD8+ T cells, (ii) preparing high-concentration potassium ion medium, (iii) mixing 500000 CD4+ T cells with the CD8+ T cells, and resuspending with 0.5 ml high-concentration potassium ion medium to obtain a cell suspension, adding the cell suspension to a 24-well cell culture plate, dripping magnetic bead suspension, placing in a 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, and culturing for 24 hours, (iv) performing lentivirus infection, (v) isolating modified T cells, (vi) expanding the modified T cells, and (vii) after culturing for 9 days, performing killing test when the T cells grow into a plateau phase to detect the killing efficiency.
   USE - Optimized culture method for transformation and expansion of chimeric antigen receptors.
   ADVANTAGE - The method introduces certain concentration of potassium ions during the transformation and expansion of the chimeric antigen receptor of T cells to improve the activation level of CAR T cells and killing ability, has simple operation, excellent repeatability, low cost, and high application value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves performing cell counting, and separating and purifying CD4+ and CD8+ T cells, where the cell counting is performed using a cell counting plate or a cell counter. The CD4+ T cells and CD8+ T cells are separated and purified from human peripheral blood peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC). In step (iii), the magnetic bead suspension is prepared by (a) amplifying magnetic beads on 2 million CD3 (cluster of differentiation-3)/CD28 cells, adding 5 times high-concentration potassium ion medium, and (b) using a magnet to adsorb the CD3/CD28 cell expansion magnetic beads and removing the liquid to complete a wash, and repeating steps (a) and step (b) 3 times and resuspending with 0.5 ml high-concentration potassium ion medium to obtain the magnetic bead suspension. The step (iv) involves adding lentivirus loaded with the CAR T gene (chimeric antigen receptor T-cell therapy), adding the high-concentration potassium ion medium until the liquid in the well is full, after 3 days of culture, aspirating 2 ml culture supernatant, and adding 2 ml high-concentration potassium ion medium. The step (v) involves separating the modified T cells from the magnetic beads by pipetting after culturing for 5 days, and removing the magnetic beads using a magnet. The step (vi) involves placing the T cells in a 6-well cell culture plate, adding high-concentration potassium ion medium/ml of the 500000 T cells to continue culturing, culturing for 7-8 days, adding high-concentration potassium ion medium/ml of the 500000 T cells. Preferred Composition: The mass concentration of potassium chloride in the high-concentration potassium ion medium is 2500-4800 mg/l. Preferred Conditions: In step (vii), the killing test is a luciferase target cell killing test.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-F04B1A; B04-F04B1E; B04-K01R0E; B05-A01A; B05-C04; B11-A04A; D05-H08B
IP C12N-005/0783; C12N-005/10

PT P
PN CN111430832-A; CN111430832-B
TI Discharge pretreatment free waste ternary lithium-ion battery full-resource recovery method, involves separating separator from coarsely shredded waste battery particles, and combining lithium-containing waste liquid to recover lithium.
AU ZHOU X
   TANG J
   YANG J
   WANG H
   MA Y
   ZHOU H
   LIU X
   WANG P
   ZHOU J
   ZHOU C
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
   HUNAN XIFU ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECH (HUNA-Non-standard)
   HUNAN CHENYU FUJI NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202070191P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing a waste ternary lithium-ion battery with dry ice. The waste ternary lithium-ion battery is crushed under a protective atmosphere. Temperature of a system is adjusted. Solid-liquid separation is performed to obtain coarsely crushed waste battery particles and a fluid enriched with waste electrolyte. Carbon dioxide in the fluid is separated to obtain a waste electrolyte. The waste electrolyte is separated to obtain an organic solvent and a lithium-containing conductive agent. A separator is separated from coarsely shredded waste battery particles. Lithium-containing waste liquid is combined to recover lithium.
   USE - Discharge pretreatment free waste ternary lithium-ion battery full-resource recovery method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing full resource recovery of each component without pre-discharging the waste battery in the product obtained from the recovery, regenerating electrolyte, reutilziing positive electrode active material and the negative electrode active material directly and improving performance of the recovered conductive agent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a Discharge pretreatment free waste ternary lithium-ion battery full-resource recovery method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

AU ZHAN Z
   SHAN S
   LIN Z
   ZOU X
   WANG P
   ZHANG J
AE FUJIAN SINO-SCI BIOLOGY CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202053671D
AB    NOVELTY - A phenolic compound ZKYY-037 (I), is                new.
   USE - The phenolic compound ZKYY-037 (I) is useful                in preparation of tumor cell proliferation                inhibitors, or a medicament for treating tumor                diseases, preferably liver cancer or lung cancer                (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A phenolic compound ZKYY-037 of formula (I),                is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) method for preparing the compound (I)                involves (a) successively subjecting inflorescences                of hemp plants to carbon dioxide supercritical                extraction and ethanol extraction to obtain crude                extract, (b) dissolving obtained crude extract and                passing through normal phase silica gel column                chromatography, macroporous adsorption resin column                chromatography, first-stage medium-pressure normal                phase silica gel column chromatography, two-stage                medium pressure normal phase silica gel column                chromatography, three-stage medium pressure normal                phase silica gel column chromatography, and                high-pressure reverse-phase HPLC chromatography to                separate the compound (I);
   (2) use of compound ZKYY-037 (I) in                preparation of tumor cell proliferation inhibitors,                or a medicament for treating tumor diseases;                and
   (3) tumor cell proliferation inhibitor drug or                anti-tumor drug comprising an active ingredient and                a auxiliary material, where the active ingredient                comprises compound (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The medicament                  comprises compound (I), and the preferred                  effective dose is 572 ng/a single dose. Preferred                  Method: The method involves performing carbon                  dioxide supercritical extraction conditions by                  using ethanol for ethanol extraction in an amount                  of 15-25% of hemp plant inflorescence weight for                  30-60 minutes, completely dissolving crude                  extract with petroleum ether, performing normal                  phase silica gel column chromatography using                  n-hexane/ethyl acetate of 98:2 as eluent for                  isocratic elution, performing macroporous                  adsorption resin column chromatography using D101                  macroporous adsorption resin column, with 30-100%                  ethanol/water as eluent for gradient elution,                  performing first-stage medium-pressure                  normal-phase silica gel column chromatography                  using dichloromethane/ethyl acetate as eluent for                  gradient elution, performing second medium                  pressure normal phase silica gel column                  chromatography using n-hexane/ether as eluent for                  gradient elution, performing three-stage                  medium-pressure normal-phase silica gel column                  chromatography using n-hexane/ether as an eluent                  for gradient elution, and performing                  high-pressure reversed-phase HPLC chromatography                  uses acetonitrile/water solution as eluent for                  gradient elution.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
   ADMINISTRATION - The medicine in form of dripping pill, tablet,                capsule, granule or oral liquid is administered by                oral route or through injections (powder injection)                (all claimed).

   USE - Gel particles used in oil and gas extraction                (claimed).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator includes                  at least one of ammonium persulfate and potassium                  persulfate, the aqueous phase includes at least                  one of distilled water and deionized water.

The reinforcing agent                  is polyethylene glycol, preferably polyethylene                  glycol-200.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves configuring cationic polymer into                  solution and adding gel particles dispersed in                  solution to obtain product, injecting into                  formation in gel particles before injection front                  section plug as sacrificial agent to stratum.                  Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  cationic polymer is 500-2000 mg/L. The cationic                  polyacrylamide 8030 has concentration of 0.05-0.2                  %mass. The injection volume of profile control                  agent is 5-50%, and injection volume of                  sacrificial agent is 0.1-2% of formation pore                  volume. PN CN110974998-A
TI Degradable polyurethane foam dressing comprises            poly (gamma-butyrolactone) glycol,            2,2-dimethylolpropionic acid, diisocyanate, catalyst,            small molecule chain extender, surfactant and            anti-inflammatory bacteriostatic agent.
AU LI Z
   SHEN Y
   KANG F
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 202031471P
AB    NOVELTY - Degradable polyurethane foam dressing                comprises 20-200 pts. wt. poly ( gamma                -butyrolactone) glycol, 5-20 pts. wt.                2,2-dimethylolpropionic acid, 10-20 pts. wt.                diisocyanate, 0.5-5 pts. wt. catalyst, 1-8 pts. wt.                small molecule chain extender, 2-10 pts. wt.                surfactant and 0.5-2 pts. wt. anti-inflammatory                bacteriostatic agent.
   USE - As degradable polyurethane foam                dressing.
   ADVANTAGE - The degradable polyurethane foam dressing has                excellent biocompatibility and fast liquid                absorption, and promotes the wound recovery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the foam dressing, involving (a)                uniformly mixing poly ( gamma -butyrolactone)                glycol, 2,2-dimethylolpropionic acid,                small-molecular chain extender, catalyst,                surfactant and anti-inflammatory bacteriostatic                agent, heating at 50-120 degrees C, dripping                diisocyanate to a mixed solution containing                macromolecular polyol, and reacting for 4-8 hours                to obtain a degradable polyurethane elastomer, (b)                foaming obtained polyurethane elastomer by adopting                supercritical carbon dioxide foaming process to                obtain a degradable polyurethane closed cell foam                material, and (c) ultrasonically treating the                obtained degradable polyurethane closed cell foam                material to obtain degradable polyurethane foam                dressing with certain aperture ratio.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diisocyanate is                  aliphatic or alicyclic diisocyanate, preferably                  hexamethylene diisocyanate, isophorone                  diisocyanate, dicyclohexylmethane diisocyanate,                  1,4-cyclohexane diisocyanate, cyclohexane                  dimethylene diisocyanate, norbornane                  diisocyanate, methylcyclohexyl diisocyanate                  and/or dicyclohexyl methylene diisocyanate. The                  catalyst comprises triethylamine,                  ethylenediamine, triethylenediamine, bis                  (dimethylaminoethyl) ether, 4,4'-(1,2-ethylene)                  bismorpholine, 2,2- dimorpholinyl diethyl ether,                  triethanolamine and/or sodium bicarbonate. The                  small molecule chain extender is aliphatic or                  alicyclic small molecule chain extender,                  preferably ethylene glycol, propylene glycol,                  1,2-butanediol, 1,4-butanediol, diethylene                  glycol, Tripropylene glycol, diethylpentanediol,                  3-methyl-1,5-pentanediol, 3-methylpentanediol,                  1,5-pentanediol, 1,4-dimethylolcyclohexane,                  neopentyl glycol, 1,4-cyclohexanediamine,                  trimethylethylenediamine and/or diethanolamine.                  The surfactant is silane-based surfactant,                  preferred brand number comprising B8525, B8474,                  L6900 and/or TN1078.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The anti-inflammatory                  bacteriostatic agent comprises chitosan,                  ibuprofen and/or acetaminophen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The molecular weight                  of poly ( gamma -butyrolactone) glycol is                  500-4000 g/mol. The prepared polyurethane foam                  has closed cell structure, the expansion ratio is                  2-30, and the average cell size is 0.1-500 mu m.                  Preferred Conditions: In supercritical carbon                  dioxide foaming process, supercritical carbon                  dioxide is used as foaming agent, where the                  foaming temperature is carried out at 100-200                  degrees C under foaming pressure of 8-30 MPa for                  0.5-3 hours, and the pressure relief rate is 1-                  10 MPa/second. In process of preparing an                  open-cell foam by ultrasonically treating under                  ultrasonic frequency of 10-100 kHz for 0.5-3                  hours, and the prepared polyurethane foam has                  open cell rate of 10-100%.

   LIU H
AE NINGBO AUX ELECTRIC CO LTD (AUXS-C)
GA 202020871V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining indoor air quality parameter. Judgment is made to check whether to execute changing of air according to the indoor air quality parameter. PN CN110732016-A
TI Essential oil useful for treating dysmenorrhea, comprises cinnamon essential oil, Evodia rutaecarpa essential oil, dried ginger essential oil, Angelica essential oil, Cyperus rotundus essential oil, fennel essential oil and borneol.
AU MIAO M
   XU E
   XIONG W
AE UNIV HENAN CHINESE MEDICINE (HECO-C)
GA 202011345M
AB    NOVELTY - Essential oil comprises 5-7 ml cinnamon essential oil, 5-7 ml Evodia rutaecarpa essential oil, 5-7 ml dried ginger essential oil, 5-7 ml Angelica essential oil, 5-7 ml Cyperus rotundus essential oil, 4-6 ml fennel essential oil and 5-7 g borneol. The essential oil is obtained by dissolving and mixing uniformly the raw materials. The preparation of cinnamon essential oil comprises crushing cinnamon to obtain 10-20 mesh sieve coarse powder and carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction to obtain cinnamon essential oil, where the weight ratio of liquid to material is 1:10-20, the extraction temperature is 42-50 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 20-25 MPa, the separation temperature is 50-60 degrees C, the separation pressure is 4.5-5.5 MPa and the extraction time is 1.5-2.5 hours. The remaining essential oils are extracted according to the above method.
   USE - The essential oil is useful for treating dysmenorrhea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy to operate, produces good quality product with stable and reliable curative effect, and has economic and social benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The essential oil comprises 5.5 ml cinnamon essential oil, 6.5 ml Evodia rutaecarpa essential oil, 5.5 ml dried ginger essential oil, 5.5 ml Angelica essential oil, 5.5 ml Cyperus rotundus essential oil, 4.5 ml fennel essential oil and 6.5 g borneol.
   ACTIVITY - Analgesic; Gynecological.

PN CN110615424-A; CN110615424-B
TI Preparing nitrogen-phosphorus doped porous carbon material comprises e.g. carrying carbonization on organic phosphate in carbonization furnace, continuously introducing protective gas during carbonization process, and heating.
AU WANG S
   LIU Z
   ZHANG L
   XU Y
   REN B
AE ENERGY RES INST HEBEI ACAD SCI (ENER-Non-standard)
GA 2020018042
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen-phosphorus doped porous carbon material comprises (1) carrying carbonization on organic phosphate in carbonization furnace at high temperature under vacuum condition, continuously introducing protective gas at rate of 15-45 ml/minute during the carbonization process, heating from room temperature to 185-215 degrees C at rate of 3-8 degrees C/minute, keeping the temperature for 1.5-2.5 hours, continuously heating to 900 plus minus 10 degrees C at rate of 3-8 degrees C/minutes, keeping the temperature for 1-2.5 hours, slowly cooling to room temperature to obtain black carbon material; and (2) acid cleaning with hydrochloric acid solution, ultrasonic cleaning, half an hour each time, three times cleaning, to obtain nitrogen-phosphorus doped porous carbon material.
   USE - The porous carbon material is useful for supercapacitors and carbon dioxide adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The porous carbon material: has uniform nitrogen and phosphorus atom doping, rich and uniform pore size distribution, large specific surface area and pore volume, electrical and adsorption properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for nitrogen-phosphorus doped porous carbon material is prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, carrying hydrothermal polymerization pretreatment before step (1) by adding the organic phosphate into hydrothermal reaction kettle, heating by microwave chemical synthesizer or oven, hydrothermally reacting at 120-170 degrees C for 3-4 hours, suction filtering the product and drying. In the method, the heating parameters of microwave chemical synthesizer includes power is 800-1200 W, and pressure is 0.36-0.52 MPa. Preferred Components: The organic phosphate is bis 1,6-hexylenetriamine pentamethylenephosphonate sodium, pentasodium diethylenetriamine pentamethylphosphonic acid, hexamethylene diamine tetramethylphosphonic acid potassium salt or bis 1,6-hexylenetriamine pentamethylenephosphonate sodium. The volume ratio of the organic phosphate to water is 1:35-44. The protective gas is nitrogen (N2)/argon. The concentration of the hydrochloric acid is 0.05-0.15 mol/l.
