PT P
PN WO2019183842-A1; RU2767667-C1
TI Composite catalyst useful in preparing ethylene to            break ASF distribution law for hydrocarbon in the            Fischer-Tropsch synthesis, comprises zirconium-based            oxide and modified acidic molecular sieve which is            treated with pre-adsorbed alkali.
AU LIU S
   ZHU W
   LIU Z
   NI Y
   LIU H
   LIU Y
   MA X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2019834127
AB    NOVELTY - Composite catalyst comprises 10-90 wt.%                zirconium-based oxide and 10-90 wt.% modified                acidic molecular sieve, where the modified acidic                molecular sieve is an acidic molecular sieve                treated by a pre-adsorbed alkali.
   USE - The composite catalyst is useful in preparing                ethylene to break ASF distribution law for                hydrocarbon in Fischer-Tropsch (F-T)                synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst has high ethylene                selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for                :
   (1) preparing composite catalyst, comprising                (i) obtaining a zirconium-based oxide, (ii)                obtaining a modified acidic molecular sieve, and                (iii) combining the components comprising the                zirconium-based oxide and the modified acidic                molecular sieve by ultrasonic assisted chemical                compounding and/or physical compounding to obtain                the composite catalyst; and
   (2) preparing ethylene, comprising passing the                raw material gas containing synthesis gas through a                reactor equipped with a composite catalyst to                obtain ethylene, where the synthesis gas comprises                carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide in                molar ratio of 1:0.2-4:0-1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pre-adsorbed alkali                  treatment comprises contacting the acidic                  molecular sieve with organic alkali containing                  gas at 150-350 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours, where                  the mass space velocity of the organic alkali                  containing gas is 300-6000 ml/g1/h1. The step (i)                  comprises preparing zirconium-based oxide by                  coprecipitation method, a dipping method, and a                  mechanical mixing method, where the                  coprecipitation method comprises mixing solution                  containing element X element, M element and Zr                  element with solution containing precipitant in                  cocurrent manner under stirring, controlling the                  pH of the system to 7-9 after the completion of                  precipitation, separating the solid phase,                  washing, drying and calcining to obtain the                  zirconium-based oxide, the impregnation method                  comprises immersing the zirconia powder in a salt                  solution containing the X element and the M                  element or immersing the zirconia powder and the                  oxide of X in a salt solution containing the M                  element or zirconia, immersing the powder and the                  oxide of M in a salt solution containing X                  element, removing the solvent, drying, and                  calcining to obtain the zirconium-based oxide,                  where the M is copper, argentium, zinc,                  manganese, yttrium, niobium, gallium, indium or                  chromium. The pre-adsorbed alkali treatment                  comprises activating the acidic molecular sieve                  in an inert gas atmosphere, adjusting the                  temperature to the pre-adsorption alkali                  treatment temperature, contacting with organic                  alkali containing gas, saturating the adsorption,                  and purging the mixture to room temperature to                  obtain a modified acidic molecular sieve. The                  activation temperature is 300-500 degrees C, and                  the activation time is 3-5 hours. The aging time                  in the coprecipitation method is 2-4 hours, and                  the firing condition is 400-600 degrees C                  calcination for 1-6 hours, the immersion time in                  the dipping method is 1-6 hours, drying                  conditions are: drying at 60-200 degrees C for                  1-10 hours, the firing conditions are: calcining                  at 400-600 degrees C for 1-6 hours. In the step                  (iii), ultrasonic assisted chemical compounding                  method comprises ultrasonically dissolving a                  solution containing a zirconium-based oxide and a                  modified acidic molecular sieve, solid-liquid                  separation, drying and calcining the solid phase                  to obtain the composite catalyst, and the                  physical compounding method comprises compounding                  a mixture containing a zirconium-based oxide and                  a modified acidic molecular sieve by mechanical                  mixing, ball milling and/or shaking to obtain the                  composite catalyst. The ultrasonic assisted                  chemical compounding method is 10 minutes to 3                  hours, a drying temperature of 60-150 degrees C                  and a calcination temperature of 300-650 degrees                  C. In the preparation of ethylene, the reaction                  temperature is 250-380 degrees C, the pressure is                  1.0-8.0 MPa, and the gas mass space velocity is                  300-10000 ml/g1/h1 Preferred Components: The                  acidic molecule is screened from a molecular                  sieve having a MOR topology, molecular sieve with                  FER topology, a eutectic molecular sieve                  containing MOR topology and a FER topology or                  mixed crystal molecular sieve containing a MOR                  topology and an FER topology. The molecular sieve                  having the MOR topology is an H-MOR molecular                  sieve having a skeleton atom having                  silicon/alluminum ratio of 4-60, the molecular                  sieve having the FER topology is an H-ZSM-35                  molecular sieve having a skeleton atom                  silicon/alluminum ratio of 5-50. The                  zirconium-based oxide is the compounds having the                  formula (XaMb(ZrO2)1-a-b) (I). The X element, the                  M element, and the Zr element in the solution are                  independently derived from a nitrate, a                  hydrochloride, acetate, acetylacetonate and/or a                  sulfate. In the preparation of ethylene the raw                  material gas further comprises inert gas                  including nitrogen, argon, helium and/or methane,                  the volume content of the inert gas in the mixed                  gas is greater than or equal to 10%. The reactor                  is fixed bed reactor, a fluidized bed reactor,                  and a moving bed reactor.X = silicon, alluminum, titanium, or oxide                  of cerenium or lanthanum;M = oxide of copper, argentium, zinc,                  manganese, yttrium, niobium, gallium, indium or                  chromium;a = 0.02-0.9; andb = 0.0-0.8.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic alkali                  containing gas comprises carrier gas and organic                  alkali, the carrier gas is nitrogen, helium,                  carbon dioxide, argon and/or hydrogen; The                  organic alkali is trimethylamine, diethylamine,                  triethylamine, pyridazine, pyrimidine, pyrazine,                  pyridine, imidazole, N-methylimidazole,                  N-ethylimidazole, N-propylimidazole, and/or                  N-isopropyl imidazole, and the volume fraction of                  the organic alkali in the organic alkali                  containing gas is 0.1-10%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Three-layered stainless steel and carbon steel composite pipe manufacturing method, involves arranging interlayer between inner stainless steel covering section and steel cladding section, where temperature of interlayer is controlled.
AU CHEN J
AE LVHUA INVESTMENT CO LTD (LVHU-Non-standard)
GA 201969534C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves arranging three stainless steels and a carbon steel composite steel pipe to bend a composite plate such that a forming mold is bent to form a non-sealed pipeline, which is formed with a welding seam. Two end surfaces of a middle carbon steel layer are arranged with an inner stainless steel covering section and an outer stainless steel cladding section through a carbon dioxide gas shielded welding or a high-melting deep fast welding. Argon gas inner and outer protective welding process is performed to the inner stainless steel covering section. Transition gap is formed between the middle carbon steel layer and the inner stainless steel covering section. An interlayer is arranged between the inner stainless steel covering section and the outer stainless steel cladding section, where temperature of the interlayer is controlled at 60 degrees centigrade or less.
   USE - Three-layered stainless steel and carbon steel composite pipe manufacturing method.

TI Amphiphobic proppant particle for hydraulic fracture stimulation comprises amphiphobic material infused into internal interconnected porosity of proppant particle and degradable coating comprising degradable polymer blended with material.
AU CANOVA S
   CANNAN C
AE CARBO CERAMICS INC (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 201964312K
AB    NOVELTY - An amphiphobic proppant particle comprises proppant particle having internal interconnected porosity of 5-75%, size of 8-140 mesh, and density of less than or equal to 4 g/cm3; amphiphobic material infused into at least a portion of internal interconnected porosity; and degradable coating formed on outer surface of proppant particle, where degradable coating comprises degradable polymer blended with amphiphobic material.
   USE - An amphiphobic proppant particle for hydraulic fracture stimulation of oil and gas reservoirs.
   ADVANTAGE - The amphiphobic proppant particle reduces conductivity loss due to fracturing fluids, alters or modifies proppant wettability to control relative permeability to flow of the fluids which may be encountered in the reservoir, lubricates the proppant to allow more efficient proppant arrangement when the fracture closes, effectively increasing packing efficiency and reducing the extent of proppant crushing, reduces eventual scale buildup on proppant, and reduces chemical reactivity of proppant to materials encountered in the reservoir or well treatment, including but not limited to, oil, gas, water, brine, fracturing fluids, remedial acid treatments, caustic fluids commonly associated with steam or water injection, biological agents, or their byproducts, such as carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Proppant particle comprises 30-99.9 wt.% alumina on a calcined basis, where the interconnected porosity is 20-75%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: Amphiphobic material is selected from fluorinated poly(acrylates), fluorinated silanes, and/or fluorinated siloxanes, preferably fluorinated poly(acrylate). Fluorinated poly(acrylate) comprises monomers selected from fluorohexyl acrylate, fluoroaryl acrylate, 2-(perfluorooctyl)ethyl acrylate, heptafluorobutyl acrylate, 1H,1H,9H-hexadecafluorononyl acrylate, 2,2,3,4,4,4-hexafluorobutyl acrylate, hexafluoroisopropyl acrylate, 1H,1H,5H-octafluoropentyl acrylate, pentafluorobenzyl acrylate, pentafluorophenyl acrylate, perfluorocyclohexyl methyl acrylate, perfluoroheptoxypoly(propyloxy) acrylate, perfluorooctyl acrylate, 1H,1H-perfluorooctyl acrylate, 2,2,3,3-tetrafluoropropyl acrylate, 2,2,2-trifluoroethyl acrylate, 3-(trifluoromethyl)benzyl acrylate, 2-(N-butylperfluorooctanesulfamido) ethyl acrylate, 1H,1H,7H-dodecafluoroheptyl acrylate, 1H,1H,11H-eicosafluoroundecyl acrylate, trihydroperfluoroundecyl acrylate, trihydroperfluoroheptyl acrylate, and 2-(N-ethylperfluorooctane sulfamido) ethyl acrylate. Fluorinated poly(acrylate) comprises a combination of repeating units derived from monomer C6F13CH=CHROCOCH=CH2. Degradable coating comprises a polyvinyl alcohol.R=CH2, CH2OCH2CH2, or (OCH2CH2CH2)1.6.Preferred Properties: Outer surface of amphiphobic proppant particle has hydrophobicity value, as measured by water droplet contact angle, of greater than or equal to 90 degrees ; and oleophobicity value, as measured by an oil droplet contact angle, of greater than or equal to 90 degrees .

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon aluminate cement, involves                weighing 1.0-8.0 pts. wt. calcium carbonate,                2.0-9.0 pts. wt. aluminate minerals, 0.5-2.0 pts.                wt. a setting agent, and 0.1-0.3 pts. wt.                reinforcing agent, mixing calcium carbonate and the                aluminate mineral and grinding to form a mixture                having a specific surface area of 400-700 m2 /kg.                The powder is placed in a baking furnace for light                burning treatment, and after cooling to room                temperature, and stored under dry conditions. The                coagulant and the reinforcing agent are added to                the light-treated powder for secondary mixing, and                obtaining carbon aluminate cement.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon aluminate                cement.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare carbon aluminate                cement, which is relatively simple, operation is                easy, high temperature calcination is not needed,                promotion is convenient, raw materials are widely                available and the price is low, reduces carbon                dioxide emissions and reduces environmental                pollution.

The setting agent is dihydrate                  gypsum. The reinforcing agent is one of sodium                  hexametaphosphate, triisopropanolamine,                  triethanolamine or magnesium hexafluorosilicate.                  The light burning temperature is 300-600 degrees                  C, and the light burning time is 20-60                  minutes.

PT P
PN CN109957428-A
TI Methanating and decarburizing biomass pyrolysis gas in constant temperature fixed bed comprises e.g. transferring biomass pyrolysis gas into desulfurization treatment apparatus, cooling produced mixed gas, pressurizing and drying.
AU MENG H
   YAO Z
   ZHAO L
   CONG H
   JIA J
   HUO L
   WU Y
AE CHINESE ACAD AGRIC ENG PLANNING & DESIGN (CHAG-Non-standard)
GA 201960169K
AB    NOVELTY - Methanating and decarburizing biomass pyrolysis gas in constant temperature fixed bed comprises (i) transferring biomass pyrolysis gas coming out of reactor from gas collection tank into desulfurization treatment apparatus after compression, (ii) adding certain amount of hydrogen and water vapor and promoting methanation to obtain mixed gas, where content of hydrogen is 4-12 wt.% and water vapor is 3-10 wt.%, (iii) cooling produced mixed gas at constant temperature in a methanation reactor, transferring into heat exchanger, pressurizing mixed gas by compressor, then transferring into adiabatic methanation reactor and collecting remaining mixture by solid-liquid separator, (iv) transferring gas-water mixture into air bag and collecting liquid water in methanation reactor through circulation pump, and (v) transferring produced gaseous mixture by solid-liquid separator into absorption tower to remove carbon dioxide, scrubbing decarburized mixture and drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for methanating and decarburizing biomass pyrolysis gas in constant temperature fixed bed.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has mild reaction conditions, increased calorific value, and enhanced thermal stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The biomass pyrolysis gas from reactor has steady flow rate. The water vapor is obtained by heating of a superheater. The gas component of pyrolysis gas has hydrogen content of 4-12%. Preferred Method: The method comprises separately adding mixed pyrolysis gas into adiabatic methanation reactor and constant temperature methanation reactor, connecting parallel relationship between adiabatic methanation reactor and constant temperature methanation reactor in series achieved by timely heat exchange through two-stage heat exchanger. The method comprises recycling carbon dioxide absorbent by the action of circulation pump and high-temperature steam heating. The methanation reaction is strongly exothermic reaction carried out at 74 degrees C per 1% carbon monoxide and diluting mixture by the cycle of pyrolysis gas to reduce the concentration of carbon monoxide in the mixture to 3-5%. The presence of sulfur element has an effect on later process and equipment, and performing desulfurization treatment before the reaction. The method comprises upgrading gas after methanation reaction, performing carbon dioxide removal and desiccation treatment, subjecting air bag to cyclic heat exchange process, coupling constant temperature methanation and adiabatic methanation by series-parallel technique, transferring high temperature steam generated by methanation waste heat into air bag and analyzing heating of the tower. The method comprises transferring biomass pyrolysis gas coming out of reactor from gas collection tank into desulfurization treatment apparatus after compression, adding hydrogen gas and superheated steam generated by superheater h1 promote methanation reaction, cooling mixed gas produced by reaction of constant temperature methanation reactor R2, exchanging by heat exchanger h4, pressurizing part of mixed gas by the compressor M1, transferring adiabatic methanation reactor R1 to circulate the reaction, collecting remaining mixed gas in solid-liquid separator, and gas-water mixed state of heat exchange in air bag, adding liquid water in constant temperature methanation reactor R2 through circulation pump, achieving constant temperature process, analyzing high temperature steam in the tower, transferring gaseous mixture produced by solid-liquid separator into absorption tower to remove carbon dioxide, scrubbing decarburized mixture gas by the scrubber and drying to obtain final product.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. PT P
PN CN109762787-A
TI Isolating and cultivating primary human renal cancer cells involves obtaining fresh kidney cancer tissue under aseptic conditions and sealling it back to laboratory, and tissue treating equipment disinfection.
AU INVENTOR U
AE JIANGSU CHI SCI CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019464181
AB    NOVELTY - Isolating and cultivating primary human renal cancer cells involves obtaining fresh kidney cancer tissue under aseptic conditions and sealling it back to the laboratory at 4 degrees C, and tissue treating equipment disinfection. The kidney cancer tissue is washed 3 times with pre-cooled PBS to remove surface blood vessels, fat and connective tissue, in the aseptic operating platform. The tissue is cut and digested with preheated trypsin and type II collagenase, terminated digestion, centrifuged, and discarded the supernatant. The cell filtrate is taken and the cell activity is measured by trypan blue staining. The cells are counted and resuspended in complete medium to the coated culture flask, after counting the cells, and cultured at 37 degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide atmosphere. The differential adherence method is performed to remove fibroblasts. The cell morphology identification, cell viability assay, and immunological assay is performed.
   USE - Method for isolating and cultivating primary human renal cancer cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to isolating and cultivating primary human renal cancer cells, which has simple operation and a large number of cells obtained, and provides reliable cellular resources for experiments.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The trypsin contains 0.01% of ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid to absorb calcium ion and magnesium ion in the tissue, which further promotes cell separation. The complete medium is dulbecco's modified eagle medium/F12 (1:1) medium containing 10% fetal calf serum, 100 u/ml penicillin, 100 u/ml streptomycin, and 5 microgram/ml insulin. The culture flask is coated with 3-4 microgram/cm2 of polylysine.

AB    NOVELTY - Chemical conversion of corncob furfural residue to bioethanol comprises (1) pulverizing corncob furfural residue and adding to hydrothermal reaction kettle, and adding acid solution to reaction kettle, under action of acid, hydrolyzing corncob furfural residue into soluble sugar solution and solid lignin, (2) flowing soluble sugar solution into fixed bed reactor or slurry bed reactor, and adding supported metal catalyst M1M2copper/carrier, where M1 and M2 is respectively two different metals, the metals M1 and M2 is each one of boron, chromium, manganese, iron, cobalt, nickel, molybdenum, zinc, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, silver, tungsten, iridium, platinum, and gold, introducing mixed gas of hydrogen and carbon dioxide into fixed bed reactor or slurry bed reactor, and reacting soluble sugar solution to form bioethanol under catalyst catalysis.
   USE - The method is useful for chemical conversion of corncob furfural residue to bioethanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes hydrolysis and catalytic hydrogenolysis of furfural slag by simple and efficient steps; converts agricultural and forestry waste residue into high-value bioethanol, which realizes efficient, economical, environmentally friendly and high-value utilization of waste biomass.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid solution is organic or inorganic acid choosing from sulfuric acid, nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, phosphoric acid, formic acid, acetic acid, propionic acid, and/or lactic acid. The resulting soluble sugar solution comprises C5 sugar monomer, C6 sugar monomer, C5 sugar oligomer, and C6 sugar oligomer. The carrier is activated carbon, silicon oxide, titanium oxide, alumina, zirconium oxide, zinc oxide, lanthanum oxide, and/or tin oxide, molecular sieves having carrier mass of 50-90% of total mass of catalyst. The catalyst M1M2copper/carrier, the mass of metal copper is 10-50% of total mass of catalyst, the masses of metals M1 and M2 is 0.1-10% of total mass of catalyst. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the mass ratio of corncob furfural residue to acid ranges from 1:20-1:1, reaction temperature is 150-250 degrees C, and reaction time is 0.5-5 hours. In the step (2), reaction temperature is 250-350 degrees C, pressure is 2-4 MPa, and reaction time is 2-6 hours.

TI Method for facilitating task allocation and path planning for cooperative logistics problem, involves minimizing total transportation distance of sub-enterprise, and determining ratio of distance from order point to sub-enterprise.
AU ZHANG T
   LIN N
   SHI Y
   QU F
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201932664V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing a head office with sub-enterprises, where the sub-enterprise includes to-be-satisfied orders. Goods are transported by the sub-enterprise through utilizing models with load capacity. A total transportation distance of the sub-enterprise is minimized. A ratio of a distance from an order point to the sub-enterprise is determined. A x-axis and a y-axis are drawn. An angle between a line connecting the order point and the x-axis is calculated. A demand corresponding to an order is retrieved and recorded. A total demand of a current order is calculated. Judgment is made to check whether the current total demand satisfies a load constraint of a vehicle.
   USE - Method for facilitating task allocation and path planning for cooperative logistics problem.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing the transportation cost of the logistics enterprises and carbon dioxide emissions.

TI Facial care film substrate used in facial mask, has microporous film structure formed by processing polyolefin.
AU WANG J
   JIN P
   DENG H
   PENG W
   DU Z
   REN B
   LIU S
   LUAN L
   HONG Z
AE ANHUI XINHENG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019305042
AB    NOVELTY - Facial care film substrate has a microporous film structure which is formed by processing polyolefin having a film thickness of 5-100 mu m, a pore size of 20-80% and a micropore size of 10 nm to 10 mu m in the film.
   USE - The facial care film substrate is useful in a facial mask (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The facial care film substrate improves the utilization rate and absorption effect of the nutrient solution on the surface of the human body and has excellent adhesion to facial skin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a preparation method of surface-modified facial care film substrate, which involves placing polyolefin microporous film between two polar plates of a vacuum processing chamber, and vacuumizing until the vacuum degree reaches 40 Pa for plasma pretreatment, activating a high-frequency heating equipment, introducing nitrogen, carbon dioxide and oxygen plasma to the quartz dielectric tube under microwave power and system pressure, and exciting to surface-treat the polyolefin microporous film, where the control system pressure is higher than 400 Pa, the processing time is 5-300 seconds, and the sample is 5-7 cm away from the end face of the resonant cavity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The film substrate is obtained by modifying polyolefin microporous film with a modifier or subjecting polyolefin to plasma surface modification. Preferred Components: The film substrate comprises an inner liquid absorption layer and an outer thermal insulation layer, where the liquid absorption layer is a single layer surface grafted polyolefin microporous film and the thermal insulation layer is a polyolefin microporous film. The film substrate has an embedded microporous membrane structure prepared by blending a graft of polyethylene and a hydrophilic segment as a modifier, and blending the material with a polyolefin raw material, where the graft of the hydrophilic segment is chosen from polyethylene glycol acrylonitrile or methyl methacrylate. The film substrate is obtained by subjecting the surface of the polyolefin microporous film to microwave plasma modifying treatment at a microwave power of 180-220 W for 5-300 seconds by using a adjustable microwave source of 2450 MHz. The polyolefin microporous film has a single layer structure of polypropylene (PP) or polyethylene (PE), a double layer structure of PP/PE, or a multilayer structure of combination of PP and PE. A liquid retaining layer is further arranged between the inner liquid absorbing layer and the outer insulating layer, where the liquid retaining layer is a double layer surface grafted polyolefin film. Preferred Properties: The insulation layer has a pore diameter of 10 nm to 1 mu m, and the liquid absorption layer has a porosity of 60-80% and a pore diameter that is larger than the pore diameter of the insulation layer by 1-10 mu m. The liquid retaining layer has a porosity of 60-80%.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium carbonate-containing raw material is mineral having mass content of not greater than 95% of waste calcareous sinter or calcium carbonate; the mass ratio of the calcium carbonate-containing raw material to the biomass fuel is 20: (4-5). The calcium oxide-containing raw material is first calcareous sinter repair base material or calcium oxide having purity of not greater than 95%; the mass ratio of the calcium oxide-containing raw material to water is 1: (4-7). The mass content of carbon dioxide in the calcareous sinter curing base material is 30-70%. The calcium hydroxide-containing raw material is second calcareous sinter repair base material or a calcium hydroxide having a purity of not greater than 95%; the mass ratio of the calcium hydroxide-containing raw material to the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide-containing raw material is 1: (0.5-1.5). The mass ratio of the first calcareous sinter repair base material, the second calcareous sinter repair base material, the third calcareous sinter repair base material and the calcareous sinter is (5-10): (10-20): (5-10): (60-80). The third calcareous sinter repair base material material is replaced with a calcium bicarbonate slurry having mass concentration of 20-70%; the mass ratio of the first calcareous sinter repair base material, the second calcareous sinter repair base material, calcium bicarbonate slurry and calcareous sinter is (5-10): (10-20): (5-20): (60-80). The calcareous sinter curing base material is replaced with gas capable of maintaining curing process temperature of 100-300 degrees C and carbon dioxide volume content of 30-70%. Preferred Method: In the method, curing at 100-300 degrees C for 1-5 hours. In the method, calcination temperature is 900-1200 degrees C, and the calcination time is 1-6 hours.

AU BOGNER M
   SCHAFER C
   SLAVIC S
   SCHAEFER C
AE CONTINENTAL AUTOMOTIVE GMBH (CONW-C)
   VITESCO TECHNOLOGIES GMBH (CONW-C)
   VITESCO TECHNOLOGIES GMBH (CONW-C)
GA 201923915W
AB    NOVELTY - The compressor (30) has a compressor stator (31), a compressor rotor (13), a screening mechanism (50), an air supply channel (36), and a throttle module (70). The throttle module, which is separate from the stator, has a throttle module housing (71) accommodating an actuator (56) connected mechanically with the screening mechanism. The housing also surrounds the air supply channel, and is flanged onto the stator.
   USE - Compressor for supercharging device (claimed) of internal combustion engine of motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - Heat conduction from the stator into the throttle module housing of the screening mechanism is reduced, thus thermal load on the components of the module is reduced, and damage to components is also reduced. The throttle module is retrofittable and presents an option to users, by being provided separate from the compressor stator. The module components are compatible with different engine models, lowering costs and contributing energy- and resource-saving as well as reduced carbon dioxide emissions.

PN KR1955018-B1
TI Recovering nitrous oxide, comprises recovering nitrous oxide through pressure cycling adsorption using nitrous oxide recovery adsorbent.
AU KIM J H
   LEE S B
   PARK I J
   HA J W
   YOOK S H
   LEE K W
   LEE S G
   CHANG B J
   MOON S Y
   PARK B R
   KONG C I
   LEE C H
   PARK D
   KIM J J
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
   UNIV YONSEI IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 201923294Q
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering nitrous oxide, comprises recovering nitrous oxide through pressure cycling adsorption using nitrous oxide recovery adsorbent. The recovery adsorbent has working capacity of 0.90 which is calculated by equation (0.90 greater than or equal to W0-W1/W0) (I), where W0 is adsorption amount of nitrous oxide (mol/kg) at pressure of 10 bar in adsorption isotherm of adsorbent at 20 degrees C, W1 is adsorption amount of nitrous oxide (mol/kg) at pressure of 0.3 bar in adsorption isotherm of adsorbent at 20 degrees C, W0 is 3.50-5 mol/kg and W1 is 0.10-1 mol/kg.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering nitrous oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can efficiently recover nitrous oxide having global warming index of 310 times higher than carbon dioxide from nitrous oxide-containing gas mixture to obtain a reduction effect of greenhouse gas, recovers nitrous oxide having purity of 80-99 vol.% through post-process including low-temperature distillation process to produce ultra high purity nitrous oxide of 99.999 vol.%, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a pressure circulation adsorption apparatus for separating nitrous oxide from nitrogen oxides containing an adsorbent for recovering nitrous oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises removing oxygen and nitrogen from nitrous oxide-containing gaseous mixture. The pressure cycling adsorption is carried out by performing pressure step, adsorption step, pressure equalization cleaning step using at least two adsorption towers charged with the adsorbent, and desorption step for at least two times in each adsorption tower. The adsorption is carried out under pressure of 1.7-20 bar. The pressure of the desorption step is from vacuum to 2.1 bar. The nitrous oxide recovery method is carried out at 0-50 degrees C. Preferred Components: The adsorbent further satisfies nitrous oxide/nitrogen selectivity of greater than or equal to 7 which calculated by equation (II), as given in specification, and nitrous oxide/oxygen selectivity of greater than or equal to 7 which calculated by equation (III), as given in specification. The nitrous oxide-containing gaseous mixture comprises 5-98 vol.% nitrous oxide, 0.1-15 vol.% oxygen and 1-90 vol.% nitrogen. The adsorbent is de-aluminated zeolite Y or sylobead silica gel. The de-aluminated zeolite Y comprises silicon and aluminum content ratio of 5-30.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN109082227-A
TI Preparing heat-resistant powder coating involves mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, low-grade bauxite, additives and modifiers, and feeding them into a kiln for roasting to obtain clinker.
AU CHEN X
   CHEN Q
   TANG D
   ZHAO P
   MAO X
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201901578H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing heat-resistant powder coating involves mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, low-grade bauxite, additives and modifiers, feeding them into a kiln for roasting to obtain clinker and subjecting it to water milling, then dissolved in water, and subjected to solid-liquid separation to obtain precipitate. Sulfide is separated from the precipitate by flotation process, and is subjected to roasting. The flue gas generated by roasting is catalyzed by vanadium pentoxide, and absorbed with concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid. Carbon dioxide is added to the liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation until the white precipitate is no longer produced. The white precipitate is filtered, washed, dried and subjected to calcination to obtain alumina. The alumina is grounded and calcination is continued to form alumina powder.
   USE - Heat-resistant coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and low-cost preparation of heat-resistant coating, and improves comprehensive utilization rate of phosphogypsum and low-grade bauxite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Solid silicone resin and flatting auxiliary agent is added to alumina powder, mixed, and subjected to melt extrusion obtain extrudate that is cooled, pulverized and sieved to obtain heat-resistant powder coating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The additive is sodium carbonate or caustic soda, and the modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. Preferred Composition: 8-20 wt.% Alumina powder, 72-87 wt.% solid silicone resin and 1-8 wt.% leveling auxiliary agent are mixed. Preferred Conditions: Phosphogypsum and low-grade bauxite are mixed in a weight ratio of 1:0.4-1.1. The wt. ratio of additive to the molecular sum of sodium oxide, alumina and ferric oxide contained in the raw material is 1:1. The ratio of the modifier is 9-16 wt.% of the total wt. of raw material. The kiln is an industrial rotary kiln, an industrial tunnel kiln or an industrial vertical kiln. The calcination is performed at 1150-1280 degrees C for 1.5-2.5 hours. During dissolution, the ratio of solid-liquid is 1:3-5. The sulfide is placed in 35-45% oxygen-rich environment and calcined at 850-1150 degrees C. The white precipitate is calcined at 880-1220 degrees C for 1-3 hours. After grounding alumina, the calcination process is continued at 760-950 degrees C for 1-2.5 hours to obtain alumina powder. The melt extrusion performed at 140-160 degrees C. The heat-resistant powder coating obtained after the sieving has a particle diameter of 20-40 micrometer.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A82 (Coatings, impregnations, polishes - excluding textile finishing.); G02 (Inks, paints, polishes â€“ polymer-based paints and inks are also classified in Section A (C09D, F, G).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving shelf life of mulberry involves spraying clean water to mulberry fruit until fruit is wet and dropping water drop one day before harvesting, spraying clean water once to fruit until the fruit is wet on sunny day and do not apply water on rainy days, harvesting mulberry fruit before 10 in the morning during sunny day, picking the fruit with fruit stalk, picking 8-9 ripe fruit, removing the cooked fruit, selecting the fruit without water droplet, placing the material in ventilated basket, placing a piece of wet gauze at the bottom and around the basket, adding 5-15 kg mulberry in each frame, covering with a layer of wet gauze, maintaining the gauze wet but not dripping, placing the basket filled with mulberry in cold air treatment workshop, cooling the material at 0-3 degrees C, and placing the basket in ventilated refrigerator for storage.
   USE - The method is useful for improving shelf life of mulberry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases carbon dioxide, reduces physiological activity of spoilage bacteria and prolongs shelf life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Component: The basket is chosen from plastic basket, bamboo basket, pannier or aperture and plastic basket and bamboo basket, where the basket has a hole diameter of 0.5-1 cm. Preferred Method: The method involves washing the gauze in clean water for 2-3 times before using, adding the material in 75% ethanol solution for 30-35 minutes, removing, drying and adding the material in 20% carbon dioxide solution for 20-25 minutes, removing the material and maintaining the gauze wet but not dripping, maintaining cold air treatment workshop at 0-3 degrees C, cold air temperature is -5-0 degrees C, cold air humidity of 80-85%, and carbon dioxide concentration is 13-17%, controlling the temperature of freezing mulberry at 0-3 degrees C for less than or equal to 1.5 hours, maintaining the temperature of refrigerator at 0-3 degrees C, and maintaining gap between baskets for 10-12 cm, controlling the temperature of cold air treatment workshop which is completely equal to the temperature of refrigerator during freezing and storing the mulberry fruit, controlling the air in refrigerator, air humidity is 90-95%, carbon dioxide concentration is 5-10%, and maintaining the gauze in the basket wet but not dripping. The mulberry cold air freezing method is performed by controlling the cold air which is simultaneously blowing into front and back faces of basket for 10 minutes, controlling cold air which is simultaneously blowing into vertical surface from upper side and lower side of the basket for 10 minutes, and controlling the cold air simultaneously blowing into six surfaces of the basket for 5 minutes, performing the circulation until the temperature of mulberry reduces 0-3 degrees C.

   USE - Zirconium-based chiral catalyst is used for asymmetric catalysis, chiral separation, drug synthesis, carbon dioxide fixation, luminescent material and chiral sensing applications (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The zirconium-based chiral catalyst has excellent stability, and can be industrially prepared by environmentally-friendly, simple and economical process with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A zirconium-based chiral catalyst comprises chiral salicylaldehyde-ethylenediamine-based metal complex derivative of formula: Zr6O4(OH)4(salen-M)6 (I), and chiral induction group e.g. cyclohexanediamine, diphenyldiamine or 2-methylethylenediamine.
   alen-M = chiral salicylaldehyde-ethylenediamine-based metal complex;and
   M = catalytically active metal ion e.g. Zn2+, Cu2+, Ni2+, Co2+, Fe2+, Mn3+, Cr3+, V4+ or Ti4+.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the catalyst, which involves dissolving a zirconium salt, chiral salicylaldehyde-ethylenediamine-based metal complex and a modifying agent in a solvent, stirring, heating to 80-120 degrees C, reacting for 72-96 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering, washing with organic solvent, and drying in vacuum.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The modifying agent is formic acid, acetic acid, trifluoroacetic acid and/or benzoic acid. The solvent is dimethylformamide, methanol and/or ethanol. The organic solvent is dimethylformamide, ethanol and/or ether. The molar ratio of the zirconium salt and the complex is 1:1-1:3. The molar ratio of zirconium salt and solvent is 1:500-1:5000. The molar ratio of the zirconium salt and the modifying agent is 1:1-1:10.

TI Preparing a skid-proof brick involves shaping blank, performing first drying process to obtain a brick blank, glazing surface of brick blank, subjecting it to inkjet printing, and then subjecting it to a second drying treatment.
AU QIU J
   CHENG G
   WU Z
   LI L
   WU B
AE FOSHAN SHIWAN EAGLE BRAND CERAMIC CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 2018758606
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a skid-proof brick involves shaping blank, performing first drying process to obtain a brick blank, glazing surface of brick blank, subjecting it to inkjet printing, and then subjecting it to a second drying treatment. The method involves spraying the obtained brick blank to obtain prefabricated bricks. The prefabricated brick is placed in a kiln for the first firing, and is heated to 400 degrees C for the first time, where first program is cooled down, and is lowered to 80 degrees C, placed in a kiln for a second firing, and then heated to 1200 degrees C for a second time.
   USE - Method for preparing a skid-proof brick used for producing skid-proof tile (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a skid-proof brick, which has good non-skid property, shock resistance, anti-fouling property, and strong rupture strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The obtained brick is cooled to a normal temperature in a nitrogen atmosphere containing carbon dioxide to obtain a semi-finished product, which is subjected to grinding and edge quality inspection to obtain a finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Conditions: The first programd temperature is firstly increased to 150 degrees C for 10 minutes, maintained for 5 minutes, raised to 300 degrees C for 15 minutes, maintained for 15 minutes, and then raised to 400 degrees C for 5 minutes, and maintained for 30 minutes. The second temperature programd is carried out by increasing temperature in first stage to 300 degrees C for 10 minutes, maintained for 5 minutes, temperature in second stage is raised to 600 degrees C for 5 minutes, maintained for 20 minutes, and then temperature in third stage is raised to 1200 degrees C for 30 minutes to 30 hours. The first program cooling is carried out by cooling it down to 350 degrees C in 10 minutes, cooling down to 300 degrees C in 30 minutes, cooling down to 150 degrees C for 50 minutes, and then finally to 80 degrees C for 70 minutes. The second program cooling is carried out by firstly cooling it down to 1000 degrees C for 60 minutes, to 700 degrees C for 60 minutes, to 300 degrees C for 60 minutes, and then to 80 degrees C for 60 minutes. The temperature of first drying is 100-180 degrees C, and second drying is carried out in a constant temperature environment at a temperature of 180 degrees C, and the change value is not more than 0.1 degrees C. The glazed brick has a thickness of 1-5mm, and carbon dioxide has a fraction of 5-8 vol.%. The glazed brick contains a frit having a base dry granule, a combined dry granule and a functional dry granule, and mass ratio of base dry granule, combined dry granule and functional dry granule is 5:4:1. The base dry granule has a particle diameter of 100-200 mesh, combined dry granule has a particle diameter of 200-250 mesh, and functional dry granule has a particle diameter of 250-300 mesh. The frit is produced by mixing base dry granules, combined dry granules and functional dry granules, and heating it at 1700-1800 degrees C for 5 minutes, quenching it to room temperature and pulverizing it to 150-250 mesh. Preferred Compositions: The base dry granule contains 52 wt.% silicon dioxide, 19 wt.% aluminum oxide, 0.05 wt.% ferrous oxide, 11 wt.% calcium oxide, 3.3 wt.% magnesium oxide, 5.9 wt.% potassium oxide, 5.55 wt.% sodium oxide and 3.2 wt.% zinc oxide. The combined dry granule contains 4.5 wt.% silicon dioxide, 15 wt.% aluminum oxide, 0.1 wt.% ferrous oxide, 8.2 wt.% calcium oxide, 2.5 wt.% magnesium oxide, 7.5 wt.% potassium oxide, 9 wt.% sodium oxide, 2.5 wt.% zinc oxide, 0.5 wt.% copper(II) oxide and 0.2 wt.% cobalt(II) oxide. The functional dry granule contains 51.5 wt.% silicon dioxide, 23 wt.% aluminum oxide, 0.3 wt.% ferrous oxide, 33.5 wt.% calcium oxide, 3.3 wt.% magnesium oxide, 3 wt.% potassium oxide, 2.6 wt.% sodium oxide, 2.2 wt.% zinc oxide, 110.3 wt.% praseodymium oxide, 0.2 wt.% lanthanum oxide and 0.1 wt.% cerium(III) oxide.

GA 201872247E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting a coal sample for testing coal mine absorption in a different coal layer. An adsorption and desorption curve of the coal sample is drawn. Distance between two data points is calculated by using the adsorption and desorption curve of the coal sample. A negative pressure range between two inflection points is selected as a reasonable extracting negative pressure range. Adsorption time and determination time of the coal sample are obtained. A gas extraction period is detected by using negative pressure or low pressure extraction. Maximum possible negative pressure is extracted from the reasonable extracting negative pressure range.
   USE - Negative pressure controlling function based coal mine underground gas-extraction method.

TI Preparing Ganoderma lucidum triterpenoids            comprises e.g. extracting the Ganoderma lucidum fruit            body with ethanol, concentrating, obtaining alcohol            extract of Ganoderma lucidum, and processing the            Ganoderma lucidum fruiting body.
AU ZHANG J
   JI W
   WANG W
   XU J
   TANG C
   ZHUANG H
   YANG Y
   LIU Y
   JIA W
AE SHANGHAI AGRIC SCI ACAD (SHNY-C)
GA 2018653410
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Ganoderma lucidum triterpenoids                comprises (i) adding the Ganoderma lucidum fruit                body to 50-100% ethanol, where the liquid to                material mass ratio is 30-80:1, extracting, where                the extraction temperature is 55-100 degrees C and                the extraction time is 1-3 hours, concentrating the                extract and obtaining alcohol extract of Ganoderma                lucidum, (ii) processing the Ganoderma lucidum                fruiting body into a 10-80 mesh size, adding the                Ganoderma lucidum alcohol extract obtained in the                step (i), mixing, placing into a supercritical                extraction tank and performing supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction, where the supercritical                extraction process conditions are: the temperature                is 40-70 degrees C, the pressure is 20-50 MPa, the                volume of ethanol as cosolvent is 50-100% (v/v),                the amount is 4-10 ml/g and the time is 1-3 hours                and (iii) concentrating the extract and drying at                lea than 65 degrees C to obtain a Ganoderma lucidum                extract.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Ganoderma                lucidum triterpenoids.
   ADVANTAGE - The triterpenoids has 66.76% inhibition rate                on human prostate tumor cells LNCaP and 62.69 %                inhibition rate on 22RV1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  concentrated ethanol extract of Ganoderma lucidum                  contains ethanol with a mass fraction of 2-20%.                  Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the mass                  ratio of the Ganoderma lucidum fruit body to the                  Ganoderma lucidum alcohol extract is 10:1-1:10,                  the purity of the carbon dioxide is 99-100% and                  the supercritical extraction process conditions                  are: the temperature is 40-60 degrees C, the                  pressure is 20-35 MPa, the volume of ethanol as                  cosolvent is 80-100% (v/v), the amount is 4-8                  ml/g and the time is 1-3 hours.

PT P
PN CN108339522-A; CN108339522-B
TI Preparing amino acid doped            copper-benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate composite adsorbent            comprises adding copper nitrate and amino acid into            nano-zinc oxide solution, stirring, adding trimesic            acid solution, filtering and centrifuging.
AU LI Z
   WU Y
   ZHOU X
   XIAO J
   XIA Q
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2018619293
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing amino acid doped                copper-benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate composite                adsorbent comprises: (i) adding nano-zinc oxide                into deionized water, ultrasonically dispersing,                adding dimethylformamide to obtain nano-zinc oxide                solution, dissolving copper nitrate trihydrate and                amino acids in deionized water to obtain a mixture                of copper nitrate and amino acid, dissolving                trimesic acid in ethanol to obtain trimesic acid                solution; (ii) adding the mixture of copper nitrate                and amino acid into the nano-zinc oxide solution,                stirring uniformly, adding trimesic acid solution,                stirring and reacting for 5-10 minutes to obtain a                reaction product, filtering the reaction solution,                soaking the solid product in methanol, centrifuging                and drying to obtain blue solid powder; and (iii)                vacuum-activating the solid powder.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing amino acid                doped copper-benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate composite                adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has good water vapor stability                and high carbon dioxide adsorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                amino acid doped                copper-benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate composite                adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of                  zinc oxide and copper nitrate trihydrate is                  1:(1.25-2). The amino acid is glycine, beta                  -alanine or gamma -aminobutyric acid. The volume                  ratio of deionized water, dimethylformamide and                  ethanol is (1-1.1):(1-1.2):(1-1.3). The molar                  ratio of copper nitrate trihydrate and amino acid                  is 1:(0.2-0.5). Preferred Method: The filtration                  process utilizes an organic filter having average                  pore diameter of 0.45 mu m. The drying process is                  carried out at 60-80 degrees C for 4-8 hours. The                  vacuum activation is carried out at 120-150                  degrees C for 8-16 hours.

PN CN108217654-A
TI Preparing catalyst involves taking magnetite iron(II, III) oxide as raw material, raw material particles in fluidized bed reactor, replacing air with ammonia, discharging ammonia, adding pure hydrogen, circulating hydrogen.
AU YANG H
AE YANG H (YANG-Individual)
GA 201853396R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst involves taking magnetite iron(II, III) oxide as raw material, raw material particles in fluidized bed reactor, replacing air with ammonia, discharging ammonia, adding pure hydrogen at 0.5 megaPascal, hydrogen circulation, gas pushing catalyst layer to flow upwards, starting heater, flowing of particles, heating to 400 degrees C and keeping 24n, in the process iron(II, III) oxide is completely converted into iron metal iron, stopping heating, naturally cooling to normal temperature, recycling hydrogen, adding carbon monoxide, starting compressor, carbon monoxide cycle, gas pushing catalyst layer to flow upwards, starting heater, heating flowing of particles to 300 degrees C, keeping 24n and completely converting into iron carbide iron, measuring carbon dioxide content in exhaust gas, stopping heating, naturally cooling to normal temperature, using iron carbide as catalyst for preparing hydrogen. The catalyst space velocity is 3000n-1, generating 1000M3 of hydrogen per hour.
   USE - Method for preparing catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare catalyst which contains easily-obtained raw material, enables mass and cheap hydrogen production, is safe and convenient to transport and has wide economic application.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The firstly injecting carbon monoxide and hygrogen gas with a ratio of 1:1 at 0.3 megaPascal and 250 degrees C, starting the compressor to gas circulation, and injecting water vapor to react and gas pressure gradually rising to 0.3 megaPascal and indicating a normal process of reaction, adjusting the steam amount. The reaction is carried out in circulation compressor, separating carbon monoxide returns to the reactor inlet from the mixed gas through the gas separator bottom, returning to the inlet by upper end halfhydrogen is added into input hydrogen storage tank, continuously circulating the circulating compressor, continuously producing hydrogen and reaching the purpose of preparing hydrogen.

GA 201849903G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves irradiating a sorting                target with infrared light (3). Reflected light (4)                is received from the sorting target irradiated with                the infrared light. Correlation information is                obtained between spectral intensity excluding a                wavelength band of equal to or longer than 4.18 m                and equal to or shorter than 4.42 m of a reflection                or absorption spectrum obtained with the reflected                light and spectrum data of multiple resin types                which is acquired in advance. PT P
PN CN108184985-A
TI Preserving fresh thick-skinned melon involves            pre-cooling by ventilation gradient in the warehouse,            setting temperature to the temperature of the fruit            seed cavity, using ozone disinfection machine, and            dehumidifying evaporator.
AU PAN Y
   ZHANG T
   MENG X
   XU B
   TAN G
   XUE S
AE XINJIANG ACAD AGRIC SCI STORAGE & PROCES (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 201850497K
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving fresh thick-skinned melon involves                pre-cooling by ventilation gradient in the                warehouse for 5 days, setting temperature to the                temperature of the fruit seed cavity to 3 plus                minus 1 degrees C, using ozone disinfection machine                for 240 minutes of ozone fumigation every 24 hours.                The concentration of ozone in the reservoir is                maintained at 0.38-0.59 parts per million, and the                evaporator is dehumidified and drained in time to                control the relative humidity in the reservoir to                be 75-85%. Single melon is placed in the packaging                bag, 3 sheets of high-strength fiber absorbent                paper are placed in each bag, the pumping time is                set to 10.0 second, and the mixture of oxygen,                carbon dioxide and nitrogen is charged, and the                inflation time is 5.0 second, and sealing time 1.0                second.
   USE - Method for preserving fresh thick-skinned                melon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to better maintain fresh                quality of the fruit, reduce occurrence of diseases                during fruit storage, delay aging of fruit, and                effectively prolong storage period.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The method according                  to claim 1, where the material of the packaging                  bag is any one of polypropylene, Polyvinyl                  alcohol, and Polyethylene terephthalate. The                  packaging bag is a polypropylene material. The                  concentration of the oxygen gas in the mixed gas                  is 8.86-9.45%, preferably 9.45% and the                  concentration of the carbon dioxide gas is                  1.42-1.80%, preferably 1.55%. The thick-skinned                  melon is Xizhoumi No. 17, Xizhou Mi 25, 86-1,                  Jiashi melon thick skin, and Hybrid No. 2 Jiashi                  melon.

PT P
PN KR1868271-B1
TI Operation apparatus for supplying a fluid to a turbo machine, comprises a storage tank that is connected with first pump, second pump and transfer pipe and a sealing system is provided in the turbo machine along with fifth transfer pipe.
AU CHA S H
AE DOOSAN HEAVY IND CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
GA 201854090V
AB    NOVELTY - The operation apparatus comprises a storage tank (100) that is connected with first pump, second pump and transfer pipe. Second pump (300) boosts the voltage of the working fluid stored in the storage tank for the turbo machine (500). First pump (200) boosts the voltage of the working fluid to the first peak pressure. A sealing system is provided in the turbo machine. First transfer pipe is connected with a side of the storage tank and second transfer pipe. Fifth transfer pipe is connected with exhaust end of the fourth transfer pipe and the first transfer pipe.
   USE - Operation apparatus for supplying a fluid to a turbo machine, such as supercritical carbon dioxide electric power system and turbine.
   ADVANTAGE - The overall structure of the operation apparatus is simplified. The steady supply of fluid to the turbo machine is ensured.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of an operation apparatus for supplying a fluid to a turbo machine.
   Storage tank (100)
   First pump (200)
   Second pump (300)
   Heat engine (400)
   Turbo machine (500)

PN US2018155663-A1; US11208314-B2
TI Appliance for preparing beverage such as coffee or            tea, has mixing element configured to mix concentrated,            pressurized beverage with liquid to produce beverage,            and beverage outlet configured to dispense produced            beverage.
AU PEIRSMAN D
   VANDEKERCKHOVE S
   PELLAUD J
   DAVIS N
AE ANHEUSER BUSCH INBEV SA (ANHE-C)
GA 2018450932
AB    NOVELTY - The appliance has a receptacle for intake of a                container containing a concentrated, pressurized                beverage. A liquid intake is configured for intake                of a liquid. A mixing element is configured for                mixing the concentrated, pressurized beverage with                the liquid to produce a beverage. A beverage outlet                is configured to dispense a produced                beverage.
   USE - Appliance for preparing beverage such as                coffee or tea in home or office.
   ADVANTAGE - The consumers are allowed to quickly and                easily prepare the variety of beverages from                pre-packaged ingredients. The user is allowed to                quickly and easily prepare the single servings of a                number of different craft beers by mixing                carbonated or non-carbonated water with the                concentrated, pressurized beverages, resulting in a                beverage with a complex, delicious mouth-feel that                cannot be provided by existing products. The                exterior of the container is coated with an                inorganic oxide, to prevent gases such as oxygen                and carbon dioxide from entering or exiting the                container, and coated with a light blocking                colorant, to help prevent the light that can cause                chemical reactions with the concentrated beverage,                from entering the container.

PT P
PN CN108085035-A
TI Preparing iron coke by hot pressing comprises crushing iron ore and coal into powder, mixing, heating and pressing, loading the compact material into carbonization chamber, charring, cooling and discharging.
AU ZHOU Q
   LI J
   TANG E
   CHEN Q
   WANG P
AE WUHAN KESIRUIDI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 201843386P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing iron coke by hot pressing comprises: (i) crushing iron ore and coal into powder to a particle size of less than 0.5 mm, mixing the crushed iron ore and crushed coal to obtain a homogenous mixture; (ii) heating the mixture at 300-600 degrees C and pressing via hot press machine to obtain compact material having particle size of 15-75 mm; (iii) loading the compact material into an external thermal carbonization chamber in a carbonization shaft furnace by a charging device, reacting the briquettes in carbonization chamber at 800-1300 degrees C, preheating section at 400-800 degrees C and charring section for 5-30 hours to obtain iron coke; and (iv) cooling the iron coke to 50-150 degrees C by passing through the cooling section, then discharging outside the furnace through the discharge device.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing iron coke by hot pressing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical with high production efficiency, achieves large-scale production and reduces carbon dioxide discharge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of crushed coal and crushed iron ore is 60-90:10-40. The coal is non-coking coal, i.e. anthracite, bituminous and/or lignite. Preferred Method: The heating energy of the kneading material is obtained from the high-temperature flue gas generated in the combustion chamber of the carbonization shaft furnace. The high-temperature smoke heats the mixing material through a heating device. The amount of heat required for carbonization is obtained by external thermal carbonization chamber. The heat generated by the combustion of the combustion chamber outside the partition wall of the carbonization chamber, is transferred to the carbonization chamber through the partition wall. In the step (iii), the briquettes and the pulverized coal are mixed together and added to the carbonization chamber, after the high temperature smoke is heated by the heating device, the mixing material is heated. The preheating of combustion chamber is carried out through a heat exchanger.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)
MC H09-A02A; M24-A01B; M24-A02; M24-A05B; M24-A05C; M24-D02; M24-D03; M24-D04

TI Pyrolysis and gasification device for urban            garbage, has induction heating device that is provided            at upper end of microwave thermal cracking device for            preheating of waste by induction heating to send            pre-heated garbage to cracker.
AU PENG X
AE PENG X (PENG-Individual)
GA 201837486B
AB    NOVELTY - The device has induction heating devices and                microwave thermal cracking devices that are                arranged in a prescribed manner. The induction                heating device is provided at the upper end of the                microwave thermal cracking device for preheating of                waste by induction heating. The pre-heated garbage                is sent to a microwave thermal cracker for                cracking, so that the cracking is processed to                obtain reusable waste residue and synthesis                gas.
   USE - Pyrolysis and gasification device for urban                garbage.
   ADVANTAGE - The production of sulfur dioxide, nitrous                oxide and carbon dioxide gas is reduced, the                pollution to the environment is reduced, and the                secondary pollution is avoided. The pyrolysis and                gasification device has simple structure, strong                practicability and good pyrolysis effect, so as to                perform pre-heating treatment to the garbage                through the induction heating device.

PN CN207300310-U
TI Heat pump device detection control system, has conversion module connected with input end of processor, where output end of processor is connected with fan unit and compressor unit and output end of processor is connected to fresh air valve.
AU KE H
AE FOSHAN AOTING ENVIRONMENT EQUIP MFR CO L (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201835632Q

TI Composite catalyst used for preparing oxygen evolution anode, that is used in electrolysis of water, comprises graphene oxide, which has special structure, high specific surface area and activity, and exhibits excellent oxidative activity.
AU LI Z
   WU M
   SHANG J
   SU C
   GUO Y
AE UNIV DATONG (UDAT-C)
GA 201834509H
AB    NOVELTY - Composite catalyst comprises graphene oxide and NixCo1-xOOH, where x is 0.5-0.9.
   USE - Composite catalyst used for preparing an oxygen evolution anode, that is used in the electrolysis of water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst has a special structure, high specific surface area and high activity, and exhibits excellent oxidative, photoelectrochemical and electrocatalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A method for preparing a composite catalyst, which involves:
   (A) dissolving a certain concentration of nickel nitrate and cobalt nitrate precursors in deionized water, introducing high-purity nitrogen gas to remove dissolved oxygen and carbon dioxide, adding a certain amount of potassium hydroxide and bromine solution dropwise and reacting, transferring to the reaction in the hydrothermal reactor, and cooling naturally to obtain a product; and
   (B) mixing graphene oxide solution with the product, placing in a stainless steel high-pressure reaction vessel lined with Teflon, heating the reaction vessel and pressurizing to obtain a floc, which remained doped in a static state, washing it multiple times with large amount of deionized water and drying to obtain a composite catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The x is 0.6-0.8. The graphene oxide and NixCo1-xOOH are transformed into a transition metal oxyhydroxide-doped graphene oxide composite catalyst by a hydrothermal method.

PN WO2018046310-A1; CN109661179-A; EP3509426-A1; JP2019526264-W; ID201905625-A; EP3509426-B1; IN201927007761-A; EA36866-B1; JP7012706-B2
TI Preparing a leaf tea product with black tea involves incubating fresh tea leaf under anaerobic conditions, creating micropores on the incubated leaf, and exposing the leaf to ambient condition.
AU BASAVARAJU L
   GUTTAPADU S
   PALAGIRI S
   SINGH G
AE UNILEVER NV (UNIL-C)
   UNILEVER PLC (UNIL-C)
   CONOPCO INC DBA UNILEVER (UNIL-C)
   UNILEVER PLC (UNIL-C)
   UNILEVER NV (UNIL-C)
   UNILEVER PLC (UNIL-C)
   UNILEVER PLC (UNIL-C)
   UNILEVER IP HOLDINGS BV (UNIL-C)
GA 201821648Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a leaf tea product with black tea involves (a) incubating fresh tea leaf at 4-60 degrees C under anaerobic conditions for 4-50 hours; (b) creating micropores having diameter of 5-100 mu m on the incubated leaf; (c) exposing the leaf to 15-35 degrees C for 15 minutes to 4 hours.
   USE - The method is used for preparing a leaf tea product with black tea. The leaf tea product is used in a leaf tea blend (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - A process for producing a long leaf tea product with good and improved black tea characteristics is provided. The process does not use liquid carbon dioxide or any pressurized system and is simple, cost effective and easy to implement. The process includes an initial step of anaerobic conditioning followed by microporation and exposure at ambient condition, thus provides a long leaf tea product with black tea characteristics without comminution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a leaf tea product as obtained and/or obtainable by the process defined above; and
   (2) a leaf tea blend comprising 5-50% tea product of (1).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the process of preparation of a leaf tea product with black tea, the micropores are made using a technique selected from lasers, sand blasting, dry ice blasting, micro-pins, electroporation, and their combination. The number of micropores are 200-400 per cm2 tea leaf. There is no step of comminution of the tea leaf before step (c). The process further comprises drying the tea leaf at 50-150 degrees C. The anaerobic conditions are achieved by (i) placing the fresh tea leaf in a container, and closing the container, or (ii) placing the tea leaf in a container, purging a gas other than oxygen through the container, and closing the container, or placing the leaf in an airtight chamber or under vacuum. Preferred Leaf Tea Product: The length of the leaf in the black leaf tea product is 10-60 mm. The width of the leaf in the black leaf tea product is 3-40 mm. The individual leaf area in the black tea product is 2-5 cm2. The catechins content of the leaf tea product is 3.5-5.5 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

TI Waste gas recycling system, has air suction pipe connected with air inlet of fan by wind pipe, where air outlet of fan is connected to boiler wind chamber and inlet end of suction pipe is fixed with continuous steel belt pressing machine.
AU CHEN C
AE CHEN C (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2018204139
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a waste gas recycling system, comprising a continuous press, boiler, fan, are respectively provided with multiple air suction pipe at the two side and the outlet of the continuous press, the plurality of air suction pipe is connected with the air inlet of the fan by a wind pipe, the air outlet of said fan is connected to the boiler wind chamber; the inlet end of the air suction pipe on the continuous pressing machine for steel belt 5~10 cm. The utility model claims a waste gas recycling system, the continuous press high temperature flue gas generated when the man-made board blank by hot pressing into the boiler, so that the high temperature flue gas in formaldehyde (molecular formula: HCHO) as fuel for burning to form carbon dioxide and water, removing the formaldehyde, on the other hand, it can save boiler fuel, reduces the energy consumption of the production line.

PN CN107684618-A
TI Compound plant extract used in preparing anxiolytic medicine, comprises spina gleditsiae, citronella, turmeric and Cyrtomium fortunei, which is safe and used as medicine for external use for treating anxiety, and has less toxic effect.
AU HUANG H
AE GUANGZHOU YONGDA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201815023A
AB    NOVELTY - Compound plant extract comprises 10-30 pts. wt. spina gleditsiae, 10-30 pts. wt. citronella, 10-20 pts. wt. turmeric and 5-20 pts. wt. Cyrtomium fortunei.
   USE - Compound plant extract used in preparing anxiolytic medicine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound plant extract used as a medicine for external use for treating anxiety, which is safe and derived from pure natural plant, has less toxic effect, and it is prepared by the use of simple preparation technique.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing compound plant extract, which involves:
   (A) mixing spina gleditsiae, citronella, turmeric and Cyrtomium fortunei;
   (B) drying;
   (C) crushing;
   (D) sieving with 8-20-mesh sieve to obtain powder product;
   (E) putting the powder product into a closed extraction kettle for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction under 25-40 mega-pascal pressure at 45-55 degrees C temperature, in which the analyzing pressure is 6-10 mega-pascal, analyzing temperature is 55-65 degrees C to perform circular extraction for 2-4 hours; and
   (F) collecting the extract to obtain the desired compound plant extract.
TF ACTIVITY - Tranquilizer.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. GA 201815907L

TI Heat pump system for turbo compressor, has heat pump arrangements designed to work with heat pump mediums, and coupler adapted for thermally coupling first heat pump arrangement with second heat pump arrangement.
AU KNIFFLER O
   SUESS J
   SUSS J
AE EFFICIENT ENERGY GMBH (EFFI-Non-standard)
   EFFICIENT ENERGY GMBH (EFFI-Non-standard)
   EFFICIENT ENERGY GMBH (EFFI-Non-standard)
GA 201808730P
AB    NOVELTY - The system has first heat pump arrangement (101) designed to work with first heat pump medium that includes carbon dioxide, and second heat pump arrangement (102) designed to work with second heat pump medium that comprises water. A coupler (103) is adapted for thermally coupling the first heat pump arrangement with the second heat pump arrangement. An output section is provided with a heat exchanger through which a heat exchanger circuit is fluidically isolated from the second heat pump assembly.
   USE - Heat pump system for a fluid-flow machine e.g. radial compressor, such as turbo compressor.
   ADVANTAGE - The coupler is adapted for thermally coupling the first heat pump arrangement with the second heat pump arrangement, thus ensuring efficient heat transmission from the first heat pump arrangement to a surrounding environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for manufacturing a heat pump system
   (2) a method for operating a heat pump system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a heat pump system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Heat pump arrangements (101, 102)
   Coupler (103)

GA 2018083355
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a water purifier with filter core, comprising a shell, the shell is provided with a water inlet and a water outlet is set in the shell of the brucite filter medium. The utility model uses the brucite as filtering medium, capable of effectively removing the hydrated carbon dioxide in drinking water, stabilizing and regulating the pH value of the drinking water to 6.5~8.5 and improve drinking water taste. Because the normal mineral associated with silicon dioxide, aluminium oxide, associated mineral as framework can stabilize the whole mineral brucite, the brucite will not gradually dissolved and disappeared as the process of purifying water, ensure the structural stability of the whole filter medium, at the same time, the stable structure of brucite, not dissolved and disappeared in the water purification process, the formation of large pores, Therefore, it is possible to ensure the filtering precision is stable and unchanged. In addition, the dissolution of magnesium ion brucite is capable of increasing the mineral content in the water, increase the healthy drinking water.
