PN CN111393248-A
TI Desulfurizing oil and gas and recovering of light hydrocarbons comprises e.g. condensing oil and gas from upstream device, cooling, sending to gas-liquid separation tank for gas-liquid separation and pressurizing at bottom of tank.
AU HUANG M
   WU D
   DING Y
   LI J
   YAO A
   JIANG S
AE SINOPEC ENG INC (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 202066709L
AB    NOVELTY - Desulfurizing oil and gas and recovering of light hydrocarbons comprises e.g. condensing the oil and gas from the upstream device and cooling and sending to the gas-liquid separation tank I for gas-liquid separation, pressurizing gas-liquid separation tank I liquid phase at the bottom of the tank and sending to the gasoline cutting tower, and boosting gas phase at the top of the tank by a compressor and sending to the gasoline cutting tower, entering the gas and liquid phases from step (1) to the gasoline cutting tower, condensing the vapor phase from the top of the tower into the reflux tank at the top of the tower, compressing the gas phase at the top of the reflux tank at the top of the tower and cooling, sending to the gas-liquid separation tank II, and sending liquid phase at the bottom of the tank to the liquid phase removal unit, producing at least part of the bottom liquid phase of the gasoline cutting tower as a heavy gasoline product.
   USE - The method is useful for desulfurizing oil and gas and recovering of light hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has a wide range of applications; uses common catalytic cracking, catalytic cracking, delayed coking and other processes commonly in chemical production; and has high gas yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Desulfurizing oil and gas and recoverying of light hydrocarbons comprises (1) condensing the oil and gas from the upstream device and cooling and sending to the gas-liquid separation tank I for gas-liquid separation, pressurizing gas-liquid separation tank I liquid phase at the bottom of the tank and sending to the gasoline cutting tower, and boosting gas phase at the top of the tank by a compressor and sending to the gasoline cutting tower, (2) entering the gas and liquid phases from step (1) to the gasoline cutting tower, condensing the vapor phase from the top of the tower into the reflux tank at the top of the tower, compressing the gas phase at the top of the reflux tank at the top of the tower and cooling, sending to the gas-liquid separation tank II, and sending liquid phase at the bottom of the tank to the liquid phase removal unit, producing at least part of the bottom liquid phase of the gasoline cutting tower as a heavy gasoline product, (3) separating again phase and the liquid phase, after the gas-liquid balance of the material in the gas-liquid separation tank II, sending to the gas phase decontamination unit and the liquid phase decontamination unit for impurity removal, (4) optionally pressurizing the top gas phase separated by gas-liquid separation tank II, cooling, carrying out gas-liquid balance in gas-liquid separation tank III, using lean amine liquid as the absorbent to remove hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide in the rich gas desulfurization tower, using lye as absorbent to remove mercaptans in the gas-rich caustic scrubber, and optionally boosting and sending to the cooler, (5) pressurizing the bottom liquid phase from the top reflux tank of the gas and oil cutting tower, gas-liquid separation tank II and optional gas-liquid separation tank III and sequentially removing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide in the liquid hydrocarbon desulfurization tower, removing the mercaptans in the liquid hydrocarbon sweetening reactor and sending to the after cooler, (6) initially cooling the gaseous light hydrocarbons after removal of impurities in the cooler and sending to the aftercooler, mixing with the liquid light hydrocarbons in the aftercooler and cooling again, and sending to the feed tank, (7) mixing the mixture stream from the aftercooler, pre-absorbing and gas-liquid balanced in the feed tank, sending the gas phase at the top of the tank to the absorption tower, and sending liquid phase at the bottom of the tank to the separation unit, (8) using the mixed C4/C5 or C4/C5/C6 as the absorbent to absorb C2 and the components above C2 in the gas phase from the top of the feed tank in the absorption tower, absorbing simultaneously part of the methane, sending the gas phase at the top of the absorption tower to the downstream device, and returning liquid phase at the bottom of the tower to the aftercooler, (9) passing the liquid phase from the bottom of the feed tank through the demethanizer, depropanizer, deethanizer, and optional propylene rectification tower in the separation unit to further separate C2 components, C3 components, and mixed C4/C5 or C4/C5/C6 components, extracting the C2 component and C3 component as C2 product and C3 product, sending at least a part of the mixed C4/C5 or C4/C5/C6 component to the absorption tower as absorbent and separating the rest from light gasoline and (10) further separating the liquid phase components from the bottom of the depropanizer in the debutanizer for the mixed C4/C5 or C4/C5/C6 components, extracting the separated C4 components from the upper part of the debutanizer, and extracting bottom components as light gasoline products. AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM are also included for device for desulfurizing oil and gas and recovering of light hydrocarbons.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the process of desulfurizing oil and gas and recovering of light hydrocarbons.

house, reducing the temperature of the                mushroom house during Day 4, controlling the                temperature of the mushroom house to 15.5-17.5                degrees C without to 15-17 degrees C                without changing the other conditions of the                mushroom house, maintaining the temperature at                15-17 degrees C in 6-7 days under the light                intensity of 500-1000 lux, controlling the carbon                dioxide concentration to not exceed 5000 PPM,                maintaining the humidity at 70-90%, controlling the                number of stages of mushroom buds on the 8th day by                removing the collar, controlling the concentration                of carbon dioxide in the mushroom room to 5000-7000                ppm, culturing, reducing the temperature of the                mushroom house in 11 days, controlling the                temperature of the mushroom house to 14.5-16.5                degrees C, the humidity not lower than 80%,                reducing the temperature of the mushroom house in                12th-13th day, controlling the temperature of the                mushroom house to 14-16 degrees C, allowing the                mushroom buds to develop smoothly to 6-8 cm,                followed by handling the elongation stage of the                mushroom by reducing the temperature of the                mushroom house, controlling the temperature of the                mushroom house to 13.5-15.5 degrees C and the                humidity not lower than 85%, controlling the carbon                dioxide concentration in the mushroom room to be                6000-8000 PPM, cultivating the mushroom in 15th day                by reducing the temperature of the mushroom house,                controlling the temperature of the mushroom house                to be 13-15 degrees C, reducing the temperature of                the mushroom house in 16th day, controlling the                temperature of the mushroom house to be 12.5-14.5                degrees C, lowering the temperature of the mushroom                house in 17th day, controlling the temperature of                the mushroom room to 12-14 degrees C, lowering the                temperature of the mushroom house in 18th day,                controlling the temperature of the mushroom house                to be 11.5-13.5 degrees C, forming the shaped                mushroom, cultivating commercial mushroom on the                19th day by reducing the carbon dioxide                concentration in the mushroom room, controlling the                indoor carbon dioxide concentration in the mushroom                room to be 1000-1500PPM, promoting the mushroom                cover to expand, and thickening the mushroom for                forming the mushroom.

TI Weather resistant full welding manufacturing method of welded steel stiff girder bridge structure comprises e.g. manufacturing weathering steel stiffening beam forming rods, cleaning the manufactured rod, removing mark,and sandblasting.
AU XU X
   GAO J
   HU G
   TIAN Z
   BEI Y
   FU C
   LIU Z
   CHEN Y
   YU Y
   CHANG G
   GU X
   GU C
AE CHINA RAILWAY SHANGHAI GUAN BRIDGE GROUP (CREN-C)
GA 201839491T
AB    NOVELTY - Weather resistant full welding manufacturing method of welded steel stiff girder bridge structure comprises (i) manufacturing weathering steel stiffening beam forming rods, box-shaped rods, anchor-pull plate units and tuyere units, core carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, automatically welded by submerged arc, (ii) cleaning the manufactured rod, removing mark, spatter and other foreign objects, sandblasting, storing, and sprinkling to accelerate corrosion, (iii) assembling plurality of weathering steel stiffening beam members into beam segments, assembling from the middle to the two sides in sequence, and completing the secondary cutting as needed after the welding is completed, and (iv) pre-assemble the weathering steel stiffening beam to the predetermined position on the bridge, measuring relevant data, carrying out multi-point welding positioning, weld the weathering steel on the welded bridge and stiffen the beam section, weathering steel on the welded bridge.
   USE - The method is useful for welding weather resistant completely welded steel structure stiffened suspension bridge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: ensures manufacture and installation accuracy of entire bridge and reduces welding distortion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Weather-resistant full-welding manufacturing method of welded steel stiff girder bridge structure comprises (i) manufacturing weathering steel stiffening beam forming rods, box-shaped rods, anchor-pull plate units and tuyere units, in addition to the main longitudinal beam of the main beam, the main angle welds, the hanging lugs and the extension, the fillet weld is made of core carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, and the other main fillet welds are automatically welded by submerged arc, the tuyere plate unit is automatically welded by flux cored wire robot, the other flat welds are solid welding wire carbon dioxide gas protection semi-automatic welding , allowing to stand and upright welds are semi-automatic welding with flux cored carbon dioxide gas protection, (ii) cleaning the manufactured rod, removing the mark, spatter and other foreign objects, sandblasting, storing, and sprinkling to accelerate corrosion, (iii) assembling plurality of weathering steel stiffening beam members into beam segments, assembling from the middle to the two sides in sequence, and completing the secondary cutting as needed after the welding is completed, (iv) pre-assemble the weathering steel stiffening beam to the predetermined position on the bridge, measuring relevant data, carrying out multi-point welding positioning, weld the weathering steel on the welded bridge and stiffen the beam section, weathering steel on the welded bridge, where the technical measures for the full-section weld between the stiffened beam sections include (a) The top plate, bottom plate and web adopt double-sided V-shaped groove, the groove angle is 40 degrees , the gap is 6mm, and the back is lined with ceramic liner, the top plate and the bottom plate are connected by solid wire carbon dioxide gas protection welding and the cored wire carbon dioxide gas, welding method for protecting the filling of the cover surface, the web is butt jointed with a flux-cored wire carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, (b) full-section welding adopts the wrong seam design, the butt welds of the top plate, the web and the bottom plate are not in one plane, when the main joint weld between the web and the top and bottom plates is welded in the workshop, the end is reserved for 200 mm without welding, and the site is installed, when the un-welded part is used, the wrong side and the wrong side of the web and the top and bottom plates are adjusted, after welding the top and bottom plates and the web butt welds, the main fillet weld portion is reserved, (c) the welds shall be arranged symmetrically as far as possible, the main longitudinal beams and the wind nozzles on both sides shall be symmetrically arranged, the cross-section welds of the full-section butt joints of the beam and the main longitudinal beam shall be symmetrically arranged, and the longitudinal joints of the small longitudinal beams shall be symmetrically arranged, and (d) the transverse welding sequence is welded from the central axis of the bridge deck to both sides, and symmetric welding is applied, and the same welding parameters are used for welding in the same direction, the arcing and arc-receiving positions of the symmetric weld are consistent, large welds, welds with low weld rigidity, after the completion of the tack weld, the butt welds of the weld top floor, the butt welds of the back welds, and the reserved portion of the welds of the welder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: the utility model is characterized in that the solid welding wire carbon dioxide gas protection semi-automatic welding is matched with a solid welding wire with a grade of CHW-55NHQ1 and a diameter of phi 1.2 mm. Flux cored wire carbon dioxide gas protection semi-automatic welding is matched with a flux-cored wire with grade of CHT71NHQ and diameter of phi 1.2mm. The flux-cored wire carbon dioxide gas protection robot is automatically welded with a flux-cored wire of JQ.YJ501NiCrCu-1 and diameter of phi 1.4 mm. The manual arc welding is matched with a weathering steel electrode with diameter of E5015-G and diameter of phi 4.0mm. Automatic submerged arc welding, matching, welding wire with the grade CHW55NHQ3 and diameter is phi 4.0mm, with the flux of CHF105NHQ. Preferred Method: The utility model is characterized in that the preheating and the inter-channel temperature are controlled before the welding, the steel plate with the thickness less than or equal to 24 mm does not need to be preheated before welding, and the temperature between the channels is controlled at less than or equal to 200 degrees C. The minimum preheating temperature of steel plate with thickness is greater-than 24-40 mm is 80 degrees C, and the temperature between channels is controlled at 80-200 degrees C. The minimum preheating temperature of the steel plate with a thickness of greater-than 40-60mm is 120 degrees C, and the temperature between the channels is controlled at 120-200 degrees C. The prefabricated tire frame is provided at a predetermined position, and the beam segment assembling process is performed on the prefabricated tire frame.

PT P
PN CN114525187-A
TI Brewing technique of blueberry fruit wine comprises e.g. adding blueberry pomace into clear water and placing in supercritical carbon dioxide solution for extraction, centrifuging extracted fruit slurry, adding sucrose, inoculating with Saccharomyces cerevisiae, and carrying out pre-fermentation.
AU SONG Z
   YE Z
   SHEN M
AE ANHUI CHANGRONG AGRIC COMPREHENSIVE DEV (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202274380G
AB    NOVELTY - Brewing technique of blueberry fruit wine comprises (1) selecting blueberry raw materials, (2) adding cellulase, pectinase and hemicellulase to the blueberry pomace for enzymatic hydrolysis for 2-3 hours, (3) adding above-mentioned blueberry pomace into clear water and then placing it in a supercritical carbon dioxide solution for extraction to obtain an extracted fruit slurry, (4) adding sucrose to the above-mentioned supernatant and blueberry juice to adjust the sugar content, (5) mixing the above-mentioned pre-fermentation wine liquid A and pre-fermentation wine liquid B, (6) adding the above-mentioned mixed fermented wine liquid with a clarifying agent for mixing and stirring to separate the precipitation after overnight precipitation, and (7) adjusting the alcohol content of the above-mentioned clarified wine liquid to 10-13% V/V, pasteurizing and storing to obtain blueberry fruit wine.
   USE - The method is useful for brewing technique of blueberry fruit wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: overcomes the deficiencies of the prior art, can improve the content of nutrients in the blueberry fruit wine through multiple steps of extraction and separate fermentation of different strains of blueberries, increases the content of flavor substances, and improves the quality of blueberry fruit wine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Brewing technique of blueberry fruit wine comprises (1) selecting blueberry raw materials, removing the bad fruits, cleaning and drying, then adding water to the blueberries and mixing, then grinding, and filtering them through a filter to obtain blueberry pomace and blueberry juice, (2) adding cellulase, pectinase and hemicellulase to the blueberry pomace for enzymatic hydrolysis for 2-3 hours, then freezing at low temperature, and then thawing in microwave environment to obtain thawed pomace, (3) adding the above-mentioned blueberry pomace into clear water and then placing it in a supercritical carbon dioxide solution for extraction to obtain an extracted fruit slurry, and centrifuging the extracted fruit slurry to obtain a supernatant, (4) adding sucrose to the above-mentioned supernatant and blueberry juice to adjust the sugar content, then inoculating the supernatant with Lamander Saccharomyces cerevisiae, carrying out pre-fermentation in a low temperature environment to obtain pre-fermented wine liquid A, inoculating blueberry juice with Pichia kudriavzevii, and performing pre-fermentation in a low-temperature environment to obtain pre-fermented wine liquid B, (5) mixing the above-mentioned pre-fermentation wine liquid A and pre-fermentation wine liquid B, carrying out normal temperature fermentation for 35-40 days, and mixing fermented wine liquid, (6) adding the above-mentioned mixed fermented wine liquid with a clarifying agent for mixing and stirring to separate the precipitation after overnight precipitation, and then filtering the wine liquid and clarifying by a microporous membrane filter to obtain a clarified wine liquid, and (7) adjusting the alcohol content of the above-mentioned clarified wine liquid to 10-13% V/V, pasteurizing and storing to obtain blueberry fruit wine.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-E; D05-A02C
IP C12G-003/024; C12R-001/84; C12R-001/865

PN KR2236011-B1; WO2022102887-A1
TI Culturing natural killer (NK) cells by separating peripheral blood mononuclear cell pellet, treating with RBC lysis buffer, adding e.g. RPMI and interleukin-2 in T75 flask, incubating it, performing subculture, and activating NK cells.
AU KANG D
   KANG H C
   KANG D W
AE HANBIO CO LTD (HANB-Non-standard)
   KANG D (KANG-Individual)
   KANG D W (KANG-Individual)
GA 2021384695
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing natural killer (NK) cells involves (a) removing red blood cells by using Ficoll, performing centrifugation to separate a pellet of peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) containing immune cells from blood, and treating the pellet with an RBC lysis buffer, (b) adding RPMI, plasma, interleukin (IL)-2, IL-15, anti-CD56 antibody, anti-NKp46 antibody and anti-NKp30 antibody in a T75 flask coated with an anti-CD16 antibody, and incubating it, (c) adding cell culture suspension, RPMI, albumin, IL-2, IL-15, anti-NKp30 antibody, anti-NKp46 antibody and anti-CD56 antibody, performing primary subculture and performing secondary subculture, and (d) activating NK cells by treating NK cells with 20 ng/ml IL-2 and 50 ng/ml IL-15 cytokines in a medium composition based on subcultured PBMCs with an addition of RPMI and albumin, anti-NKp30 antibody, anti-NKp46 antibody, and anti-CD56 antibody.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing NK cells, which is used for promoting apoptosis or killing ability of cancer cells and preventing or treating cancer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for culturing natural killer (NK) cells involves (a) removing red blood cells by using Ficoll, performing centrifugation to separate a pellet of peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) containing immune cells from blood, and treating the pellet with an RBC lysis buffer, (b) adding 8 ml RPMI, 2 ml plasma, 20 ng/ml interleukin (IL)-2, 50 ng/ml IL-15, 5 ng/ml anti-CD56 antibody, 5 ng/ml anti-NKp46 antibody and 5 ng/ml anti-NKp30 antibody if the number of isolated PBMC cells is 3x 107 or less, in a T25 flask coated with an anti-CD16 antibody, and if the number of cells is 3x 107 or more, in a T75 flask coated with an anti-CD16 antibody, and floating the PBMC pellet with 10 ml RPMI, and incubating it in a 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 3-4 days, (c) adding 20 ml cell culture suspension, 72 ml RPMI, 8 ml albumin, 20 ng/ml IL-2, 50 ng/ml IL-15, 5 ng/ml anti-NKp30 antibody, 5 ng/ml anti-NKp46 antibody and 5 ng/ml anti-CD56 antibody in 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 3-5 days, performing primary subculture on suspension of 50 ml with an addition of 180 ml RPMI, 20 ml albumin, 20 ng/ml IL-2, and 50 ng/ml IL-15 and performing secondary subculture for 5-7 days in a 37 degrees C, and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, and (d) activating NK cells by treating NK cells with 20 ng/ml IL-2 and 50 ng/ml IL-15 cytokines in a medium composition based on subcultured PBMCs with an addition of RPMI and albumin, anti-NKp30 antibody, anti-NKp46 antibody, and anti-CD56 antibody.
TF ACTIVITY - Cytostatic; Apoptotic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Cell therapy.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN111412677-A; WO2021218147-A1
TI Cab carbon dioxide cooling medium frequency conversion air conditioner includes economizer comprising inlets and outlets, mass flow meter, check valve, expansion valve, and regenerator comprising inlets and outlets.
AU CHENG X
   CHEN G
   PANG X
   WEI Z
   DUO J
   ZENG L
AE CRRC DALIAN INST CO LTD (CRRC-C)
   CRRC DALIAN INST CO LTD (CRRC-C)
GA 202068935D
AB    NOVELTY - Cab carbon dioxide (CO2) cooling medium frequency conversion air conditioner includes economizer (17) and regenerator (3). The economizer comprises a first inlet, second inlet, first outlet and second outlet, where the first inlet is connected with the first outlet, and the second inlet and second outlet are connected with each other. The first inlet and second inlet are connected with an outlet of a gas cooler. First mass flow meter, check valve and second expansion valve are arranged between the first inlet and gas cooler. A second mass flow meter is set between the second inlet and gas cooler. The regenerator comprises a third inlet, fourth inlet, third outlet and fourth outlet, where the third inlet and third outlet are connected with each other, and the fourth inlet and fourth outlet are connected with each other. The third outlet, first expansion valve and evaporator are sequentially communicated.
   USE - Used as cab CO2 cooling medium frequency conversion air conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The economizer and regenerator are used to cool the cooling medium multiple times to improve the energy efficiency ratio of the unit, and the pressure relief valve, temperature sensor, and pressure sensor are set to realize the active protection of the system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cab CO2 cooling medium frequency conversion air conditioner comprises condensation cavity, evaporation cavity, economizer (17) and regenerator (3). The condensation cavity is provided with a compressor (1), gas cooler (2), and condensation fan (7). The evaporation cavity is internally provided with an evaporator and first expansion valve. The economizer comprises a first inlet, second inlet, first outlet and second outlet. The first inlet is connected with the first outlet. The second inlet and second outlet are connected with each other. The first inlet and second inlet are connected with the outlet of the gas cooler. First mass flow meter, check valve and second expansion valve are provided between the first inlet and gas cooler. A second mass flow meter is provided between the second inlet and gas cooler. The compressor is provided with auxiliary air inlet. The regenerator comprises a third inlet, fourth inlet, third outlet and fourth outlet. The third inlet and third outlet are connected with each other. The fourth inlet and fourth outlet are connected with each other. The second outlet is connected with the third inlet. The third outlet, first expansion valve and evaporator are sequentially connected. The evaporator outlet is connected with a gas-liquid separator, which is connected with the fourth outlet, fourth outlet and compressor. The outlet side of the gas cooler is provided with a first pressure sensor and pressure relief valve. The suction side of the compressor is provided with a second pressure sensor and a first temperature sensor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic diagram of cab carbon dioxide cooling medium frequency conversion air conditioner.
   Compressor (1)
   Gas cooler (2)
   Regenerator (3)
   Condensation fan (7)
   Economizer (17)
DC Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))
MC Q75-A02A; Q75-T03; Q75-T09; Q75-T20

PT P
PN CN110199988-A
TI Complex herbicide obtained by adding thionyl chloride solution to microporous starch, adding diethyl ether, reacting mixture, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting mixture, adding mixed coating agent, heating, filtering and cooling mixture.
AU LUO H
AE LUO H (LUOH-Individual)
GA 201978622J
AB    NOVELTY - Complex herbicide is obtained by (a) weighing a certain amount of corn starch, adding acetic acid solution having a pH of 5-5.5, mixing the mixture under stirring, adding enzyme formulation, and drying the mixture to obtain microporous starch, (b) adding thionyl chloride solution to the microporous starch to obtaining a chlorinated starch base, (c) adding diethyl ether to the chlorinated starch base, reacting the mixture under stirring, introducing carbon dioxide into reaction vessel, reacting mixture, filtering the mixture, and drying the mixture to obtain a polycarboxy-microporous starch, (d) weighing ethametsulfuron and acetone solution into the reaction kettle, stirring the mixture, adding polycarboxylated microporous starch, reacting the mixture, and drying to obtain a starch-supported herbicide, and (e) adding mixed coating agent to stirred tank, heating the mixture, adding starch-supported herbicide, filtering mixture and cooling mixture.
   USE - As complex herbicide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Complex herbicide is obtained by (a) weighing a certain amount of corn starch, adding acetic acid solution having a pH of 5-5.5, mixing the mixture under stirring, adding enzyme formulation, stirring the reaction mixture at room temperature for 8-9 hours, adjusting a pH of the reaction solution to 10, continually stirring the reaction mixture for 5-10 minutes, filtering product to neutrality, and drying the mixture to obtain microporous starch, (b) adding thionyl chloride solution to the microporous starch, ultrasonically dispersing for 30-40 minutes, adding dispersion to the reaction vessel, increasing the temperature to 70-80 degrees C to stir reaction mixture for 6-7 hours, filtering the mixture, washing filtrate, again washing the mixture, and drying the mixture to obtain a chlorinated starch base, (c) adding diethyl ether to the chlorinated starch base, ultrasonically dispersing for 30-40 minutes to obtain a chlorinated starch-based dispersion, slowly adding the chlorinated starch-based dispersion to anhydrous ether soaked with magnesium chips, reacting the mixture under stirring at room temperature for 40-50 minutes, continuously introducing carbon dioxide into the reaction vessel, reacting the mixture for 80-90 minutes, filtering the mixture, adding filtered product to deionized water, stirring the mixture, mixing for 1-2 hours, filtering the mixture, and drying the mixture to obtain a polycarboxy-microporous starch, (d) weighing a certain amount of ethametsulfuron and acetone solution into the reaction kettle, stirring the mixture, dissolving the mixture, adding polycarboxylated microporous starch, reacting the mixture under stirring at room temperature for 40-50 minutes, subjecting the mixture to filtration-washing and drying to obtain a starch-supported herbicide, and (e) adding mixed coating agent to stirred tank, heating the mixture at 90 degrees C to be stirred and melted, adding starch-supported herbicide, maintaining ultrasonic dispersion for 20-30 minutes while maintaining room temperature, filtering the mixture and cooling the filtered mixture to obtain complex herbicide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves adding thionyl chloride in an amount of 5-6 ml/g of microporous starch. The step (c) involves adding diethyl ether in an amount of 10-12 ml/g of chlorinated starch base, and magnesium in an amount of 0.13 g. The step (d) involves adding ethametsulfuron-methyl in an amount of 0.24-0.26 g/g of polycarboxy microporous starch, and 8.5-9 ml acetone solution. The step (e) involves adding 0.23-0.26 g starch-loaded herbicide to the melt of mixed coating agent, where the mixed coating agent is prepared by mixing polyethylene oxide and a degradable polymer in a ratio of a mass ratio of 1:0.23-0.25. The degradable polymer is prepared by weighing a certain amount of trimethylolpropane triglycidyl ether into acetone in a ratio of 1:1, stirring the mixture, uniformly mixing the mixture, adding glutamic acid to it, heating the mixture at 50-60 degrees C, stirring the mixture for 2-3 hours, subjecting to filtration-washing and drying to obtain a degradable polymer.
   ACTIVITY - Herbicide.
DC C07 (Apparatus, formulation, general. including veterinary syringes, general formulations where the active compound is not central to the invention (e.g. wettable powders) and analysis.); C02 (Heterocyclic.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A82 (Coatings, impregnations, polishes - excluding textile finishing.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
MC C04-C02B3; C04-C03C; C07-A03; C07-D13; C10-A10; C10-C04E6; C10-H01; C14-S18; C14-V; D05-A02; D05-A04; A03-A00A; A10-E04A; A10-E23; A11-A03; A12-W04C; A12-W11L; P13-A04

PT P
PN EP3363770-A1; WO2018149641-A1; CA3049733-A1; AU2018221479-A1; IN201947036864-A; CN110352175-A; EP3583067-A1; US2020055738-A1; BR112019016817-A2; RU2019128252-A; IN373833-B; EP3583067-B1; RU2759379-C2; US11286168-B2
TI Synthesis of ammonia from natural gas by conversion of desulfurized natural gas and steam into synthesis gas, treatment by shift reaction of carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide, and separation of carbon-dioxide-depleted gas as fuel fraction.
AU BARATTO F
   OSTUNI R
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 2018648657
AB    NOVELTY - Ammonia is synthesized from natural gas by converting charge of desulfurized natural gas and steam, with oxygen-enriched air or with oxygen, into a synthesis gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide (CO), and carbon dioxide (CO2), in a conversion section; treating synthesis gas by shift reaction of CO into CO2 and subsequently separating CO2 from the gas to obtain CO2-depleted synthesis gas and CO2-rich gaseous flow containing CO2 separated from the gas; optionally, methanating synthesis gas and/or adding nitrogen to synthesis gas; and separating a portion of CO2-depleted synthesis gas as fuel fraction, which is fed as fuel to greater than or equal to 1 furnace.
   USE - Synthesis of ammonia from natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves extremely low CO2 emission levels, corresponding to almost total capture ( greater than or equal to 98%), while maintaining competitive energy consumption levels. The separation of CO2 is performed from a synthesis gas which has a high concentration of CO2 and is at a relatively high pressure, making the separation of CO2 from this flow easier and require a lower energy consumption than CO2 separated from combustion fumes. The system does not require solvent to capture CO2 from fumes. The use of oxygen-enriched air gives rise increases the heat value of the synthesis gas; allows for combustion of synthesis gas to generate less fumes than combustion of natural gas, for the same amount of heat released, because the combustion of hydrogen (contained in the synthesis gas) occurs with a reduction of the number of mols, unlike the combustion of methane, so that the convective current of the reformer is smaller and requires an induced draft fan which is less powerful; limits the flue loss of nitrogen (which is a reagent), avoiding wastage of energy for nitrogen separation and compression; does not increase generation of nitrogen for synthesis; and allows a high temperature for conversion of the natural gas to be maintained, free from the constraint of avoiding the introduction of excessive nitrogen in relation to the stoichiometry of ammonia synthesis (typical of conventional air-fired processes) with a high conversion of the methane, resulting in minimal CO2 emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for revamping a plant for synthesis of ammonia from natural gas or ammonia/urea plant, comprising a reforming section including primary steam reformer and air-fired secondary reformer and a section for treatment of synthesis gas, which involves providing flow of enriched air or oxygen and using the flow as oxidant, instead of air, for the secondary reformer; separating a portion of synthesis gas, downstream of decarbonation, for use as fuel fraction, and allocating remaining process fraction of synthesis gas for ammonia conversion; feeding fuel fraction of the gas, which has reduced CO2 content, as fuel for greater than or equal to 1 furnaces of the plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Processes: Separation of fuel fraction involves splitting CO2-depleted synthesis gas into 1st stream and 2nd stream, where streams have the same composition, 1st stream forms fuel fraction, and 2nd stream is process gas intended for the synthesis of ammonia, optionally after further purification. Fuel fraction is separated upstream of the methanation step or upstream of the nitrogen addition step. Fuel fraction of synthesis gas feeds steam production and/or superheating furnaces; burners of primary steam reformer; and/or furnaces for pre-heating the charge of an autothermal reformer or partial oxidation reactor or natural-gas desulfurization section. Purge gas of ammonia synthesis process, containing hydrogen and methane, is treated to recover the hydrogen contained in it, obtaining a hydrogen-rich gas and a tail gas containing methane, where a portion of the tail gas is recycled as process gas and subjected to the conversion of natural gas into synthesis gas. A portion of CO2-depleted syngas is treated in a unit for separation of nitrogen, obtaining a 1st stream of nitrogen-depleted syngas and a 2nd nitrogen enriched stream, where greater than or equal to 1 portion of 1st stream, preferably all of it, is used as fuel, where any remaining portion of 1st stream is fed to synthesis of ammonia together with 2nd stream. The method further includes expansion, with production of mechanical work, of CO2-depleted syngas, optionally after heating it. Fuel fraction is produced in excess with respect to the fuel necessary for the conversion section, the amount of fuel fraction exceeding that required being exported from the process; and regulation of the conversion section is performed by modulating the amount of fuel fraction directed towards the conversion section and the amount exported from the process. Conversion of natural gas charge into synthesis gas involves autothermal reforming, optionally with pre-reforming, or a step of partial oxidation, where step of autothermal reforming or partial oxidation is performed with a steam/carbon ratio of not greater than 2. Conversion of natural gas into synthesis gas involves primary reforming of natural gas in the presence of steam and secondary reforming performed with oxygen flow or enriched air. Revamping method includes installing an air separation unit to provide oxygen or enriched air flow, where separation unit also provides a nitrogen flow which is added to the process fraction of the synthesis gas to obtain the desired hydrogen/nitrogen ratio. Preferred Compositions: Fuel fraction comprises 1-40%, preferably 10-30% of the total amount of CO2-depleted syngas. CO2-depleted syngas contains not greater than 1000 ppm by volume of CO2. Enriched air contains greater than or equal to 50%, preferably greater than or equal to 90% oxygen. Overall steam/carbon ratio in the conversion step is greater than or equal to 2, preferably greater than or equal to 2.7. Synthesis gas fuel fraction forms all of the fuel of the furnaces used in the conversion section, or an amount of natural gas is provided as trim fuel, which accounts for not greater than 15% of the total combustion heat. CO2 emissions are less than or equal to 0.5 tons of CO2 emitted per ton of ammonia produced.

PN CN107603820-A
TI Processing pearl plum wine for reducing blood pressure, involves placing mature pearl plum in ripening storeroom, maintaining temperature, relative humidity, ethylene concentration and carbon dioxide, and maintaining uniform flow of gas.
AU HE X
   LI C
   LI J
   LI L
   LI Z
   LING D
   LIU G
   SHENG J
   SUN J
   TANG Y
   WU C
   XIN M
   ZHENG F
AE CHINESE ACAD TROPICAL AGRIC SCI AGRO-PRO (CTAG-C)
   TIANE GANDONG XIANGTAI AGRIC TECHNOLOGY SERVICE PROFE (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201809653C
AB    NOVELTY - Processing pearl plum wine involves placing the 8 mature pearl plum in ripening storeroom, maintaining the temperature at 25-30?pC, relative humidity at 90%-95%, ethylene concentration at 20-40ppm, carbon dioxide content is 0.8-1%, maintaining the uniform flow of gas and ripening for 20-24 hours. The core of mature pearl plum is removed and juiced into pearl plum fruit pulp, pectinase is added to pearl plum pulp in an amount of 1-2 wt.%, and enzymolysis is carried out at 30-45 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours.
   USE - Method for processing pearl plum wine for reducing blood pressure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to process pearl plum wine, which has increased color and flavor, reduced acidity, has a strong aroma and a high content of aromatic substances including aroma component phenylethyl alcohol that accounts for 27.38% of the aroma substances, and increases the content of procyanidins up to 368 mg/100 mL.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing pearl plum wine involves placing the 8 mature pearl plum in ripening storeroom, maintaining the temperature at 25-30?pC, relative humidity at 90%-95%, ethylene concentration at 20-40ppm, carbon dioxide content is 0.8-1%, maintaining the uniform flow of gas and ripening for 20-24 hours. The core of mature pearl plum is removed and juiced into pearl plum fruit pulp, pectinase is added to pearl plum pulp in an amount of 1-2 wt.%, and enzymolysis is carried out at 30-45 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours. The crushed black rice noodles flour, black fungus powder, sugar and enzymatic hydrolysis of pearl plum pulp are mixed in a ratio of by 3-5:2-5:1-2:88-94 to form mixed pearl plum pulp, and the soluble solid content of the mixed pearl plum pulp is adjusted to 20-30% with white sugar. The mixed pearl plum pulp after being heat-sterilized in water bath at 80-100 degrees C for 10-30 minutes is added to fermenter, the temperature is reduced to 30-45 degrees C, the 0.1-0.5 wt.% mixed fermented bacterial strain, and fermentation is carried out at a constant temperature until the alcoholic degree of the fermented liquid is 10-15 degrees to obtain fermented wine. The mixed fermentative strain is prepared by mixing Lalvin EC1118, selenium-enriched yeast and Lactobacillus plantarum ST-III in a ratio of 3-5:1-2:1-2. The fermented wine is canned and the temperature is lowered to 5-10 degrees C for settling and aging. The fermented wine is filled in the glass bottle, packaged using aseptic filling technology, sterilization is carried out after filling, rubber cap is added to it to obtain pearl plum wine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The ripening conditions includes maintaining the temperature at 25-30 degrees C, relative humidity at 90-93%, ethylene concentration of 20-30 ppm and the carbon dioxide content of 0.8-1% for 20-24 hours. The enzymatic hydrolysis is carried out at a temperature of 35-45 degrees C for 2-3 hours. The crushed black rice noodles, black fungus powder, protein sugar and hydrolyzed pearl plum pulp are mixed in a ratio of 3-4:2-3:1-2:91-94 to form a mixed pearl plum pulp, and the soluble solid content of the mixed pearl plum pulp is adjusted to 20-28% with white sugar. The heat sterilization is carried out in water bath at 85-95 degrees C for 10-30 minutes. The temperature is lowered to 5-8 degrees C for settling and aging.

PN CN115184306-A
TI Optical waveguide carbon dioxide gas sensor based            on vernier effect, has input waveguide connected with            optical splitter, where Mach-Zehnder type sensing            interferometer and Mach-Zehnder type reference            interferometers form vernier effect to realize high            sensitivity.
AU ZHOU Q
   YAO W
   XU M
   LIU J
   JING C
   ZHOU A
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 2022D21054
AB    NOVELTY - The sensor has an input waveguide (1)                connected with a splitter. The splitter (2) divides                light of the input wave guide into a first branch                waveguide (3), a second branch waveguide (4) and a                third branch waveguide (5) through a light                coupling. The light of the first branch waveguide,                the second branch waveguide and the third branch                waveguide is coupled through the optical combiner                and output to the output waveguide. The first                branch waveguide and the second branch waveguide                form an asymmetric Mach-Zehnder type sensing                interferometer. The second branch waveguide and the                third branch waveguide form an asymmetric                Mach-Zehnder type reference interferometer. The                Mach-Zehnder type sensing interferometer and                Mach-Zehnder type reference interferometer form a                vernier effect to achieve high-sensitivity sensing                measurement of CO2 gas.
   USE - Optical waveguide carbon dioxide (CO2) gas                sensor based on vernier effect for detecting CO2                gas concentration by output potential.
   ADVANTAGE - The sensor forms a Mach-Zehnder interferometer                of three beams through three branch waveguides,                using the interferometry output spectrum to form                the vernier sensitization effect, realizing fast                measurement of the CO2 gas, and the sensitivity is                greatly improved, since the envelope output                spectrum generated based on the vernier effect of                the invention, compared with the single sensing                interferometers, obtains higher sensitivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the use                method of the optical waveguide CO gas sensor based                on vernier effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a basic structure diagram                of the optical waveguide CO2 gas sensor.
   1Input waveguide
   2Optical splitter
   3First branch waveguide
   4Second branch waveguide
   5Third branch waveguide
   6Combiner

PN JP2022145656-A
TI Solid electrolyte electrolysis device used for            producing polycarbonate and polyurethane, comprises            cathode, anode, electrolytic cell, solid electrolyte,            and voltage applying unit and treated material contains            benzene or olefin.
AU CHEN H W
   JIA Q X
   XIONG A K
AE IDEMITSU KOSAN CO LTD (IDEK-C)
GA 2022C8801T
AB    NOVELTY - A solid electrolyte electrolysis device (100)                comprises a cathode (101) which undergoes a                reduction reaction, an anode (102) forming a pair                of electrodes with the cathode, an electrolytic                cell (108) holding an material to be electrolyzed                by the anode, a solid electrolyte (103) interposed                in contact between the cathode and the anode, and a                voltage applying unit (106) which applies a voltage                between the cathode and the anode. The treated                material contains benzene or olefin.
   USE - Solid electrolyte electrolysis device used for                producing polycarbonate and polyurethane (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The solid electrolyte electrolysis device                reduces the energy required for electrolysis by                reducing the applied voltage required for                electrolysis at the anode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   production of polycarbonate using the solid                electrolyte electrolytic device, which involves                electrolyzing benzene at the anode to produce at                least phenol, and carbon monoxide from carbon                dioxide at the cathode, and reacting the phenol                with acetone to produce bisphenol A, reacting the                carbon monoxide with chlorine to generate phosgene,                and reacting the bisphenol A and the phosgene to                produce a polycarbonate; and
   production of polyurethane using the solid                electrolyte electrolytic device, which involves                electrolyzing olefin at the anode to produce at                least olefin oxide, and carbon monoxide from carbon                dioxide at the cathode, and reacting the olefin                oxide with water to produce olefin glycol, reacting                the carbon monoxide with chlorine to generate                phosgene, reacting the phosgene with a primary                amine to generate an isocyanate compound, and                reacting the olefin glycol and the isocyanate                compound to produce polyurethane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                solid electrolyte electrolytic device. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
   100Solid electrolyte electrolytic                device
   101Cathode
   102Anode
   103Solid electrolyte
   104Current collector
   105Support plate
   106Voltage applying unit
   108Electrolytic cell
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process for producing                  polycarbonate and polyurethane further involves                  generating the chlorine by electrolysis of salt                  water in the solid electrolyte electrolysis                  device (D2) containing cathode (C2) which                  undergoes a reduction reaction, a anode (A2)                  forming a pair of electrodes with the cathode                  (C2), an electrolytic cell holding salt water,                  which is the material to be electrolyzed in the                  anode (A2), a solid electrolyte interposed in                  contact between the cathode (C2) and the anode                  (A2), and a voltage applying unit that applies a                  voltage between the cathode (C2) and the anode                  (A2).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The olefin contains                  ethylene or propylene.

PN CN115096060-A
TI Device useful for producing agricultural planting            fertilizer, has coking furnace with both ends            symmetrically provided with first end cover and second            end cover, and temperature sensor, humidity sensor and            carbon dioxide detector installed on lower surface of            wire arranging pipe.
AU LI Y
   WU J
AE GAOTANG YATAI FERTILIZER CO LTD (GAOT-Non-standard)
GA 2022C5470D
AB    NOVELTY - Device has a coking furnace (3) with both ends                symmetrically provided with first end cover (2) and                second end cover (10). The coking furnace is                provided with an air pipe (6) and a wire arranging                pipe (8). The surface of the air pipe is                symmetrically provided with nozzles (17)                distributed with equal distance. A temperature                sensor (4), a humidity sensor (7) and a carbon                dioxide detector (9) are respectively installed on                the lower surface of the wire arranging pipe. A                transmission wire is inserted in the wire arranging                pipe. The outer wall of one side of the first end                cover is provided with a feeding hopper (1). PT P
PN CN114308043-A
TI Preparing acidified two-dimensional layered vermiculite-supported nickel-based catalyst for catalytic synthesis of carbon monoxide and hydrogen, involves adding nickel nitrate solution dropwise to acid-treated vermiculite powder carrier.
AU GUO D
   HUANG S
   LV J
   ZHAO Y
   WANG S
   MA X
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202256235N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing an acidified two-dimensional layered vermiculite-supported nickel-based catalyst, involves (i) immersing the expanded vermiculite in the hydrochloric acid solution under stirring condition, and vigorously stirring and refluxing for 4-6 hours at 60-90degrees Celsius. The obtained bright yellow precipitate is washed with deionized water, suction filtered, and dried to obtain an acid-treated vermiculite powder carrier, where the concentration of hydrochloric acid solution is 1-3 mol/L. (ii) The nickel nitrate solution is added dropwise to acid-treated vermiculite powder carrier, dried in the air at 100-120degrees Celsius for 6-10 hours to obtain dry powder, where each 1 mL of nickel nitrate solution contains 0.06-0.17 gram nickel nitrate hexahydrate 0.5-3 mL mixed solution is added dropwise to each 1 gram acid-treated vermiculite carrier. (iii) The dried powder is heated at 500-600degrees Celsius in an air atmosphere, and calcined for 3-5 hours to obtain a target catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing acidified two-dimensional layered vermiculite-supported nickel-based catalyst used for catalytic synthesis of carbon monoxide and hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple catalyst composition, simple and convenient preparation process, easy operation and cost-effective production. The catalyst prepared without adding an auxiliary agent subsequently has uniform dispersion of metal particles, small average particle size, strong sintering resistance and carbon deposition resistance at high temperature, and has good industrial prospect when applied to the reforming reaction of methane and carbon dioxide to prepare the synthesis gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an application of method for preparing an acidified two-dimensional layered vermiculite-supported nickel-based catalyst used for catalytic synthesis of carbon monoxide and hydrogen, by using carbon dioxide and methane as raw materials
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The acidified two-dimensional layered vermiculite supported nickel-based catalyst is heated at 450-550degrees Celsius in a nitrogen atmosphere, and then the gas is switched to a pure hydrogen atmosphere to be reduced for 1-3 hours. The supported amount of nickel in the catalyst is 2.5-7.5 wt.%. The 5-15 gram expanded vermiculite is added to every 200 ml hydrochloric acid solution. The rotation number of the violent stirring is 700-900 revolution per minute. The vacuum drying temperature is 60-90degrees Celsius, and the time is 3-5 hours. The heating rate is 1-3degrees Celsius/min. The hydrochloric acid treatment concentration of the acidified two-dimensional exfoliated porous vermiculite is 2.5 mol/L. The water absorption capacity of each 1 gram acid-treated vermiculite carrier is 2.3 mL, and the vacuum drying temperature is 80degrees Celsius, the heating rate is 2degrees Celsius/min, the roasting temperature is 550degrees Celsius, and the roasting time in the air atmosphere is 4 hours, the reduction temperature is 500degrees Celsius, and the reduction time in a pure hydrogen atmosphere is 2 hours. The catalyst is prepared by a slight excess impregnation method. The loading amount of nickel is 5 wt.%, and the particle size of nickel is 3-5 nm.Preferred Process: The catalyst is mixed with quartz sand and put into a fixed bed reactor at 700-800degrees Celsius with space velocity of 90000-100000 mLh-1 gcat-1, and added methane, carbon dioxide and nitrogen to obtain carbon monoxide and hydrogen, where the raw material ratio of methane, carbon dioxide and nitrogen is 1:1:2.

PN US2022106430-A1
TI Polyol premix composition for foamable composition            and for preparing a polyurethane or polyisocyanurate            foam, comprises combination of a blowing agent, a            polyol, a silicone surfactant, and a sterically            hindered amine catalyst.
AU NALEWAJEK D
   NAIR H K
   WILLIAMS D J
   VAN DER PUY M
AE HONEYWELL INT INC (HONE-C)
GA 202249406L
AB    NOVELTY - Polyol premix composition comprises a                combination of a blowing agent, a polyol, a                silicone surfactant, and a sterically hindered                amine catalyst. The blowing agent comprises a                hydrohaloolefin, preferably a hydrocarbon,                fluorocarbon, chlorocarbon, fluorochlorocarbon,                halogenated hydrocarbon, carbon dioxide (CO2)                generating material, or combinations. The                sterically hindered amine catalyst is represented                by linear formula with the proviso that the                sterically hindered amine catalyst has a sum of                Charton's steric parameters of about 1.65 or                greater.
   USE - Polyol premix composition used for foamable                composition and for preparing a polyurethane or                polyisocyanurate foam (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyol premix composition provides foam                with good quality, and with a fine and regular cell                structure
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polyol premix composition comprises a                combination of a blowing agent, a polyol, a                silicone surfactant, and a sterically hindered                amine catalyst. The blowing agent comprises a                hydrohaloolefin, preferably a hydrocarbon,                fluorocarbon, chlorocarbon, fluorochlorocarbon,                halogenated hydrocarbon, carbon dioxide (CO2)                generating material, or combinations. The                sterically hindered amine catalyst is                R1R2N-(A-NR3)nR4, where R1-R4 is H, a 1-8C alkyl                group, 1-8C alkenyl group, 1-8C alcohol group, or                1-8C ether group; A is 1-5C alkyl group, a 1-5C                alkenyl group, or an ether; n is 0-3, with the                proviso that the sterically hindered amine catalyst                has a sum of Charton's steric parameters of about                1.65 or greater.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   1. a foamable composition comprises a mixture                of an organic polyisocyanate and the polyol premix                composition; and
   2. a method of preparing a polyurethane or                polyisocyanurate foam, which involves reacting an                organic polyisocyanate with the polyol premix                composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hydrohaloolefin                  comprises fluoroalkene or chloroalkene containing                  from 3-4 carbon atoms and at least one                  carbon-carbon double bond. The hydrohaloolefin                  comprises a trifluoropropenes, a                  tetrafluoropropenes, a pentafluoropropene, a                  chlorodifluoropropene, a chlorotrifluoropropene,                  a chlorotetrafluoropropene, or combinations,                  preferably the hydrohaloolefin comprises                  1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene,                  2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene,                  1,1,3,3-tetrafluoropropene,                  1,2,3,3,3-pentafluoropropene,                  1,1,1-trifluoropropene, 3,3,3-trifluoropropene,                  1,1,1,3-tetrafluoropropene,                  1,1,1,3,3-pentafluoropropene,                  1,1,2,3,3-pentafluoropropene,                  1,1,1,2-tetrafluoropropene,                  1,1,1,2,3-pentafluoropropene,                  1-chloro-3,3,3-trifluoropropene,                  1,1,1,4,4,4-hexafluorobut-2-ene or structural                  isomers, geometric isomers or stereoisomers or                  combinations. The blowing agent comprises water,                  formic acid, organic acids that produce CO2 when                  they react with an isocyanate, hydrocarbons,                  ethers, esters, aldehydes, ketones, halogenated                  ethers, pentafluorobutane, pentafluoropropane,                  hexafluoropropane, heptafluoropropane, trans-1,2                  dichloroethylene, methyl formate,                  1-chloro-1,2,2,2-tetrafluoroethane,                  1,1-dichloro-1-fluoroethane,                  1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane,                  1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane,                  1-chloro-1,1-difluoroethane,                  1,1,1,3,3-pentafluorobutane,                  1,1,1,2,3,3,3-heptafluoropropane,                  trichlorofluoromethane or combinations. The                  silicone surfactant comprises a polysiloxane                  polyoxyalkylene block co-polymer. The polyol                  premix composition further comprising a                  non-silicone, non-ionic surfactant. The polyol                  comprises one or more of a sucrose containing                  polyol, phenol, a phenol formaldehyde containing                  polyol, a glucose containing polyol, a sorbitol                  containing polyol, a methylglucoside containing                  polyol, an aromatic polyester polyol, glycerol,                  ethylene glycol, diethylene glycol, propylene                  glycol, graft copolymers of polyether polyols                  with a vinyl polymer or a copolymer of a                  polyether polyol with a polyurea. The sterically                  hindered teriary amine catalyst comprises                  cyclohexyldimethylamine, dicyclohexylmethylamine,                  ethyldiisopropylamine, dimethylcyclohexylamine,                  dimethylisopropylamine,                  methylisopropylbenzylamine,                  methylcyclopentylbenzylamine,                  isopropyl-sec-butyl-trifluoroethylamine,                  diethyl-(alpha-phenylethyl)amine,                  tri-n-propylamine or combinations. The polyol                  premix composition further comprising a non-amine                  catalyst comprising an organometallic compound                  containing bismuth, lead, tin, titanium,                  antimony, uranium, cadmium, cobalt, thorium,                  aluminum, mercury, zinc, nickel, cerium,                  molybdenum, vanadium, copper, manganese,                  zirconium, potassium, sodium, or combinations.                  The organic polyisocyanate comprises a                  polymethylene polyphenyl isocyanate,                  methylenebis(phenyl isocyanate), toluene                  diisocyanate, or combinations.
   EXAMPLE - A polyol (B Component) formulation was made up                of 100 pts. wt. polyol blend, 1.5 pts. wt. Niax                L6900 silicone surfactant, 3 pts. wt. water, 8 pts.                wt. triethyl phosphate flame retardant, 0.7 pts.                wt. N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine (sold as Polycat 8                by Air Products) catalyst and 8 pts. wt.                trans-1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene blowing agent. The                total B component composition, when freshly                prepared and combined with 217.3 pts. wt. Lupranate                M20S polymeric isocyanate yielded a good quality                foam with a fine and regular cell structure. Foam                reactivity was typical of a slow reacting pour in                place foam with a gel time of 285 seconds. The                total B-side composition (119.7 pts. wt.) was then                aged at 120degrees Fahrenheit for 62 hours, and                then combined with 217.3 pts. wt. M20S Iso                polyisocyanate to make a foam. The foam was normal                in appearance without cell collapse. Gel time was                300 seconds. No discoloration was noted during                aging. The low fluoride produced in the test                successfully predicted that good foam could be made                with this amine, even after aging.

PN CN113578326-A
TI Double-layer core-shell catalyst with nickel supported on silicon dioxide used for preparing methane carbon dioxide reforming reaction, comprises core layer and shell layer is covering core layer, where core layer is silicon dioxide.
AU ZHONG Z
   ZHOU Y
   LI J
   WAN Z
   TAO Y
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY SHENZHEN GRADUATE (HAIT-C)
GA 2021C6979N
AB    NOVELTY - Double-layer core-shell catalyst with nickel supported on silicon dioxide comprises core layer and shell layer is covering core layer. The core layer is silicon dioxide loaded with nickel. The shell layer includes cerium dioxide, aluminum oxide, silicon dioxide, titanium dioxide, lanthanum oxide, praseodymium oxide, samarium oxide, magnesium oxide, zirconium dioxide and yttrium oxide.
   USE - Double-layer core-shell catalyst with nickel supported on silicon dioxide used for preparing methane carbon dioxide reforming reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The double-layer core-shell catalyst includes shell layer has a secondary protective effect on nickel particle, and the shell layer can provide abundant oxygen vacancy, promote the oxidation of carbon, so as to reduce the formation of carbon deposit, with dual protection function of anti-sintering and anti-carbon deposit and a synergistic catalytic effect, and has better stability than conventional catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing double-layer core-shell catalyst with nickel supported on silicon dioxide, which involves: (a)dissolving the nickel salt in an alcohol solvent;(b)adding a complexing agent, stirring to obtain a nickel salt dispersion;(c)dissolving the silicon dioxide carrier in the alcohol solvent;(d)stirring uniformly to obtain the silicon dioxide carrier dispersion;(e)adding the obtained silicon dioxide carrier dispersion to the obtained nickel salt dispersion;(f)stirring uniformly;(g)calcining to obtain nickel-loaded silicon dioxide material;(h)putting the obtained nickel-loaded silicon dioxide material in an alcohol solvent;(i)stirring, adding the salt corresponding to the shell oxide;(j)performing hydrothermal reaction to obtain hydrothermal reactant;(k)calcining the obtained hydrothermal reactant to obtain silicon dioxide supported nickel double-layer core-shell catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant is cetyltrimethylammonium bromide. The hydrolysis regulator is urea. The silicon source includes tetraethyl orthosilicate or methyl orthosilicate. The nickel salt is selected from nickel acetate, nickel nitrate, nickel sulfate, and nickel chloride. The salt corresponding to the shell layer is selected from nitrate, sulfate, chloride, and acetate. The complexing agent is selected from o-phenanthroline, ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid disodium, ethylenediamine, triethanolamine. Preferred Process: The preparation of silicon dioxide carrier involves adding the surfactant and the hydrolysis adjusting auxiliary to the water, stirring to obtain the first solution, adding n-pentanol to the obtained first solution, stirring, adding continuously silicon source and cyclohexane, stirring continuously after the reaction, performing centrifugal separation, drying the centrifugal product, and calcining at 300-700degrees Celsius for 3-6 hours to obtain silicon dioxide carrier. Preferred Conditions: The hydrothermal reaction is performed at temperature of 100-180degrees Celsius, and the time is 10-20 hours. The hydrothermal reactant is calcining at temperature of 300-700degrees Celsius, and the time is 3-6 hours. The silicon dioxide carrier dispersion is added to the nickel salt dispersion, stirred uniformly and calcining at 300-700degrees Celsius for 3-6 hours to obtain the nickel-loaded silicon dioxide material.

PN CN113151168-A
TI Human cord blood natural killer (NK) cell culture system comprises human hepatitis B immunoglobulin, human albumin, serum-free medium, activating factor and autologous plasma.
AU WANG J
   HU X
   JI Y
AE SUZHOU KEBEI BIOTECH CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202191644P
AB    NOVELTY - Human cord blood natural killer (NK) cell culture system comprises human hepatitis B immunoglobulin, human albumin, serum-free medium, activating factor and autologous plasma.
   USE - The human cord blood NK cell culture system is useful in preparation of medicine for treating and/or preventing tumor (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The culture system provides expansion efficiency and high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation method of human cord blood NK cells, involves adding mixture of human hepatitis B immunoglobulin and human albumin into the culture flask after 24-48 hours before inoculation, mixing, maintaining at room temperature for 0.5-2 hours, placing in refrigerator at 4 degrees C, discarding the supernatant in the coated culture flask and washing with phosphate buffered saline (PBS), performing umbilical cord blood gradient centrifugation to prepare cord blood mononuclear cells and autologous plasma, adding umbilical cord blood mononuclear cells to the coated culture flask according to the inoculation density 0.5-2x 106, adding serum-free medium, activating factor and autologous plasma to 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, where the day of inoculation is the 0th day of cell culture, changing the medium in the original culture flask on the 4th day of culture, adding autologous plasma and activating factor, transferring the cells to the culture bag on the 6th day of culture, adding serum-free medium, activating factor and autologous plasma, culturing in a 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, and harvesting cells on the 12th day of culture; and
   (2) use of human cord blood NK cells prepared by the culture system, in preparation of medicine for treating and/or preventing tumor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of human hepatitis B immunoglobulin is 10-100 mu g/ml, preferably 50 mu g/ml. The human albumin concentration is 50-500 mu g/ml, preferably 200 mu g/ml. The serum-free medium is AIM-V, SCGM or X-VIVO 15. The activating factors include interleukin 2 (IL)-2, IL-15 and low molecular weight heparin. The content of the autologous plasma is 1-10%. The serum-free medium is SCGM. The final concentration of the IL-2 is 100-1000 IU/ml, the final concentration of the IL-15 is 100-800 IU/ml, and the concentration of the low molecular weight heparin is 200-600 IU/ml. In the inoculation step, the final concentration of the IL-2 is 100-1000 IU/ml, the final concentration of the IL-15 is 100-800 IU/ml, the concentration of the low molecular weight heparin is 200-600 IU/ml, the final concentration of the IL-2 is 100-1000 IU/ml, the final concentration of the IL-15 is 500 IU/ml, and the concentration of the low molecular weight heparin is 200-600 IU/ml. In the bagging step, and the final concentration of IL-2 is 100-500 IU/ml. The content of autologous plasma is 10%. In the liquid exchange step, the content of autologous plasma is 1%. In the bagging step, the content of autologous plasma is 1%. The concentration of human hepatitis B immunoglobulin is 10-100 mu g/ml, preferably 50 mu g/ml. The concentration of human albumin is 50-500 mu g/ml, preferably 200 mu g/ml. The volume ratio of human hepatitis B immunoglobulin and human albumin in the mixed solution is 1:1. The volume of the mixed solution is 5-15 ml, preferably 10 ml. The culture flask is a 75 cm2 culture flask. The time at room temperature is 1 hours. The cord blood mononuclear cells and autologous plasma are prepared by obtaining umbilical cord blood from the umbilical cord of full-term healthy newborns, adding with sodium citrate for anticoagulation, separating within 24 hours after collection, diluting the cord blood according to cord blood and phosphate buffered saline (PBS) in a ratio of 1:1, slowly adding human lymphocyte separation solution, centrifuging at 800 g for 20 minutes, collecting the albuginea layer of mononuclear cells into a new centrifuge tube, adding PBS to wash twice, centrifuging at 600 g for 10 minutes, discarding the supernatant, obtaining cord blood mononuclear cell pellet, obtaining plasma in a new centrifuge tube, inactivating the plasma at 56 degrees C for 30 minutes, centrifuging at 800 g for 10 minutes, and transferring the supernatant to a new centrifuge tube to store at 4 degrees C. The method involves transferring the 12-day culture solution to a centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 500 g for 10 minutes, discarding the supernatant, adding 50 ml PBS to resuspend the cell pellet, centrifuging at 500 g for 10 minutes and discarding the supernatant to obtain NK cells.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
