PN US2021115477-A1
TI Converting carbon dioxide produced during industrial process to methane by preparing culture of hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea, e.g. Methanococcus voltaei, in bioreactor and supplying output gas from industrial process to bioreactor.
AU METS L
AE UNIV CHICAGO (UYCG-C)
GA 202142053G
AB    NOVELTY - Converting carbon dioxide (CO2) produced during an industrial process to methane involves: (a) preparing a culture of hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea in a bioreactor; and (b) supplying an output gas from an industrial process to the bioreactor. The output gas comprises CO2 and 0.02-6.7% oxygen (moles/volume of output gas). The hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea converts the output gas to continuously produce methane.
   USE - The method is useful for converting CO2 produced during an industrial process to methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces the CO2 emissions from industrial processes, including ethanol production, by using a bioreactor system that uses the emissions to produce methane (natural gas). The integrated electrolysis/methane fermentation system can be viewed as converting an intermittent energy source (e.g. inexpensive off-peak electricity from power plants) to a stable chemical energy store, using hydrogen as an intermediate and methane as the final energy carrier.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a cascaded bioreactor comprising a first reactor vessel a second reactor vessel a culture of hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea which is present in the first and second reactor vessels; a source of an output gas from an industrial process comprising CO2 that feeds into the first reactor vessel; a source of hydrogen gas that feeds into the first reactor vessel; a gas feed from the first reactor vessel to the second reactor vessel; a feed to the first reactor vessel for providing fresh medium; a feed to the second reactor vessel for providing fresh medium; a feed to the first reactor vessel for removing the hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea culture; and a feed to the second reactor vessel for removing the hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea culture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: Alternatively, converting CO2 produced during an industrial process to methane involves: (a) contacting a culture comprising hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea with hydrogen (H2) gas and an output gas from an industrial process comprising CO2 gas in a bioreactor; (b) supplying an amount of H2 gas to maintain a redox potential in the bioreactor under -100 mV or less, where no additional constituent other than the H2 gas is added to the bioreactor to maintain the redox potential in the bioreactor under -100 mV or less; and (c) whereithe hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea converts the H2 gas and the CO2 gas to methane.Converting CO2 produced during an industrial process to methane involves: (a) preparing an initial culture of hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea and placing the culture in a bioreactor; (b) supplying an output gas from an industrial process to the bioreactor; where the output gas comprises CO2; (c) supplying fresh medium to the culture; (d) where the hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea converts the output gas to continuously produce methane; and (e) where no sulfur is added in the method, other than the presence of sulfur in the initial culture.Converting CO2 produced during an industrial process to methane using a cascaded bioreactor process, involves: (a) preparing a culture of hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea, where the culture is present in a first and second reactor vessel; (b) supplying fresh medium to the first and second reactor vessels through a first medium feed line attached to the first reactor vessel, and a second medium feed line attached to the second reactor vessel; (c) supplying H2 gas to the first reactor vessel through hydrogen gas feed line; (d) supplying an output gas from an industrial process to the first reactor vessel; where the output gas comprises CO2, through an output gas feed line; (e) where the hydrogcnotrophic methanogenic archaca culture in the first reactor converts H2 gas and CO2 gas to produce methane; (f) transferring at least a portion of gas in the first reactor vessel to the second reactor vessel by a first gas feed line; and (g) where the hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea culture in the second reactor converts H2 gas and CO2 gas to produce methane.H2 is supplied in an amount to maintain a redox potential in the bioreactor under -100 mV or less, and where no additional constituent other than the H2 gas is added to the bioreactor to maintain the redox potential in the bioreactor under -100 mV or less. The industrial process is coal gasification, biomass gasification, or liquid fuel production by biomass fermentation. The method further involves: (a) preparing a culture of hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea, where the culture is present in a third reactor vessel; (b) supplying fresh medium to the third reactor vessels through a third medium feed line attached to the third reactor vessel; (c) transferring at least a portion of gas in the second reactor vessel to the third reactor vessel by a second gas feed line; and (d) where the hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea culture in the third reactor converts H2 gas and CO2 gas to produce methane. H2 is supplied in an amount to maintain a redox potential in the first reactor vessel under -100 mV or less, and where no additional constituent other than the H2 gas is added to the first reactor vessel to maintain the redox potential in the first reactor vessel under -100 mV or less.Preferred Components: The culture is a substantially pure culture of one hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archaea species. The hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archea species is selected from Methanobacterium alcaliphilum, Methanobacterium bryantii, Methanobacterium congolense, Methanobacterium defluvii, Methanobacterium espanolae, Methanobacterium formicicum, Methanobacterium ivanovii, Methanobacterium palustre, Methanobacterium thermaggregans, Methanobacterium uliginosum, Methanobrevibacter acididurans, Methanobrevibacter arboriphilicus, Methanobrevibacter gottschalkii, Methanobrevibacter olleyae, Methanobrevibacter ruminantium, Methanobrevibacter smithii, Methanobrevibacter woesei, Methanobrevibacter wolinii, Methanothermobacter marburgensis, Methanothermobacter thermautotrophicum, Methanothermobacter thermoflexus, Methanothermobacter thermophilus, Methanothermobacter wolfeii, Methanothermus sociabilis, Methanocorpusculum bavaricum, Methanocorpusculum parvum, Methanoculleus chikuoensis, Methanoculleus submarinus, Methanogenium frigidum, Methanogenium liminatans, Methanogenium marinum, Methanosarcina acetivorans, Methanosarcina barkeri, Methanosarcina mazei, Methanosarcina thermophila, Methanomicrobium mobile, Methanocaldococcus jannaschii, Methanococcus aeolicus, Methanococcus maripaludis, Methanococcus vannielii, Methanococcus voltaei, Methanothermococcus thermolithotrophicus, Methanopyrus kandleri, Methanothermobacter the rmoautotroiphicus, Methanocaldococcus fervens, Methanocaldococcus indicus, Methanocaldococcus infernus, and Methanocaldococcus vulcanius. The hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archea is selected from Methanosarcina barkeri and Methanococcus maripaludis. It is Methanothermobacter thermoautotroiphicus. The hydrogenotrophic methanogenic archea is selected from Methanocaldococcus fervens, Methanocaldococcus indicus, Methanocaldococcus infernus, and Methanocaldococcus vulcanius. The industrial output gas further comprises carbon monoxide. No sulfur is in the process other than sulfur in the culture medium.Preferred Condition: The conditions include a temperature of 35-37degrees Celsius or 60-65degrees Celsius or 80-100degrees Celsius.Preferred Composition: The industrial output gas comprises at least 0.8% oxygen (moles/volume of output gas), at least 1.7% oxygen (moles/volume of output gas), at least 3.4% oxygen (moles/volume of output gas), or 3.4-6.7% oxygen (moles/volume of output gas). TI Method of fixing chain link cross units, involves utilizing welding material for welding in second step is welding wire whose strength and composition are matched with chain link, and welding speed is less than or equal to specified value.
AU WU J
   TAO A
   TAO L
   ZHANG W
   SHAO Y
   XU Z
   HUANG Y
   LI J
AE JIANGSU ASIAN STAR ANCHOR CHAIN CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021405045
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preheating the chain link structure and cross unit respectively. The preheating temperature is less than or equal to 300 degrees C. The gas shielded welding is used to weld the side of the cross unit and the structure of the chain link together. The full circle welding or half circle welding or 1/4 circle welding is carried out on the portion where one or both sides of the cross unit is in contact with the chain link structure. The gas used for welding in the second step is a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and argon. The proportion of argon is 70 percentage to 90 percentage. The remainder is carbon dioxide. A direct current (DC) welding machine is used for welding. The welding voltage is 20-30V. The welding current is 200-300A. The welding material used for welding in the second step is a welding wire whose strength and composition are matched with the chain link, and the welding speed is less than or equal to 0.6 m/min.
   USE - Method of fixing chain link cross units.
   ADVANTAGE - The link cross unit has good stability, and is safe and reliable. The link cross unit has high strength, wear resistance, abrasion, corrosion, good deformation performance, and effectively avoids the cross unit loosening or falling. The link cross unit is configured to greatly prolong the service life of the chain ring, and has low using cost.

PN CN112552528-A; CN112552528-B
TI Removing solvent in polymer solution used for            treating e.g. soluble imide polymer, by mixing solution            composed of polymer and good solvent with poor solvent,            filtering, extracting residual good solvent in filter            cake, condensing and drying.
AU ZHANG X
   CHEN H
   LIU B
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 2021341969
AB    NOVELTY - Removing solvent in high temperature resistant                polymer solution involves: (a) mixing a solution                composed of heat-resistant polymer and a good                solvent (I) with a poor solvent (II), precipitating                the polymer, pulverizing the mixture into a slurry,                and filtering to obtain a filtrate and a filter                cake; (b) adding the filter cake and a desorbing                agent to an extraction tank, sealing, introducing                supercritical carbon dioxide, extracting the                residual good solvent (I) in the filter cake, and                transferring the extract to a separation tank; and                (c) depressurizing the extraction tank, leaving                still, condensing at low temperature, separating                into gaseous carbon dioxide to obtain a dry polymer                powder, washing, and drying. The desorbing agent is                an esterified product of sorbitol cracking                product.
   USE - The method is useful for removing solvent in                high temperature resistant polymer solution used                for treating soluble imide polymer including                polyimide, polyetherimide, polyesterimide,                polyamidoimide and/or polyarylene sulfide imide,                sulfone polymer including polysulfone,                polyethersulfone, polyphenylsulfone,                polyarylsulfone and/or polyimidesulfone, ketone                polymer including polyetherketone,                polyetheretherketone, polyetherketoneketone, and/or                polyaryletherketone, or aryl ether polymer                including polyphenylene ether, polyphenylene                sulfide and/or polyaryl ether nitrile (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the difficulty of solvent                recovery and the amount of waste liquid produced in                the polymer solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) the desorbing agent, which is an                esterification product of organic acid and polyol                obtained after sorbitol cracking; and
   (2) preparation of the desorbing agent, which                involves (S1) hydrocracking of sorbitol aqueous                solution under alkaline conditions to obtain a                mixture-containing alkaline aqueous solution, and                (S2) subjecting the alkaline aqueous solution to                dehydrating, acidifying, desalting, and then                esterifying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the                  pulverizing step is carried out by using a                  continuous pulverization equipment including                  high-speed stirring and dispersing disc,                  high-speed shearing pump, colloid mill, coaxial                  anisotropic stirring tank, and/or wet pulverizer.                  In step (a), the filtration method includes                  suction filtration, pressure filtration and/or                  centrifugation. In step (S2), the salt in the                  obtained mixture is acidified to an acid, and the                  esterification reaction is carried out between                  the organic acid in the mixture and polyol after                  acidification. Preferred Conditions: In step (a),                  the temperature of the solution is 0-160 degrees                  C, preferably 10-100 degrees C. The temperature                  of the poor solvent is 0-80 degrees C, preferably                  20-40 degrees C. The pressure of the                  supercritical carbon dioxide in the extraction                  tank is 8-18 MPaG, preferably 9-12 MPaG. The                  outlet pressure of the extraction tank is 0.1-0.5                  MPaG, preferably 0.2-0.4 MPaG. The extraction                  temperature is 120-200 degrees C, preferably                  150-180 degrees C. The extraction time of the                  extraction tank is 4-12 hours, preferably 5-8                  hours. The pressure of carbon dioxide in the                  separation tank and the outlet pressure of the                  separation tank are both consistent with the                  outlet pressure of the extraction tank. The                  temperature of the separation tank is 0-250                  degrees C, preferably 10-30 degrees C. The                  low-temperature condensation temperature is -20                  degrees C to 50 degrees C. The washing                  temperature is 90-120 degrees C, and the number                  of washing times is 4-8. The drying temperature                  is 120-170 degrees C, and the drying pressure is                  -0.095 MPaG to -0.1 MPaG. Preferred Components:                  The solvent (I) is an aprotic polar solvent                  including tetrahydrofuran, N,N-dimethylformamide,                  N,N-dimethylacetamide, dimethyl sulfoxide,                  N-methylpyrrolidone, sulfolane, diphenyl sulfone,                  phenol and/or cresol. The poor solvent (II)                  includes water, toluene, xylene and/or ethanol.                  The concentration of the solution is 5-40 wt.%,                  preferably 10-35 wt.%. The usage amount of                  desorbing agent is 0.1-2.3 wt.% based on the                  total mass of the heat-resistant polymer. The                  mixture in step (S1) contains formate, acetate,                  lactate, 2,3-butanediol, 1,2-butanediol,                  1,2-propanediol, 1,4-butanediol, glycerol and                  remaining sorbitol. Preferred Composition: The                  volume ratio of the solution to the poor solvent                  (II) is 1:3.5-1:10, preferably 1:4-1:7.

PT P
PN WO2021050441-A1; CA3150012-A1; CN114423709-A; IN202217012106-A; EP4028158-A1; US2022289573-A1
TI Production of porous carbon material e.g.            hierarchical porous carbon material, involves providing            phase homogeneous polymeric mixture, reacting to form            polymeric gel by self-assembling, drying, shaping, and            pyrolyzing at preset conditions.
AU SAYLER F
   KOTBAGI T
   BROWN J
   LEIBENGUTH K
AE THRUPORE TECHNOLOGIES INC (THRU-Non-standard)
   THRUPORE TECHNOLOGIES INC (THRU-Non-standard)
   THRUPORE TECHNOLOGIES INC (THRU-Non-standard)
GA 202128114G
AB    NOVELTY - Production of a porous carbon material                involves providing carbon in the form of a phase                homogeneous polymeric mixture, reacting the                polymeric mixture at 40-130 degrees C for 1-60                minutes to form a polymeric gel by self-assembling,                drying the polymeric gel at 40-140 degrees C for 1                minute to 12 hours, shaping the dried polymeric gel                to produce a shaped polymeric gel, and pyrolyzing                at 500-1300 degrees C for 10 minutes to 12                hours.
   USE - Production of porous carbon material e.g.                hierarchical porous carbon material (claimed) for                capturing or storing gases (e.g. methane, carbon                dioxide and hydrogen) such as selective capture of                carbon dioxide (CO2 sequestration) from power plant                flue gas. Can also be used for purifying drinking                water and air, extracting and purifying metals,                treating sewage, treating poison, diarrhea or                overdoses, as catalyst support material, and                filters for gas masks, respirators or compressed                air.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is continuous and automated                requiring very little interruption by operators for                only monitoring by computer or PLC panel, and                provides carbon material with desired shapes,                dimensions, and portions of mesopores and                macropores.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system                for manufacturing the carbon material, which a                reactor configured for reacting a self assembling                thermoset polymeric mixture to produce a polymeric                gel, containing carbon, a drying device configured                for drying the polymeric gel, an extruder                configured for extruding the dried polymeric gel,                and a pyrolysis device configured for pyrolyzing                the extruded polymeric gel. The reactor is selected                from a pipe, plug-flow reactor and tube-in-tube                heat exchanger.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the                manufacturing method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The extruder is                  selected from a screw extruder, a food extruder,                  a sieve extruder, a basket extruder, a roll                  extruder, a ram extruder, a pressure extruder, a                  hydraulic extruder, and a devolatilizing                  extruder. The system further comprises a mixing                  tank configured for producing and transporting                  the polymeric mixture to the reactor, a delivery                  device for delivering an initiator compound to                  the polymeric mixture. The drying device and the                  extruder are combined in a single extruding                  device, preferably a devolatilization                  extruder.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous carbon                  material comprises macropores defined by a wall                  comprising mesopores defined by a wall. The walls                  of the macropores and mesopores comprise a                  continuous carbon phase. Preferred Properties:                  The macropores have a diameter of 0.05-100 mu m.                  The mesopores have a diameter of 2-50 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine is a primary                  amine, preferably 1,6-diaminohexane. The aldehyde                  is formaldehyde, trioxane, butyraldehyde, or                  benzaldehyde. The phenolic compound is                  benzenediol or phenol, preferably                  1,3-benzenediol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves mixing an organic polymer composition to                  produce the phase homogeneous polymeric mixture                  by automatic continuous process. The above                  process except providing step is an automatic                  continuous process. The reacting step further                  involves adding an initiator compound containing                  aldehyde to the reactor concurrently with the                  addition of the polymeric mixture. The shaping                  step is carried out by injection molding, pour                  molding, casting, extrusion, or                  extrusion-spheronization. The drying and shaping                  are performed in an extruder. The pyrolysis step                  is performed under an inert atmosphere. Preferred                  Components: The self-assembling thermoset polymer                  composition comprises an amine, an aldehyde and a                  phenolic compound, and further contains a                  surfactant, pore-forming solid and/or a solvent.                  Preferred Conditions: The reacting step is                  performed preferably at 75-85 degrees C for 1-10                  minutes. The drying step is performed preferably                  at 100-130 degrees C for 1-10 minutes. The                  pyrolyzing step is performed preferably at                  600-1000 degrees C for 1-10 minutes. The inert                  atmosphere comprises nitrogen and is devoid of                  oxygen.

TI Dry-type transformer fireproof mechanism, has sliding rod whose upper end is connected with lower end of heat conducting plate, and shell whose inner top unit is fixed with side wall of heat conducting plate through heat conducting strip.
AU SUN F
AE NINGBO QIANYI ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202129347D
AB    NOVELTY - The mechanism has a shell (1) fixed in multiple ventilation holes (2). An upper end of a sliding rod is fixedly connected with a lower end of a heat conducting plate (4). An inner top unit of the shell is fixedly connected with an air outlet pipe (8). An inner wall of a reaction cavity (6) is fixedly connected with an inner wall of the air outlet pipe through an exhaust pipe (9). An inner top unit of the shell is fixedly connected with a side wall of the heat conducting plate through a heat conducting strip (10). Multiple ventilation holes (2) are fixedly connected with an elastic cylinder (11).
   USE - Dry-type transformer fireproof mechanism.
   ADVANTAGE - The mechanism reaches deformation temperature by the temperature of a memory spring, so that memory spring shrinks to drive the heat conducting plate to slide, thus sliding a sliding rod on a top unit of a reaction cavity. The sliding rod is formed with a cone-shaped structure, so that hydrochloric acid solution in the sliding plug cavity enters into the reaction cavity when the sliding rod slides. The mechanism reacts with calcium carbonate particles to generate carbon dioxide so as to input large amount of carbon dioxide into the shell through the exhaust pipe, thus extinguishing fire in the shell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of a dry-type transformer fireproof mechanism.
   Shell (1)
   Ventilation holes (2)
   Heat conducting plate (4)
   Reaction cavity (6)
   Air outlet pipe (8)
   Exhaust pipe (9)
   Heat conducting strip (10)
   Elastic cylinder (11)

PT P
PN CN112459847-A
TI Method for controlling fluid working medium in non-blade turbine, involves determining attribute of fluid working medium, and determining whether turbine includes medium-low power requirement when medium is detected to be gas and liquid.
AU LUO Y
AE SHENZHEN PUBAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202126197H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting gas before fluid working medium enters a turbine, and determining an attribute of the fluid working medium, where the attribute comprises nano fluid, ultra-critical carbon dioxide, general gas and liquid. The fluid working medium is controlled to realize pressurization through a nozzle of a pressurization air inlet pair and ensure that the fluid is uniformly and stably sprayed out when the fluid working medium is detected to be the nano fluid. Volume fraction of particles is simultaneously measured in the nano fluid. Pressurization is not carried out on the nano fluid, and current pressure is maintained when the volume fraction reaches an arranged value and/or gain of the nano fluid on output power of the non-blade turbine exceeds a power value. The ultra-critical carbon dioxide is introduced into ultra-critical fluid for treatment to act on the turbine through a turbine air inlet.
   USE - Method for controlling fluid working medium in a non-blade turbine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables ensuring uniform and stable ejection of the fluid, improving operation efficiency of a machine body, analyzing fluid stability between disks of the turbine, judging whether current efficiency is in accordance with current efficiency, and reducing frequency of turbulence.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Determination is made whether the turbine includes medium-low power requirement when the fluid working medium is detected to be general gas and liquid. The fluid working medium is conducted through an air inlet and the nozzle without considering power loss of the nozzle and the air inlet to the turbine. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a non-blade turbine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for controlling fluid working medium in a non-blade turbine. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
DC Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M))

TI Forming building component involves mixing organic            solvent with coal under solvent extraction conditions,            heating the formed coal extraction residue under            pyrolysis conditions to form pyrolysis char, mixing the            char with water and with e.g. clay to create mixture            and molding and curing.
AU TAN G

TI Producing hydrogen by reforming natural gas with magnetic rotating non-equilibrium sliding arc comprises e.g. flowing natural gas out of natural gas cylinder, introducing into desulfurization device, and mixing with inert gas.
AU LIU Q
AE BEIJING JIARUITECH CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202116421E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen by reforming natural gas with magnetic rotating non-equilibrium sliding arc comprises (i) flowing natural gas out of the natural gas cylinder, introducing into desulfurization device, mixing with inert gas, and entering second gas collecting tank, (ii) deoxidizing, and entering the ultrasonic generator to prepare steam, (iii) introducing the steam into second gas collecting tank, and mixing with the processed natural gas to obtain methane steam, (iv) generating slip arc between the cylinder body and slip arc discharge cathode electrode, slip arc breakdown discharge generates discharge current, where the discharge current magnetically rotates and slides upward to form non-thermally balanced plasma that collides with the methane water vapor to generate hydrogen and carbon dioxide, where the reaction formula is represented as given in specification, and (v), collecting hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and separating to obtain hydrogen gas.
   USE - The method is useful for producing hydrogen by reforming natural gas with magnetic rotating non-equilibrium sliding arc.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves cost and safety problems of the hydrogen production, uses magnetic rotating unbalanced slip arc to reform natural gas to produce hydrogen, uses activation of free electrons in the plasma and highly active free radicals to promote the progress of the reaction, avoids technical and cost problems of catalyst deactivation and replacement, uses ultrasonic atomization to produce water vapor, and reduces energy consumption of water vapor production. The micro flow channel improves reaction efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the method for producing hydrogen by reforming natural gas with magnetic rotating non-equilibrium sliding arc (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the inert gas is helium gas or neon gas.

PN CN112175891-A
TI New genetically engineered bacteria i.e.            Cyanobacteria overexpressing e.g. malto-oligosaccharyl            trehalose synthase (MTSase) gene and            malto-oligosaccharyl trehalose hydrolase (MTHase) gene,            useful in production of trehalose.
AU LV X
   QIAO Y
   WANG W
AE QINGDAO INST BIOENERGY & BIOPROCESS TECH (CAQD-C)
GA 2021062291
AB    NOVELTY - A genetically engineered bacteria producing                trehalose i.e. Cyanobacteria overexpressing a                malto-oligosaccharyl trehalose synthase (MTSase)                gene or a gene homologous to the MTSase gene and a                malto-oligosaccharyl trehalose hydrolase (MTHase)                gene or a gene homologous to the MTHase gene, is                new. The nucleotide sequence of the MTSase gene                comprises a 2766 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 1)                fully defined in the specification. The homologous                gene of the MTSase gene is a gene encoding an amino                acid sequence exhibiting more than 84% sequence                homology with the amino acid sequence encoded by                the MTSase gene. The nucleotide sequence of the                MTHase gene comprises a 1863 base pair sequence                (SEQ ID NO: 2) fully defined in the specification.                The homologous gene of the MTHase gene is a gene                encoding an amino acid sequence exhibiting more                than 82% sequence homology with the amino acid                sequence encoded by the MTHase gene.
   USE - The genetically engineered bacteria are useful                in production of trehalose (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The genetically engineered bacterium solves                the problem that the current production of                trehalose needs to artificially provide organic                carbon sources as raw materials and increase the                production cost and realizes the direct fixation of                carbon dioxide by solar energy to synthesize                trehalose in the photosynthetic autotrophic                microorganism Cyanobacteria, where the source of                energy for trehalose synthesis is solar energy, and                the source of carbon source is carbon dioxide. The                synthesis of trehalose is not restricted by                insufficient raw materials and energy and does not                increase carbon emissions. The trehalose is a true                zero-emission trehalose product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) method for constructing the genetically                engineered bacteria; and
   (2) use of the genetically engineered bacteria                in production of trehalose.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation (Claimed): The genetically                  engineered bacteria are constructed by (a)                  constructing a recombinant plasmid carrying                  MTSase and MTHase genes and (b) introducing the                  obtained recombinant plasmid into host bacteria                  to obtain genetically engineered bacteria, where                  the host bacteria are Cyanobacteria. Preferred                  Biomolecules: The genetically engineered bacteria                  also overexpress the trehalose transporter                  (TRET)1 gene or a homologous gene to the TRET1                  gene. The nucleotide sequence of TRET1 comprises                  a 1515 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 3) fully                  defined in the specification. The homologous gene                  of the TRT1 gene is a gene encoding an amino acid                  sequence exhibiting more than 58% sequence                  homology with the amino acid sequence encoded by                  the TRET1 gene. The Cyanobacteria are                  Synechococcus, preferably Synechococcus strain                  PCC7942 or Synechococcus strain Sye7942-C252Y.                  Preferred Method: The method further involves                  constructing a recombinant plasmid carrying the                  TRET1 gene, and introducing all the recombinant                  plasmid carrying the TRET1 gene and the                  recombinant plasmid carrying the MTSase and                  MTHase genes into the host bacteria to obtain                  genetically engineered bacteria. In the step (a),                  the MTSase and MTHase genes are connected through                  a P3 connecting peptide gene, and the P3                  connecting peptide gene comprises a base pair                  sequence of SEQ ID NO: 16. The trehalose is                  produced by culturing the genetically engineered                  bacteria in a column photoreactor containing BG11                  medium without antibiotics at 28-30 degrees C,                  preferably 30 degrees C under light intensity of                  100-200 mu mol.photons.m-2s-1, preferably 150 mu                  mol.photons.m-2s-1, where the inoculation                  concentration OD730 is 0.1 using a gas which is a                  mixture of 3% carbon dioxide and 97% air,                  culturing the engineered bacteria to the 4th day,                  adding isopropyl beta -d-1-thiogalactopyranoside                  (IPTG) to induce the expression of the                  transporter TRT1, and after 24 hours of induction                  with IPTG, separating and purifying trehalose.                  gaagctgcggcaaaagaagctgcggcaaaa (SEQ ID NO:                  16).

PN CN112186136-A; CN112186136-B
TI Pre-treatment method for curing and supporting formation of plates for lead-acid battery, involves placing product in saturated lead acetate aqueous solution, controlled by programmable logic controller (PLC), and pass carbon dioxide gas.
AU ZHAO X
   XIAO L
   LIU W
   LI Q
   CHENG K
AE TIANENG GROUP HENAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (TINN-C)
GA 202105262L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pre-curing for 3-8 hours at a temperature of 30-40 degrees C and an ambient humidity of 95-99% to facilitate the completion of a good lead paste in the subsequent curing process. An ambient temperature of 55-60 degrees C is maintained, and the ambient humidity is set to 70-80%, and the product is processed in a plasma generator. The product is placed in saturated lead acetate aqueous solution, controlled by PLC automatic control module, pass carbon dioxide gas into the curing chamber, and react with the lead oxide in the electrode plate under the action of water to form basic lead carbonate. The ambient temperature of 55-60 degrees C is kept, the moisture exhaust door is fully opened, and dried for 4-6 hours. The temperature is adjusted to 75-80 degrees , the moisture exhaust door is opened, and heating is stopped after drying for 8-12 hours. The curing is completed after the temperature of the electrode plate drops to room temperature.
   USE - Pre-treatment method for curing and supporting formation of plates for lead-acid battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The technique solves the problem of free lead oxidation in the polar plate, and improves the strength of the polar plate after curing. The polar plate lead paste cured by the process has better bonding strength. The technique reduces the lead paste softening in the battery use. The technique avoids the problem that the internal temperature of the battery is raised due to the sulfate and exothermic reaction of the lead oxide after adding acid to the battery to save the energy consumption of cooling the battery after cooling acid and adding acid.

GA 202101376S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves crushing the RDF that                enters the kiln (8), and the size of the RDF after                crushing reaches 15-30mm. The oxygen content in the                calciner is 1%~3%, and coke, cracked gas and CO are                generated during the cracking and incomplete                combustion in the calcining furnace. The coke,                cracked gas and carbon monoxide produced are                transported to the cement rotary kiln, where the                denitrification reaction occurs. The gas generated                is entered into the gas separation equipment (11)                after being detected by the gas detector (10). The                harmless gas is discharged through the gas                discharge port (12). The unreacted nitrogen oxide                and portion of carbon dioxide are returned to the                decomposition furnace (7) through the return pipe                (13) and fan (14) and reacted with the carbon                element in RDF to generate carbon monoxide reducing                gas for reuse.

The                denitration effect is higher than 70 %.

AU KIM D G
AE UNIV DANKOOK IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYDK-C)
GA 202062065A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting (S100) the number of passengers of each train in real time. The congestion level for each passenger car is estimated (S200) based on information and statistical data on the number of passengers. The basic congestion is estimated (S210) based on the accumulated statistical data. The real-time congestion level is grasped (S220) based on the number of real-time users. The real-time congestion level is reflected to the basic congestion level to determine the number of passengers in the passenger car. The number of passengers is identified by using the carbon dioxide sensor mounted in each passenger car, and in the dispersal of the crowded person. The information of each passenger car is transmitted (S300) to passengers through a lamp and an application.
   USE - Method for dispersing number of passengers in vehicle e.g. electric vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The congestion level measured in real time is reflected in the basic congestion data estimated through the number of passengers using the trains of each train by time and place, so it is possible to more accurately and quickly grasp the congestion level of each train.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for dispersing passengers. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for detecting the number of passengers of each train in real time (S100)
   Step for estimating congestion level for each passenger car (S200)
   Step for estimating basic congestion based on the accumulated statistical data (S210)
   Step for grasping real-time congestion level based on the number of real-time users (S220)
   Step for transmitting information of each passenger car to passengers through a lamp and an application (S300)

PT P
PN WO2020113267-A1
TI Porous liquid used for removing contaminant e.g. carbon dioxide from fluid i.e. liquid or gas, contains oligomers containing aromatic residues connected via bond or linking group i.e. methylene group.
AU SMITH S
   WOOD C D
   MULET X
AE COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
GA 202050758A
AB    NOVELTY - Porous liquid comprises at least one oligomer, where each oligomer contains 4-35 aromatic residues. The aromatic residues in each oligomer are connected via a bond or a linking group. The linking group is a methylene group. Each aromatic residue in the oligomer is substituted phenyl residue (I).
   USE - The porous liquid is useful for removing a contaminant from a fluid, where the fluid is a liquid or a gas, and the contaminant comprises carbon dioxide (preferred), sulfur dioxide and trioxide, nitrogen oxides, or hydrogen sulfide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The porous liquid can be recycled.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Porous liquid comprises at least one oligomer, where each oligomer contains 4-35 aromatic residues. The aromatic residues in each oligomer are connected via a bond or a linking group. The linking group is a methylene group. Each aromatic residue in the oligomer is substituted phenyl residue of formula (I).
   R1 = alkyl, alkenyl or alkynyl;
   B1 = bond to another aromatic moiety within the compound (I) or bond to linking group;
   n, m = 0-5; and
   o = 1-5, where n+m+o is 6.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing (m1) the porous liquid, comprising reacting an aromatic phenyl compound of formula (III) with an oxygen containing linking agent in presence of a Lewis acid, where the mole ratio of oxygen containing linking agent to aromatic compound (III) is less than 10; and
   (2) removing (m2) a contaminant from a fluid, comprising contacting a fluid containing the contaminant with the porous liquid under conditions to allow the liquid to absorb the contaminant, and separating the fluid from the porous liquid to produce a liquid with enriched contaminant levels and a fluid with depleted contaminant levels.
   p = 0-5; and
   q = 1-6, where p+q is 6.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The Lewis acid comprises aluminum chloride, iron(III) chloride (both preferred), aluminum bromide, gallium trichloride, antimony pentachloride, zirconium tetrachloride, tin chloride, boron chloride, boron trifluoride or antimony trichloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxygen containing linking agent comprises dimethoxymethane (preferred), chloromethoxy methane, trimethoxymethane, or triisopropyloxy methane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Each oligomer contains 4-15 aromatic residues, preferably 4-8 aromatic residues. In the oligomer(s) in the liquid, each aromatic residue has on average 1.2-2 connections to other aromatic residues. Preferred Method: In method (m2), the porous liquid with enriched contaminant levels is subjected to a process to remove contaminant from the liquid followed by recycling of the liquid back into the process.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1 = -CH3 (preferred), -C(CH3)3, -CH2CH3, -(CH2)2CH3, -(CH2)3CH3, -(CH2)3CH3, -(CH2)5CH3, -(CH2)6CH)3, -(CH2)7CH3, -(CH2)8CH3, -(CH2)9CH3, -(CH2)10CH3 or -(CH2)11CH3;
   m = 0-4, where m+o is 5;
   p = 1; and
   q = 0.
   EXAMPLE - 30 mmol Aromatic compound, 2.4 g iron chloride and 5.3 ml dimethoxymethane were dissolved in a Schott Bottle of solvent (90 ml dichloromethane) and left to react overnight. After forming a burgundy/brown solution, unreacted Lewis acid was removed by successive additions of methanol and water, until the decanted aqueous layer became colorless. Solvent and any unreacted starting materials were then removed by gradual heating under vacuum to produce liquid composition.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. AB    NOVELTY - Preparing syngas or fuel gas from waste circuit board resin comprises preparing resin powder including conveying the waste circuit board from which the electronic components have been removed to a crusher through a belt, pulverizing resin with a diameter of less than 20mm, sending the granular resin to the ball mill, grinding to about 200 mesh resin powder, transferring to the resin powder silo with a bucket lift, transporting the 200 mesh granular bituminous coal powder to the pulverized coal bin with a bucket lift. The gasification process comprises conveying the slurry in a high-level slurry tank to the process burner by high-pressure slurry pump, atomizing the slurry, reacting to produce a of hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide gas, discharging the mixed gas through the upper part of the quenching buffer tank, entering the washing tower through humidification, removing fine particles and performing multi-stage desulfurization treatment to obtain syngas or industrial gas.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing syngas or fuel gas from waste circuit board resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures that the after dedusting and purification treatment, the gas can be used as chemical synthesis gas for the production of synthetic ammonia, urea, acetic acid, methanol, polyol, olefin, liquefied natural gas; used in the process of carbon supplement, hydrogen production, carbon monoxide extraction, and industrial or civil gas; turns waste into treasure; is economical and practical; has good economic benefits and environmental protection value, and has broad application prospects.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method of preparing syngas or fuel gas from waste circuit board resin (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The additive comprises limestone which is calcium carbonate which is used to reduce the melting point of the gasification reaction ash. The dispersant is sodium lignin naphthalene sulfonate which is used to improve the fluidity, stability and slurry concentration of the slurry.

PN WO2020084545-A1; KR2021082468-A; CN113227674-A; EP3870909-A1; US2021396431-A1; JP2022509452-W
TI Refrigeration apparatus has secondary economizer branch that comprises control device adapted to divert portion of fraction of coolant coming from economizer branch, and outlet section that is arranged downstream of reciprocating compressor.
AU ASCANI M
AE TURBOALGOR SRL (TURB-Non-standard)
   TURBOALGOR SRL (TURB-Non-standard)
   TURBOALGOR SRL (TURB-Non-standard)
GA 2020363084
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) has a main branch (M) which comprises a reciprocating compressor (6) which is arranged between evaporator and main compressor (2) and provided with cylinder, rod and piston. The piston is integrally constrained to one rod and translatable inside cylinder. The secondary economizer branch (100) comprises a control device (50) for controlling the actuation of the rod. The control device is adapted to divert a portion (X2) of fraction (X1) of coolant coming from the economizer branch to drive the displacement of the piston and compress the coolant coming from evaporator (4) and contained in cylinder and to reintroduce a portion of fraction of coolant into economizer branch during the displacement of the piston while suctioning the coolant from the evaporator for the outflow of a portion of fraction of coolant through outlet section of the economizer branch. The outlet section of the economizer branch is arranged downstream of the reciprocating compressor.
   USE - Refrigeration apparatus using carbon dioxide as coolant.
   ADVANTAGE - By using carbon dioxide as coolant, the efficiency is increased and the complexity of structure is avoided. Since the reciprocating compressor is of double-acting type, the additional piston is in the suction step and in the compression step which allows to considerably increases the flow rate of coolant which can be circulated inside the closed circuit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of operating a refrigeration apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic sectional longitudinal view of the refrigeration apparatus.
   Refrigeration apparatus (1)
   Main compressor (2)
   Evaporator (4)
   Reciprocating compressor (6)
   Control device (50)
   Secondary economizer branch (100)
   Main branch (M)
   Fraction of coolant (X1)
   Portion of fraction of coolant (X2)

PT P
PN KR2078467-B1
TI Recycling refractory materials comprises e.g. collecting waste fireproof material and removing slag and impurities to select raw material, immersing separated raw material in treated water, and discharging carbon dioxide and binder.
AU KIM D C
AE KIM D C (KIMD-Individual)
GA 2020151004
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling refractory materials comprises (a) collecting waste fireproof material and removing slag and impurities to select the raw material, (b) immersing separated raw material in the treated water, and discharging carbon dioxide and binder, (c) drying the water-treated raw material to remove water and residual gas, and then crushing the same to a uniform size, (d) separating the crushed raw material to obtain raw material in range of 1-5 mm, excluding alumina and silicon dioxide contained in fine powders of less than or equal to 0.5 mm, (e) adding obtained raw material to the carbonization tank in which ignition material is ignited step by step over first to third order and removing graphite and carbon, (f) cooling the raw material passed through the carbonization process to separate and discharge from the carbonization tank, and (g) separating the cooled raw materials into particle sizes to selectively obtain regenerated refractory raw materials in the range of 0.1-1 mm, 1-3 mm, and 3-5 mm.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling refractory materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces high quality recycled refractory raw materials using carbonaceous waste refractory materials which are widely used as fire retardants for steel furnaces, is economical, effectively recycles and manufactures regenerated refractory material by carbonizing and removing carbon and foreign substances generated in refractory material by reaction with the melt, and minimizes energy consumption through a series of configurations i.e. sustaining thermal power stably and facilitating cooling and discharging of carbonized raw materials in process of carrying out long carbonization process using large amount of waste refractory raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for recycling system of refractory material.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids.    (2) a method for preparing a mesenchymal stem                cell preparation, which comprises inoculating the                mesenchymal stem cells passaged to the P4                generation into alpha-MEM medium containing 10 wt.%                fetal bovine serum and 10-50 mM mannose,                andculturing in a carbon dioxide incubator at 37                degrees C in a concentration of 5 wt.% for 48h; and                digesting and counting the mannose-treated                mesenchymal stem cells, and then uniformly                suspending in physiological saline or PBS buffer to                a density of 1x 106 cells/mL to 5x 106 cells/mL to                obtain the mesenchymal stem cell preparation.

The mesenchymal stem cell source comprises a                  human or animal umbilical cord, cord blood, bone                  marrow, teeth or adipose tissue.

AE NOL-TEC EURO SRL (NOLT-Non-standard)

GA 201950414W
AB    NOVELTY - A device for degassing granular or powder material comprises containment chamber (2) for granular or powder material and gas produced by the material; and unit (5) for supplying injection gas and consists of greater than or equal to 1 blower element (6) for feeding the injection gas into the chamber to mix and/or homogenize the material and related gas produced by the material itself with injection gas.
   USE - A device for degassing granular or powder material.
   ADVANTAGE - The device provides a structurally simple degassing device having low costs and is small to avoid the use of great number of silos, and at the same time capable of removing the gas produced by great amount of material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) degassing granular or powder material which involves providing in a containment chamber (2) the granular or powder material; and releasing in the containment chamber greater than or equal to 1 injection gas where releasing the injection gas is carried out by mixing and/or homogenizing the material with the injection gas;
   (2) use of blower element for degassing granular or powder material in which blower element consists of pulse valves (7) for supplying gas injection to pressure and flow rate values determined as function of material and according to timed opening and closing sequence of the valves; and
   (3) use of blower element (6) for removing the carbon dioxide generated from food products in granular or powder form, in which the product and carbon dioxide are mixed and/or homogenized with an inert gas supplied by blower element.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective and schematic view of the device for degassing granular or powder material.
   Containment chamber (2)
   Silo (3)
   Unit (5)
   Blower element (6)
   Pulse valves (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Devices: The blower element is of pneumatic type and comprises greater than or equal to 1 pulse valve for supplying the injection gas at predetermined pressure and flow rate values and at predetermined intervals. The pneumatic element for feeding the injection gas comprises pulse valves. The device comprises a silo having containment chamber and flaring bottom portion where the pneumatic element for feeding the gas is arranged in correspondence of bottom portion. The pulse valves are arranged along a circumferential path on flaring bottom portion. Preferred Components: The injection gas is an inert gas. Preferred Process: The step of releasing gas injection is carried out by supplying injection gas through greater than or equal to 1 pulse valve at predetermined pressure and flow rate values as a function of the material. The step of releasing the gas injection is carried out by supplying injection gas through pulse valves according to a timed opening and closing sequence of valves determined as a function of the material. The step of releasing the gas injection is carried out by directing the injection gas from the bottom of containment chamber and toward the material.

PN WO2019099181-A1
TI Device for killing insects e.g. mosquitoes, has screen that covers exterior of mesh, and insects are attracted to light source that pass through screen and are killed by insecticide treated mesh.
AU KOEHLER P G
   PEREIRA R M
   NHAT M U
AE UNIV FLORIDA RES FOUND (UYFL-C)
   DONE THANH INCENSE LLC (DONE-Non-standard)
GA 2019458858
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a housing having a light source that is powered by a power source for attracting insects into the housing that has a opening. A food source attractant inside the housing attracts the insects into the housing. The food source attractant that is formed from a degradable plastic comprises of carbon dioxide, octenol or lactic acid. A mesh is treated with insecticide for covering the opening into the housing. The insecticide includes permethrin and the mesh includes a fabric. A screen (8) covers an exterior of the mesh and comprises of metal, plastic or nylon. The insects is attracted to the light source pass through the screen and are killed by the insecticide treated mesh (3). The light source includes a UV LED (2) that is 365 nm. The power source includes a solar panel (5) electrically coupled to a rechargeable battery (6). The solar panel charges the rechargeable battery. The carbon dioxide is formed from titanium oxide and a carbon coating.
   USE - Device for killing, controlling and monitoring insects e.g. mosquitoes that transmit arboviruses e.g. Aedes mosquitoes for use in military, commercial and residential applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can be portable so as to be easily transportable for different applications and locations. The opening allows mosquitoes access to the insecticide treated mesh and prevents people from contacting the insecticide on the insecticide treated mesh. The device for monitoring and control of mosquitoes has low-maintenance and low costs and does not require electrical power supplies or heavy batteries.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the front view of an insect killing device.
   UV LED (2)
   Insecticide treated mesh (3)
   Solar panel (5)
   Rechargeable battery (6)
   Screen (8)

PT P
PN WO2019092017-A1
TI Extracting fatty acids e.g. oleic acid from triglyceride oil, by contacting triglyceride oil having fatty acids with aqueous solution, separating oil phase from aqueous phase, and adding carbon dioxide and organic solvent to aqueous phase.
AU HEYMANN J
   WILLY B
   BAHLMANN M
   BRAMSIEPE C
AE EVONIK DEGUSSA GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 201943569T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting fatty acids from triglyceride oil, involves (a) contacting triglyceride oil (T1) comprising fatty acids with an aqueous solution (W1) comprising at least one salt chosen from alkaline earth metal hydrogen carbonate, alkali metal bicarbonate and at least one acid of a carboxylic acid, where the carboxylic acid is saturated or unsaturated having 1-8C and 1-3 carboxy functions besides one or more OH functions to obtain triglyceride oil phase (T2) and a aqueous phase (W2), where oil phase (T2) has a reduced fatty acid content compared to triglyceride oil (T1) and aqueous phase (W2) has a higher fatty acid content than aqueous solution (W1), (b) separating triglyceride oil phase (T2) from aqueous phase (W2), and (c) adding carbon dioxide and optionally an organic solvent to the aqueous phase (W2) to obtain an aqueous phase (W3), which has a lower content of fatty acids than aqueous phase (W2), and a fatty acid-containing organic phase (FO).
   USE - The method is useful for extracting fatty acids from triglyceride oil. The fatty acids are unsaturated fatty acids such as myristoleic acid, palmitoleic acid, sapienic acid, oleic acid, elaidic acid, vaccenic acid, linoleic acid, linolelaidic acid, alpha -linolenic acid, arachidonic acid, eicosapentaenoic acid, erucic acid and docosahexaenoic acid, or saturated fatty acids such as caprylic acid, capric acid, undecanoic acid, lauric acid, tridecane acid, myristic acid, palmitic acid, margaric acid, stearic acid, nonadecane, arachidic acid, heneicosanoic acid, behenic acid, lignoceric acid and cerotic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides efficient separation of fatty acids, rapidly separates the phases, achieves complete phase separation, avoids the clouding of oil phase and emulsion formation in the aqueous phase after extraction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The salts in aqueous solution (W1) are chosen from potassium bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate or calcium bicarbonate. The salts of carboxylic acid in aqueous solution (W1) are chosen from salts, preferably potassium salts of acetic acid, lactic acid and citric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves (d) separating aqueous phase (W3) from organic phase (FO), and (e) contacting triglyceride oil (T3) comprising fatty acids with aqueous phase (W3) to obtain a triglyceride oil phase (T4) and a aqueous phase (W4), where triglyceride oil phase (T4) has a reduced fatty acid content than triglyceride oil (T3) and aqueous phase (W4) has a higher fatty acid content than aqueous phase (W2). The step (a) is carried out at 100 degrees C. In step (b), the triglyceride oil phase (T2) is separated from the aqueous phase (W2) using a centrifuge. In step (c), an organic solvent is added to the aqueous phase (W2). Preferred Composition: In step (a), the volume ratio of triglyceride oil (T1) to aqueous solution (W1) is 2:1-1:200. In aqueous solution (W1), the molar ratio of the salts of saturated or unsaturated carboxylic acid is 9:1-1:9. The concentration of salts in aqueous phase (W1) is 0.4 mol/l. The volume ratio of the aqueous phase (W2) to the organic solvent is 1:100-100:1.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D10-A01; D10-A02
IP C11B-001/00; C11B-003/06; C11B-003/14

PN WO2019074922-A1
TI Breath analysis device for analyzing breath of subject to identify levels of gases such as oxygen and carbon dioxide, in which moisture is exchanged between sample of exhaled breath present in gas flow passages.
AU ATSALAKIS A
   PAPADIAMANTIS P
   DALAKAS K
AE ENDO MEDICAL INC (ENDO-Non-standard)
GA 201936379P
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an exhaled breath flow pathway for passage of exhaled breath from an inlet (12) to an outlet (13). Multiple sensors (4-6) for analyzing a property of exhaled breath. A moisture reduction module has a first gas flow passage and a second gas flow passage, passages being separated by a selectively permeable membrane which permits transfer of moisture. The gas flow passages forms portion of exhaled breath flow pathway, and the other of gas flow passages is a pathway for ambient air and is in communication with the ambient environment. The moisture can be exchanged between a sample of exhaled breath present in one of gas flow passages and ambient air present in the other of gas flow passages through the selectively permeable membrane, thus reducing the moisture content of the sample of exhaled breath.
   USE - Breath analysis device for analyzing breath of subject to identify levels of gases such as oxygen and carbon dioxide, for use in indirect calorimetry.
   ADVANTAGE - The arrangement of an outer tube about a selectively permeable inner tube is effective since the inner selectively permeable tube is kept enclosed in the device and not exposed to the environment, whilst still able to function effectively by allowing moisture exchange with ambient air. The devices allow the sensor response time is reduced, thus improving the accuracy and efficiency of the device. The arrangement allows the device to have fewer openings on outer surface for access to ambient air, which increases the robustness, durability and esthetic quality of the device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of measuring breath property of subject.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a device.
   Sensors (4-6)
   Merge point (10)
   Downstream portion (11)
   Inlet (12)
   Outlet (13)

GA 201934233A

A joint detection part is provided with a voltage sensor, a current sensor, a temperature sensor, a humidity sensor, a smoke sensor and a carbon dioxide sensor. An alert portion monitors a measured value of the joint detection part, produces status information and produces an alarm signal. A display section outputs status information. A cut-out part cuts off power, which is supplied from a lead-in wire according to alarm signal generation of the alert portion. A relay unit (150) collects the status information, which is transmitted through a network cable. A solenoid improves action force of an elastomer part according to electric power supply. An emergency power supply unit supplies separate emergency power source to the joint detection part, the alert portion, the display section, the relay unit and a locking apparatus.
   USE - Fire detection and alarm system for a distribution panel utilized in an electric power facility of a building i.e. house, and an electric power substation.
   ADVANTAGE - The system imports current and voltage by fixing a hybrid sensing module so as to improve visibility for sensing humidity, smoke, and carbon dioxide through the digital compact display, minimize need of wiring and parts, improve stability, reduce maintenance cost and manufacturing time, prevent fire and electric shock accident of the distribution panel and effectively prevent expansion in event occurrence.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a fire detecting and alarming system.

PT P
PN CN109546162-A; CN109546162-B
TI Method for preparing microporous iron-nitrogen doped carbon catalyst material, involves dissolving soluble ferric source compound in water, and pouring nitrogen-containing polymer monomer, followed by mixing filtrate with hydrogen peroxide.
AU YANG S
   XUE X
   LI H
   YUAN S
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2019316810
AB    NOVELTY - A microporous iron-nitrogen doped carbon catalyst material preparing method involves dissolving a soluble ferric source compound in water to form a solution, rapidly pouring a certain amount of the nitrogen-containing polymer monomer under stirring, stirring for a period of time, performing solid-liquid separation, and collecting the filtrate and wet solid without the need for washing and drying processes. The wet solid is mixed with a certain amount of molten salt, and dried to obtain a molten salt outsourcing precursor. The molten salt outsourcing precursor is placed into a tube furnace, and subjected to high temperature carbonization treatment under an inert gas atmosphere to obtain a carbonized product. The carbonized product is pulverized, pickled, washed with water, and dried to obtain a microporous iron-nitrogen doped carbon material. The filtrate is stirred and mixed with hydrogen peroxide to prepare a ferric iron source solution to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing a microporous iron-nitrogen doped carbon catalyst material for an electrocatalyst in the field of metal-air, fuel cell, water decomposition or carbon dioxide reduction, and electrode material in a capacitor, a lithium ion battery, or a gas detection field (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing microporous iron-nitrogen doped carbon catalyst material with mild experimental condition and better repeatability, in simple and environmentally-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a microporous iron-nitrogen doped carbon catalyst material prepared by the method
   (2) an use of the microporous iron-nitrogen doped carbon catalyst material for an electrocatalyst in the field of metal-air, fuel cell, water decomposition or carbon dioxide reduction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The soluble ferric iron source compound is ferric chloride, ferric nitrate or a hydrate. The molten salt is lithium, sodium, potassium, magnesium, calcium, aluminum, and zinc chloride or a hydrate. The inert gas is high-purity nitrogen, argon or argon-hydrogen mixture. The acid is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid and acetic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen-containing polymer monomer is pyrrole, aniline and phenylenediamine.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation);

PN CN109201026-A; CN109201026-B
TI Preparing visible light responsive titanium (III) ion self-doped titanium dioxide photocatalyst for treating high-concentration organic pollutant wastewater, involves boiling the distilled water, sealing and cooling to normal temperature.
AU WANG T
   LI Y
   PAN J
   YAN K
   WU L
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG GONGSHANG (UYZG-C)
GA 201907611M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing visible light responsive titanium (III) ion self-doped titanium dioxide photocatalyst involves boiling the distilled water for 8 to 12 minutes, sealing and cooling to a normal temperature to obtain a distilled water without carbon dioxide, reacting pure titanium dioxide powder catalyst with mixed crystal structure, water-soluble reducing agent and mixing carbon dioxide-free distilled water, ultrasonically dispersing until a stable suspension system is formed, raising the ultrasonic water bath temperature to 75-85 degrees C. The pre-treated suspension system is cooled to room temperature, is completely transferred to a high-pressure reaction vessel lined with polytetrafluoroethylene, and after sealing, the thermal reduction reaction is carried out at a constant temperature. The heat treatment reaction liquid is cooled and separated, is obtained by multiple washing-centrifugation and finally drying.
   USE - Method for preparing visible light responsive titanium (III) ion self-doped titanium dioxide photocatalyst for treating high-concentration organic pollutant wastewater (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare visible light responsive titanium (III) ion self-doped titanium dioxide photocatalyst which removes methyl orange contamination in high-salt wastewater system under the excitation of weak visible light efficiency degradation and effectively controls content of photocatalyst crystal appearance and visible light catalyzing and degrading performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for treating high-concentration organic pollutant wastewater, which involves:
   (A) applying the titanium (III) ion self-doped visible light responsive titanium dioxide nanophotocatalyst to a high salinity wastewater system, and adsorbing in the dark for half an hour; and
   (B) irradiating on a visible light source for 3 to 5 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the pure titanium dioxide powder catalyst, the reducing agent to the carbon dioxide-free distilled water is 0.1 to 2.0 g:0.50 to 5.0 g:120 mL. The pure titanium dioxide powder catalyst exhibits a mixed crystal structure of rutile and anatase, and has a specific surface area of 50 to 100 m2/g and a particle diameter of 20-100 nm. The titanium (III) self-doped visible light responsive titanium dioxide nanophotocatalyst is added in an amount of 1.0 to 5.0 mg/mL. Preferred Condition: The mass ratio of the pure titanium dioxide powder catalyst, the reducing agent to the carbon dioxide-free distilled water is 0.1 to 2.0 g:0.50 to 5.0 g:120 mL. The pure titanium dioxide powder catalyst exhibits a mixed crystal structure of rutile and anatase, and has a specific surface area of 50 to 100 m2/g and a particle diameter of 20-100 nm. The titanium (III) self-doped visible light responsive titanium dioxide nanophotocatalyst is added in an amount of 1.0 to 5.0 mg/mL. The pre-reaction time is 1 to 5 hours. The reaction temperature is 150 to 180 degrees C and the reaction time is 5 to 12 hours. Preferred Components: The reducing agent is ascorbic acid, sodium citrate or glucose.

PN CN109179329-A
TI Deiodination by phosphoric acid by-product fluorosilicic acid used for producing anhydrous hydrogen fluoride includes producing anhydrous hydrogen fluoride, mixing, reacting, distilling, filtering, separating, heating and collecting.
AU MA Z
   YAN J
   PAN G
   ZHANG Q
   MAO W
   PAN C
AE FUJIAN WENGFU LANTIAN FLUORINE CHEM CO (WENF-C)
GA 201909367J
AB    NOVELTY - Deiodination by phosphoric acid by-product fluorosilicic acid includes producing anhydrous hydrogen fluoride (AHF), placing fluorosilicic acid solution in stirred tank, adding sulfuric acid, mixing at 30-40 revolutions per minute (rpm), reacting for 12-15 minutes, adsorbing with concentrated gas, distilling concentrated sulfuric acid absorption liquid, performing supercritical extraction, filtering carbon dioxide, compressing, separating to obtain extract, performing extract treatment, adding sodium hydroxide, mixing at 60-90 rpm for 5-8 minutes, adding dilute hydrochloric acid, acidifying, separating to obtain solid product, washing solid product to obtain crude iodine, refining crude iodine, standing for 20-30 minutes, heating, performing sublimation and collecting.
   USE - Method of deiodination by phosphoric acid by-product fluorosilicic acid used for producing anhydrous hydrogen fluoride (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, high iodine recovery rate and low production cost and is easy to recycle and can be used repeatedly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Deiodination by phosphoric acid by-product fluorosilicic acid comprises producing anhydrous hydrogen fluoride (AHF), placing fluorosilicic acid solution in stirred tank, adding sulfuric acid, turning on mixer, mixing at 30-40 revolutions per minute (rpm), reacting for 12-15 minutes, adsorbing with concentrated gas, distilling concentrated sulfuric acid absorption liquid to obtain mixture of the product hydrogen fluoride and water, performing supercritical extraction, placing in extraction tank, filtering carbon dioxide through filter, compressing by compressor, sending to extraction tank, performing cyclic extraction under 18-24 MPa at 300-350 K for 1 hour, separating to obtain extract, performing extract treatment, adding sodium hydroxide, mixing at 60-90 rpm for 5-8 minutes, adding dilute hydrochloric acid, acidifying, separating to obtain solid product, washing solid product to obtain crude iodine, refining crude iodine, placing crude iodine in low temperature drying oven, standing for 20-30 minutes, heating, performing sublimation at atmospheric pressure and collecting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of the concentrated sulfuric acid is 98%. The mass fraction of sodium hydroxide is 30-35%. The mass fraction of dilute hydrochloric acid is 5-6%.
