TI Multifunctional composite catalyst useful for carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed atmosphere conversion reaction in a fixed bed, a slurry bed or a fluidized bed, comprises e.g. molybdenum-containing composite metal oxide.
AU KANG J
   WANG Y
   LIU Z
   ZHANG Q
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 201899537D
AB    NOVELTY - Multifunctional composite catalyst comprises 20-60 wt.% molybdenum-containing composite metal oxide, a multi-stage pore zeolite molecular sieve modified with a metal element, and a multi-stage pore zeolite molecular sieve modified with a metal element.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed atmosphere conversion reaction in a fixed bed, a slurry bed or a fluidized bed (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the multifunctional composite catalyst, comprising (i) adding water in the molybdenum salt, stirring to prepare the solution A, (ii) adding the solution A, the A element-containing salt, continuously stirring to obtain solution B, (ii) adding the solution B, and hard template agent, continuously stirring to obtain the solution C, (iv) continuously stirring the solution C at 40-90 degrees C to obtain the solid-liquid mixture, grinding the dried sample, roasting, the obtained product is the composite metal oxide containing molybdenum, marked as AOx-MoO3 (m), where m is the molar ratio of A to molybdenum element, (v) dissolving the mixture containing IIA group elements, IIIA group elements, Group IIB elements, Group IIIB elements, Group IVB elements, Group VIIB elements salt in water to prepare solution D, and adding micro-pore, porous ZSM-5 mesoporous, ZSM-11, ZSM-34, Y, Bcta, MOR, MCM-22, X molecular sieve, continuously stirring the solution D at 40-90 degrees C, drying to obtain solid-liquid mixture, grinding and roasting, to obtain the multi-level hole molecular sieve modified by metal elements, and (vi) forming the AOx-MoO3 and metal-modified molecular sieve mixed grinding tablet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molybdenum-containing composite metal oxide is AOx-MoO3, where A is group IB element, a group IIB element, a group IIIB element, a group IVB element, a VB group element, a VIB group element, a VIIB group element, and a VIIIB group element. The molar ratio of A to molybdenum is 1. (0.005-5), where x represents the number of oxygen atoms in the stable oxide of A. The molybdenum-containing composite metal oxide is prepared by a template method, and the templating agent is carbon nanotubes, activated carbon, carbon black, porous carbon spheres, and ordered mediation and the pore carbons. The metal element modified multi-stage pore zeolite molecule is porous ZSM-5, ZSM-11, ZSM-34, Y, beta, MOR, MCM-22, X molecular sieves containing micropores and mesopores and modified by Group IIA element, a Group IIIA element, a Group IIB element, a Group IIIB element, a Group IVB element, and a Group VIIB element. The IB group element, the IIB group element, the IIIB group element, the IVB group element, the VB group element, the VIB group element, the VIIB group element, and the VIIIB group element oxide are copper, zinc, nickel, cobalt, iron, manganese, chromium, vanadium, titanium, strontium, cadmium, palladium, rhodium, ruthenium, niobium, zirconium, yttrium, tantalum, and tungsten. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the molybdenum salt is molybdenum sulfate, molybdenum chloride, molybdenum acetate, ammonium molybdate, sodium molybdate, the water is de-ionized water, the mass percentage concentration of the solution A is 0.01-5 wt.%, the condition of the stirring is stirring at 40-90 degrees C for 0.1-5 hours. In the step (ii), the condition of continuously agitating at 40-90 degrees C for 0.1-5 hours, the salt is nitrate, hydrochloride, carbonate, sulfate, and acetate. In the step (iii), the hard template agent can be carbon nano tube, activated carbon, carbon black, porous carbon ball, ordered mesoporous carbon in at least one of the hard template agent with the mass ratio of molybdenum can be (10-10000):1, the continued stirring time can be 0.1-5 hours. In the step (iv), continuously stirring at 40-90 degrees C of solution C for 1-10 hours, the drying condition is drying 1-24 hours under the roasting is 300-700 degrees C, muffle furnace for 1-10 hours. In the step (v), the mass percentage of the metal element and the molecular sieve can be 0.02-0.2% at 60-120 degrees C in the vacuum drying box, the continued stirring time can be 1-10 hours, drying is drying 1-24 hours, the roasting in 300-600 degrees C muffle furnace for 1-10 hours at 60-120 degrees C in the vacuum drying box.

A fixed beam (2-1) is fixed on                top portion of each of the two upright posts in a                width direction. Two longitudinal beams (2-2) are                fixed on the top of the beam. A knot rope (5) is                attached at a distance from the longitudinal beam                to form an outer frame. A column support is                horizontally fixed between each two opposite                columns on a platform network (3) to form a                multi-layer planting platform. A LED lamp strip is                connected with a power source. A carbon dioxide gas                hose is connected with a carbon dioxide generator.                A main water pipe is provided outside the outer                frame for injecting water into the water tank. The                rainwater collected into the fishpond is injected                into water tank and discharged from the water tank                at regular intervals. After the water is submerged,                the vegetables are breathed and photosynthesis is                performed after a certain period of time.
   USE - Method for culturing vegetables, insects and                fish in integrated three-dimensional planting                frame.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and convenient to achieve                pesticide-free vegetable de-worming at low-cost.                The social, environmental and economic benefits are                achieved. The occurrence of insect pests is                inhibited at an early stage without any external                medium pollution so as to obtain the true organic                vegetable. The need for large amount of labor is                omitted.

PN CN108576083-A
TI Chinese medicinal composition useful for preventing cucumber anthracnose comprises ginger, fructus psoraleae, clove, Rhododendron, garlic, rhizoma gastrodiae, and water celery.
AU REN B
AE GUIZHOU MINGZHIYUAN PLANTING SPECIALIZED COOP (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018838639
AB    NOVELTY - Chinese medicinal composition comprises 3-6 pts. wt. ginger, 5-9 pts. wt. fructus psoraleae, 7-10 pts. wt. clove, 3-9 pts. wt. Rhododendron, 10-13 pts. wt. garlic, 1-2 pts. wt. rhizoma gastrodiae, and 15-17 pts. wt. water celery.
   USE - The medicinal composition is useful for preventing cucumber anthracnose (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The medicinal composition: avoids the problems of drug resistance; and does not produce residue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing Chinese medicinal composition comprising (i) providing fructus psoraleae, adding wolfberry wine in the material-liquid ratio of 1:6-7, extracting at 42-46 degrees C for 2-3 hours, extracting at normal temperature for 1-2 hours, filtering, collecting filtrate to obtain fructus psoraleae extract, (ii) providing ginger and subjecting for carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to obtain ginger extract, (iii) providing Rhododendron and clove, adding wolfberry wine in the material to liquid ratio of 1:2.5-3.2, extracting at 36-40 degrees C for 3-4 hours, filtering, and collecting filtrate to obtain Rhododendron/clove extract, (iv) providing garlic and rhizoma gastrodiae, mixing, grinding by 60 meshes sieve, subjecting for carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to obtain garlic and rhizoma gastrodiae extract, (v) providing water celery, adding water in the material to liquid ratio of 1:1.8-2.3, extracting at 80-85 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, extracting at 54-66 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, and filtering to obtain water celery extract, and (vi) mixing obtained extracts, distilling at 36-40 Pa at 77-86 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, distilling at 55-62 Pa at 48-54 degrees C for 8-12 minutes, combining distillate to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The Chinese medicinal composition comprises 4 pts. wt. ginger, 7 pts. wt. fructus psoraleae, 9 pts. wt. clove, 6 pts. wt. Rhododendron, 12 pts. wt. garlic, 1.7 pts. wt. rhizoma gastrodiae, and 15.7 pts. wt. water celery. The fermenting agent comprise 10-15 pts. wt. active dry yeast powder, 2-3 pts. wt. Lactobacillus acidophilus powder, 5-8 pts. wt. malt, 22-26 pts. wt. soybean milk and 3-5 pts. wt. honey. Preferred Method: During carbon dioxide supercritical extraction tank pressure is 4.8-5.2 MPa, extraction tank pressure is 23.2-23.8 MPa, analytical kettle pressure is 5.0-5.8 MPa, extraction kettle temperature is 43-48 degrees C and extraction time is 1-1.5 hours. The wolfberry wine is prepared by (1) providing 8-11 pts. wt. wolfberry leaves, 20-30 pts. wt. wolfberry, 110-130 pts. wt. drinking water, and 0.01-0.02 pts. wt. fermenting agent, (2) placing wolfberry leaves under 35-40 degrees C for 1-2 hours, adding drinking water and extracting to obtain wolfberry leaves juice, (3) placing wolfberry under 0-10 degrees C for 6-10 hours, adding wolfberry leaves juice, and boiling for 5-8 minutes, and (4) lowering temperature to room temperature, adding fermenting agent, sealing and storing at 10-15 degrees C for 7-10 days. During process, the boiling is four stage boiling in first stage heating at 15-20 degrees C for 10 minutes, in second stage heating at 30-35 degrees C for 15 minutes, in third stage heating at 68-76 degrees C for 20 minutes and in fourth stage boiling.
   ACTIVITY - Fungicide.
DC C05 (Biological control - excluding veterinary medicine, but including use of microorganisms, predators and natural products.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN GB2559477-A; GB2559477-B
TI Gas turbine facility with multiple combustion gas streams has low-temperature combustion gas supply pipe, which guides portion of cooled combustion gas cooled in heat exchanger to space between casing and combustor.
AU IWAI Y
   ITOH M
   MORISAWA Y
   SUZUKI S
AE TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
GA 2018613020
AB    NOVELTY - The gas turbine facility has a combustor (20) that is provided in a casing (70) to combust a fuel and an oxidant. A turbine (25) is rotated by a combustion gas exhausted from the combustor. A heat exchanger cools the combustion gas exhausted from the turbine and heats a portion of the cooled combustion gas. A high-temperature combustion gas supply pipe (42) guides the portion of the cooled combustion gas heated in the heat exchanger to the combustor. A passage (103) is disposed in a longitudinal direction between the casing and the combustor to guide the combustion gas into the combustor. A low-temperature combustion gas supply pipe (41) guides another portion of the cooled combustion gas cooled in the heat exchanger to a space between the casing and the combustor. An exhaust pipe exhausts a remaining portion of the combustion gas cooled in the heat exchanger to an outside.
   USE - Gas turbine facility with multiple combustion gas streams.
   ADVANTAGE - The manufacturing cost for the gas turbine facility is reduced by making a portion of the combustor casing to be made of inexpensive iron-based heat resistant steel. The coupling portions of the high-temperature combustion gas supply pipes are set at more upstream portions of the upstream-side casing, which decreases a portion exposed to the high-temperature carbon dioxide and increases a portion exposed to the low-temperature carbon dioxide, in the upstream-side casing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the sectional view of a combustor and a combustor casing provided in a gas turbine facility.
   Combustor (20)
   Turbine (25)
   Low-temperature combustion gas supply pipe (41)
   High-temperature combustion gas supply pipe (42)
   Casing (70)
   Passage (103)

PT P
PN US2018187887-A1; US10767861-B2
TI Method for reducing carbon dioxide during generating power from carbon-based fuels, involves introducing carbon-containing fuel to upstream boiler and downstream boiler, and burning flue gas and carbon-containing fuel in downstream boiler.
AU AXELBAUM R L
   KUMFER B M
   XIA F
   GOPAN A
   DHUNGEL B
AE UNIV WASHINGTON ST LOUIS (UNWL-C)
   AXELBAUM R L (AXEL-Individual)
   KUMFER B M (KUMF-Individual)
   XIA F (XIAF-Individual)
   GOPAN A (GOPA-Individual)
   DHUNGEL B (DHUN-Individual)
GA 201858390M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing an oxidizer to an upstream boiler (22a). Carbon-containing fuel is introduced to the upstream boiler. The oxidizer and carbon-containing fuel in the upstream boiler are burned. Flue gas emitted from the upstream boiler is transmitted to a downstream boiler (22c). The flue gas is introduced to the downstream boiler for use as an oxidizer. Carbon-containing fuel is introduced to the downstream boiler. The flue gas and carbon-containing fuel in the downstream boiler are burned. Flue gas emitted from the second boiler is transported to a third boiler. The flue gas is introduced from the second boiler to the third boiler for use as an oxidizer.
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide during generating power from carbon-based fuels.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables capturing sulfur oxides, and nitrogen oxides simultaneously so as to ensure better economic effect. The method enables adjusting flow rates of the liquid in a column and the column height so as to allow the top stages of a cooler to be at low temperature for promoting the overall rate-limiting reaction, while allowing cooling and condensation to occur in the bottom stages. The method enables realizing staged combustion as the rate of heat transfer is higher for radiation than convection so as to minimize the required boiler tube surface area for reducing capital costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for processing carbon-based byproducts during burning carbon-containing fuel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system for heating a working fluid.
   Upstream boiler (22a)
   Downstream boiler (22c)
   Working fluid inlet (32)
   Vent (64)
   Gas compression and purification unit (84)

PN EP3315833-A1; JP2018071702-A
TI Shaft seal device for refrigerator compressor for            compressing carbon dioxide in automotive air            conditioner, has auxiliary seal for sealing rotating            shaft, where refrigerator oil is stored to height that            reaches part of sliding surfaces.
AU NAKAHARA N
AE EAGLE IND CO LTD (EAGI-C)
   EAGLE KOGYO KK (EAGI-C)
GA 2018335391
AB    NOVELTY - The device (10) has a mechanical seal (3)                including a stationary sealing ring (40), which is                held by a housing (1). A rotating sealing ring (30)                is mounted to a rotating shaft (2), where a sliding                surface (30a) of the rotating sealing ring slidably                contacts the sliding surface (40a) of the                stationary sealing ring. An auxiliary seal (90)                seals the rotating shaft, where refrigerator oil                (L) is stored to height that reaches a part of the                sliding surfaces in an intermediate chamber (C),                which is formed between the mechanical seal and the                auxiliary seal.
   USE - Shaft seal device for a refrigerator                compressor for compressing a refrigerant i.e.                carbon dioxide (claimed), in a refrigeration cycle                device i.e. automotive air conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The mechanical seal is arranged on an inside                of a refrigerator compressor, so that a shaft seal                of refrigerant of high pressure is ensured. The                device avoids increasing pressure of the                intermediate chamber, so that differential pressure                between the inside of the mechanical seal and the                intermediate chamber is not largely fluctuated,                thus performing sealing processes without                significantly losing pressure balance acting on the                mechanical seal in an efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The auxiliary seal is a lip seal. The                mechanical seal is an inside mechanical seal.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of a                shaft seal device of a refrigerator                compressor.
   Intermediate chamber (C)
   Refrigerator oil (L)
   Housing (1)
   Rotating shaft (2)
   Mechanical seal (3)
   Device (10)
   Rotating sealing ring (30)
   Sliding surface of rotating sealing ring                (30a)
   Stationary sealing ring (40)
   Sliding surface of stationary sealing ring                (40a)
   Auxiliary seal (90)

PT P
PN CN107934986-A; CN107934986-B
TI Biomass ash resource utilization method comprises pre-processing the biomass ash, soaking the primary biomass ash in acid solution, filtering, separating, drying, digesting with alkali solution, filtering and separating.
AU LI G
   CHEN J
   GONG Y
   GUAN X
   DU D
   AO C
AE WUHAN KAIDI ENG TECHNOLOGY RES INST CO (WUAH-C)
   WUHAN KAIDI ENG TECHNOLOGY RES INST CO (WUAH-C)
GA 201832790C
AB    NOVELTY - Biomass ash resource utilization method comprises: (i) pre-processing the biomass ash to obtain primary biomass ash; (ii) soaking the primary biomass ash in acid solution, filtering, separating and drying; (iii) digesting the biomass ash with alkali solution, filtering and separating; (iv) filling carbon dioxide gas into one part of sodium silicate, neutralizing, adding flocculant, filtering and drying; (v) baking the solid sample to obtain nano-silica; and (vi) preparing porous grade zeolite socony mobil-5 molecular sieve, then crystallizing mother liquor.
   USE - Used as biomass ash resource utilization method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the processing efficiency of waste residue, has zero discharge, zero pollution and achieves efficient utilization of biomass raw material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Biomass ash resource utilization method comprises: (i) pre-processing the biomass ash to remove scum and water-soluble impurities to obtain primary biomass ash; (ii) soaking the primary biomass ash in acid solution, filtering, separating and drying to obtain biomass ash and waste acid byproduct; (iii) digesting the biomass ash with alkali solution, filtering and separating to obtain sodium silicate and byproduct residue; (iv) filling carbon dioxide gas into one part of sodium silicate, neutralizing the solution pH to 9-11, adding flocculant into solution in a mass ratio of 1:40-100 to carry out flocculation, filtering and drying to obtain a solid sample and byproduct inorganic salt solution; (v) baking the solid sample to obtain nano-silica; and (vi) preparing porous grade zeolite socony mobil-5 molecular sieve using another part of sodium silicate, nano-silica as a silicon source, aluminum source, deionized water and seed crystal, then crystallizing mother liquor, where the molar ratio of silicon source, aluminum source, deionized water and seed crystal is 1:0.001-0.02:0.05-0.3:5-50:0.02-0.1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the residue is activated to obtain activated carbon. The method further comprises uniformly mixing the waste acid of step (ii), inorganic salt solution of step (iv) and crystallization mother liquor of step (vi), subjecting to vacuum distillation to obtain nitrogen fertilizer, phosphate fertilizer, nitrogen-phosphorus-potassium fertilizer, ammonium phosphate, ammonium bicarbonate, inorganic sodium fertilizer and distilled water, then recycling distilled water. In the step (vi), mixing sodium silicate with nano-silica, soaking, sequentially adding deionized water, aluminum source and seed crystals, melting and stirring at 60-90 degrees C for 4-8 hours, crystallizing to obtain sodium zeolite socony mobil-5 molecular sieve and crystallized mother liquor; subjecting the sodium-type zeolite socony mobil-5 molecular sieve to ammonium exchange and calcination to obtain catalytically active solid acid zeolite socony mobil-5 molecular sieve, i.e. bulk-stacked porous grade zeolite socony mobil-5 molecular sieve. In the step (vi), the crystallization is carried out at 150-180 degrees C for 24-36 hours. In the step (vi), the ammonium exchange condition comprises: the mass ratio of ammonium salt solution to molecular sieve is 10-15:1, soaking is carried out at 60-100 degrees C for 1-3 hours; and the ammonium exchange reagent is 5-15% ammonium sulfate, ammonium chloride, ammonium nitrate or ammonium phosphate solution; the calcination is carried out at 500-600 degrees C with programmed heating rate of 1 degrees C/minute for 2-10 hours. In the step (v), the baking temperature is 500-550 degrees C, the baking time is 2-10 hours, and the particle size of nano-silica is 60-80 nm. Preferred Components: The acid is 0.5-2 mol/l nitric acid or phosphoric acid. The primary biomass ash is mixed with acid solution in a mass ratio of 1:5-20. The alkali solution is 1-3 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution or potassium hydroxide solution. The primary biomass ash is mixed with alkali in a mass ratio of 1:5-20. The seed crystal is a small crystallite molecular sieve with high crystallinity and particle size of 100-200 nm. The flocculant is an inorganic ammonium salt. The sodium silicate has a modulus of 0-2.9. The porous zeolite socony mobil-5 molecular sieve has: a specific surface area of 370-390 m2/g; an average particle size of 3-5 mu m; a total pore volume of 0.3-0.5 cm3/g; and a mesopore pore volume of 0.1-0.4 cm3/g.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.)

PN CN107903166-A; CN107903166-B
TI Preparing a feed-grade copper citrate comprises            e.g. adding citric acid and water into the reactor to            form a citric acid aqueous solution, and heating,            turning on stirrer, the ratio of citric acid and basic            copper carbonate.
AU YAN J
   ZHANG C
   TANG L
   KUANG S
AE SICHUAN ANIMAL SCI ACAD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2018301033
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a feed-grade copper citrate                comprises e.g. adding citric acid and water into                the reactor to form a 20-25% citric acid aqueous                solution, and heating to 80-100 degrees C, turning                on stirrer, the ratio of citric acid and basic                copper carbonate is 1:(0.8-1), adding basic copper                carbonate for neutralization to obtain copper                citrate, and adding citric acid into reaction                solution, maintaining pH, where the generated                carbon dioxide gas is absorbed into the absorption                tank through the exhaust port, filtering generated                copper citrate, concentrating, cooling to room                temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a                feed-grade copper citrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a feed-grade copper citrate                comprises (1) adding citric acid and water into the                reactor to form a 20-25% citric acid aqueous                solution, and heating to 80-100 degrees C, (2)                turning on stirrer, the ratio of citric acid and                basic copper carbonate is 1:(0.8-1), adding basic                copper carbonate for neutralization to obtain                copper citrate, and adding citric acid into                reaction solution, maintaining pH to 4-6, where the                generated carbon dioxide gas is absorbed into the                absorption tank through the exhaust port, (3)                filtering generated copper citrate, concentrating,                cooling to room temperature, placing in a                centrifuge and dehydrating to obtain copper citrate                crystals, and separating waste liquid and                discharging into a waste liquid pool for recycling                and (4) using obtained copper citrate crystals,                drying in a desiccator at 100-120 degrees C for 1-2                hours, and removing part of the crystallization                water to obtain a feed-grade copper citrate                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The citric acid is a                  food grade raw material and the basic copper                  carbonate is a feed grade raw material. In the                  step (1) water is a mixture of deionized water                  and the separated waste liquid from step (3). The                  absorption liquid in the absorption tank is a                  sodium hydroxide or calcium hydroxide                  solution.

PN CN107874165-A; CN107874165-B
TI Preparing sea cucumber oil involves taking fresh sea cucumber internal organs, washing, draining and cutting into sea cucumber purtenance crushed blocks, salt, magnesium chloride and polypropylene fiber non-woven cloth.
AU SONG F
   NING J
AE RONGCHENG HAIRUIXIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (RONG-Non-standard)
GA 201828141U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sea cucumber oil involves taking fresh sea cucumber internal organs, washing, draining and cutting into 2-5 millimeter in 100 pts. wt. sea cucumber purtenance crushed blocks of into 80-100 pts. wt., 1.5-3 pts. wt. salt, 2-4 pts. wt. magnesium chloride and 5-7 pts. wt. polypropylene fiber non-woven cloth, and stirring uniformly, adding the mixture in the reactor and heating at 105-110 degrees C for 2-4 hours, unloading the pressure and cooling, evaporating obtained mixture in vacuum to obtain solid, and performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to obtain crude sea cucumber oil.
   USE - Method for preparing sea cucumber oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare sea cucumber oil which is highly-efficient extracting sea cucumber oil, and used for processing and utilization of sea cucumber purtenance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing sea cucumber oil involves taking fresh sea cucumber internal organs, washing, draining and cutting into 2-5 millimeter in 100 pts. wt. sea cucumber purtenance crushed blocks of into 80-100 pts. wt., 1.5-3 pts. wt. salt, 2-4 pts. wt. magnesium chloride and 5-7 pts. wt. polypropylene fiber non-woven cloth, and stirring uniformly, adding the mixture in the reactor and heating at 105-110 degrees C for 2-4 hours, unloading the pressure and cooling, evaporating obtained mixture in vacuum to obtain solid, and performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to obtain crude sea cucumber oil, using 0.10-0.20 mole per liter sodium carbonate aqueous solution in obtained crude oil, adding 0.10-0.20 mole per liter hydrochloric acid, and using clean water for washing until pH of washing water is 6-7 to obtain sea cucumber oil is filled in soft capsule to obtain sea cucumber oil capsule.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The fresh sea cucumber viscera is taken, cleaned, drained and cut into pieces of 3 millimeter in 100 pts. wt water, 2.4 pts. wt. salt, 3.5 pts. wt. magnesium chloride and 6 pts. wt. polypropylene fiber non-woven fabric strips are added to crushed sea cucumber viscera and stirred well. The mixture is added in reactor, and heated at 107 degrees C for 2.5 hours, and unloading pressure and temperature. The obtained mixture is decompressed and evaporated to obtain solid, and performed carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to obtain crude sea cucumber oil. The obtained crude sea cucumber oil is trated by using 0.15 mole per liter sodium carbonate solution, 0.15 mole per liter of hydrochloric acid, and reached pH of the washing water to 6.5 clean water washing. The sea cucumber oil is further dehydrated by using a molecular sieve. The sodium carbonate solution is washed at 3-6 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, maintained nitrogen bubbling in washing process, where nitrogen flow of mixed solution is 200-400 milliliter per minute. The cucumber sea oil is filled by adding vitamin E to reached 50-70 milligram per gram sea cucumber oil. Preferred Condition: The width of polypropylene fiber non woven cloth is 10-15 millimeter, and thickness is 90-120 per square meter.

PN CN107760942-A
TI Preparing aluminum-copper based composite material comprises e.g. crushing tungsten concentrate to obtain tungsten concentrate powder, mixing aluminum powder, copper powder and carbon powder to obtain original alloy powder.
AU CHEN G
AE CHEN G (CHEN-Individual)
GA 201819947G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aluminum-copper based composite material comprises (1) crushing the tungsten concentrate to obtain tungsten concentrate powder; (2) mixing aluminum powder, copper powder and carbon powder to obtain original alloy powder; (3) adding tungsten concentrate powder and the original alloy powder into ball mill, mixing and milling to obtain mixed powder; and (4) compressing the mixed powder material to thickness of 15-25 mm, using carbon dioxide laser processing machine to ignite the surface of the compact with high-energy laser beam, and initiating compact self-propagating sintering.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aluminum-copper based composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical and saves energy; and has short processing cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing aluminum-copper based composite material comprises (1) crushing the tungsten concentrate to particle size below 100 mesh to obtain tungsten concentrate powder; (2) mixing aluminum powder, copper powder and carbon powder with particle size of less than or equal to 200 mesh sieves to obtain original alloy powder; where the mixing mass ratio of aluminum powder, copper powder and carbon powder is 100:20-40:5-7; (3) adding tungsten concentrate powder and the original alloy powder into ball mill, mixing and milling at milling speed of 100-200 revolutions/minute for 4-8 hours to obtain mixed powder, where the mixing mass ratio of original alloy powder and tungsten concentrate powder is 100:6-8; and (4) compressing the mixed powder material to thickness of 15-25 mm with pressure of 50-70 KN, using carbon dioxide laser processing machine to ignite the surface of the compact with high-energy laser beam, and initiating compact self-propagating sintering.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, the reaction speed of self-propagating sintering is 1-2 mm/second.

TI Preparation of washing liquid used for killing mites involves preparing elecampane and lavender extract, extracting radix isatidis, radix bupleuri, and Aloe, mixing extracts, and stirring in thickener and ethylene bis-stearamide.
AU SHAO J
   LIU R
   LIU Q
AE CHANGZHOU XIEWANG TEXTILE CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018088326
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of washing liquid involves crushing elecampane, adding water at mass ratio of 1:3-4, and ultrasonically extracting; mixing lavender and water at ratio of 1-2:4-5, distilling, adding 0.01-0.02 wt.% sodium chloride, standing for 2-3 hours, adding 20-30 wt.% activated carbon, standing for 20-30 minutes, and filtering; mixing 10-15 pts. wt. water, 7-8 pts. wt. radix isatidis, 5-6 pts. wt. radix bupleuri, and 3-4 pts. wt. Aloe, standing for 40-50 minutes, and super-critical carbon dioxide extracting.
   USE - Method for preparing washing liquid used for killing mites (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The washing liquid has faint scent and antibacterial effect, improves whipstitches strength, is harmless to human body, and solves usage of sodium hypochlorite and chlorophenols having strong oxidation resistance and are unstable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of washing liquid comprises crushing elecampane, adding water at mass ratio of 1:3-4, and ultrasonically extracting; mixing lavender and water at ratio of 1-2:4-5, distilling, adding 0.01-0.02 wt.% sodium chloride, standing for 2-3 hours, adding 20-30 wt.% activated carbon, standing for 20-30 minutes, and filtering; mixing 10-15 pts. wt. water, 7-8 pts. wt. radix isatidis, 5-6 pts. wt. radix bupleuri, and 3-4 pts. wt. Aloe, standing for 40-50 minutes, and super-critical carbon dioxide extracting; and taking 40-50 pts. wt. product in first step, 40-50 pts. wt. product in second step, 10-15 pts. wt. product in third step, 2-3 pts. wt. thickener, 1-2 pts. wt. lactic acid, and 0.8-1 pt. wt. ethylene bis-stearamide, stirring, and collecting mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ultrasonic extraction is done at 40-60 degrees C for 24-40 minutes. The distilling is done at 68-80 degrees C and 7-8 MPa. The supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction is done at 15-20 MPa and 40-60 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The thickener is Propylene glycol alginate or carboxymethyl cellulose.
   ACTIVITY - Acaricide.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of Chinese herbal medicine feed comprises wheat germ degreasing into disk, oven vacuuming at 60 degrees C into 3% moisture content, cooling at room temperature, crushing through 40 meshes sieve, and supercritically extracting at 45 degrees C for 3-3.5 hours under 4.5 L/minute carbon dioxide flow rate and 50 MPa pressure to obtain wheat germ oil; mixing Forsythia, plantain, dandelion, Agastache rugosa, bitter almonds, Acanthopanax senticosus, ultrafinely crushing into 200 meshes sieve to obtain Chinese herbal medicine, decompressing Chinese herbal medicine by Chinese herbal multi-function extractor at 55-60 degrees C under - 0.05 MPa negative pressure, concentrating by boiling alcohol, and filtering to obtain extract; and mixing 50-65 wt.% corn, 20-40 wt.% wheat bran, 5-10 wt.% rapeseed powder, 7-13 wt.% fish powder, 0.01-0.05 wt.% salt, 1.2-1.3 wt.% calcium chloride, 2-3 wt.% compound vitamin, adding wheat germ oil and extract, stirring uniformly, drying and naturally cooling at room temperature.

TI Apparatus useful for manufacturing molten iron, comprises a fluidized-bed reactor provided with a space for reducing iron ore to produce reduced iron, a reaction furnace to supply exhaust gas and reduced iron, and a gas supply unit.
AU CHANG K K
   MYOUNG K S
   DAL HOI L
AE POSCO (POSC-C)
GA 2018132710
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises a fluidized-bed reactor (200) provided with a space for reducing iron ore to produce reduced iron, a reaction furnace (300) connected to the fluidized-bed reactor to supply some amount of the exhaust gas generated during manufacturing of the reduced iron and some amount of reduced iron, and provided with a space for producing syngas, and a gas supply unit to supply a gas for producing the syngas, connected to the reaction furnace. The apparatus further comprises a dust collector for supplying in the reactor when the exhaust gas contains carbon dioxide.
   USE - The apparatus is useful for manufacturing molten iron (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus is environmentally friendly and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing syngas, comprising (a) introducing the iron ore into the reducing furnace and supplying the reducing gas to produce the reduced iron, (b) introducing partial amount of the reduced iron into the reactor, (c) supplying exhaust gas and methane-containing gas generated during production of the reduced iron into the reactor, and (d) reacting the exhaust gas with methane-containing gas using the reduced iron as catalyst to produce synthesis gas containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The exhaust gas passes through only the dust collector and is supplied into the reactor. The synthesis gas is produced by reacting carbon dioxide contained in the exhaust gas with the methane-containing gas to produce a synthesis gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the apparatus.

   Fluidized-bed reactor (200)

PN CN114560457-A
TI Preparing density graded carbon aerogel based on            self-diffusion-condensation synergistic effect            comprises mixing resorcinol, aqueous formaldehyde            solution, sodium carbonate and water, carrying out            carbon dioxide supercritical drying and inert            atmosphere protection high temperature treatment.
AU YUAN Z
   CUI Z
   CHENG F
   MA D
   LI E
   SHEN Y
AE UNIV XIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYXT-C)
GA 2022771942
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing density graded carbon aerogel based                on self-diffusion-condensation synergistic effect                comprises (i) mixing resorcinol, aqueous                formaldehyde solution, sodium carbonate and water                to obtain mixed solution A with high concentration;                (ii) placing mixed solution A in a constant                temperature box and allowing to stand; (iii) mixing                resorcinol, aqueous formaldehyde solution, sodium                carbonate and water to obtain mixed solution B with                low concentration; (iv) placing mixed solution B in                a constant temperature box and allowing to stand;                (v) transferring sol D and chromatographic column                to a constant temperature box and allowing to stand                for 3 days to obtain gel; (vii) taking alcohol gel                and carrying out carbon dioxide supercritical                drying; and (ix) subjecting to the aerogel G sample                obtained in step (vii) to inert atmosphere                protection high temperature treatment to remove the                residual organic matter in the sample.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing density                graded carbon aerogel based on                self-diffusion-condensation synergistic                effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing density graded carbon aerogel based                on self-diffusion-condensation synergistic effect                comprises (i) uniformly mixing and stirring                resorcinol, aqueous formaldehyde solution, sodium                carbonate and water for 1-2 hours by magnetic force                to obtain mixed solution A with high concentration;                (ii) placing mixed solution A in a constant                temperature box and allowing to stand; (iii)                uniformly mixing and stirring resorcinol, aqueous                formaldehyde solution, sodium carbonate and water                for 1-2 hours by magnetic force to obtain mixed                solution B with low concentration; (iv) placing                mixed solution B in a constant temperature box and                allowing to stand; (v) transferring mixed solution                B into a beaker, transferring mixed solution A into                beaker by a peristaltic pump A, continuously and                uniformly stirring by a magnetic stirrer to obtain                mixed solution C, continuously pumping the mixed                solution C in beaker into chromatographic column by                a peristaltic pump B, forming a sol D whose density                is gradually changed along the vertical direction                in the chromatographic column; (vi) transferring                sol D and chromatographic column to a constant                temperature box and allowing to stand for 3 days to                obtain gel; (vii) removing the gel from the                chromatographic column, transferring gel E into                beaker under the condition of water bath, using                absolute ethyl alcohol to soak and washing for 5                times for 1 day, and completely removing water to                obtain alcohol gel; (viii) taking alcohol gel and                carrying out carbon dioxide supercritical drying;                and (ix) subjecting to the aerogel G sample                obtained in step (viii) to inert atmosphere                protection high temperature treatment to remove the                residual organic matter in the sample.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                density graded carbon aerogel based on                self-diffusion-condensation synergistic effect,                prepared as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  formaldehyde solution is a formaldehyde solution                  with a mass fraction of 38 wt.%, and the mass of                  resorcinol, formaldehyde solution, sodium                  carbonate and water is 1:1.5:0.01-0.1:10-20. In                  the step (ii), the temperature of the incubator                  is 45-50&#8451;. In the step (iii), the                  formaldehyde raw material is an aqueous                  formaldehyde solution with a mass fraction of 38                  wt.%, and the mass ratio of resorcinol, aqueous                  formaldehyde, sodium carbonate and water is                  1:1.5:0.001-0.01:1-2. In the step (iv), the                  temperature of the incubator is 45-50&#8451;.                  In the step (v), the flow rate of peristaltic                  pump A is 0.5-1 ml/minute, the flow rate of                  peristaltic pump B is 2-5 ml/ minute, the                  diameter of chromatography column is 30-80 mm,                  and the height is 150-200 mm. In the step (vi),                  the temperature of the incubator is                  45-50&#8451;. In the step (vii), the                  temperature of the water bath is                  65-70&#8451;. In the step (viii), the                  temperature of supercritical drying is                  40-42&#8451;, the air pressure is set to                  10-11 MPa, the heating rate is set to                  0.5-1&#8451;/minute, the constant temperature                  time is 3-4 hours, and the decompression rate is                  1-3 MPa/hour. In the step (ix), the heat                  treatment temperature is 850-1050&#8451;, the                  inert atmosphere is high-purity nitrogen, the                  heating rate is 8-10&#8451;/minute, and the                  constant temperature time is 3-4 hours.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

TI Container for storage and transport of liquid, contains liquid comprising saturated aliphatic carboxylic acid or its mixture, and gas phase above liquid, where and gas phase is inert gas phase containing nitrogen, helium, neon, argon, krypton, xenon, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and/or carbon monoxide.
AU TELES J H
   HAMANN J N
   LIANG S
   RITTINGER S
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 202256339F
AB    NOVELTY - Container with an inner volume of 0.05-10000 m3, contains (A) a liquid comprising a saturated aliphatic 6-12C carboxylic acid or its mixture, where the liquid has a saturated aliphatic 6-12C carboxylic acid content of 99-100 wt.% and occupies 1-99% of the inner volume of the container, and (B) a gas phase above the liquid, where (C) the container is a metallic container, and (D) the gas phase above the liquid is an inert gas phase containing nitrogen, helium, neon, argon, krypton, xenon, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide or their mixtures, having a molecular oxygen content of 0-100 vol. ppm.
   USE - The container is used for storage and transport of a liquid comprising a saturated aliphatic 6-12C carboxylic acid or its mixture and a gas phase (claimed). The stored saturated aliphatic 6-12C carboxylic acid can be used as intermediates for the production of a wide variety of compounds such as metal salts and metal soaps, flavors, fragrances, pharmaceutical and agrochemical ingredients, cosmetic ingredients, plasticizers, paints, coating additives, coolants, lubricants or catalysts for polymer processing. The container is used for storage tanks in tank farms.
   ADVANTAGE - The container can improve the storage and transport of saturated aliphatic 6-12C carboxylic acids in a way that it does not, or only slightly impair their color stability under thermal stress and/or prevent from, or at least strongly reduce the discoloration of products derived from the stored or transported saturated aliphatic 6-12C carboxylic acids, even if they are stored or transported for a long period of time such as for months. The container ensures improved storage and transport which provide easy to perform, preferable in easily available standard containers, and the saturated aliphatic 6-12C carboxylic acids do not be contaminated with detrimental compounds. The container allows a very broad range of filling levels so that the filling degree is 1-99% based on the inner volume of the container.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process for the storage and transport of a liquid comprising a saturated aliphatic 6-12C carboxylic acid or its mixture, where the liquid has a saturated aliphatic 6-12C carboxylic acid content of 99-100 wt.%, by filling the liquid into a container with an inner volume of 0.05-10000 m3 in an amount that the liquid occupies 1-99% of the inner volume of the container, where the liquid is kept in it for more than 1 hour, where the container is a metallic container, and the gas phase in the container is inertized with an inert gas containing nitrogen, helium, neon, argon, krypton, xenon, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide or their mixtures, having a molecular oxygen content of 0-100 vol. ppm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Product: The container is a stainless steel container. The container is a mobile container with an inner volume of 0.05-120 m3. Preferred Composition: The inert gas phase has a molecular oxygen content of less than or equal to 10 vol. ppm. The inert gas phase contains greater than or equal to 99.9 vol.% nitrogen, calculated on a basis, in which the vapor of the saturated aliphatic 6-12C carboxylic acids vaporized from the liquid was deducted and the remaining gas phase set to 100 vol.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The saturated aliphatic 6-12C carboxylic acid is octanoic acid, 2-ethylhexanoic acid, nonanoic acid, 3,5,5-trimethylhexanoic acid, decanoic acid, 2-propylheptanoic acid or dodecanoic acid, or their mixtures, preferably 2-ethylhexanoic acid. The liquid has an active oxygen content of 0-100 wt.ppm. Preferred Conditions: The liquid occupies 90-99% of the inner volume of the container. The liquid is kept in it for greater than or equal to 1 week.

PN DE102020122082-A1; WO2022043154-A1
TI Solid oxide fuel cell device for fuel cell vehicle, has recirculation line that branches off downstream of heat exchanger of stage, which opens again into storage line upstream of compressor, and which is assigned expansion valve to expand exhaust gas.
AU LUCAS C
AE AUDI AG (NSUM-C)
   VOLKSWAGEN AG (VOLS-C)
GA 202227634S
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has a solid oxide fuel cell (2) comprising a cathode, an anode and a solid electrolyte layer separating the cathode from the anode, in which the anode is provided with an anode supply line (4) for the supply of fuel and an anode exhaust line for the discharge of anode waste gas is fluidically connected, and in which the cathode is connected to a cathode gas supply (5). The anode exhaust gas line for storing carbon dioxide from the anode exhaust gas in a tank (6) is fluidically connected to a storage line (7). A stage (10) consists of a compressor (8) for compressing the anode exhaust gas and a heat exchanger (9) downstream of the compressor for cooling the anode exhaust gas compressed by the compressor is connected. A recirculation line (11) branches off downstream of the heat exchanger of the stage, which opens again into the storage line upstream of the compressor. The recirculation line is assigned an expansion valve (12) to expand the exhaust gas.
   USE - Solid oxide fuel cell device for fuel cell vehicle (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The temperature difference compared to the ambient temperature of the solid oxide fuel cell device or of the fuel cell vehicle encompassing has to be overcome so that sufficient condensation of liquid water is achieved. The fluid at the inlet of the compressor of the first stage is subject to increased saturation. The vapor pressure of the anode exhaust gas is reduced to an extent that water condenses in the anode exhaust gas, since the gas is cooled below ambient temperature. The improved energy utilization and improved protection of the compressor stages are achieved. The highest possible degree of conversion of the fuel is achieved. The heat is extracted from the exhaust gas and transferred to the fresh cathode gas, so that reliable operating temperatures are achieved within the solid oxide fuel cell. Reliable separation and safe storage of carbon dioxide in the tank are achieved by using a flushing valve. The fluid entering the first stage compressor is even more saturated. The optimization is achieved by integrating another heat exchanger. The higher degree of purity of carbon-di-oxide and leads to a lower risk of damage to a compressor. The solid oxide fuel cell device condenses and damage to one of a number of compressor stages is avoided. The proportion of H2O is reduced so drastically that compressor systems are not damaged and at the same time a large amount of carbon dioxide is stored in the tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of a first solid oxide fuel cell device.
   Solid oxide fuel cell device (1)
   Solid oxide fuel cell (2)
   Anode supply line (4)
   Cathode gas supply (5)
   Tank (6)
   Storage line (7)
   Compressor (8)
   Heat exchanger (9)
   Stage (10)
   Recirculation line (11)

PN CN113618075-A
TI Preparing ultra-low resistivity conductive slurry filled with high-purity nano-materials comprises e.g. adding oxalic acid into mixed aqueous solution of copper nitrate and silver nitrate, reacting, finishing reaction, filtering, and introducing argon gas to clean air in roasting furnace.
AU SUN H
AE SUN H (SUNH-Individual)
GA 2021D2007G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultra-low resistivity conductive slurry filled with high-purity nano-materials comprises (1) adding oxalic acid into the mixed aqueous solution of copper nitrate and silver nitrate, reacting, finishing reaction, and filtering to obtain the mixture of copper oxalate and silver oxalate, and (2) adding the mixture of copper oxalate and silver oxalate into roasting furnace, firstly introducing argon gas to clean the air in the roasting furnace, introducing mixed gas of argon gas and carbon monoxide, maintaining the atmosphere of argon gas and carbon monoxide, heating the roasting furnace, reacting at 250-400degrees Celsius, where the reaction process is as specified in the given equation, the reaction generates carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide to form gas explosion, cutting copper simple substance, silver simple substance into nano powder, reinforcing the air explosion effect to cut the copper simple substance into nano powder with smaller granularity.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ultra-low resistivity conductive slurry filled with high-purity nano-materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method significantly enhances gas explosion effect of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the roasting furnace, avoids generation of oxides, improves purity of the nano-material, reduces resistivity by 12.6-26.9 cm, is close to the theoretical resistivity of copper 1.75 cm, and has remarkable effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ultra-low resistivity conductive slurry filled with high-purity nano-materials comprises (1) adding oxalic acid into the mixed aqueous solution of copper nitrate and silver nitrate, reacting, finishing reaction, and filtering to obtain the mixture of copper oxalate and silver oxalate, (2) adding the mixture of copper oxalate and silver oxalate into roasting furnace, firstly introducing argon gas to clean the air in the roasting furnace, introducing mixed gas of argon gas and carbon monoxide, maintaining the atmosphere of argon gas and carbon monoxide, heating the roasting furnace, reacting at 250-400degrees Celsius, where the reaction process is as specified in the given equation, the reaction generates carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide to form gas explosion, cutting copper simple substance, silver simple substance into nano powder, reinforcing the air explosion effect to cut the copper simple substance into nano powder with smaller granularity, and (3) stop heating, continuously introducing argon gas, and mixed gas of carbon monoxide until the furnace temperature is reduced to not less than 40degrees Celsius, stop introducing argon gas and carbon monoxide, removing out the obtained nano copper powder, adding into ethanol solution with volume content of 1% oxalic acid, ultrasonically vibrating and dispersing uniformly, allowing to stand at normal temperature, filtering, drying the obtained solid nano copper powder in 60degrees Celsius vacuum thermostat, naturally cooling to not less than 40degrees Celsius, removing out the nano copper powder, vacuum sealing, packaging and storing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the adding amount of silver nitrate is 0.001-2 wt.% of the total amount of copper nitrate and silver nitrate. In the step (1), finishing the reaction, adjusting the pH of the reaction solution to 1.16-4.16 using aqueous ammonia, and filtering. In the step (2), the purity of the argon gas is 99.6-99.9%. In the step (2), introducing 90-99.9 vol.% argon gas and 0.1-10 vol.% carbon monoxide.

PN CN112919468-A
TI Recycling carbon dioxide by oxygen-enriched            combustion-supporting calcined limestone involves            crushing limestone, sieving, sending oversize material            to calcining furnace, and gasifying liquified natural            gas.
AU DOU M
   YANG J
   ZOU S
   LI H
   DENG F
   LI K
   HUANG F
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 202165879M
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling carbon dioxide by oxygen-enriched                combustion-supporting calcined limestone involves                crushing the limestone to a particle size of                100-150mm by a crusher, using the vibrating screen                pore of 50mm diameter for sieving; recycling the                undersize material as the raw material for making                bricks, sending the oversize material to the                calcining furnace to calcine; gasifying the                liquefied natural gas (LNG) by the gasification                device and mixing with the oxygen-enriched air                pipeline to be sent to the calcining furnace to                burn, calcining the oversize material in the                calcining furnance, crushing the generated unslaked                lime, after cooling, generating quicklime finished                product, discharging the flue gas by the calcining                furnace, firstly passing through a heat exchanger                for heat exchange, then passing through the spray                tower to intially cooling down and removing dust,                then sending into the filling tower by the fan;                where the filler in the filler tower is made of                molecular sieve.
   USE - Method for recycling carbondioxide by                oxygen-enriched combustion-supporting calcined                limestone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce high-quality                quicklime and liquid carbondioxide with a purity of                more than 98%, realizes room temperature                liquefaction and separation of carbondioxide gas                and other gasses produced by calcining limestone.                The system structure is simple, continuous,                convenient to operate, energy saving, and suitable                for scale continous production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling carbon dioxide by oxygen-enriched                combustion-supporting calcined limestone involves                crushing the limestone to a particle size of                100-150mm by a crusher, using the vibrating screen                pore of 50mm diameter for sieving; recycling the                undersize material as the raw material for making                bricks, sending the oversize material to the                calcining furnace to calcine; gasifying the                liquefied natural gas (LNG) by the gasification                device and mixing with the oxygen-enriched air                pipeline to be sent to the calcining furnace to                burn, calcining the oversize material in the                calcining furnance, crushing the generated unslaked                lime, after cooling, generating quicklime finished                product, discharging the flue gas by the calcining                furnace, firstly passing through a heat exchanger                for heat exchange, then passing through the spray                tower to intially cooling down and removing dust,                then sending into the filling tower by the fan;                where the filler in the filler tower is made of                molecular sieve to absorb the water vapor in the                flue gas, sending the gas from the filler tower to                the low temperature heat exchanger, reducing the                temperature of the gas to 14-16 degrees C, then                removing the redundant particulate matter by the                bag-type dust remover, finally compressing to                2.5MPa by the compressor, then multi-stage                compressing to 5.3MPa, generating liquid                carbondioxide, separating liquid carbondioxide,                recycling and storing by gas-liquid                separator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of method for                recycling carbondioxide by oxygen-enriched                combustion-supporting calcined limestone.(Drawing                includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Concentration: The oxygen content                  of the oxygen-enriched air is 40 % to 50 %.                  Preferred Component: The filler of the absorption                  tower is zeolite molecular sieve. Preferred                  Condition: The LNG is provided by a barrier                  explosion-proof skid-mounted LNG storage tank,                  and then it is sent to a gasification device for                  gasification.

PN CN112753757-A; CN112753757-B
TI Low-temperature sterilization and air-conditioning integrated fruit and vegetable preservation method by pretreating fresh fruits and vegetables and adding pretreated fresh fruits and vegetables into preservation device to preserve.
AU XIAO K
   WU Y
   ZHU L
   YIN Y
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   GUANGDONG HUAKAI MINGXIN TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202154024M
AB    NOVELTY - Low-temperature sterilization and air-conditioning integrated fruit and vegetable preservation method involves (a) pretreating fresh fruits and vegetables and (b) adding the pretreated fresh fruits and vegetables into a preservation device, which is equipped with manganese dioxide/bismuth ethylene removal membrane, plasma gas sterilizer and semiconductor refrigeration membrane, and sealed with a modified atmosphere membrane, using the ethylene produced by the manganese dioxide/ bismuth membrane to remove ethylene from the catalytic degradation of ethylene produced by fruits and vegetables, adjusting oxygen concentration and carbon dioxide concentration in preservation device through the modified atmosphere membrane, using a plasma gas-phase sterilizer to sterilize fresh fruits and vegetables, and using semiconductor refrigeration chips to reduce the temperature of the environment in the preservation device to preserve fresh fruits and vegetables.
   USE - The method is useful for low-temperature sterilization and air-conditioning integrated fruit and vegetable preservation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is safe, spontaneously reduces oxygen concentration and increases carbon dioxide concentration, catalyzes the decomposition of ethylene produced by fruits and vegetables, reduces breathing intensity of fruits and vegetables, promotes fruits and vegetables to reach a dormant state in a short period of time, prolongs shelf life of fruits and vegetables by 7-14 days, and ensures that the fruits and vegetables maintain a fresh and plump appearance, and has no phenomenon of microbial growth and rot.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Low-temperature sterilization and air-conditioning integrated fruit and vegetable preservation method involves (a) pretreating fresh fruits and vegetables and (b) adding the pretreated fresh fruits and vegetables into a preservation device, which is equipped with manganese dioxide/bismuth ethylene removal membrane, plasma gas sterilizer and semiconductor refrigeration membrane, and sealed with a modified atmosphere membrane, using the ethylene produced by the manganese dioxide/bismuth membrane to remove ethylene from the catalytic degradation of ethylene produced by fruits and vegetables, adjusting oxygen concentration and carbon dioxide concentration in preservation device through the modified atmosphere membrane, using a plasma gas-phase sterilizer to sterilize fresh fruits and vegetables, and using semiconductor refrigeration chips to reduce the temperature of the environment in the preservation device to preserve fresh fruits and vegetables. The manganese dioxide/bismuth ethylene removal membrane is prepared by dissolving manganese dioxide in 5 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution, uniformly mixing to obtain a manganese dioxide solution, weighing suitable amount of metallic bismuth, dissolving in a 2 mol/l nitric acid solution, diluting with water, dripping solution containing metallic bismuth to the manganese dioxide solution, where the molar ratio of manganese to bismuth is 0.05-2:1, stirring for 1-3 hours, washing the product with water, drying to obtain manganese dioxide/bismuth black powder and spraying the black powder evenly on the surface of the non-woven fabric with a spraying amount of 0.1-2 g/cm2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves rinsing fruits and vegetables with 100-300 mg/l edible hypochlorous acid solution for is 5-30 minutes and removing surface moisture. The step (b) involves adjusting oxygen concentration to 3-15% and carbon dioxide concentration to 2-20% using modified atmosphere membrane having thickness of 10-80 mu m. The step (b) involves using semiconductor refrigeration membrane to reduce the temperature of the packaging environment to 8-10 degrees C. In step (b), the casing of the preservation device is made of polypropylene or polyethylene terephthalate. In step (b), the input current of the plasma gas phase sterilizer is 20-70 mA, and the concentration of positive and negative ions is 2-5 million/cm3. In step (b), the preservation device is equipped with a charging power supply system, which includes two rechargeable batteries, each battery has an electric capacity of 6000 mA, which supplies power for the plasma gas phase sterilizer and the semiconductor refrigeration sheet, and can be used for 7-14 days after a single charge.

TI Transversely welding BB41-BF material hot blast            stove shell comprises grooving, adjusting, assembling            and fixing the upper and lower sections, arranging            alumina ceramic electric heaters, welding, cooling, and            non-destructive testing.
AU LI J
   WU B
   SHAO S
   WANG Y
   WEN B
AE SHANGHAI ERSHIYE CONSTR CO LTD (CMEG-C)
   CHINA MCC20 GROUP CORP LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 2020772892
AB    NOVELTY - Transversely welding BB41-BF material hot                blast stove shell comprises grooving, adjusting,                assembling and fixing the upper and lower sections                of the hot blast stove shell made of BB41-BF,                removing the rust, moisture and oil stains groove                surface and both sides, clamping the welding torch                of the carbon dioxide gas shielded welding                equipment on the automatic welding carriage,                arranging alumina ceramic electric heaters along                the groove, and preheating the weld, setting up a                welding carriage track, using MIG welding for                clockwise bottom welding, performing multi-layer                continuous filling and covering of the weld seam,                firstly welding the weld on the side with the                smaller groove angle, cleaning the root after                welding, then welding the weld on the side with                larger groove angle, using an angle grinder,                performing weld bottoming, continuous filling and                covering, insulating, cooling, and non-destructive                testing of welds.
   USE - The method is useful for transversely welding                BB41-BF material hot blast stove shell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can develop new welding process                for thick plates made of BB41-BF through automatic                welding trolleys and carbon dioxide gas shielded                welding equipment; greatly improves welding                efficiency, which has beautiful weld seam                appearance; and improves the first pass rate of                welds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Transversely welding BB41-BF material hot                blast stove shell comprises (a) grooving,                adjusting, assembling and fixing the upper and                lower sections of the hot blast stove shell made of                BB41-BF, removing the rust, moisture and oil stains                within 20 mm of the groove surface and both sides,                then checking and confirming, modifying the support                rod of the automatic welding machine clamping                welding torch to be longer and thicker, and                clamping the welding torch of the carbon dioxide                gas shielded welding equipment on the automatic                welding carriage, (b) arranging 600 mmx 180 mm                alumina ceramic electric heaters along the groove,                and preheating the weld at 80-130 degrees C, (c1)                setting up a welding carriage track of more than                500 mm above the weld for ensuring the welding gun                to swing down to the weld, (c2) using MIG welding                along the 0 degrees direction of the upper and                lower furnace shell groups for clockwise bottom                welding, (c3) performing multi-layer continuous                filling and covering of the weld seam by using an                erected automatic welding gun, and (c4) firstly                welding the weld on the side with the smaller                groove angle, cleaning the root after welding, then                welding the weld on the side with larger groove                angle, using an angle grinder for grinding the                whole circle along the bottom, then repeating step                (c3), continuing step (c4), performing weld                bottoming, continuous filling and covering, and (d)                arranging 600mmx 180mm alumina ceramic electric                heating plate along the weld immediately after                welding, covering with 30-50 mm thick asbestos,                insulating at 605-645 degrees C for 2.5-5 hours,                cooling to room temperature, and performing                non-destructive testing of welds.

PN US10486955-B1
TI Gas exhaust system used to collect and exhaust gases i.e. carbon dioxide from beverage fountain systems, comprises expelled gas receiver, component-receiver coupler, external-environment gas exhaust pathway, and directional restrictor.
AU GRIEGO M J
   DUFFY D T
   CRUZ R
AE ARROWHEAD SUPERIOR BEVERAGE LLC (ARRO-Non-standard)
GA 201999207Q
AB    NOVELTY - A gas exhaust system (100) comprises expelled gas receiver(s) (110) structured and arranged to receive the expelled gasses from multiple components of beverage dispensing apparatus(es), where such multiple components are situated within enclosed environment(s) (195) occupiable by a person(s); component-receiver coupler(s) structured and arranged to couple component(s) of such multiple components, which expels the expelled gasses, with expelled gas receiver(s), where component-receiver coupler comprises fluid pathway isolator(s); external-environment gas exhaust pathway(s) structured and arranged to exhaust to atmosphere the expelled gasses from the expelled gas receiver to an external unenclosed environment (190) external and adjacent to the enclosed environment; and directional restrictor(s) structured and arranged to restrict directional flow of the expelled gasses toward such external unenclosed environment and away from such multiple components.
   USE - The gas exhaust system is used for expelled gasses from components of beverage dispensing apparatuses (192) positioned in enclosed spaces. It is used to collect and exhaust gases, particularly carbon dioxide, from beverage fountain systems or fountain drink machines (193).
   ADVANTAGE - The system exhausts the carbon dioxide, from large component fountain drink machines, in a place other than the enclosed environments (i.e. building) in which they typically sit, prevents back-pressure of exhausted gas into components of the fountain system preventing harm to such components, and is efficient, inexpensive, and handy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A gas exhaust system (100) comprises expelled gas receiver(s) (110) structured and arranged to receive the expelled gasses from multiple components of beverage dispensing apparatus(es), where such multiple components are situate within enclosed environment(s) (195) occupiable by a person(s); component-receiver coupler(s) structured and arranged to couple component(s) of such multiple components, which expels the expelled gasses, with expelled gas receiver(s), where component-receiver coupler comprises fluid pathway isolator(s) structured and arranged to isolate a fluid pathway, between such a component(s) and expelled gas receiver(s), from such enclosed environment(s); external-environment gas exhaust pathway(s) structured and arranged to exhaust to atmosphere the expelled gasses from the expelled gas receiver to an external unenclosed environment (190) external and adjacent to the enclosed environment; and directional restrictor(s) structured and arranged to restrict directional flow of the expelled gasses toward such external unenclosed environment and away from such multiple components, where the expelled gasses from such multiple components of such beverage dispensing apparatus(es) are exhausted to such external unenclosed environment instead of such enclosed environment. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for gas exhaust method, relating to expelled gasses from components of beverage dispensing apparatuses positioned in enclosed spaces.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view illustrating the gas exhaust system.

PN CN109950521-A; CN109950521-B
TI Method for preparing tin-graphite composite electrode of lithium ion battery, involves preparing tin nanoparticle as active material, and graphite as matrix material, and mixing, followed by performed laser ultra-steady-state diffusion.
AU WANG C
   ZHANG X
   ZHAO Z
   GE L
   DONG X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201958259W
AB    NOVELTY - A tin-graphite composite electrode of lithium ion battery preparing method involves preparing tin nanoparticle as an active material, and graphite as a matrix material, mixing tin nanoparticles and graphite in a mass ratio of 1:1-1:4, vacuum ball milling, and carrying out the ball milling at a rotation speed of 100-300 rpm for 20-24 hours to obtain a mixed powder having a uniform composition, utilizing deionized water as solvent and sodium carboxymethyl cellulose as binder, preparing the ball-milled mixed powder and the binder into a paste to a mass ratio of 8:1, and coating it with a coating film having a thickness of 25-100 mu m on copper current collector, vacuum drying. The copper current collector of the coating film is placed in an inert gas protective cover of a numerical control machine tool, and subjected to laser ultra-non-steady-state diffusion treatment using a continuous carbon dioxide laser and performed laser ultra-steady-state diffusion to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing tin-graphite composite electrode of lithium ion battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing tin-graphite composite electrode of lithium ion battery with improved electrochemical performance, prolonged cycle life, better interface combination, high preparing efficiency, eco-friendly, in simple and convenient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A tin-graphite composite electrode of lithium ion battery preparing method involves preparing tin nanoparticle having a purity of 99.9% and a particle size of 80-200 nm as an active material, and graphite having a mass purity of 99.90% and a particle size of 1.0-10 nm as a matrix material, mixing tin nanoparticles and graphite in a mass ratio of 1:1-1:4, vacuum ball milling, and carrying out the ball milling at a rotation speed of 100-300 rpm for 20-24 hours to obtain a mixed powder having a uniform composition, utilizing deionized water as solvent and sodium carboxymethyl cellulose as binder, preparing the ball-milled mixed powder and the binder into a paste to a mass ratio of 8:1, and coating it with a coating film having a thickness of 25-100 mu m on copper current collector, vacuum drying at 80-95 degrees C for 20-24 hours. The copper current collector of the coating film is placed in an inert gas protective cover of a numerical control machine tool, and subjected to laser ultra-non-steady-state diffusion treatment using a continuous carbon dioxide laser and performed laser ultra-steady-state diffusion to obtain the finished product, where the process parameters of laser ultra-steady-state diffusion treatment is utilized 30-100W carbon dioxide laser power, spot diameter of 10-20 mm, scanning speed of 10-30 mm s-1, lap rate of 5-20%, inert gas flow of 2-4L min-1, and the inert gas time of 5-8 minutes.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded.
