PN CN109881144-A
TI Surface nitrocarburizing heat treating of steel useful for coal mine machinery bearing, comprises e.g. immersing the steel work piece into a rust removing cleaning liquid, ultrasonically treating, cooling, oiling, and completing the process.
AU CHEN C
   XIA H
AE ANHUI XINKUANG HYDRAULIC MACHINERY CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201955643K
AB    NOVELTY - Surface nitrocarburizing heat treating of steel comprises e.g. (i) immersing the steel work piece into a rust removing cleaning liquid, ultrasonically treating, washing and drying; (ii) placing the steel work piece in a preheating furnace, heating, and heat preserving; (iii) placing the steel work piece into the salt bath nitrocarburizing agent; (iv) cleaning and drying the steel work piece, placing into nitriding furnace, continuously introducing ammonia and carbon dioxide and reacting to obtain a second layer of nitriding carburizing film; and (v) introducing argon into the nitriding furnace, continuously heating to, heat preserving to obtain austenite layer, water quenching and rapidly cooling the work piece, heat preserving, and subjecting to isothermal aging treatment to obtain a bainite wear resistant layer, cooling, polishing, oiling, and completing the nitro carburizing heat treatment process.
   USE - The steel is useful for coal mine machinery bearing (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The steel has excellent wear resistance, corrosion resistance and fatigue resistance properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Surface nitrocarburizing heat treating of steel comprises (i) immersing the steel work piece into a rust removing cleaning liquid, ultrasonically treating for 10-15 minutes with double-frequency for removing surface oil and rust spots, washing with distilled water for 3 times, and drying at 120-130 degrees C; (ii) placing the steel work piece in a preheating furnace, heating to 340-380 degrees C with a heating rate of 15 degrees C/minutes, and heat preserving for 25-35 minutes and preheating uniformly; (iii) placing the steel work piece into the salt bath nitrocarburizing agent, controlling the salt bath temperature at 450-500 degrees C for 40-50 minutes forming a layer of nitriding carburizing film in situ on the surface of the work piece, where the salt bath nitrocarburizing agent comprises 25-45 wt.% urea, 25-35 wt.% sodium glycinate, 16-20 wt.% potassium cyanate, 11-15 wt.% sodium carbonate, 1-2 wt.% potassium fluoride and 2-3 wt.% thiourea; (iv) cleaning and drying the steel work piece after the salt bath, placing into a nitriding furnace, controlling the temperature of the nitriding furnace at 500-550 degrees C, continuously introducing ammonia and carbon dioxide with a flow rate of 6-12 m3/hour and 0.5-2 m3/hour, and reacting for 2.5-3 hours to obtain a second layer of nitriding carburizing film; and (v) introducing argon into the nitriding furnace, exhausting the reaction of residual ammonia and carbon dioxide, continuously heating the nitriding furnace to 650-680 degrees C, heat preserving for 2-3 hours to obtain austenite layer, water quenching and rapidly cooling the work piece to 160-210 degrees C, heat preserving for 6-14 hours, and subjecting to isothermal aging treatment to obtain a bainite wear resistant layer, cooling, polishing, oiling, and completing the nitro carburizing heat treatment process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The rust removing cleaning liquid comprises 3-6 wt.% formic acid, 2-4 wt.% tannic acid, 4-6 wt.% cocoyl glutamic acid, 0.5-1 wt.% sodium dodecyl sulfate, 1-2 wt.% hexamethylenetetramine, and water (remaining amount). Preferred Method: The frequency of double-frequency ultrasonic treatment is 20-25 KHz and 60-70 KHz.
DC M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).)
MC M13-D02B; M13-L

PN CN109588208-A; CN109588208-B
TI Preparing biological porous material comprises e.g. mixing auxiliary material, and modified starch, soaking main material in lime water, sealing, sterilizing, inoculating, culturing, preparing starch liquid, inactivating, and drying.
AU WEI J
   LU D
   ZHAO Y
   CHEN J
   ZHANG W
AE INST BIOLOGY GANSU ACAD SCI (BIOL-Non-standard)
GA 201935114L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biological porous material comprises e.g. mixing rice bran or bran with corn flour, wheat flour, and Sorghum flour as the auxiliary material, and modified starch in the ratio of 1:1-1:3, soaking main material in 1% lime water for 8 hours, washing, drying, pulverizing, mixing with auxiliary materials, where the amount of main material is 70-90%, and the auxiliary material is 10-30%, adding water to form medium with water content of 60%, placing medium in ventilated culture bag, sealing, sterilizing, cooling to below 30 degrees C, inoculating 10% Basidiomycota, culturing at 25-27 degrees C under 60-70% humid condition with below 800 ppm carbon dioxide content in dark light for 5-10 days to obtain a culture, preparing starch liquid containing 10-30% starch, 1-10% rice bran, 2% is, and 50-80% sterile water, and mixing 85-95 wt.% culture with 5-15 wt.% starch solution to obtain mycelium mixture having water content of 50-60%.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing biological porous material.
   ADVANTAGE - The biological porous material has latticed structure, good insulativity, and flame resistance property; and is safe, environmentally friendly and biodegradable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing biological porous material comprises (i) mixing rice bran or bran with corn flour, wheat flour, and Sorghum flour as the auxiliary material, and modified starch in the ratio of 1:1-1:3, soaking main material in 1% lime water for 8 hours, washing, drying, pulverizing, mixing with auxiliary materials, where the amount of main material is 70-90%, and auxiliary material is 10-30%, and adding water to form medium with water content of 60%, (ii) placing medium in ventilated culture bag, sealing, sterilizing, cooling to below 30 degrees C, inoculating 10% Basidiomycota, and culturing at 25-27 degrees C under 60-70% humid condition with below 800 ppm carbon dioxide content in dark light for 5-10 days to obtain a culture, (iii) preparing starch liquid containing 10-30% starch, 1-10% rice bran, 2% is, and 50-80% sterile water, (iv) mixing 85-95 wt.% culture with 5-15 wt.% starch solution to obtain mycelium mixture having water content of 50-60%, molding mycelium mixture into three-dimensional structure, placing in sterile culture chamber, and culturing in for dark light at 22-25 degrees C under 50-60% humid environment and below 800 ppm carbon dioxide concentration, and ending when the surface of the mycelial culture is completely white, and (v) inactivating mycelial culture, drying until moisture content is less than 15% to obtain biological porous material. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for biological porous material prepared by above mentioned method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flow diagram of the preparing biological porous material (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The main material is agricultural and forestry waste e.g. straw, wood chips, cotton husks, waste paper, waste cotton, and/or household waste such e.g. waste wood. The grain size of main material is 0.5-1 cm. The mass ratio of auxiliary material, and modified starch is 1:1.5. The Basidiomycota is oyster mushroom, shiitake mushroom, Ganoderma lucidum or wood ear. The fungus strain is kernel strain or liquid strain. The starch liquid comprises 20 wt.% Sorghum powder, and 3 wt.% rice bran. Preferred Method: In the step (iv), adding glass particles, leather chips, and natural polymer resins in mixture of the medium mycelium. In the process, inactivating mycelium culture by heating or performing ultraviolet irradiation.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PN CN108191730-A
TI Preparing high-purity lutein crystals from            marigold extract comprises e.g. adding marigold extract            to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank,            extracting, purifying, adding saponified marigold            extract liquid into saponification pot.
AU ZHANG W
AE HANDAN MEIKANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HAND-Non-standard)
GA 201851441V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity lutein crystals from                marigold extract comprises e.g. (i) adding marigold                extract to the supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction tank, extracting and purifying,                extracting at the pressure of 30Mpa, at temperature                of 40 degrees C, the extracting time is 0.5-1 hour                to obtain the high purity marigold extract; (ii)                using the resulting high purity saponified marigold                extract liquid I placing into the saponification                pot, the mass ratio of high purity saponified                marigold extract liquid I is 1:1.5, then reaction                temperature in the saponification tank is 65-70                degrees C, completely stirring at speed of 500                revolutions per minute (rpm) to obtain the                unsaponifiable matter; and (iii) adding the                resulting saponified product I into the stainless                steel tank, then adding softened water of 10-12                times of total mass, then mixing the softened water                and saponified marigold extract product I, and then                stirring for 3 hours to wash, stirring, and adding                mixed solution into centrifuge for                separating.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity                lutein crystals from marigold extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively improve the lutein                ester to free lutein conversion and inhibit                oxidation and isomerization of xanthin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-purity lutein crystals from                marigold extract comprises (i) adding marigold                extract to the supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction tank, extracting and purifying,                extracting at the pressure of 30Mpa, at temperature                of 40 degrees C, the extracting time is 0.5-1 hour                to obtain the high purity marigold extract; (ii)                using the resulting high purity saponified marigold                extract liquid I placing into the saponification                pot, the mass ratio of high purity saponified                marigold extract liquid I is 1:1.5, then reaction                temperature in the saponification tank is 65-70                degrees C, completely stirring at speed of 500                revolutions per minute (rpm) to obtain the                unsaponifiable matter; (iii) adding the resulting                saponified product I into the stainless steel tank,                then adding softened water of 10-12 times of total                mass, then mixing the softened water and saponified                marigold extract product I, and then stirring for 3                hours to wash, stirring at speed of 300 rpm, at                last, adding the mixed solution into the centrifuge                for separating for many times, until there is no                liquid, collecting the obtain material II; (iv)                adding the resulting material II into the stainless                steel tank, adding absolute ethanol, mass of the                anhydrous ethanol is 5-6 times of the quality of                material II, stopping stirring after stirring to                mix uniformly; the temperature is at 5-15 degrees                C, and then allowing to stand for crystallization                for 12 hours; (v) placing the crystal liquid into                centrifuge for solid-liquid separation to obtain a                xanthophyll crystal, and (vi) scraping the                resulting lutein crystal in the stainless steel                pan, dispersing uniformly, and then drying in a                vacuum drying oven to obtain a finished product of                lutein crystal
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the                  saponification time is 4 hours. In the step                  (iii), the centrifuging is an upper discharging                  bag centrifuge, and the rotation speed is 3000                  rpm. In the step (v), the centrifuge is high                  speed tube centrifuge, and rotating speed is                  10000 rpm. In the step (vi), the vacuum drying                  box, the vacuum drying condition is: tank                  temperature is 40 degrees C, the vacuum degree is                  0.09 MPa, and the drying time is 12-24 hours.                  Preferred Components: In the step (i), the total                  content of carotenoids in the marigold extract is                  10-18 wt.%. In the step (ii), the saponification                  solution comprises potassium hydroxide,                  demineralized water, propylene glycol, and                  ethanol, according to mass ratio of                  1:0.5:4:1.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC E10-E04C2; E11-Q01C; E12-A01

PN CN108165526-A
TI Extracting and cultivating human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells by enzymatic hydrolysis, comprises e.g. taking normal maternal of normal delivery or caesarean section umbilical cord tissue, and cutting into tissue homogenate.
AU YAO L
   MENG D
   YANG M
   SONG D
   LIU D
AE BEIJING BIOTECHNOLOGY BIOMEDICAL GENE (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201849461X
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting and cultivating human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells by enzymatic hydrolysis, comprises e.g. taking normal maternal 39-40 weeks of normal delivery or caesarean section umbilical cord tissue, separating of Huatong's gum from umbilical cord tissue, placing Huatong's glue in a petri dish, cutting into tissue homogenate, transferring to a centrifuge tube, adding pre-warmed 0.1% collagenase I to a carbon dioxide incubator at a ratio of 1:1 by volume, constant temperature enzymatic hydrolysis at 37 degrees C, zymohydrolysis at constant temperature once every 20 minutes violent vibration, enzymolysis for 2 hours, stopping enzymolysis, extracting the enzymolysis solution with 400-mesh cell sieve to filter, adding phosphate buffered saline heavy suspension cells in the filtrate 300G, centrifuging for 5 minutes, removing the supernatant, re-suspending cell and centrifugally washing with phosphate buffered saline again, and re-suspending the cells.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting and cultivating human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells by enzymatic hydrolysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the damage of the cell and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting and cultivating human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells by enzymatic hydrolysis, comprises (i) taking normal maternal 39-40 weeks of normal delivery or caesarean section umbilical cord tissue, (ii) separating of Huatong's gum from umbilical cord tissue, (iii) placing Huatong's glue in a petri dish, cutting into tissue homogenate, transferring to a centrifuge tube, adding pre-warmed 0.1% collagenase I to a carbon dioxide incubator at a ratio of 1:1 by volume, constant temperature enzymatic hydrolysis at 37 degrees C, zymohydrolysis at constant temperature once every 20 minutes violent vibration, enzymolysis for 2 hours, enzymolysis effect observed when the tissue block has been enzyme into paste injector for stopping enzymolysis, extracting the enzymolysis solution with 400-mesh cell sieve to filter, adding phosphate buffered saline heavy suspension cells in the filtrate 300G, centrifuging for 5 minutes, removing the supernatant, re-suspending cell and centrifugally washing with phosphate buffered saline again, re-suspending the cells and transferred to a T75 culture flask for cell culture solution, (iv) placing the UCMSC T75 culture flask in a carbon dioxide culture box, culturing temperature at 37 degrees C, observing the growth condition of the UCMSC when the cell fusion degree reaches 80%, performing a P0-P1 operation, and (v) using pipette to remove T75 cell culture liquid in the culture bottle, washing two times with phosphate buffered saline, culturing to T75 proteolytic enzyme solution to a 4 times dilution of 2 ml, digesting for 1-2 minutes at 1000 revolutions/minutes for 5 minutes, collecting cells, removing the supernatant, D-phosphate buffered saline for cell collection 1000 revolutions/minutes for 5 minutes, washing once, removing supernatant by passage culture liquid re-suspending and passage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), storing the method of the umbilical cord tissue is: under the condition of operating room clean, placing the umbilical cord tissue into tissue protective liquid pre-filling with 100 ml T75 culture bottle, sealing, storing at 2-8 degrees C. In the step (iv), during the culture: 1 day before the culture, allowing to stand, do not shake the T75 flask, allowing the single cells fully adhere to the wall, starting the third day of culture, half a half change every 2 days. Preferred Components: The composition of the tissue protection solution is: 2500 IU heparin, 16000 IU gentamicin, and 100 ml 0.9% physiological saline. In the step (iii), step (iv), and step (v), the components of the cell culture solution used were: 475 ml alpha - minimum essential medium (MEM) liquid medium, 25 ml UltraGRO cell nutrient supplement, 1000 IU of heparin sodium, and 40,000 IU gentamicin. In the step (v), the passage density was 10,000-15,000 cells/cm2.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN108102829-A
TI Preparing raspberry wine by soaking grape in carbon dioxide, adding yeast, fermenting mixture into original grape wine, extracting raspberry fruit, adding original grape wine, adding attapulgite for clarification treatment and storing wine.
AU ZHAO X
AE UNIV TAISHAN (UYTA-Non-standard)
GA 201844982X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing raspberry wine involves soaking the grape in a pressure environment filled with carbon dioxide, adding yeast, fermenting the mixture, heating, adding lactic acid bacteria, fermenting the mixture, adding sulfur dioxide to terminate the fermentation to obtain the original grape wine, washing the picked raspberry fruit, adding deionized water to the raspberry fruit, extracting, filtering, concentrating the filtrate under reduced pressure, adding the extract to ethanol, leaving, then filtering the extract, collecting the filtrate, concentrating, adding the extract to the original grape wine, uniformly stirring the mixture, adding attapulgite for clarification treatment, storing the wine, continuously refrigerating wine and then filtering the wine.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing raspberry wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves taste, aroma and healthcare value of the wine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing raspberry wine involves (a) collecting fresh and mature grape with no mechanical damage and pests and diseases and manually sorting, where prohibit spraying pesticides one month before harvesting, (b) soaking the grape in a pressure environment filled with carbon dioxide for 5-8 days at 10-15 degrees C and 2.5-2.8 bar in a soaking tank, (c) transferring the soaking liquid into a fermentation tank, adding 30 mg/l yeast and fermenting the mixture at 15-18 degrees C until the residual sugar is less than 4 g/l, (d) transferring the fermented wine into a malic acid fermentation tank, heating at 18-20 degrees C, adding 10 g/T lactic acid bacteria, fermenting the mixture at 18-20 degrees C, performing real-time monitoring, when the malic acid content is reduced to zero, adding 45 mg/l sulfur dioxide to terminate the fermentation to obtain the original grape wine, (e) washing the picked raspberry fruit, adding 8-10 times deionized water to the raspberry fruit, extracting 3 times, first extracting for 2 hours, again extracting for 2.5 hours and again extracting for 1.5 hours, filtering the extract (each time), mixing the filtrates and concentrating the filtrate under reduced pressure into an extract, (f) adding the extract obtained in the step (e) to 95-98% ethanol, leaving overnight, then filtering the extract, collecting the filtrate and concentrating under reduced pressure into an extract, (g) adding the extract obtained in step (f) to the original grape wine obtained in step (d) and uniformly stirring the mixture under sterile conditions to obtain a mixed solution, (h) adding 0.4 g/l attapulgite to the mixed solution obtained in step (g) and subjecting to clarification treatment at 15-18 degrees C for 8-10 hours, (i) storing the wine obtained in step (h) at 14-18 degrees C and maintaining the free sulfur dioxide at 20-40 mg/l for 10 months and (j) continuously refrigerating wine at -4 degrees C to 0 degrees C for 1-4 months and then filtering the wine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (c) involves adding the yeast to 10 times 30-35 degrees C warm water, evenly stirring, leaving for 10-20 minutes, filtering, cooling to 16-25 degrees C, adding into the fermentation tank for fermentation, stopping fermentation once in every 8 hours, where the circulation amount is 30% the tank capacity and opening the cycle once in every 24 hours, where the circulation amount is 30-50% the tank capacity. The step (i) involves subjecting to infusion 4 times during storage, first subjecting to infusion for 1 month, again subjecting to infusion for 1 month, then subjecting to infusion for 3 months and finally subjecting to infusion for 5 months. In the method, the amount of raspberry fruit required for every 500 ml grape wine is 200-300 g.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-E
IP C12G-001/00; C12G-001/022

PN CN114888063-A
TI Repairing polluted soil around buried sewage            pipeline used in e.g. municipal engineering, by            backfilling fluid-state remediation polluted soil            prepared using remediation agent and soil around            pipeline, inserting blind pipe for injecting carbon            dioxide into soil and applying concrete structure            layer.
AU TAO J
   BAI L
   LI M
   LI J
   ZHANG M
   LIU W
   YIN Z
   WANG H
   FENG Y
   WANG S
AE JIANGSU PROVINCIAL ACAD ENVIRONMENTAL SC (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022A7641Q
AB    NOVELTY - In-situ repairing method of polluted soil                around buried sewage pipeline involves: (1)                excavating the polluted soil around pipeline, and                screening construction waste or gravel debris with                larger than 10 cm; (2) determining the proportion                of remediation agent and water added to the                excavated polluted soil; (3) fully stirring the                remediation agent and water determined in step (2)                and the polluted soil treated in step (1) to obtain                fluid-state remediation polluted soil; (4)                backfilling the polluted soil around the pipeline                after excavation; (5) inserting a blind pipe for                injecting carbon dioxide into the polluted soil;                (6) introducing carbon dioxide into the blind pipe;                (7) filling the blind pipe with a hydraulic filling                material; (8) paving a geomembrane on the surface                layer of the rapidly carbonized backfill and                solidified fluid remediation polluted soil; and (9)                applying road or concrete structure layer after the                upper layer of geomembrane is covered with                soil.
   USE - In-situ repairing method of polluted soil                around buried sewage pipeline, which is industrial                park sewage pipeline or urban sewage pipeline (all                claimed) used in geotechnical engineering and                municipal engineering.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can fully achieve the rapid                restoration and safe reuse of the polluted soil in                the irregular area. The effect of heavy metal                fixation and organic substance removal in the                fluidized solidified soil is further enhanced                through the rapid carbonization, and the strength                of the backfilled fluidized solidified soil is                rapidly increased. The method effectively fills                pipeline in the factory building corner, special                area around the production workshop device base,                and avoids the influence of construction                disturbance caused by mechanical compaction on the                production activity. The repairing polluted soil                reduces the usage amount of traditional filler such                as sand, and avoids the pollution of the potential                environmental risk in the soil                transportation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - In-situ repairing method of polluted soil                around buried sewage pipeline involves: (1)                excavating the polluted soil around pipeline, and                screening construction waste or gravel debris with                larger than 10 cm; (2) determining the proportion                of remediation agent and water added to the                excavated polluted soil; (3) fully stirring the                remediation agent and water determined in step (2)                and the polluted soil treated in step (1) using a                stirrer to obtain fluid-state remediation polluted                soil; (4) backfilling the fluid-state remediation                polluted soil around the pipeline after excavation                in step (1); (5) inserting a blind pipe for                injecting carbon dioxide into the polluted soil;                (6) introducing carbon dioxide into the blind pipe;                (7) filling the blind pipe with a hydraulic filling                material, and cutting the blind pipe that is higher                than the backfill fluid state to repair the                polluted soil; (8) paving a geomembrane on the                surface layer of the rapidly carbonized backfill                and solidified fluid remediation polluted soil; and                (9) applying road or concrete structure layer after                the upper layer of geomembrane is covered with                soil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The polluted soil is                  soil polluted by pollutants, which are heavy                  metal pollutants, organic pollutants or                  pollutants compounded by heavy metals and organic                  substances.Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  heavy metal pollutants is 4000-10000 mg/kg. The                  concentration of the organic pollutant is                  10000-15000 mg/kg. The concentration of heavy                  metal pollutants and organic pollutants is                  4000-10000 mg/kg and 10000-15000 mg/kg                  respectively.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heavy metal is                  lead, zinc, nickel, cadmium, and/or copper.Preferred Composition: The addition amount                  of water ensures that the slump of the                  fluid-state remediation polluted soil obtained in                  step (3) is 100-200 mm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic substance                  in the organic pollutants is petroleum                  hydrocarbon, chlorinated hydrocarbon, and/or                  benzene. The pollutants compounded by the heavy                  metals and organic compounds comprises lead,                  zinc, nickel, cadmium or copper, and one of                  petroleum hydrocarbon, chlorinated hydrocarbon,                  and benzene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The remediation agent                  is divided into type-I remediation agent and                  type-II remediation agent. The type-I remediation                  agent is cement, lime, steel slag, and/or slag.                  The type-II remediation agent is a mixture of                  type-I remediation agent and sodium persulfate in                  a mass ratio of 6:1-1:3.Preferred Composition: The usage amount of                  the type-I remediation agent is 1-25% of the dry                  weight of the polluted soil. The total usage                  amount of type-I remediation agent and sodium                  persulfate is 1-25% of the dry weight of the                  polluted soil.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

PN IN202131046789-A
TI Polymerizing monomer derived from carbon dioxide            to obtain polymer involves using cationic and acyclic            diene metathesis reactions.
AU KATIYAR V
   MULCHANDANI N M
   KIMURA Y
   SAKURAI S
   MASUTANI K
AE INDIAN TECHNOLOGY INST (INTE-Non-standard)
GA 2022A3369L
AB    NOVELTY - Process for polymerizing a monomer derived                from carbon dioxide to obtain a polymer involves                using cationic and acyclic diene metathesis (ADMET)                reactions.
   USE - The process is used for polymerizing a monomer                derived from carbon dioxide to obtain a                polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides a reaction scheme for                reacting &#945; -ethylidene-&#948;                -vinylvalerolactone (EVV) by ADMET, and the                reaction of comonomers by employing                trifluoromethanesulfonic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method                involves synthesizing the monomer by reacting 1,3                butadiene with carbon dioxide by a process                comprising: (a) charging palladium (II)                acetylacetonate catalyst along with a co-catalyst                in an autoclave, where the co-catalyst is chosen                from triphenylphosphine and/or tetrabutylammonium                acetate, (b) evacuating the autoclave at a pressure                of 10-50 Pa for 2-5 minutes followed by addition of                a solvent, (c) cooling the autoclave to a                temperature of -20 to -40&#176; C followed by                addition of 1,3-butadiene, (d) adding carbon                dioxide into the autoclave at a pressure of 1-7 MPa                for reacting with 1,3 butadiene at a temperature of                70-85&#176; C for 16-24 hours to obtain the                monomer. The acyclic diene metathesis (ADMET)                reaction involves charging the catalyst in a flask                followed by drying over vacuum followed by addition                of a solvent to obtain a solvent-catalyst mixture,                where the solvent is chosen from trichloromethane,                1,4-dioxane, benzene or its derivatives, more                specifically the solvent used is toluene, (b)                feeding the monomer into the solvent-catalyst                mixture in a reaction tube and heating the tube at                a temperature of 60-120&#176; C for 1-15 days                followed by purification to obtain the polymer. The                cationic reaction involves charging the monomer                along with a solvent in a flask followed by                stirring, and adding the catalyst either in                presence or absence of an alcohol into the flask                and heating the flask at a temperature of                60-180&#176; C for 0.1 to 24 hours followed by                purification to obtain the polymer. Preferred                Conditions: The cationic reaction is performed at a                temperature of 60-180&#176; C for 0.1-24 hours.                The cationic reaction is conducted in solution or                bulk system, where the polymer is isolated by                solvent such as diethyl ether, where partial vinyl                polymerization is obtained in diethyl ether soluble                product and preferential vinyl polymerization is                obtained in diethyl ether insoluble part. ORGANIC                CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent in                step (b) is acetonitrile. The ADMET reaction                employs a catalyst chosen from Grubb's catalyst and                Hoveyda Grubbs catalyst, where the Grubb's catalyst                is chosen from Grubbs I, II and III generation                catalysts, and the Hoveyda Grubb's catalyst is                chosen from Hoveyda Grubbs I and II generation                catalysts. The cationic reaction employs a                trifluoromethanesulfonic acid as a catalyst. The                polar solvent in step (a) is chosen from                chloroform, nitromethane, nitrobenzene, toluene,                tetrahydrofuran, benzene, 1,4-dioxane, and/or                p-dioxanone.
TF EXAMPLE - The example describes the general preparation                of a monomer derived from carbon dioxide that does                not support the claims.

TI Carbon neutralization-based microbial culture device, has feeding pipe arranged on movable block, and support mechanism arranged inside protective box to support and fix multiple breeding boxes placed inside protective box.
AU BI X
   TANG M
   DONG Y
   LI S
AE SHENYANG INST ENG (SYIE-C)
GA 2022421164
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a support plate (3) whose side wall is fixedly provided with a breeding box (2) for culturing microorganisms. Multiple biological supplementary lights (6) are arranged inside the protective box (1). A suction bottle (14) for absorbing carbon dioxide is provided outside the protective box. A Stirring cylinder (22) is arranged on the side wall of the protective box. The bottom end of the stirring cylinder is provided with a feeding pipe (20) that communicates with the interior of the protective box. The inner side wall of the protective box is provided with a movable block (19). The feeding pipe (20) is arranged on the movable block. An air intake mechanism is installed outside the protective box for ventilating the microorganisms inside the breeding box. The support mechanism is arranged inside the protective box to support and fix multiple breeding boxes placed inside the protective box.
   USE - Carbon neutralization-based microbial culture device used in process of cultivating microorganisms under suitable environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The microbial culture device based on carbon neutrality ventilatesthe microorganisms inside the culture box through the intake fan, and the suction bottle absorbs the carbon dioxide produced by the microorganism. The carbon-neutral microbial culture device drives the intake fan and the vertical rod to rotate at the same time through the dual-shaft motor, and the intake fan ventilates the microorganisms inside the breeding box. The stirring rod outside the vertical rod stirs the nutrient solution used for microbial cultivation, which speeds up the preparation of the nutrient solution, and the suction bottle absorbs the carbon dioxide produced by the microorganism, offsets the carbon dioxide produced by the microorganism itself, and realizes zero emission of carbon dioxide. The magnet block can drive the movable block and the feeding pipe to move up and down through the magnetic field, so that the nutrient solution is added to multiple breeding boxes in turn, which is convenient to use and effectively avoids the contamination of the culture medium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of using a carbon neutralization-based microbial culture device.

PN CN111607443-A; CN111607443-B
TI Biogas purification using microalgae to solidify and adsorb carbon dioxide comprises e.g. passing biogas in filter tower for preliminary filtration, setting filter screen, passing biogas, mixing microalgae liquid, and culturing.
AU NIU X
   LV M
   ZHANG L
   GUO H
   TANG Z
   LIU M
   LI X
AE UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEMICAL TECHNOLOG (UNGP-C)
   UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEM TECHNOLOGY (UNGP-C)
GA 202090127K
AB    NOVELTY - Biogas purification using microalgae to solidify and adsorb carbon dioxide comprises e.g. passing biogas into the filter tower for preliminary filtration, setting filter screen in the filter tower to 200-400 mesh, passing biogas processed into drying tower, adopting use of attapulgite biogas desiccant in the drying tower to absorb the moisture in the biogas, heating biogas to 20-35 degrees C, passing into the desulfurization tower, adopting use of desulfurization solution sprayed inside the desulfurization tower to contact the biogas to achieve biogas desulfurization, where the desulfurization solution is 25 vol.% sodium carbonate solution, the injection volume of the desulfurization solution is 1-3 t/hour, inoculating microalgae species into the culture solution to obtain a microalgae solution, mixing microalgae liquid and culture liquid in the volume ratio of 1:8-1:25 to obtain a mixed liquid, and passing mixed solution into a photobioreactor for culturing.
   USE - The method is useful for biogas purification using microalgae to solidify and adsorb carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high efficiency of biogas extraction and purification; and is suitable for mass production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Biogas purification using microalgae to solidify and adsorb carbon dioxide comprises (1) passing biogas into the filter tower for preliminary filtration, setting filter screen in the filter tower to 200-400 mesh, passing biogas processed into drying tower, and adopting use of attapulgite biogas desiccant in the drying tower to absorb the moisture in the biogas, (2) heating biogas to 20-35 degrees C, passing into the desulfurization tower, adopting use of desulfurization solution sprayed inside the desulfurization tower to contact the biogas to achieve biogas desulfurization, where the desulfurization solution is 25 vol.% sodium carbonate solution, and the injection volume of the desulfurization solution is 1-3 t/hour, (3) inoculating microalgae species into the culture solution to obtain a microalgae solution, mixing microalgae liquid and culture liquid in the volume ratio of 1:8-1:25 to obtain a mixed liquid, passing mixed solution into a photobioreactor for culturing for 3-8 days, where the culture solution includes citric acid solution, sodium nitrate solution and potassium phosphate solution in the volume ratio of 1:20:45, the light intensity of the photobioreactor is 1500-3500 Lux, introducing biogas from the bottom of the photobioreactor, controlling the light intensity of the photobioreactor to 2600-6900 Lux and the temperature to 10-30 degrees C, adopting use of photosynthesis of microalgae in the photobioreactor to solidify and adsorb carbon dioxide in the biogas, and (4) introducing biogas into the treatment tube at flow rate of 3-5 m/second to obtain pure biogas, where the processing tube is a horizontal tube filled with adsorbing desiccant, the adsorption desiccant is formed by mixing activated alumina particles and activated carbon particles in a volume ratio of 1:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (2) specifically comprises heating treated biogas to 30-35 degrees C, introducing from bottom of the desulfurization tower with an air flow of 2-5 m3/hour, flowing biogas from bottom to top inside the desulfurization tower and overflowing from the top of the desulfurization tower, contacting with the desulfurization solution sprayed inside the desulfurization tower to achieve desulfurization, passing the biogas processed into the desulfurization dehumidifier, spray drying calcium oxide powder into the desulfurization dehumidifier, mixing biogas with dry calcium oxide powder, absorbing water in the biogas by calcium oxide to form calcium hydroxide, and removing residual sulfide in the biogas by calcium hydroxide. In the step (3), placing microalgae liquid in the SE culture medium, culturing in light incubator for 3-8 days, selecting dark green monoclonal algae colonies for culturing, during culturing, introducing 10-30 vol.% carbon dioxide gas, screening high-quality algae species by comparing absorbance values, where the SE medium includes 15-45 pts. wt. sodium chloride, 8-15 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 6-24 pts. wt. iron (III) chloride hexahydrate, and 15-36 pts. wt. distilled water. The mutagenesis and culturing of microalgae specifically includes mixing microalgae seeds into a culture solution rich in trace elements, where the number of cells are at least 8-15 cells/ml, under sterile conditions, adopting use of X-rays to mutagenize microalgae, where the lethality rate is 50-75%, and the mutagenic radiation time is 50-120 seconds. In the step (2), placing desulfurization solution under normal temperature and pressure conditions, passing through activated carbon adsorption column at space velocity of 0.5-1.6/hour and under the conditions of 20-45 degrees C and space velocity of 0.5-3/hour, the anion exchange resin layer removes sulfate ions contained in the desulfurization solution. Preferred Components: The microalgae is Isochrysis, Phaeodactylum tricornutum, or Thalassiosira weissflogii. In the step (2), placing desulfurization solution at 20-45 degrees C under space velocity of 0.5-3/hour, passing through the anion exchange resin layer, and passing through the activated carbon adsorption column at a space velocity of 0.5-1.6/hour under normal temperature and pressure conditions.

PN CN109809541-A; CN109809541-B
TI Preparing polyaluminum ferric silicate flocculant by pulverizing coal gangue, fly ash and red mud, mixing with industrial soda ash, calcining, passing carbon dioxide-rich gas, mixing residue and calcium aluminate and reacting mixture.
AU SONG L
   YU Y
   WANG S
AE UNIV LINYI (UNLY-C)
GA 201949494A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing polyaluminum ferric silicate flocculant involves (a) pulverizing one or more of coal gangue, fly ash and red mud, (b) mixing the mixed raw material with industrial soda ash, (c) calcining in an oxygen-enriched air environment, and obtaining clinker and carbon dioxide rich gas, (d) adding the calcined clinker to 3 times water, dipping the filtrate in next batch of calcined clinker, circulating for 3-10 times, and finally obtaining filtrate A and filter residue B, (e) passing carbon dioxide-rich gas to filtrate A for carbonation reaction at 0.35 MPa and 40 degrees C and obtaining filtrate C and filter residue D, and (f) mixing filter residue B, D and calcium aluminate, crushing the mixture, adding 3-10% industrial hydrochloric acid, reacting, allowing to stand for 2 hours, obtaining a solution F, slowly adding the solution F to filtrate C, stirring, reacting, and allowing to stand for 6-12 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polyaluminum ferric silicate flocculant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective, saves resource, avoids environment pollution, and consumes less energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing polyaluminum ferric silicate flocculant involves (a) pulverizing one or more of coal gangue, fly ash and red mud to 100 mesh or more as mixed raw material by a ball mill, where ratio of silicon/aluminum/iron in mixed raw material is 5-30/20-50/10-40, (b) mixing the mixed raw material with industrial soda ash to prepare a raw meal, where weight ratio of soda ash to mixed raw material is 1:(1-3), (c) calcining in an oxygen-enriched air environment at 700-950 degrees C for 1-3 hours, and obtaining clinker and carbon dioxide rich gas, (d) adding the calcined clinker to 3 times water, continuously stirring at 90 degrees C for 2 hours, after solid-liquid separation, dipping the filtrate in next batch of calcined clinker, continuously stirring at 90 degrees C for 2 hours, circulating for 3-10 times, and finally obtaining filtrate A and filter residue B, (e) passing carbon dioxide-rich gas to filtrate A for carbonation reaction at 0.35 MPa and 40 degrees C, stirring uniformly for 1 hour, and obtaining filtrate C and filter residue D, and (f) mixing filter residue B, D and calcium aluminate, crushing the mixture, adding 3-10% industrial hydrochloric acid, reacting the mixture at 60-80 degrees C, allowing to stand for 2 hours, obtaining a solution F, slowly adding the solution F to filtrate C, stirring at not less than 150 rpm, reacting at 40 degrees C, adjusting pH to 3.5-4 with 3% industrial hydrochloric acid and allowing to stand for 6-12 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The soda ash and mixed raw material are in mass ratio of 1:1.5. Amount of the calcium aluminate is 2-5%. The pH is adjusted to 2.2-2.5.

PN CN109247183-A
TI Cultivating Ganoderma lucidum bonsai comprises preparing culture medium, sterilizing, inoculating, cooling, mixing, transplanting, compacting, flattening, increasing concentration of carbon dioxide and controlling temperature.
AU ZHAO Z
AE HEZHOU JINZISHAN ECOLOGICAL GANODERMA (HEZH-Non-standard)
GA 201910842S
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating Ganoderma lucidum bonsai comprises (i) mixing wood chips, wheat bran, crop straw, sugar, traditional Chinese medicine residue, carbamide, riboflavin and gypsum powder, mixing uniformly, adding water, adding pH adjuster; (ii) sterilizing culture medium bag, filling lower part, filling upper part, adding culture medium, compacting material in culture bag, inoculating, sealing hole, sterilizing; (iii) cooling mushroom stick, accessing original Ganoderma lucidum, mixing mushroom stick into fermentation chamber, controlling temperature and humidity and culturing; (iv) transplanting Ganoderma lucidum, compacting, flattening, inputting nutrient solution or water; (v) carrying out bonsai management, controlling direction of light, increasing concentration of carbon dioxide and way of grafting, cultivating Ganoderma lucidumafter transplanting, increasing concentration of carbon dioxide, controlling temperature, humidity and light intensity.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating Ganoderma lucidum bonsai.
   ADVANTAGE - The Ganoderma lucidum bonsai invention can be kept in living state after being stored for 3-6 years and is of great significance for further improving economic and social benefits of the production of Ganoderma lucidum.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating Ganoderma lucidumbonsai comprises (i) mixing 30-40 pts. wt. wood chips, 15-20 pts. wt. wheat bran, 10-15 pts. wt. crop straw, 5-10 pts. wt. sugar, 5-10 pts. wt. traditional Chinese medicine residue, 1-3 pts. wt. carbamide, 2-4 pts. wt. riboflavin and 2-4 pts. wt. gypsum powder to obtain culture medium, mixing uniformly, adding proper amount of water to make moisture content of culture medium reach 50-70%, adding pH adjuster to adjust pH to 5-6; (ii) sterilizing culture medium bag by placing prepared culture medium in polyethylene culture bag, filling lower part of culture medium bag partially with culture medium, filling upper part with pebbles, adding culture medium, where added culture medium fills gap of pebbles, compacting material in culture bag to be mushroom stick, inoculating, sealing hole, sterilizing in sterilizing boiler at 125-135 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours and cooling to 25-32 degrees C after boiling to complete sterilization operation; (iii) cooling mushroom stick after inoculation, accessing original Ganoderma lucidum under sterile conditions, mixing mushroom stick into fermentation chamber for cultivation, controlling temperature of fermentation room at 23-32 degrees C and humidity at 65-75% and culturing in Ganoderma lucidum cultivar for 25-35 days; (iv) transplanting Ganoderma lucidum in Ganoderma lucidum cultivar with normal growth and culture medium at foot of fungus into bonsai container, compacting, flattening, inputting nutrient solution or water into bonsai container regularly; (v) carrying out bonsai management by anoxic treatment, controlling direction of light, increasing concentration of carbon dioxide and way of grafting, cultivating Ganoderma lucidumafter transplanting, increasing concentration of carbon dioxide to 1% after main body of Ganoderma lucidumhas been formed, controlling temperature at 22-25 degrees C, humidity to be 80-85% and light intensity to be 2000-3000 lx.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The culture medium comprises 35 pts. wt. wood chips, 17 pts. wt. wheat bran, 12 pts. wt. crop straw, 7 pts. wt. sugar, 7 pts. wt. traditional Chinese medicine residue, 2 pts. wt. carbamide, 3 pts. wt. riboflavin, 3 pts. wt. gypsum powder and proper amount of water. The lower part containing culture medium, upper part containing mixture of pebbles and culture medium are microbial stick and part of mixture of pebbles and culture medium in microbial stick accounts for 1/3- one quarter of total weight of microbial stick. The nutrient solution comprises 20-30 pts. wt. enzyme fermentation liquid, 15-20 pts. wt. grape seeds, 3-5 pts. wt. effective microorganism bacteria solution, 1-3 pts. wt. citric acid, 1-3 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. vitamin-B1 and proper amount of water. The substrate in middle bonsai container comprises 50-60 pts. wt. sandy soil, 25-35 pts. wt. rice hull ash, 15-25 pts. wt. sawdust and 5-10 pts. wt. vermiculite. Preferred Method: In the step (iv), cleaning and disinfecting bonsai container with 4-6% sodium hypochlorite solution before transplanting Ganoderma lucidum.

PN WO2022170429-A1
TI Delaying spoiling and ripening of produce            comprises executing first process with respect to            quantity of produce, where first process delays            spoiling and ripening of produce.
AU ATEYA SOLIMAN R R
   RADY ATEYA R R
   RADY ATEYA I R
AE ATEYA SOLIMAN R R (SOLI-Individual)
   RADY ATEYA R R (ATEY-Individual)
   RADY ATEYA I R (ATEY-Individual)
GA 2022A6632Y
AB    NOVELTY - Delaying (M1) spoiling and ripening of                produce, comprises: executing a first process with                respect to a quantity of produce, where the first                process delays at least one of a spoiling and a                ripening of the produce.
   USE - The methods are useful for: delaying at least                one of a spoiling and a ripening of the produce;                reducing emissions from a diesel engine; applying                organic byproduct to soil (all claimed) and                reducing the impacts of chemicals e.g. carbon                dioxide, sulfur dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide,                methanol, formaldehyde and ethylene or the disposal                of some waste products from farming which causes                multiple environmental, economic and ecological                issues.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods prevent environment and                products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   toring (M2) and shipping seeds, comprising at                least one of storing and shipping at least one of                many seeds and many seedlings with container, where                the container comprises a casing, a permeable                membrane forming a predetermined portion of the                casing, and calcium hydroxide powder disposed                within the container, and the calcium hydroxide                absorbs at least one of moisture and carbon dioxide                within the vicinity of many seeds and many                seedlings;
   applying (M3) organic byproduct to soil,                comprising providing an organic byproduct,                providing an additive comprising a calcium                hydroxide solution and coffee grounds in a first                predetermined ratio, adding the additive to the                organic byproduct at a second predetermined ratio,                and applying the organic byproduct with additive to                soil;
   cleaning (M4) comprising applying a calcium                hydroxide solution to a predetermined region of an                indoor environment, where the calcium hydroxide                solution acts as an absorber of carbon dioxide                within the indoor environment and at least one of                an antimicrobial agent and an antiviral                agent;
   extracting (M5) acetic acid, comprising (a)                acquiring carbon dioxide, (b) reacting the acquired                carbon dioxide with hydrogen to generate formic                acid, (c) acquiring methane gas from organic                byproducts breaking down, (d) reacting the methane                with formic acid in the presence of heat and a                bacterial catalyst, (e) capturing hydrogen                generated from step (d), and (f) extracting the                acetic acid generated from step (d);
   olution for combination with diesel exhaust                gases comprising urea, water, and calcium                hydroxide;
   reducing (M6) emissions from a diesel engine                comprising injecting a solution at specific flow                rate into an exhaust of the diesel engine when the                diesel engine is running, where the solution                comprises urea, water and calcium hydroxide;                and
   coating (M7) produce with calcium hydroxide                powder, comprising at least one of extending                freshness and delaying ripening of produce with                calcium hydroxide, where the calcium hydroxide is                applied by at least one of a spraying a calcium                hydroxide solution onto the produce, misting the                calcium hydroxide solution onto the produce,                dipping the produce into the calcium hydroxide                solution, storing the produce with calcium                hydroxide powder, and coating the produce with                calcium hydroxide powder.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates exemplary images of                aubergines (eggplants) showing reduced                spoiling/ripening from storage within container                with housing containing powder.
   610Housing
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The produce comprises one                  of a fruit, a vegetable and an herb, and the                  first process comprises applying at least one of                  a solution and a powder to the produce comprising                  calcium hydroxide, the at least one of the                  solution and the powder absorbs at least one of                  carbon dioxide in the vicinity of the produce via                  a first chemical reaction and sulfur dioxide in                  the vicinity of the produce via a second chemical                  reaction. The produce comprises one of a fruit, a                  vegetable and an herb, and the first process                  comprises applying at least one of a solution and                  a powder to the produce comprising calcium                  hydroxide and pectin extract, the at least one of                  the solution and the powder absorbs at least one                  of carbon dioxide in the vicinity of the produce                  via a first chemical reaction and sulfur dioxide                  in the vicinity of the produce via a second                  chemical reaction, when applied at least one of                  the solution and the powder forms a thin film                  over a portion of the produce, and the pectin                  acts as a food preservative. The produce                  comprises one of a fruit, a vegetable and an                  herb, and the first process comprises applying at                  least one of a solution and a powder to the                  produce comprising calcium hydroxide and a                  gelling agent, the solution absorbs at least one                  of carbon dioxide in the vicinity of the produce                  via a first chemical reaction and sulfur dioxide                  in the vicinity of the produce via a second                  chemical reaction, the solution once applied                  forms a thin film upon the produce, and the thin                  film prevents at least one of a pollutant and a                  bacteria within the environment contacting the                  outer surface of the produce over that portion of                  the produce covered by the solution. The produce                  comprises one of a fruit, a vegetable and an                  herb, and the first process comprises applying at                  least one of a solution and a powder to the                  produce comprising calcium hydroxide and a                  gelling agent, the solution absorbs carbon                  dioxide in the vicinity of the produce via a                  chemical reaction, the solution once applied                  forms a thin film upon the produce, and the step                  of applying comprises at least one of spraying                  and dipping. Storing the produce in association                  with the container comprises one of, laying the                  produce upon the container so that the permeable                  membrane of the container is disposed towards the                  produce, placing the produce within the container                  so that the permeable membrane of the container                  is disposed towards the produce, and wrapping the                  produce within the container so that the                  permeable membrane of the container is disposed                  towards the produce. The first process comprises                  at least one of applying a solution comprising                  calcium hydroxide to the produce or applying the                  solution to a sheet which it then wrapped around                  the produce, where the solution is applied to the                  at least one of the produce and the sheet by at                  least one of dipping, spraying, misting, and                  coating. The reduction in at least one of                  moisture and carbon dioxide within the vicinity                  of the many seeds and the many seedlings reduces                  at least one of a rate of germination of the many                  seeds and a rate of root spoilage of the many                  seedling. Either the calcium hydroxide is added                  to the urea and water as a solution of calcium                  hydroxide, or the water and calcium hydroxide are                  mixed to form a solution of calcium hydroxide to                  which the urea is added. The solution reduces                  emissions of nitrogen oxides from the exhaust of                  the diesel engine via a reaction with the                  nitrogen oxides, and the calcium hydroxide                  reduces emissions of at least one of, carbon                  dioxide via a first reaction with the carbon                  dioxide from the exhaust of the diesel engine,                  and sulfur dioxide via second reaction from the                  exhaust of the diesel engine. The calcium                  hydroxide is applied in conjunction with at least                  one activated carbon and pectin. Preferred                  Components: The produce comprises one of a fruit,                  a vegetable and an herb, the first process                  comprises storing the produce in association with                  a container, the container comprises a casing, a                  permeable membrane forming a predetermined                  portion of the casing, and calcium hydroxide                  powder disposed within the container, and carbon                  dioxide released by the produce enters the                  container through the permeable membrane and is                  absorbed by the calcium hydroxide powder via a                  chemical reaction. The permeable membrane allows                  a further gas within the environment surrounding                  the produce to pass through from the environment                  into the casing, and the further gas reacts with                  the calcium hydroxide powder within the                  container. The produce comprises one of a fruit,                  a vegetable and an herb, and the first process                  comprises storing the produce in association with                  a container comprising a casing, a permeable                  membrane forming a predetermined portion of the                  casing, calcium hydroxide powder disposed within                  the container, and activated carbon disposed                  within the container, and carbon dioxide released                  by the produce enters the container through the                  permeable membrane and is absorbed by the calcium                  hydroxide powder via a chemical reaction. The                  produce comprises one of a fruit, a vegetable and                  an herb, and the first process comprises storing                  the produce in association with a container                  comprising a casing, a permeable membrane forming                  a predetermined portion of the casing, and                  calcium hydroxide powder disposed within the                  container, carbon dioxide released by the produce                  enters the container through the permeable                  membrane and is absorbed by the calcium hydroxide                  powder via a chemical reaction, and the container                  forms a predetermined portion of a storage                  container within which the produce is disposed.                  The additive further comprises an acid for                  adjusting a pH of either the additive or the                  additive combined with a fluid. The fluid is                  water. The organic byproduct is dry manure, the                  first predetermined ratio is 1 kg of coffee                  grounds per 4 l of calcium hydroxide solution,                  the second predetermined ratio is 4 kg of organic                  byproduct to each kg of coffee grounds, and the                  calcium hydroxide solution comprises 2 kg of                  calcium hydroxide per 1000 l of water. The organic                  byproduct is liquid manure, the first                  predetermined ratio is 1 kg of coffee grounds per                  2 l of calcium hydroxide solution, the second                  predetermined ratio is 4 l of organic byproduct                  to each kg of coffee grounds, and the calcium                  hydroxide solution comprises 2 kg of calcium                  hydroxide per 1000 l of water.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

GA 202012835D
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of toner comprising toner particles involves mixing fine resin particles, an organic solvent (s1), and a binder resin with a dispersion medium comprising carbon dioxide to form a dispersion in which droplets comprising the binder resin whose surface is covered with the resin fine particles are dispersed in the dispersion medium, and flowing carbon dioxide under pressure through the dispersion to remove the organic solvent (s1) contained in the droplets and the dispersion medium. The binder resin is a crystalline resin. The resin particles comprises a resin (r1) comprising a structure (a) and a structure (b), a resin (r2) comprising structure (b) and a structure (c), an acidic functional group, and a resin (r3) comprising only of structure (c) which is insoluble in the organic solvent (s1). The resin fine particles have an acid value of 2.0-20.0 mg potassium hydroxide/g. The organic solvent (s1) has a solubility parameter value of 19.0-45.0 (J/cm3)0.5.
   USE - Manufacture of toner used in image forming apparatus e.g. copier, printer or facsimile for image forming method e.g. electrophotography and electrostatic printing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of stably obtaining a large amount of toner comprising core-shell-type toner particles having a sharp particle size distribution, a uniform shell thickness, and improved low-temperature fixability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of toner comprising toner particles involves mixing fine resin particles, an organic solvent (s1), and a binder resin with a dispersion medium comprising carbon dioxide to form a dispersion in which droplets comprising the binder resin whose surface is covered with the resin fine particles are dispersed in the dispersion medium, and flowing carbon dioxide under pressure through the dispersion to remove the organic solvent (s1) contained in the droplets and the dispersion medium. The binder resin is a crystalline resin. The resin particles comprises a resin (r1) comprising a structure (a) and a structure (b), a resin (r2) comprising structure (b) and a structure (c), an acidic functional group, and a resin (r3) comprising only of structure (c) which is insoluble in the organic solvent (s1). The structure (a) comprises a portion which can take a crystal structure. The resin (r1) comprises only of the structure (a) and a resin (r2) comprises only of the structure (b) which are soluble in the organic solvent (s1). The resin fine particles have an acid value of 2.0-20.0 mg potassium hydroxide/g. The organic solvent (s1) has a solubility parameter value of 19.0-45.0 (J/cm3)0.5.

PN CN114602394-A
TI Preparing organic aerogel with removed surface            dense layer by mixing resorcinol, aqueous formaldehyde            solution, sodium carbonate, soaking glass container in            sodium carbonate solution, filling sol, sealing,            washing wet gel with absolute ethyl alcohol, carrying            out carbon dioxide supercritical drying.
AU LI E
   YUAN Z
   CUI Z
   MA D
   CHENG F
   SHEN Y
AE UNIV XIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYXT-C)
GA 202283323B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing organic aerogel with removed surface                dense layer comprises (a) mixing resorcinol,                aqueous formaldehyde solution, sodium carbonate and                water, stirring for 1-2 hours by magnetic force to                form mixed solution, (b) transferring mixed                solution obtained in (a) to an incubator to stand                for 1-2 days to obtain a sol, (c) washing and                soaking glass container and glass cover with an                aqueous sodium carbonate solution, rinsing with                deionized water and drying to obtain a hydrophilic                glass container and glass cover, (d) filling the                sol obtained in the (b) into hydrophilic glass                container obtained in the (c) and filling                container, covering with hydrophilic glass cover,                sealing and placing in incubator to stand to obtain                wet gel, (e) soaking and washing wet gel with                absolute ethyl alcohol to obtain an alcohol gel and                (f) carrying out carbon dioxide supercritical                drying on the alcohol gel obtained in (e) to obtain                organic aerogel with removed surface dense                layer.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing organic                aerogel with removed surface dense layer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and easy; uses cheap and                easy to obtain preparation device; uses the                alkaline aqueous solution to wash the glass                container, so that the container is hydrophilic,                which can avoid the formation of organic gas gel                surface dense layer and is good for demolding the                organic aerogel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                organic aerogel with removed surface dense layer,                which is prepared by above method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a flow chart of a method                for preparing organic aerogel with removed surface                dense layer. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the                  formaldehyde solution is a formaldehyde solution                  with mass fraction of 38%. The ratio of                  resorcinol, formaldehyde solution, sodium                  carbonate and water is                  1:1.5:0.001-0.01:1-10.Preferred Method: In the step (b), the                  temperature of incubator is 45-50&#8451;. In                  the step (c), the concentration of sodium                  carbonate aqueous solution is 0.5-1 M, and                  soaking time is 12-24 hours. In the step (e),                  soaking and washing with absolute ethyl alcohol                  for 3-4 days and the solvent is replaced every 1                  day. In the step (f), the temperature of                  supercritical drying is set to 40-42&#8451;,                  the air pressure is set to 10-11 MPa, the heating                  rate is set to 0.5-1&#8451;/minutes, the                  constant temperature time is 3-4 hours and the                  decompression rate is 1-3 MPa/hour.

PN CN114540553-A
TI Preparing sugarcane sulfur-free white granulated            sugar comprises adding lime milk into sugarcane juice            obtained by squeezing sugarcane, introducing carbon            dioxide, two-carbon saturating, neutralizing with            phosphoric acid, treating with phosphoric acid and            flocculant, cooking and refining.
AU YANG X
   LIN D
   PENG D
   CHEN Y
   ZHAO H
   YUAN S
   YIN Y
   LAN F
   LUO W
   YANG J
   XU D
   LI X
   LI R
   LI Y
AE GENGMA NANHUA OVERSEAS CHINESE SUGAR IND (GENG-Non-standard)
GA 202275425Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sugarcane sulfur-free white                granulated sugar comprises (1) adding lime milk                into sugarcane juice obtained by squeezing                sugarcane, and adjusting the pH, (2) heating the                pre-ashed sugarcane juice, and gradually adding                lime milk ash, (3) introducing carbon dioxide in                the sugarcane juice after the main ash for                saturation, continue to add the lime milk, and                maintaining the pH to obtain one-carbon clear                juice, (5) performing two-carbon saturation of the                one-carbon clear juice, (6) heating the two-carbon                saturated juice, and filtering to obtain two-carbon                clear juice, (7) neutralizing the two-carbon clear                juice with phosphoric acid to obtain crude syrup,                (8) treating the crude syrup with phosphoric acid                and flocculant, cooperating with a brewing system                to float the syrup in the syrup reactor, and                removing the fine non-sugar impurities and some                pigment substances in the syrup to obtain refined                syrup, and (9) cooking and refining the refined                syrup to obtain final product
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sugarcane                sulfur-free white granulated sugar.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts the two-carbon saturation                combined with the syrup floating process, which                completely replaces the sulfur fumigation process                of the traditional process; improves the                clarification effect of sugarcane juice by the                progressive ash adding process of pre-ash and main                ash; ensures the color value and turbidity index of                the product are better than those prepared by the                traditional sulfurous acid method and carbonic acid                method; and ensures the product is completely                sulfur-free, and the quality and safety are                significantly improved.

   ADVANTAGE - The system can adopt different intelligent                estimation mechanism respectively estimate future                each time section in park moving vehicle total                number and future time sections in park green plant                entire carbon dioxide absorption capability, so as                to maintain the carbon neutralizing effect of                island park in time division.
