PT P
PN WO2022084392-A1
TI Manufacture of pigment for cosmetic, paint and coating applications, by combining surface-reacted calcium carbonate with water-soluble iron compound in aqueous medium, adding treatment agent, dewatering mixture and thermally treating mixture.
AU FTOUNI J
   ORLANDO F
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 2022563241
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of pigment comprises (a) providing at least one surface-reacted calcium carbonate, (b) providing at least one water-soluble iron compound, (c) providing at least one treatment agent, (d) combining the surface-reacted calcium carbonate with the water-soluble iron compound in an aqueous medium, (e) adding the treatment agent to the mixture, (f) dewatering the mixture, (g) thermally treating the mixture at a temperature of 80-150 degrees C, where the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction product of ground natural calcium carbonate-containing mineral (GNCC) or precipitated calcium carbonate (PCC) with carbon dioxide and one or more hydronium ion donors and where the carbon dioxide is formed in situ by the hydronium ion donor treatment and/or is supplied from an external source.
   USE - The method is used for the manufacture of pigment used in cosmetic, paint and coating applications (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The pigment obtained by the method has light-fastness and sensitivity for damage from ultraviolet light, has improved heat stability, tinting strength, staining, dispersion, control of opacity or transparency, resistance to alkali and acid, low toxicity, and improved reactions and interactions between pigments. The pigment is not harmful to health e.g. if applied to the skin, have pleasant sensorial effects, improved optical properties, including their UV absorption properties and IR reflectance, e.g. provides UV protection. The pigment has good color consistency, dispersibility, good safety and sustainability profile.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for manufacture of a cosmetic product, paint or coating comprising the above steps (a) to (e) and (i) adding the pigment to a cosmetic formulation, paint or coating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The surface-reacted calcium carbonate has a volume median particle size d50 (vol) of 1-75 mu m, preferably 5-15 mu m and/or top cut particle size d50 (vol) of 2-150 mu m, preferably 10-30 mu m and/or Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) specific surface area of 10-200 m2/g, preferably 50-100 m2/g measured by BET nitrogen method and/or an intra-particle intruded specific pore volume of 0.1-2.3 cm3/g, preferably 0.6-1.6 cm3/g calculated from mercury porosimetry measurement. Preferred Components: The water-soluble iron compound is selected from iron(II) sulfate, iron(III) sulfate, iron(II) halides, such as iron(II) chloride or iron(II) bromide, iron(III) halides, such as iron(III) chloride or iron(III) bromide, iron(II) nitrate, iron(III) nitrate, iron(II) phosphate, iron(III) phosphate, iron(II) oxalate, iron(III) oxalate, iron(II) acetate, iron(III) acetate, hydrates and their mixtures. The treatment agent is selected from precipitation agents forming a water-insoluble iron compound when combined with the water-soluble iron compound, preferably alkaline and alkaline earth hydroxides, such as sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, ammonia and their mixtures. The treatment agent is selected from reducing agents forming elemental iron when combined with the water-soluble iron compound, preferably sodium borohydride, lithium borohydride, sodium hydride, lithium aluminum hydride, hydrogen, hydrazine, sodium citrate and their mixtures. Preferred Composition: The water-soluble iron compound is added in an amount of 0.05-40 wt.%, preferably 5-6 wt.% relating to the iron content in relation to the total dry weight of the surface-reacted calcium carbonate. The water-soluble iron compound of step (b) is added in an amount such that the amount of water-insoluble iron compound and/or the amount of elemental iron resulting from the reaction of the water-soluble iron compound of step (b) and the treatment agent of step (c) is 0.05-40 wt.%, preferably 5-6 wt.% based on the total dry weight of the surface-reacted calcium carbonate. The treatment agent is provided in a molar ratio of treatment agent: Fe (iron) of 1:1-15:1, preferably 3:1-5:1. Preferred Conditions: The step of combining surface-reacted calcium carbonate with water-soluble iron compound and adding one treatment agent to the mixture are independent from each other and are carried out under stirring and/or at a temperature of 25-95 degrees C, preferably 50 degrees C. The thermal treatment step is carried out at a temperature of 90-140 degrees C, preferably 110-125 degrees C. The thermal treatment further comprises a thermal treatment at a temperature of 150-600 degrees C, preferably 350-400 degrees C. Preferred Method: The dewatering step is carried out by filtration, centrifugation, spray drying, evaporation, optionally under vacuum.
   EXAMPLE - Surface-reacted calcium carbonate was dried overnight at 125 degrees C. Subsequently, water was added into the reactor, a 3-neck round bottom flask, followed by the surface-reacted calcium carbonate. The slurry was stirred thoroughly during 30 minutes at room temperature. An aqueous solution of iron(II) sulfate heptahydrate was prepared, by solubilizing it in water. Subsequently, the solution was added dropwise to the surface-reacted calcium carbonate slurry. The mixture was kept under thorough mixing for 60 minutes. A sodium hydroxide solution was prepared by adding the required amount to water. Then, the solution was added to the slurry and kept under mixing for 60 minutes. The mixture was dewatered via a filtration procedure using a filter paper grade 589, washed with water (50% of the total water volume used during the preparation) and finally dried overnight at 125 degrees C in an oven. Finally, the powder was manually deagglomerated and calcined at 300-500 degrees C for 2-3 hours in a muffle oven. The sample was thermally treated at 125 degrees C (drying), and subsequently at 300 degrees C and 500 degrees C. During the dewatering process, the sodium sulfate was removed with the filtrate. Iron hydroxide species were generated on the surface of the surface-reacted calcium carbonate and within its pores.
DC D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); G01 (Inorganic pigments and non-fibrous fillers (C09C).);

PT P
PN US2022126281-A1
TI Regenerating catalyst comprising gallium by combusting fuel gas with oxygen in the presence of the catalyst to provide a regenerated catalyst.
AU PAN W
   KULPRATHIPANJA S
   JOHNSON R A
   SENETAR J J
   BUCHBINDER A M
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 202257023U
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprising gallium is regenerated by combusting fuel gas with oxygen in the presence of the catalyst to provide a regenerated catalyst, where the oxygen is provided in an oxygen supply gas that has an oxygen concentration of greater than 21 vol.%.
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating a catalyst comprising gallium.
   ADVANTAGE - Regenerating the catalyst by combusting coke and fuel gas together in the presence of enriched oxygen restores activity to the catalyst to bring back to adequate activity level while reducing or obviating the need for the oxygen treatment step. Activity is restored even on catalysts that deactivated despite accumulating negligible amounts of coke. The regeneration occurs despite the presence of large amounts of steam and carbon dioxide which are generated as combustion products. By increasing oxygen concentration sufficiently in an oxygen supply gas provided for combustion of fuel gas in the presence of the catalyst and combustion of coke on the catalyst, the need for the air treatment step may be reduced or eliminated altogether, and that the regeneration occurs with increased oxygen concentration despite the presence of steam and/or carbon dioxide generated from combustion of fuel and coke.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for regenerating a catalyst comprising noble metal and gallium which involves feeding a stream of coked catalyst to a regenerator vessel; feeding a stream of oxygen supply gas having a concentration of oxygen greater than 21 vol.% to the regenerator vessel; feeding a stream of fuel gas to the regenerator vessel; combusting coke from the coked catalyst and combusting the fuel gas with the oxygen supply gas to provide a regenerated catalyst and flue gas; and separating the regenerated catalyst from the flue gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The method further comprises combusting coke from the catalyst and the fuel gas together. The method further comprises separating the regenerated catalyst from flue gases and passing at least a portion of the hot regenerated catalyst to a dehydrogenation reactor. The method comprises recycling a portion of the regenerated catalyst to the combustion step. Combustion occurs in a combustion chamber of the regenerator vessel and further comprises separating the regenerated catalyst from flue gases in a separation chamber of the regenerator vessel and further comprises passing the hot regenerated catalyst from the regenerator vessel to a dehydrogenation reactor. The method comprises recycling a portion of the regenerated catalyst from the separation chamber to the combustion chamber. The method further comprises distributing a stream of oxygen supply gas to the separation chamber. The method further comprises stripping the catalyst with an oxygen supply gas after the regenerated catalyst is separated from the flue gas. The method further comprises discharging flue gas from the regenerator vessel comprising 4-15 vol.% oxygen.Preferred Compositions: Oxygen supply gas has an oxygen concentration of 21-40 vol.%. Catalyst comprises less than 1000 ppm carbon by weight before the combustion step. Concentration of oxygen in the flue gas after combustion is 4-15 vol.%. Oxygen supply gas has a concentration of 22-40 vol.%.Preferred Conditions: Combustion step occurs at 670-750degrees Celsius. Spent catalyst is in contact with the oxygen supply gas for an average of under 2 minutes before initial separation. Catalyst is in regenerator vessel for an average residence time of less than 2 minutes before it enters the separation chamber.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E35-F; E10-J02C3; E11-Q01B; E11-E03; H06-C01A; J04-E09; J04-E05; J04-E04; J04-E01; J01-E02D; N07-C03; N03-G04

PN CN114317852-A
TI Low-carbon metallurgical iron-making method of            large blast furnace gas carbon circulation, involves            adding raw fuel used by blast furnace from top of            furnace according to coke layer and ore layer, and            adjusting theoretical combustion temperature.
AU JIA Z
   XU X
   JI S
AE XINJIANG BAYI IRON & STEEL CO LTD (BAOS-C)
GA 202256651Y
AB    NOVELTY - Low-carbon metallurgical iron-making method of                2500m3 large blast furnace gas carbon circulation                comprises (i) using raw fuel used in blast furnace                is weighed under the groove, the conveying belt is                composed of blast furnace top charging and                distributing device, according to the coke bed                layer and the ore layer, adding blast furnace from                the top of the furnace, (ii) using platform and                funnel distributing mode, ensuring reasonable                distribution of the edge air flow and the central                air flow, stabilizing the center air flow through                the funnel of 1.0-1.5m deep, and obtaining stable,                smooth edge airflow, (iii) adding waste steel into                the furnace grade is more than 59%, (iv) using                blast furnace adopts more than 90% ultra-high                oxygen or full oxygen smelting, coke oven gas,                European smelted furnace decarburization gas and                2500m3 blast furnace dearburation gas and adjusting                the theoretical combustion temperature
   USE - Method of 2500m3 large blast furnace gas                carbon circulation low carbon metallurgy iron                making method used in steel industry. Can also be                used in iron and steel enterprises.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem that the                traditional blast furnace iron-making fuel ratio                reduction technology is limited and the ton iron                carbon dioxide discharge is high, so as to reduce                the blast furnace solid fossil fuel. It improves                the iron making metallurgy efficiency of the blast                furnaces and reduces the carbon dioxide (CO2)                discharge in the furnace iron making process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a 2500m3 blast furnace gas                carbon circulation of low carbon metallurgy iron                making method process flow diagram. (Drawing                includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN CN114308112-A
TI Vanadium-doped octahedral molecular sieve supported titanium dioxide material used as catalyst for catalyzing nitrogen monoxide and carbon monoxide in flue gas at low temperature to generate non-toxic gases nitrogen and carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG J
   JIANG Y
   WANG T
   ZHEN S
   DONG F
   WANG Z
   ZHU G
   HE H
   WANG J
AE TANGSHAN HEXING WASTE INTEGRATED UTILIZA (TANG-Non-standard)
   WANG J (WANG-Individual)
GA 2022797376
AB    NOVELTY - Vanadium-doped octahedral molecular sieve (OMS-2) supported titanium dioxide material is obtained by loading vanadium-doped OMS-2 onto titanium dioxide.
   USE - Vanadium-doped octahedral molecular sieve supported titanium dioxide material used as a catalyst for catalyzing nitrogen monoxide and carbon monoxide in flue gas at low temperature in presence of sulfur dioxide at 100-300degrees Celsius to generate non-toxic gases nitrogen and carbon dioxide, where the flue gas is discharged from power plants, smelters, waste incineration plants, cement plants, and petrochemical plants (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The vanadium-doped octahedral molecular sieve supported titanium dioxide material achieves the purpose of denitrification at low temperature and treating waste with waste, where the denitration rate can reach more than 80% at 150degrees Celsius and 90% at 300degrees Celsius, and can maintain a denitration rate of 62.3% at 150degrees Celsius, and 87.8% at 300degrees Celsius.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Vanadium-doped octahedral molecular sieve (OMS-2) supported titanium dioxide material (V-OMS-2(x)@TiO2) is obtained by loading vanadium-doped OMS-2 onto titanium dioxide, where x is 0.1-0.7, preferably 0.7.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing vanadium-doped octahedral molecular sieve supported titanium dioxide material, which involves dispersing or dissolving titanium dioxide, vanadium source, manganese source, and potassium permanganate in deionized water, stirring the obtained solution to carry out hydrothermal reaction, and washing, centrifuging, drying and grinding the product to obtain V-OMS-2(x)@TiO2.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the X-ray diffraction (XRD) pattern of the prepared vanadium-doped octahedral molecular sieve supported titanium dioxide material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The vanadium-doped OMS-2 means that V replaces the K position in OMS-2 to obtain V-OMS-2. The titanium dioxide comprises anatase-type titanium dioxide and rutile-type titanium dioxide, preferably anatase-type titanium dioxide. The vanadium source comprises sodium orthovanadate and vanadium pentoxide, preferably sodium orthovanadate. The manganese source includes manganese acetate, manganese sulfate and manganese nitrate, preferably manganese acetate.Preferred Conditions: The obtained final product is cleaned by deionized water one to two times, and then anhydrous ethanol is added to carry out ultrasonic cleaning for 5-10 minutes.

TI Automobile electric reduction intelligent charging method based on short-term carbon emission factor, involves performing linear fitting to total carbon dioxide discharge amount of same period of area to be investigated and total power generation amount of a power system.
AU LIU Z
   WANG X
   LI J
AE UNIV HEBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHT-C)
GA 2022519015
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing linear fitting to total carbon dioxide discharge amount of same period of area to be investigated and total power generation amount of a power system. Electric vehicle ordered charging mathematical model considering carbon discharge is constructed based on data of different time periods. Charging starting time of electric vehicle is obtained. Battery electric quantity is counted and fitted when the vehicle is in charge electric the vehicle data set electric the charging station. Charge starting time and actual charging electric quantity of the same vehicle are obtained. Upper and lower layers of genes are operated by genetic algorithm at same time or respectively the same time. The vehicle state is divided into flameout, passenger, empty three states.
   USE - Automobile electric reduction intelligent charging method based on short-term carbon emission factor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing a carbon emission reduction intelligent charging method based on short-term carbon emission factor electric reducing carbon electric of the vehicle and ensuring stable operation of the power grid, reduces the burden of the large scale electric the vehicle access network after the grid, allows the carbon emission and network mean variance as the optimization target to optimize the charging behavior of the electric setting double-layer non-dominance genetic algorithm, constructs population according to the charging behaviour, improves the quality of the iterative efficiency and optimal solution, and effectively solves the multi-target problem of considering carbon discharge and grid load variance.

PN WO2022053672-A1
TI Capsule for use in beverage preparation machine, comprises gas mixture of carbon dioxide and at least one other protective gas.
AU LEIPNER M
   MARVILLE F
   DOGAN N
AE SOC PROD NESTLE SA (NEST-C)
GA 202241396H
AB    NOVELTY - The capsule (1) has a hollow capsule case (2) comprises walls extending axially towards a top opening (22) and a bottom outlet (23). An injection membrane (3) closes top opening, where injection membrane being attached to the case and adapted to be punctured for liquid injection into the capsule. A wall (5) is provided inside the chamber so as to delimit with the injection membrane an ingredient chamber (21). A bed (RG) of roast and ground coffee to be extracted provided in ingredient chamber. An opening device (7) provided inside the hollow capsule case chamber and adapted to open the ingredient chamber by relative engagement of said opening device with the wall under the effect of the liquid pressure increase in the ingredient chamber during injection of liquid. The capsule comprises a gas mixture of CO2 and other protective gas, gas mixture comprising 35 vol. percentage carbondioxide (CO2), and not more than 80 vol. percentage CO2.
   USE - Capsule for use in a beverage preparation machine used in a food science and consumer goods area for preparing a beverage e.g. coffee-based beverage such as espresso or brew-like coffee cup.
   ADVANTAGE - Reduces risk of jet of liquid sprays through the hole in the injection membrane pierced by the injection needle and out of the capsule, thus reducing the whale effect. The capsule can be designed simply, since the capsule only requires the injection of a defined quantity of carbon dioxide into the capsule.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for manufacturing a capsule being adapted for use in a beverage preparation machine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a profile cut view of the capsule.

GA 2021E28177

The nitrogen and carbon dioxide are introduced to the reaction chamber under annealing process. The relation of the carbon dioxide flow control is satisfied using specific formula relating oxygen flow and reaction time. The sapphire substrate is taken out from the reaction cavity and maintained in pressure range of 150- 300mbar, and the temperature is controlled within 1100 to 1200 degrees Celsius in the reaction process. The silicon doping concentration is 4x (1018-5)x 1018atoms/cm^3 and the n-type GaN layer (3) is doped with silicon and grown at the temperature of 700-800, and cooled with the furnace for 20-30min.

PN CN113617388-A
TI Silver nano catalyst based on porous pyridyl covalent organic framework useful for synthesis of oxazolidinone by addition of propargylamine and carbon dioxide, comprises silver-2,6 farnesyl pyrophosphate-triacetone triperoxide or silver-3,5 farnesyl pyrophosphate-triacetone triperoxide.
AU BAI G
   ZHANG Y
   LAN X
AE UNIV HEBEI (UYHB-C)
GA 2021D0117Q
AB    NOVELTY - Silver nano catalyst based on porous pyridyl covalent organic framework comprises silver-2,6 farnesyl pyrophosphate-triacetone triperoxide (Ag-2,6-FPP-TAPT) or Ag-3,5-FPP-TAPT, based on the pyridyl covalent organic framework material 2,6-FPP-TAPT or 3,5-FPP-TAPT as the carrier prepared by encapsulating silver nanoparticles in the pores of the carrier. The 2,6-FPP-TAPT material is based on 2,4,6-tris(4-aminophenyl)-1,3,5-triazine and 4,4'-(pyridine-2,6-diyl) synthesis of dibenzaldehyde by reaction. The 3,5-FPP-TAPT material is based on 2,4,6-tris(4-aminophenyl)-1,3,5-triazine and 4,4'-(pyridine-3,5-di Base) dibenzaldehyde reaction synthesis.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for synthesis of oxazolidinone by addition of propargyl amine and carbon dioxide (CO2) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst exhibits a higher catalytic activity and stability, and realizes continuous method of grade-scale synthesis of oxazolidinone.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the catalyst, comprising adding 2, 4, 6-tri (4 phenyl) -1, 3, 5-triazine and 4, 4' -(pyridine-2, 6-diyl) benzophenone aldehyde or 4, 4' - (-3, 5-diyl) formaldehyde in the vacuum reaction container, adding organic solvent and ultrasonically dispersing, mixing uniformly, adding acetic acid solution, vacuum sealing the reaction system, and reacting for 2-7 days at 80-160degrees Celsius to obtain the covalent organic framework material, and (ii) adding the covalent organic framework material and silver boride into the organic solvent, ultrasonically mixing underthe protection of dark and inert gas, placing in the oil bath of 70-90degrees Celsius to reflux and reacting for 20-28 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the vacuum reaction vessel is a thick-walled pressure-resistant bottle with a vacuum shut-off door, 2,4,6-tris(4-aminophenyl)-1,3,5-triazine. The molar ratio of 4'-(pyridine-2,6-diyl)benzaldehyde or 4,4'-(pyridine-3,5-diyl)benzaldehyde is 1:0.5-3. In the step (i), the organic solvent is tert-butanol, 1,4-dioxane, n-butanol, mesitylene, o-dichlorobenzene, and tetrahydrofuran in a volume ratio of 1: 0.5-3 mixed double solvent. In the step (i), the concentration of the acetic acid solution is 3-9 mol/l, and the dosage is 0.05-0.5 times of the total organic solvent. In the step (ii), the amount of silver tetrafluoroboride is 0.01-0.5 times the mass of the covalent organic framework material, and the precipitate obtained at the end of the reaction is washed with methanol, tetrahydrofuran, and acetone, and then vacuum dried. The organic solvent is tetrahydrofuran. The solvent for the synthesis reaction is acetonitrile, the organic base is 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene, the reaction gas is CO2, the reaction pressure is normal pressure, and the reaction temperature is 25-50degreesC. The continuous reaction device of grading scale is established, the catalyst is placed in the micro reaction tube, the reaction liquid is injected into the microreaction tube through the peristaltic pump and the heating reaction is carried out, and the continuous circulation catalysis is realized.

TI Manufacture of solid solution catalyst doped with            zinc element used in carbon dioxide thermal catalytic            hydrogenation for forming methanol, involves            synthesizing catalyst comprising zinc-cadmium-zirconium            oxide with solid solution structure using zinc nitrate,            cadmium nitrate and zirconium nitrate.
AU MA B
   MA X
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 202193837P
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of solid solution catalyst doped                with zinc element involves synthesizing catalyst                comprising zinc-cadmium-zirconium oxide with a                solid solution structure using zinc nitrate,                cadmium nitrate and zirconium nitrate as raw                materials, after co-precipitation method.
   USE - Manufacture of solid solution catalyst doped                with zinc element used in carbon dioxide thermal                catalytic hydrogenation for forming methanol                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical manufacture of                the solid solution catalyst under mild preparation                conditions. The high-temperature calcination method                directly synthesizes the catalyst with the solid                solution structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of solid solution catalyst doped                with zinc element involves synthesizing catalyst                comprising zinc-cadmium-zirconium oxide of formula:                Zn-Cd-ZrOx with a solid solution structure using                zinc nitrate, cadmium nitrate and zirconium nitrate                as raw materials, after co-precipitation                method.
   x = number of moles of the corresponding                divalent oxygen atom content required for the metal                atom mole content under the metal valence                corresponding to Zn, Cd, and Zr of 2, 2, and 4,                respectively.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacture of the solid solution catalyst, which                involves adding 0.01-0.612 g, preferably 0.290 g                zinc nitrate, 0.125-0.392 g, preferably 0.392 g                cadmium nitrate and 4.192-6.691 g, preferably 4.192                g zirconium nitrate and dissolving in 100-300 ml,                preferably 200 ml deionized water at 50-80 degrees                C, preferably 70 degrees C, dripping 80-300 ml,                preferably 100 ml containing 2.25-2.45 g,                preferably 2.34 g ammonium carbonate aqueous                solution, and after the white precipitate appears,                continuing the stirring, and washing the                precipitate and obtaining zinc carbonate-cadmium                carbonate-zirconium carbonate after drying and                calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The                  zinc-cadmium-zirconium oxide is prepared in an                  amount of 0.3-10% comprising 0.3-10 %mass,                  preferably 5 %mass zinc, which is ratio of mass                  of zinc element with respect to the sum of zinc                  element and cadmium-zirconium oxide. The amount                  of cadmium in zinc-cadmium-zirconium oxide is                  0.0397-0.0672, which is ratio of the amount of                  cadmium with respect to the sum of amount of                  zinc, amount of zirconium and amount of cadmium.                  Preferred Conditions: The time for continuing the                  stirring treatment after the white precipitation                  appears is 90-120 minutes, preferably 120                  minutes. The drying temperature is 80-120 degrees                  C, preferably 110 degrees C. The drying time is                  2-5 hours, preferably 4 hours. In an air                  atmosphere, the calcination temperature is                  300-700 degrees C, preferably 500 degrees C for                  1.5-5 hours, preferably 3 hours. The heating rate                  from room temperature to drying temperature to                  calcination temperature is 2-10 degrees C/minute,                  preferably 2 degrees C/minute. Preferred Process:                  After stirring, the solution is cooled to 20-40                  degrees C and the supernatant is removed. The                  precipitate is washed with deionized water 3 to 5                  times in sequence. The zinc carbonate-cadmium                  carbonate-zirconium carbonate are grinded for                  0.5-2 hours and transferred it to a muffle                  furnace for calcination. After calcination, it is                  cooled to 20-40 degrees C to obtain 0.3-10.0%                  catalyst.

   ADVANTAGE - The electrochemical device has improved compactness. The arrangement of the heating member inside the clamping plate facilitates the regulation of the temperature of the electrochemical unit. The conformation of the clamping plate and of the heating member within the electrochemical device results in a reduction in heat losses to the outside of the electrochemical device.

   MIAO J
AE UNIV WYOMING (UYWO-C)

GA 2021424376
AB    NOVELTY - Processing hydrocarbon feed stock comprises                contacting hydrocarbon feed stock with doped                ceria-supported metal catalyst comprising doped                ceria-supported metal, thus generating syngas                product comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide,                where the doped ceria-supported metal catalyst is                metal oxide complexes (I) and the feedstock                comprises methane with at least one additional                hydrocarbon components and carbon dioxide or the                hydrocarbon feedstock is a product from cement                processing, steel processing, coal refining, or                petrochemical refining, crude oil production,                natural gas production, coal mining or minerals                mining.
   USE - The method is useful for processing                hydrocarbon feed stock, preferably production of a                syngas product and/or for dry reforming of methane                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method involves the doped ceria-supported                metal catalysts exhibiting good activity and                stability for commercially relevant dry reforming                of methane process conditions including low                temperature and long term operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing a hydrocarbon feed stock comprises                contacting the hydrocarbon feed stock with a doped                ceria-supported metal catalyst comprising a doped                ceria-supported metal, thus generating a syngas                product comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide,                where the doped ceria-supported metal catalyst is                of formula (M1/Ce1-xB1xO2-delta) (FX1) and the                feedstock comprises methane with at least one                additional hydrocarbon components and carbon                dioxide or the hydrocarbon feedstock is a product                from cement processing, steel processing, coal                refining, or petrochemical refining, crude oil                production, natural gas production, coal mining or                minerals mining.
   M1 = at least one metals comprising Ni, Co, Pd                and/or Pt;
   B1 = at least one dopant comprising Ti, Zr, Mn                or La;
   x = 0.05-0.5; and
   delta = oxygen deficiency.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                catalyst comprising the doped ceria-supported metal                (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The feedstock is obtained                  or derived from an industrial process that                  generates both carbon dioxide and methane or an                  industrial process that generates carbon dioxide                  in proximity to a source of methane.Preferred Components: The feedstock                  comprises a product from at least one process                  comprising (i) a coal pyrolysis process, (ii) a                  petrochemical oxidization process, (iii) a                  sintering process, (iv) a furnace process, (v) a                  kiln process, (vi) a steam reforming process,                  (vii) an ammonia production process, (viii) a                  fuel production or treatment process, (ix) a                  mining process and (x) any process that produces                  carbon dioxide. The doped ceria-supported metal                  catalyst comprises the metals (M1) dispersed on a                  doped catalyst support having of formula                  (Ce1-xB1xO2-delta) (II), where the doped catalyst                  support maintains the structure of pure ceria and                  produces mixed metal oxides. The metals (M1) are                  provided as particles or clusters having an                  average size dimension up to 1microm and the                  weight percent of the metals (M1) in the catalyst                  is 0.1-20 wt.%. The metals (M1) in (I) is nickel                  and nickel has a weight percent in the catalyst                  1.5-7 wt.%, preferably 2.4plus minus0.5 wt%. The                  ratio of cerium to B1 in (I) comprising 0.1-10.                  The dopants (B1) in (I) is titanium, where the                  ratio of cerium to titanium is 2.3plus minus0.3.                  The dopant (B1) in (I) is at least two different                  dopants. The metals (M1) in (I) is nickel, where                  the weight percent of nickel in the catalyst                  comprising 2-3 wt.% and the ratio of cerium to                  titanium is 2-2.7. The method is characterized by                  a methane conversion efficiency greater than or                  equal to70% at a temperature of greater than or                  equal to650oC. The method is characterized by a                  ratio of hydrogen produced to monoxide produced                  greater than or equal to90% at a temperature of                  greater than or equal to650degreesC.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - The power generation system has a compressor                (10) that is provided for compressing working                fluid. An expander (16) is provided for generating                work. A primary heat exchanger (20) is provided for                adding heat to the power generation system. A                temperature control valve (26) is located upstream                of the compressor. A mass management system is                located downstream of the compressor. The mass                management system comprises a pressure control                valve (14), which is provided for adding mass to                the power generation system, and a backpressure                control valve (15), which is provided for removing                mass from the power generation system. The                temperature valve is configured to maintain working                fluid outside of a liquid state.
   USE - Supercritical Brayton cycle power generation                system using high pressure working fluids e.g.                supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The power generation system is optimized, so                as to minimize cost per unit power output, to                reduce system stall times, improve mobility, and to                present cleaner options, while maintaining                acceptable power and efficiency profiles. The                high-density power generation system is allowed to                quickly react to fluctuating power requirements.                The ressure drop reduces efficiency of the overall                power generation system, so designers combat                pressure drop by designing for larger heat                exchangers to reduce friction. The mass of the heat                exchanger material as well as fluid inventory                within the heat exchanger is reduced, which leads                to a heat exchanger to quickly respond to changes                in energy input.

PN CN111632626-A; CN111632626-B
TI Catalyst used for high-efficiency catalytic synthesis of salicylic acid through carboxylation of phenol and supercritical carbon dioxide comprises metal organic frameworks solid-supported ionic liquid catalyst.
AU LI N
   SHI W
   ZHOU J
   CHEN C
   KAN D
   WANG N
   WANG K
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 202089625J
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises recyclable metal organic frameworks (MOFs) solid-supported ionic liquid catalyst.
   USE - The catalyst is used for high-efficiency catalytic synthesis of salicylic acid through carboxylation of phenol and supercritical carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is highly recyclable, can maintain high catalytic activity after recycling, and has good catalytic performance, high selectivity, and environment-friendly preparation method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the catalyst comprising:
   (A) dispersing iron oxide in mixed methanol solution of zinc nitrate or cobalt nitrate and 2-methylimidazole, ultrasonicating for 20 minutes, magnetically stirring to 40-60 degrees C for 1-4 hours, collecting product using magnet, and washing unreacted raw material with methanol to obtain magnetic core-shell microsphere iron oxide-ZIF catalyst carrier;
   (B) weighing 1-methylimidazole and halogenated n-butane respectively at molar ratio of 1:1.05-1.20, slowly dropping halogenated n-butane to 1-methylimidazole while rapidly stirring at 50-80 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours, refluxing for 6-12 hours, cooling, repeatedly washing with ethyl acetate, rotary evaporating to remove residual ethyl acetate, and vacuum drying at 60-80 degrees C until weight is constant to obtain ionic liquid intermediate; slowly adding Lewis acid at molar ratio is 1:0.1-1, heating to 100-150 degrees C while stirring for 1-5 hours, and cooling to obtain transparent and viscous Lewis acid ionic liquid catalyst; and
   (C) mixing viscous Lewis acid ionic liquid catalyst and anhydrous methanol as solvent, dispersing 5-75 wt.% carrier, stirring, ultrasonicating for 10 minutes, soaking at room temperature for 12-48 hours, separating catalyst using a magnet, vacuum drying, and calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The recyclable MOFs solid-supported ionic liquid catalyst comprises catalytically active component and catalyst carrier. The catalytically active component is Lewis acid and imidazole ionic liquid. The Lewis acid is aluminum bromide, iron chloride and/or copper chloride. The catalyst carrier is magnetic core-shell MOFs nanocomposite iron oxide-ZIFs. The catalyst carrier is iron oxide/zinc-ZIF-8 and/or iron oxide/cobalt-ZIF-67.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The imidazole ionic liquid is 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide and/or 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium chloride.

PT P
PN CN111538115-A; CN111538115-B
TI Graded layer calcium carbonate-calcium silicate all-reflection film includes a substrate, a calcium carbonate layer, a calcium silicate layer and a silicon oxide layer arranged in sequence.
AU WEN Z
AE PERFET PEAK NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (PERF-Non-standard)
GA 2020810644
AB    NOVELTY - Graded layer calcium carbonate-calcium silicate all-reflection film includes a substrate, a calcium carbonate layer, a calcium silicate layer and a silicon oxide layer arranged in sequence, and the calcium carbonate layer, the calcium silicate layer and the silicon oxide layer are sequentially attached to the substrate.
   USE - Graded layer calcium carbonate-calcium silicate all-reflection film.
   ADVANTAGE - The graded layer calcium carbonate-calcium silicate all-reflection film has easily available raw materials, low in price, environmentally friendly and non-toxic, and as an optical total reflection film, it can be designed into a calcium carbonate/calcium silicate/silicon oxide multilayer structure to obtain different wavelengths Refraction and reflection control of light, to achieve total reflection of light of different wavelengths, and the reflection effect is good.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a graded layer calcium carbonate-calcium silicate total reflective film, which involves:
   (A) cleaning the substrate and air drying;
   (B) sending the air-dried substrate into the reaction chamber of the equipment, and evacuating the reaction chamber of the equipment;
   (C) heat-treating the substrate and then cooling down;
   (D) rotating the substrate, and pass in a gas source containing cyclopentadiene calcium and a carrier gas in the rotating state;
   (E) introducing carbon dioxide gas to deposit a calcium carbonate layer on the surface of the substrate;
   (F) turning off the carbon dioxide gas, pass in silane, and depositing a calcium silicate layer on the surface of the calcium carbonate layer;
   (G) turning off the gas source containing calcium cyclopentadiene, continue to pass in silane, and form a silicon oxide layer on the surface of the calcium silicate layer; and
   (H) turning off all gas sources and carrier gas, cool down, and obtaining a graded layer calcium carbonate-calcium silicate total reflective film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The thickness of the calcium carbonate layer, the calcium silicate layer and the silicon oxide layer are 10-100 nanometer, 15-150 nanometer, and 8-80 nanometer, respectively. The heat-treating temperature of the substrate is 690-750 degrees C, the treatment time is 15-20 minutes, and the temperature of the temperature drop is 500 degrees C. The gas flow rates of the gas source and carrier gas containing calcium cyclopentadiene are 9-12 standard cubic centimeters per minute respectively. The method further includes introducing oxygen gas to deposit and form a calcium oxide layer on the surface of the substrate. Preferred Components: The substrate is indium tin oxide glass. The graded layer calcium carbonate-calcium silicate total reflection film further includes a calcium oxide layer, and the calcium oxide layer is located between the substrate and the calcium carbonate layer. The carrier gas is argon.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. TI Device for capturing carbon dioxide (CO2) from            air, has inlet and outlet for receiving and discharging            air located on one end and other end of circumferential            wall which allows air to flow from inlet to outlet            though circumferential wall.
AU DE NEVE H
   SOPPE W J
   PIETERSE J A Z
   ELZINGA G D
   FRIJTERS C H
   VAN DER WERF C H
AE NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
GA 202068962L
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a cylinder provided with a                circumferential wall (a1) centered around a                vertical central axis. The circumferential wall is                permeable for air. An element is provided for                rotating the cylinder around the vertical axis. The                sorption chamber is delimited by portion of                circumferential wall and dividing wall. The                regeneration chamber is delimited by another                portion of circumferential wall and dividing wall.                An inlet is provided for receiving air located on                one end of the circumferential wall and an outlet                is for discharging air depleted in CO2 located on                the other end of the circumferential wall in the                sorption chamber. The device is configured to allow                air to flow from the inlet to the outlet though the                circumferential wall. An element is provided for                flowing stripping gas through the circumferential                wall into the regeneration chamber. The outlet is                for discharging CO2 located in the regeneration                chamber. The heating element is for heating the                regeneration chamber.
   USE - Device for capturing carbon dioxide from                air.
   ADVANTAGE - The thicknesses allow efficient passage of                gases with a minimal pressure drop over the                membrane while still affording efficient sorption                of the CO 2 molecules comprised in the air onto the                sorbent. The over beds of granular sorbents is                provided through which the air flows without                contacting the sorbent as such reducing the                interaction and lowering the sorption efficiency.                The operating costs of the device are significantly                reduced. The device is fully scalable to the needs                of the user.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process                for capturing CO2 from air.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                cylindrical device.
   Circumferential wall (a1)

PT P
PN RU2723509-C1
TI METHOD FOR MYOCARDIUM PROTECTION AGAINST REPERFUSION INJURIES IN HEART OPERATIONS (EMBODIMENTS).
AU KHUBULAVA G G
   VOLKOV A M
   ROMANOVSKII D YU
   BIRYUKOV A V
   SKIBRO I R
   BUTUZOV A G
   LYUBIMOV A I
   NAUMOV A B
   KUSAI A S
AE KIROV MILITARY MEDICAL ACAD (KIRO-Non-standard)
GA 202057925A
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to surgery, in particular to cardiosurgery. Before heart cavity opening, carbon dioxide gas is fed through rotameter into cavity of opened pericardium with pressure of 0.20.4 MPa and volume rate of 2-3 l/min. Carbon dioxide is supplied during the whole main stage of operation, and stops after sealing of heart cavities and removal of clamp from aorta. Second embodiment of the invention involves: prior to removal of a clamp from the aorta, retrograde thermal blood perfusion is performed with an IC apparatus with addition of magnesium at rate of 200 ml/min for 2 minutes. In the perfusate of the IC apparatus, the following parameters should be achieved by this time: hematocrit - 2530%, Ca2+ - 1.21.3 mmol/l, K+ - 4.55.5 mmol/l, Hb - 90100 g/l, glucose - norm, BE: + 23. After removal of a clamp from the aorta, complete IC with left ventricular unloading was continued. Duration of reperfusion depends on aortic clamping time and ranges from 15 to 25 minutes. Count is started after removing the clamp from the aorta and continued for not less than 10 minutes from the moment of restoration of the regular rhythm or the beginning of the temporary cardiac pacing. During this period cardiac preparations were not introduced. Reperfusion completion criteria are stable rhythm normalization, return of the ECG complex to the preoperative pattern, as well as adequate myocardial response to the volume load.
   USE - Medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - Method enables reducing the frequency of use, dosage and the number of antiarrhythmic and inotropic preparations when disconnected from artificial blood circulation, minimizing the effect of damaging factors on the myocardium in the first minutes after the period of global ischemia, in order to prevent and restore the function of reversibly depressed cardiomyocytes.

TI Energy-saving carbon dioxide (CO2) dual-machine dual-stage refrigeration multi-line system, has air outlet pipe that is arranged at bottom of regenerator is connected to air inlet pipe of subcritical compressor.
AU ZHAO D
   CAO D

AE NANJING JIUDING PRECISION MACHINERY REFR (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020403182
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a subcritical compressor (1), a mixing barrel (6), a transcritical compressor (7), an air storage tank (14), an intercooler (21) and a regenerator (23). A first gas cooler (4) is connected to the output pipe of the subcritical compressor. An oil separator (11) is connected to the output end of the transcritical compressor. A drying filter (17) is connected to the top air inlet pipe and the bottom air inlet pipe of the intercooler. The top air outlet pipe of the intercooler is connected to the side air inlet pipe of the mixing barrel. The air outlet pipe at the bottom of the intercooler is connected to the air inlet pipe at the top of the regenerator. The air coolers (25) are connected to the top air outlet of the regenerator. The air outlet pipes of the air coolers are connected in parallel to the air inlet pipe at the bottom of the regenerator. The air outlet pipe at the bottom of the regenerator is connected to the air inlet pipe of the subcritical compressor.

PN CN110964373-A
TI Antibacterial soybean ink comprises, gardenia blue pigment, alpha-D-Lactose monohydrate, chitosan, xanthan gum, and soybean oil binder including e.g. high-viscosity phenolic resin, soybean oil, tung oil, gelling agent and auxiliary resin.
AU YE J
AE NINGBO LONGMAO CULTURE & EDUCATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202029815U
AB    NOVELTY - Antibacterial soybean ink comprises 50-60 wt.% soybean oil binder, 0.1-1 wt.% gardenia blue pigment, 30-40 wt.% alpha-D-Lactose monohydrate, 5-10 wt.% chitosan, 0.1-0.5 wt.% xanthan gum, where soybean oil binder comprises 20-30 wt.% high-viscosity phenolic resin, 40-60 wt.% soybean oil, 10-30 wt.% tung oil, 1-3 wt.% auxiliary resin, 1-3 wt.% gelling agent and 1-3 wt.% acrylic modifier composition.
   USE - Used as antibacterial soybean ink.
   ADVANTAGE - The ink: ensures health and safety of printing workers and products, and low harm to human body; has low cost; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An independent claim is also included for preparing antibacterial soybean ink comprising (i) adding soybean oil and tung oil into the reactor, passing nitrogen or carbon dioxide, heating at 150 degrees C, adding high-viscosity phenolic resin, heating at 200 degrees C and adding acrylic modifier after dissolving resin, (ii) sampling to measure viscosity, heating at 220 degrees C, maintaining the temperature for more than 30 minutes and re-sampling to measure viscosity, (iii) cooling down to 200-150 degrees C, sampling to measure viscosity, adding gelling agent and heating for 30 minutes, (iv) adding auxiliary resin, cooling down to less than 100 degrees C, sampling to measure viscosity, turning off nitrogen or carbon dioxide, adjusting the viscosity by passing soybean oil, discharging, filtering to obtain soybean oil binder, and (v) mixing soybean oil binder, gardenia blue pigment, alpha-D-Lactose monohydrate, chitosan and xanthan gum, stirring and grinding to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The high-viscosity phenolic resin is high viscosity rosin modified alkyl phenolic resin and the gelling agent is organoaluminum. The rosin-modified alkylphenol of high viscosity phenolic resin includes para-tert-butylpheonol, 4-tert-octylphenol, p-nonylphenol and/or p-dodecylphenol. The auxiliary resin is soybean oil modified isophthalic acid resin. The mass ratio between tung oil and soybean oil is 1:2-4. Preferred Composition: The soybean oil binder comprises 24 wt.% high-viscosity phenolic resin 49 wt.% soybean oil, 21 wt.% tung oil, 2 wt.% auxiliary resin, 2 wt.% gelling agent and 2 wt.% acrylic modifier composition. The antibacterial soybean ink comprises 50 wt.% soybean oil binder, 0.3% gardenia blue pigment, 40 wt.% alpha-D-Lactose monohydrate, 9.5 wt.% chitosan and 0.2 wt.% xanthan gum. Preferred Method: The step (v) comprises mixing gardenia blue pigment, alpha-D-Lactose monohydrate, chitosan and xanthan gum in mixer, rolling the uniformly mixed slurry on three-roller machine, grinding and dispersing for 4-5 times, taking sample, and checking fineness up to the standard, adding drier and rolling again to measure the viscosity.

Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.);

PN WO2020053715-A1; CA3112528-A1; EP3849941-A1; CN113165870-A; US2022041441-A1
TI Producing synthesis gas from methane by contacting reactant gas stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and methane with catalyst comprising chemically inactive metal oxide core to produce gaseous product stream comprising hydrogen.
AU DSOUZA L
   LAKDAWALA S T
   HADHRAMI-AL A E
   AL-HOWAISH I K
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 2020219922
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing synthesis gas from methane involves contacting a reactant gas stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, and water with a catalyst material under conditions sufficient to produce a gaseous product stream comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide in a hydrogen/carbon monoxide molar ratio of 1.4-2, where the catalyst material comprises a chemically inactive metal oxide core, a redox metal oxide layer deposited on a surface of the metal oxide core, the redox metal oxide layer comprising a dopant and a catalytically active metal deposited on the surface of the redox metal oxide layer.
   USE - The method is useful for producing synthesis gas from methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts the catalyst which has increased mechanical strength and decreased cost during the preparation process when compared with currently available bi-reforming of methane-based catalyst, improved thermal integrity and high catalytic activity and excellent resistance to oxidation, coking and sintering in bi-reforming of methane reaction and can withstand conditions that promote both oxidation and/or carbon deposition, is economical, mechanically strong and highly efficient catalyst for use during a dry reformation of methane reaction and improves performance and quality of syngas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for direct reduction of iron ore comprising a reforming unit capable of producing synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide in the hydrogen/carbon monoxide molar ratio of 1.6-2 from the gaseous reactant stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane and water, where the reforming unit comprises a reaction zone comprising the gaseous reactant feed and a catalyst material and a furnace in fluid communication with the reformer, where the furnace is capable of reducing iron ore using the synthesis gas received from the reformer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reaction conditions comprise a temperature of 700-1000 degrees C, a pressure of 0.1-2 MPa and a gas hourly space velocity of 500-100000 h1. Preferred Method: The method involves contacting the catalyst at a temperature of at least 550 degrees C with a carbon dioxide stream comprising at least 50 vol.% carbon dioxide for at least 6 hours prior to contacting the catalyst with the gaseous reactant stream. The method further involves replacing a portion of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide stream with CTL, water, carbon monoxide and hydrogen to produce the gaseous reactant stream. The method involves replacing a portion of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide stream by (i) introducing methane to the carbon dioxide stream and contacting heated catalyst with the carbon dioxide/methane stream at the temperature of at least 600 degrees C for at least 1 hour, (ii) increasing the concentration of methane in the carbon dioxide/CTL stream relative to the amount of carbon dioxide over time to produce a carbon dioxide/CTL stream comprising equal amounts of carbon dioxide and methane, (iii) introducing water to the carbon dioxide/CTL stream at the temperature of at least 700 degrees C to form a carbon dioxide/CTL/water stream and (iv) introducing carbon monoxide and hydrogen to the carbon dioxide/methane/water stream, forming the gaseous reactant stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane and water at the temperature of at least 700 degrees C. The step (ii) further comprises increasing the temperature to 600 degrees C to at least 700 degrees C at a rate of 5-10 degrees C per hour, where the coke formation on the catalyst is substantially or completely inhibited and the pressure remains constant for at least 600 hours or at least 1200 hours. The method further involves comprising providing the product stream to a direct reduced iron unit and reducing iron oxide to iron. Preferred Components: The catalyst has a core/shell structure, where the redox-metal oxide layer surrounds the core and preferably the core is an alumina or alkaline earth metal aluminate core. The alkaline earth metal aluminate core is magnesium aluminate, calcium aluminate, strontium aluminate, barium aluminate or their combination. The alkaline earth metal aluminate core is magnesium aluminate. The redox-metal oxide layer is a cerium oxide layer. The metal dopant is niobium, indium, lanthanum, gallium or their alloy and combination, where the active metal is nickel. The chemically inactive metal oxide core is alumina or magnesium aluminate. Preferred Composition: The reactant stream comprises 25-40 vol.% hydrogen, 5-30 vol.% carbon monoxide, 5-20 vol.% carbon monoxide, 10-30 vol.% methane and 10-30 vol.% water. The reactant stream comprises 30-35 vol.% hydrogen, 10-20 vol.% carbon monoxide, 10-15 vol.% carbon monoxide, 15-20 vol.% CTL and 15-20 vol.% water. The hydrogen/carbon monoxide molar ratio is 1.6-2, preferably 1.85. The chemically inactive metal oxide core contains 65-85 wt.% alumina or magnesium aluminate. The redox-metal oxide layer contains 10-20 wt.% cerium oxide. The nickel is present in an amount of 5-10 wt.%, where 0.5-2 wt.% niobium or indium is incorporated into the lattice framework of the cerium oxide layer. Preferred Property: The redox-metal oxide layer has a thickness of 1-500 nm, preferably 1-10 nm.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC E31-A01; H04-E04; H04-F02E; H04-F06; N01-B; N01-C02; N01-C03; N02-C01; N03-A01; N03-A02A; N03-C03; N03-G01; N03-G04; N06-C07; N06-C09; N07-J

PT P
PN WO2020028963-A1; BR102018016306-A2; AR115933-A1; AU2019317222-A1; CA3112134-A1; CN113164918-A; US2021362129-A1; BR102018016306-B1
TI Catalytic mixture comprises nanocatalyst and low-value carbonaceous raw material as a fuel selected from petroleum coke, coal, and/or residual heavy oil fraction.
AU FERRAZ DE SOUZA W
   PASSOS DE SOUZA V
   ANDRADE RODRIGUES M
   DE ALMEIDA DUMANI DOS SANTOS
   DE SOUZA V P
   RODRIGUES M A
   DE ALMEIDA DUMANI DOS SAN
   FERRAZ D S W
   PASSOS D S V
   ANDRADE R M
   DE ALMEIDA D D S
   SOUZA W F D
   SOUZA V P D
   SANTOS A D A D
AE PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
   PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
   PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
GA 202013948G
AB    NOVELTY - A catalytic mixture comprises 1-50 wt.% nanocatalyst and 50-99 wt.% low-value carbonaceous raw material as a fuel selected from petroleum coke, coal, and/or residual heavy oil fraction, based on the total weight of the catalytic mixture. Nanocatalyst consists of 80-99.99 wt.% carbon nanomaterial and 0.01-20 wt.% greater than or equal to 1 alkali metal based on total weight of nanocatalyst, where specific surface area of nanocatalyst is 400-1300 m2/g.
   USE - A catalytic mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon nanomaterial presents regions made up of polycondensed aromatic ring systems. These regions can provide attractive intermolecular interactions of pi - pi type with aromatic structures of the carbonaceous raw material, dispersed in the reaction medium during the gasification process. The intermolecular interactions allow the optimization of the contact of these aromatic structures with the catalytic sites of alkali metals present on the nanocatalyst surface, which makes it possible to achieve better conversion results for the carbonaceous raw material to be gasified. The nanocatalyst combines the large specific surface area of carbon nanomaterials with the presence of catalytic alkali metal sites favorable to the gasification reaction. The gasification process obtains greater conversions at the same temperature, or even equal conversions at a lower temperature, when compared to processes that do not use the catalytic mixtures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for gasification process of a catalytic mixture which involves introducing the mixture in an aerator; heating mixture in the presence of an oxidizing agent selected from air, pure oxygen, carbon dioxide, and/or water vapor to 200-1300 degrees C; and obtaining gaseous product comprising hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), and methane (CH4).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Nanocatalyst consists of 95-99.99 wt.% carbon nanomaterial and 0.01-5 wt.% greater than or equal to 1 alkali metal based on total weight of nanocatalyst. Carbon nanomaterial is selected from nanospheres, nanofilaments, nanotubes, or graphenes, preferably nanospheres and nanofilaments. Carbon nanomaterial comprises alkali metals selected from sodium, potassium, rubidium, and/or cesium. Carbonaceous raw material of fossil origin is petroleum coke, coal, and/or residual heavy petroleum fraction. Mixture comprises 50% nanocatalyst and 50% carbonaceous raw material of fossil origin. Preferred Properties: Nanocatalyst has specific surface area of 500-800 m2/g. Preferred Condition: Step of heating the mixture is carried out at 900-1200 degrees C. Preferred Process: Oxidizing agent may be diluted in an inert gas selected from noble gas.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN110669663-A
TI Directional fixed enzyme membrane reactor comprises pervaporation membrane module comprising pervaporation membrane compounded with microporous filter membrane.
AU YE P
   ZHU D
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG SCI-TECH (UZST-C)
GA 202006171L
AB    NOVELTY - Directional fixed enzyme membrane reactor comprises pervaporation membrane module, where the pervaporation membrane module comprises pervaporation membrane compounded with a microporous filter membrane, where the microporous filter membrane is carbon dioxide cascade reaction catalyzed by methanol which is sequentially fixed with three multi-enzyme/metal-organic framework (MOF) complexes, where the multi-enzymes in the multi-enzyme/MOF complex are formate dehydrogenase, formaldehyde dehydrogenase, ethanol dehydrogenase and glutamate, amino acid dehydrogenase and reduced coenzyme I, and embedding three kinds of multiple enzymes in MOF.
   USE - As directional fixed enzyme membrane reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for preparing directional fixed enzyme membrane reactor, involves dissolving three combined enzyme molecules in deionized water to prepare three multi-enzyme solutions, mixing zinc nitrate solution and 2-methylimidazole solution with the three multi-enzyme solutions, reacting at room temperature for at least 30 minutes, maintaining the mixture for 3-5 hours, centrifuging mixture at 6000 revolutions per minute (rpm) for at least 10 minutes to collect product, washing the mixture with deionized water for at least three times, dispersing products in deionized water, freeze-drying to obtain white powder, mixing white powder with deionized water, uniformly dispersing by ultrasound to obtain three kinds of multi-enzyme/MOF complex solution, sequentially filtering and fixing three multi-enzyme/MOF complex solutions on the microporous membrane, washing with deionized water at least twice, compounding microporous membrane on the pervaporation membrane, and fixing pervaporation membrane in the pervaporation membrane module; and
   (2) method for preparing methanol using the enzyme membrane reactor, involves obtaining 2-5 ml glutamic acid and 2-5 ml NADH solution, mixing the materials with carbon dioxide gas for at least 30 minutes, subjecting reaction solution to an enzyme-catalyzed reaction through enzyme membrane reactor, and collecting permeate passing through the enzyme membrane reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The pervaporation membrane is polydimethoxysiloxane/polyvinylidene fluoride (PDMS/PVDF) membrane. The microporous filter membrane is PVDF membrane, multi-enzyme/MOF complex and microporous filter membrane fixed by suction filtration. The ZIF-8 is used as the MOF in the multi-enzyme/MOF complex. The pore size of the microporous filter membrane is 1-3 mu m, and all the multiple enzyme/MOF complexes on the unit area microporous filter membrane fixed amount is 0.1-0.5 mg/cm2. The permeation flux of the enzyme membrane reactor is 10-50 g/m2.hour. The concentration of the zinc nitrate solution is 0.2-0.5 mol/l. The concentration of the 2-methylimidazole solution is 1.11-1.42 mol/l. The volume ratio of zinc nitrate solution, 2-methylimidazole solution and multi-enzyme solution is 2:10:1. The liquid storage tank is further provided on one side of the enzyme membrane reactor, and the enzyme membrane reactor is connected to the storage liquid through a feeding pipe, a material liquid pump, and a liquid storage tank. The other side of the enzyme membrane reactor is provided with a cold trap, a drying tower, a buffer bottle and a vacuum pump, and the enzyme membrane reactor passes through the first two discharge pipes are connected to the cold trap, and a pump is arranged between the cold trap and the drying tower.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC D05-A01A3; D05-A01A5; D05-A01B1; D05-A03; E10-E04E1; E11-M; A04-E10B; A12-W11A; A12-W11K; A12-W11L

PN CN110665363-A; CN110665363-B
TI Combined smell-eliminating agent for use in packaging box, comprises photochlorophyll, lavender flavor, bamboo charcoal, quicklime, volatile oil and soap base, where volatile oil is mixture of aliphatic compound and aromatic compound.
AU 
AE SHEN H (SHEN-Individual)
   GUANGDONG ZHIJIA ZHIXIN TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202007752D
AB    NOVELTY - A combined smell-eliminating agent comprises 5-10 pts. wt. photochlorophyll, 5-10 pts. wt. lavender flavor, 30-40 pts. wt. bamboo charcoal, 40-50 pts. wt. quicklime, 10-15 pts. wt. volatile oil and 40-50 pts. wt. soap base. The bamboo charcoal particle has a diameter of 2-5 mm, and the volatile oil is a mixture of an aliphatic compound and an aromatic compound.
   USE - Combined smell-eliminating agent for use in packaging box (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method of making the combined smell-eliminating agent, which involves contacting the supercritical carbon dioxide with lavender petals under high pressure, dissolving the lavender petals, reducing the density of the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid by depressurizing and increasing the temperature, and precipitating the lavender essence to complete the extraction of the lavender essence, placing the soap base into a heating bucket and heating until it completely melts into a liquid state, when the temperature of the soap base drops to 32-38 degrees C, using the prepared copper hydroxide and placing solution and photochlorophyll into a heating bucket together with the soap base, where the soap base is made of transparent and non-dyed soap base, soaking the bamboo charcoal in a mixed solution of volatile oil and lavender essence, and removing the bamboo charcoal, when the bamboo charcoal precipitates in the mixed solution, injecting the soap-based solution into the compression mold, when the temperature of the soap-based solution drops to room temperature, evenly sprinkling the bamboo charcoal on the surface of the soap-based surface, and finally pressing into a block.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The quicklime is a rectangular parallelepiped with a length of 8-10 cm, a width of 4-6 cm, and a thickness of 1-1.5 cm, attached the soap-based block to the surface of the quicklime block, and vacuum-tightly package with a plastic film.

PN CN110541311-A
TI Mat comprises mat body comprising fabric layer and functional layer coated on the fabric layer woven from warp and weft.
AU LIN M
   WEI B
AE NINGBO DONGFANG SEATS IND CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2019A51746
AB    NOVELTY - Mat comprises mat body comprising a fabric layer and a functional layer coated on the fabric layer. The fabric layer is woven from warp and weft. The functional layer includes 1.8-6.6 pts. wt. but-2,3-dienenitrile, 2.4-7.8 pts. wt. palmitic acid, 2.5-4.5 pts. wt. methyl acrylate, 3-16 pts. wt. phosphatidylethanolamine, 0.5-5 pts. wt. nano silicon fluoride powder, 15-35 pts. wt. polyurethane resin and 6-15 pts. wt. composite cage silsesquioxane.
   USE - Used as mat.
   ADVANTAGE - The mat has self-cleaning and waterproof functions, stable performance and long service life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the mat, comprising (i) placing 3.5-15 pts. wt. warp in a reaction kettle, and passing carbon dioxide into the reaction kettle, heating and boosting to maintain in the supercritical state for 1-3 minutes, swelling and relieving pressure, adding 2-5 pts. wt. composite cage silsesquioxane and 5-15 pts. wt. benzoic acid, introducing carbon dioxide and heating, maintaining the supercritical state for 1.5-3.5 minutes to obtain modified warp, (ii) adding polyvinylidene fluoride, polytetrafluoroethylene, tert-butyl peroxy propionate, dichloromethane, polyethylene oxide, silicon carbide fiber, diatomaceous earth, polyethylene glycol and composite cage silsesquioxane, mechanically stirring and uniformly mixing, granulating by twin screw extrusion at 200-220 degrees C to obtain wire drawing to obtain weft, (iii) taking butane-2,3-dienenitrile, palmitic acid, methyl acrylate, phosphatidylethanolamine, nano silicon fluoride powder, and polyurethane resin and composite cage silsesquioxane, mechanically stirring and mixing to obtain mixed emulsion, and(iv) weaving the warp threads modified in the step (i) and the weft threads obtained in the step (ii) on a knitting machine to obtain fabric layer, coating the mixed emulsion obtained in the step (iii) on the surface of the fabric layer, and drying to obtain mat.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weft comprises 80-90 pts. wt. polyvinylidene fluoride, 20-30 pts. wt. polytetrafluoroethylene, 3-5 pts. wt. tert-butyl peroxy propionate, 7.8-15.2 pts. wt. dichloromethane, 3.5-8.5 pts. wt. polyethylene oxide, 1.5-5 pts. wt. silicon carbide fiber, 0.8-4.9 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth, 6-12 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol and 5-18 pts. wt. composite cage silsesquioxane. The composite cage silsesquioxane is includes polyfluoro cage silsesquioxane with mass ratio of 1:1 and octacyclohexylamine cage silsesquioxane containing ionic liquid. The substituents of the eight vertices of the polyfluorocage silsesquioxane are dodecylfluoroheptylpropyl. The substituents at the eight vertices of the octacyclohexylamino cage silsesquioxane are all cyclohexylaminopropyl. The volume ratio of the methanol, cyclohexylaminopropyltrimethoxysilane and hydrochloric acid is 100:3-15:1-10. The molar ratio of the ammonium tetrafluoroborate and (2-(perfluorooctyl) ethyl) dimethylchlorosilane to the octacyclohexylamino cage silsesquioxane is 1.1-1.3:1.1-1.3:1. The volume and mass ratio of the toluene and the octacyclohexylamine cage silsesquioxane is 5-20:1 ml/g. The warp threads are twisted yarns includes rattan, reed, cattail or bamboo strip. Preferred Method: The octacyclohexylamine cage silsesquioxane containing ionic liquid is prepared by (i) adding methanol to the reaction kettle, adding cyclohexylaminopropyltrimethoxysilane and hydrochloric acid under magnetic stirring, (ii) heating and refluxing the reaction at 80 degrees C for 24 hours, (iii) adding tetrahydrofuran, recrystallizing, and then washing with methanol, vacuum drying at 50 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain octacyclohexylamine cage silsesquioxane, and(iv) adding the octacyclohexylamino cage silsesquioxane obtained in the step (iii) to mixed solution consisting of ammonium tetrafluoroborate, (2-(perfluorooctyl) ethyl) dimethylchlorosilane and toluene, carrying out magnetic stirring reaction at constant temperature of 70 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain octacyclohexylamine cage silsesquioxane containing ionic liquid.
