PN WO2019197257-A1; AU2019251704-A1; CA3096271-A1; BR112020020505-A2; EP3775113-A1; AU2019251704-B2; JP2021521078-W
TI Producing hydrogen and carbon products by converting natural gas in reaction zone to produce gas stream and carbon product, separating product and stream, and converting portion of gas stream to produce another gas stream and carbon product.
AU SPANU L
   MESTERS C M A
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 2019863395
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen and carbon products are produced by (a) converting natural gas in 1st reaction zone under 1st reaction conditions to produce 1st gas stream and 1st carbon product; (b) separating greater than or equal to 1 portion of 1st carbon product from 1st gas stream; and (c) converting greater than or equal to 1 portion of 1st gas stream in 2nd reaction zone under 2nd reaction conditions to produce a 2nd gas stream and 2nd carbon product.
   USE - Production of hydrogen and carbon products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not form carbon dioxide, thus, there is no need to separate carbon dioxide from hydrogen before using used in other reactions. Any unreacted methane will not negatively impact most downstream processes, including ammonia synthesis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for producing hydrogen and solid carbon which involves (a) contacting natural gas with a catalyst selected from iron, nickel, and/or cobalt in first reaction zone where the temperature is 700-1200 degrees C to produce 1st gas stream comprising hydrogen and unreacted natural gas and 1st solid carbon product comprising carbon nanotubes; (b) separating greater than or equal to 1 portion of carbon nanotubes from 1st gas stream in gas/solid separation apparatus; and (c) passing greater than or equal to 1 portion of unreacted natural gas and hydrogen through a 2nd reaction zone comprising a molten salt/metal bed where molten salt/metal bed comprises metals selected from iron, cobalt, nickel, tin, bismuth, indium, gallium, copper, lead, molybdenum, tungsten and/or salts selected from alkaline halides and alkaline earth halides in which temperature in 2nd reaction zone is 700-1200 degrees C to produce 2nd gas stream comprising hydrogen and unreacted natural gas and 2nd solid carbon product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The 1st reaction zone is fluidized bed reactor. The first reaction zone contains a supported catalyst, and catalyst comprising a transition metal or transition metal compound. The transition metal compound is iron, nickel or cobalt. The natural gas comprises methane and the 1st reaction conditions are selected such that the amount of methane converted in 1st reaction zone is 3-25 wt.%. The first gas stream comprises hydrogen and methane. The first carbon product comprises carbon nanotubes. The second reaction zone comprises a molten metal and/or molten salt. The molten metal is selected from iron, cobalt, nickel, tin, bismuth, indium, gallium, copper, lead, molybdenum, and/or tungsten. The molten salt is selected from lithium chloride, sodium chloride, potassium chloride, cesium chloride, magnesium chloride, calcium chloride, strontium chloride, and/or barium chloride. The second gas stream contains greater than or equal to 50 vol.% hydrogen. Preferred Parameters: The first reaction conditions comprise a temperature of greater than or equal to 600 degrees C, preferably 700-1300 degrees C. The second reaction conditions comprise a temperature of 600-1300 degrees C. The 2nd reaction conditions are selected such that the amount of methane converted in the 2nd reaction zone is 50-100 wt.%. The 2nd carbon product has a lower density than the molten metal and/or molten salt. Preferred Process: The natural gas conversion in 1st reaction zone is carried out in the absence of oxygen and water. Separation is carried out in a cyclone or electrostatic precipitator. The method further comprises separating the 2nd carbon product from molten metal and/or molten salt.

PN IN322169-B
TI Making diflunisal, comprises e.g. reacting (difluorophenyl)phenol with bromine in presence of acid and isolating bromo-(difluorophenyl)phenol and treating resultant with ethyl halide in presence of inorganic base and phase transfer catalyst.
AU GURJAR M K
   KALIAPERUMAL N
   BALASUBRAMANIAN P
   MUGALE B R
   BUCHUDE S B
   PANCHABHAI P P
   KALHAPURE V K
   AHIRRAO P P
   MEHTA S S
AE EMCURE PHARM LTD (EMCU-Non-standard)
GA 2019876261
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing diflunisal (I), comprises: (a) reacting 4-(2,4-difluorophenyl)phenol (II) with bromine in presence of an acid and isolating 2-bromo-4-(2,4-difluorophenyl)phenol (III); (b) treating (III) with ethyl halide in presence of an inorganic base and a phase transfer catalyst to give 2-bromo-4-(2,4-difluorophenyl)ethoxybenzene (IV); (c) reacting (IV) with magnesium and carbon dioxide in an organic solvent to give 2-ethoxy-5-(2,4-difluorophenyl)benzoic acid (V); and (d) deprotection of (V) with a mixture of hydrobromic acid and acetic acid and isolation of (I) of desired purity.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing diflunisal which is used as a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug for treating moderate pain, osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis. No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is safe and industrially applicable process for synthesis of (I); and avoids use of hazardous, high pressure reaction for introduction of carboxy in the desired molecule.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing diflunisal of formula (I), comprises: (a) reacting 4-(2,4-difluorophenyl)phenol of formula (II) with bromine in presence of an acid and isolating 2-bromo-4-(2,4-difluorophenyl)phenol of formula (III); (b) treating (III) with ethyl halide in presence of an inorganic base and a phase transfer catalyst to give 2-bromo-4-(2,4-difluorophenyl)ethoxybenzene of formula (IV); (c) reacting (IV) with magnesium and carbon dioxide in an organic solvent to give 2-ethoxy-5-(2,4-difluorophenyl)benzoic acid of formula (V); and (d) deprotection of (V) with a mixture of hydrobromic acid and acetic acid and isolation of (I) of desired purity. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for 2-bromo-4-(2,4-difluorophenyl)ethoxybenzene (IV) and a process for its preparation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The (III) is isolated by adding either aqueous methanol or water. In the process, (I) is isolated from the reaction mixture by concentrating the ethyl acetate extract, adding toluene to the residue, and partially concentrating the mixture to give (I) of desired purity. Preferred Components: The acid is either a protic acid or a sulfonic acid or a Lewis acid, preferably para toluenesulfonic acid or acetic acid or a Lewis acid. The ethyl halide is ethyl bromide or ethyl chloride. The organic solvent is tetrahydrofuran, diethyl ether or diphenyl ether.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Analgesic; Antiarthritic; Osteopathic; Antirheumatic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Stirred mixture of 130.1 g 2-ethoxy-5-(2,4-difluorophenyl)benzoic acid, 2600 ml hydrobromic acid and 1300 ml acetic acid were heated to 100-110 degrees C till completion of reaction as monitored by thin-layer chromatography. The reaction mass was then cooled and extracted with ethyl acetate, the organic layer was separated and concentrated. If required, 2500 ml toluene was added to the residue and the obtained mixture was heated to reflux, and concentrated partially, which on cooling yielded diflunisal with desired purity.

TI Preparing positive electrode material precursor            for lithium battery having meso structure, involves            mixing lithium salt solution and            nickel-cobalt-manganese mixture, and adjusting pH by            carbon dioxide-lithium hydroxide buffer system.
AU REN Y
   ZOU T
AE CHENGDU YOUNIRUIKE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201984136H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a positive electrode material                precursor for a lithium battery having a                mesostructure, involves mixing lithium salt                solution and a nickel-cobalt-manganese mixture, and                adjusting pH to 5.5-7.5 by a carbon dioxide-lithium                hydroxide buffer system at 1.01-10.0 mega Pascal at                30-80 degrees C, 100-500r/min and stirring for 2-5                hours. The molar ratio of the lithium salt solution                to the nickel-cobalt-manganese mixture is 0.01-2.                The two steps are co-synthesized at 70-120 degrees                C, and obtained product is concentrated to a volume                reduction of 1/2-2/3, and the protective gas is                passed to maintain the pressure at 1.01-10.0 mega                Pascal, and then treated at 30-80 degrees C at                100-500 revolution per minute, aging for 5-24                hours, performing solid-liquid separation,                collecting solid phase products. The solid phase                product is placed in a vacuum or a protective gas                and pyrolyzed and dried at 90-130 degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing positive electrode                material precursor for lithium battery having meso                structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a positive                electrode material precursor reaction process is                used to prepare has nano-sized, independent and                meso structured lithium-nickel-cobalt-manganese                quaternary precursor spherical particle, simplify                reaction control and cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing a lithium battery positive electrode                material precursor having a mesostructure, which                involves:
   (A) adding an excess of calcium hydroxide or                ammonium hydrogencarbonate solution to separate                liquid phase product; and
   (B) forming calcium-based precipitate and a                lithium hydroxide solution and discarding                precipitate by filtration, and returning obtained                lithium hydroxide solution to the use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The molar ratio of                  the lithium salt solution and the                  nickel-cobalt-manganese mixture is 0.8. The                  lithium salt solution has a concentration of                  0.1-15 mole per liter. The                  nickel-cobalt-manganese mixed solution has a                  concentration of 0.1-10 mole per liter. The molar                  ratio of Ni2+, Mn2+ and Co2+ in the                  nickel-cobalt-manganese mixture is (1-x-y):x:y,                  where x is from 0.01-0.99 and y is 0.01-0.99.                  Preferred Components: The nickel-cobalt-manganese                  mixture comprises a nickel salt, a cobalt salt                  and a manganese salt dissolved in water. The                  carbon dioxide gas in the carbon dioxide-lithium                  hydroxide buffer system is excessively added, and                  the flow rate is 2-5 cubic meter per hour and the                  concentration of the lithium hydroxide solution                  is 0.1-10 mole per liter. Preferred Process: The                  obtained product is concentrated at lower than                  100 degrees C, and carried out by positive                  pressure evaporation.

   (2) a load bearing shell.

PN TW201928266-A; TW638121-B1
TI Air purifying and cleaning system and method thereof characterized in that the dirt is intercepted and filtered by the filtering nets arranged layer by layer, thereby reducing PM2.5, formaldehyde and carbon dioxide in the air and ensuring the purifying quality of the air.
AU XU W
AE XU W (XUWW-Individual)
GA 2019A25033
AB    NOVELTY - The present invention provides an air purifying and cleaning system and a method thereof. The air purifying and cleaning system mainly includes: a main machine body whose two ends are respectively provided with an air inlet channel and an air outlet channel, wherein the main machine body is provided with a water storage chamber for receiving an environment-friendly washing liquid, the interior of the air inlet channel is spacedly installed with a plurality of air cleaning layers, each layer is provided with a cleaning net, an air inlet of the air inlet channel is correspondingly provided with air inlet equipment, the interior of the air outlet channel is spacedly installed with a plurality of air filtering layers, each layer is provided with a filtering net, and the outer end of the air outlet channel is provided with an air outlet; and a cleaning equipment connected between the water storage chamber and the air inlet channel. Therefore, dirty air can go through the air inlet channel and be adsorbed and cleaned by the wetted cleaning net layers in the air inlet channel, so as to effectively filter and remove the dirt and reduce the content of PM2.5, formaldehyde and carbon dioxide in the air. Moreover, the dirt is intercepted and filtered by the filtering nets arranged layer by layer in the air outlet channel at the other side, so that fine dirt in the cleaned air cannot be taken away by the moisture in the air again, thereby ensuring the purifying quality of the air at the air outlet .

PN CN109160726-A; CN109160726-B
TI High performance glass comprises silicon dioxide, sodium oxide, potassium oxide, magnesium oxide and nano-composite, prepared by mixing silicon dioxide, sodium oxide, potassium oxide, magnesium oxide and nano-composite.
AU CHEN R
   CHEN H
AE JIANGSU HUAOU GLASS CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019064551
AB    NOVELTY - High performance glass comprises 50-60 pts. wt. silicon dioxide, 7-15 pts. wt. sodium oxide, 1-3 pts. wt. potassium oxide, 3-6 pts. wt. magnesium oxide and 4-6 pts. wt. nano-composite; and the preparation of nano-composite includes mixing ceric ammonium nitrate, iron(II) chloride, polyethyleneimine and water in a ratio of 3:0.5-1.5:13:87, then introducing an inert gas and heating at 55-65 degrees C under stirring, after 50-70 minutes, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the solution under an inert gas atmosphere, after 10-15 minutes, stopping introducing carbon dioxide gas, heating at 80-90 degrees C and insulating for 60-80 minutes to obtain precursor solution, then mixing precursor solution and the ethanol solution in a volume ratio of 3:1, and then transferring to a hydrothermal kettle, heating at 120-150 degrees C for 2-3 hours, then cooling, filtering, taking the solid and drying to obtain nanocomposite.
   USE - Used as high performance glass.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-performance glass has excellent ultraviolet and infrared resistance, excellent light transmittance, high flexural strength and excellent acid resistance in the visible range.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing high performance glass, comprising (i) uniformly mixing silicon dioxide, sodium oxide, potassium oxide and magnesium oxide, heating at 1300-1400 degrees C, and melting into a glass liquid, (ii) adding nanocomposite to glass liquid prepared in step 1(i), stirring uniformly, and heating at 1600-1700 degrees C, and (iii) cooling the molten glass obtained in the step (ii) at 1100-1200 degrees C, and then tin groove molding, cooling and annealing to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inert gas is nitrogen or argon. The mass ratio between ceric ammonium nitrate, iron(II) chloride, polyethyleneimine and water is 3:1:13:87. The high performance glass further includes 1.7 pts. wt. nickel(II) oxide. The high performance glass further includes 2.2 pts. wt. clarifying agent, the clarifying agent includes 1.2 pts. wt. sodium sulfate, 0.8 pts. wt. ammonium nitrate and 0.2 pts. wt. sodium fluorosilicate. Preferred Method: The stirring speed of the stirring condition is 120-150 revolutions/minute.

PN CN108976628-A; CN108976628-B
TI Expanded polystyrene plate comprises polystyrene resin, brominated epoxy resin, polycarbonate, sodium stearate, zinc oxide, silicone oil, nucleating agent, and antioxidant, where nucleating agent is talcum powder, graphitefig.
AU PENG Q
AE WUHU XINJINGQIAO PACKAGING TECHNOLOGY CO (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018A26135
AB    NOVELTY - Expanded polystyrene plate comprises 40-50 pts. wt. polystyrene resin, 10-12 pts. wt. brominated epoxy resin, 4-6 pts. wt. polycarbonate, 1-2 pts. wt. sodium stearate, 2-3 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 1-3 pts. wt. silicone oil, 2-3 pts. wt. nucleating agent, and 0.8-1.4 pts. wt. antioxidant.
   USE - Expanded polystyrene plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The expanded polystyrene plate has abrasion resistance, aging resistance and flame retardant property, improves porosity density and earthquake-reducing effect of the plate, increases compressive and reboundability, and strengthens toughness of the material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing expanded polystyrene plate, which involves:
   (A) mixing polystyrene resin and polycarbonate, adding in reaction kettle, heating to 180-200 degrees C, mixing and stirring uniformly to obtain mixed resin;
   (B) cooling in reaction kettle and pressurizing resin and introducing supercritical carbon dioxide, mixing and stirring for 1-2 hours, slowly raising temperature after foaming process;
   (C) reducing temperature in reaction kettle to 70-80 degrees C, adding sodium stearate, silicone oil, nucleating agent and antioxidant uniformly, keeping temperature and stirring;
   (D) introducing supercritical carbon dioxide in reaction kettle, after keeping temperature for 1-2 hours, heating to 160-200 degrees C, foaming for 5-10 minutes to obtain foaming material;
   (E) mixing bromized epoxy resin and zinc oxide, putting in reaction kettle, raising the temperature to 350-400 degrees C, stirring uniformly, and keeping for 40-50 minutes to obtain mixed slurry; and
   (F) placing foaming material in mold, steam extrusion forming after the mixed slurry, uniformly spraying the plate forming, cooling and forming to obtain expanded polystyrene compressive plate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The nucleating agent is talcum powder, graphite and mica in ratio 2:1:1. Preferred Condition: The stirring time of mixed resin is 20-30 minutes. The pressurizing temperature is 40-60 degrees C. The pressure is 12-14 megapascal. The heating speed is 2 degrees C/second. The heating temperature is 140-160 degrees C for 10-15 minutes. The temperature of steam molding is 180-200 degrees C. The pressure is 0.5-0.7 megapascal. The cooling temperature of molded plate is 120-160 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN108947234-A
TI Preparing glass cup involves dispersing silicon dioxide uniformly into hexamethyldisilazane solution, filtering, drying, and putting into supercritical device in carbon dioxide as processing medium to obtain modified silicon dioxide.
AU QIAN S
AE QIAN S (QIAN-Individual)
GA 2018A0544C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing glass cup involves dispersing silicon dioxide uniformly into 3.5-5.5 wt.% hexamethyldisilazane solution for 40-50 minutes, filtering, drying it to constant weight, putting into supercritical device in a carbon dioxide as processing medium at a pressure of 15-18MPa and at a temperature of 155-160 degrees C for 15-20 minutes to obtain a modified silicon dioxide. The perlite is grounded to 300 mesh, mixed with 0.5-0.8 wt.% a sodium glutamate solution at a ratio of 150g:420mL at 68-72 degrees C for 1.5 hours, and filtered, washed once with water, immersed in absolute ethanol for 40 minutes at room temperature, filtered, dried to constant weight, calcined at 580-600 degrees C for 20 minutes, and then cooled to 40 degrees C. The method involves using plasma processing instrument for low temperature plasma treatment of perlite for 10-12 minutes at a voltage of 110V to obtain final modified perlite.
   USE - Method for preparing glass cup.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare glass cup, which has good rigidity, good wear resistance, good falling-proof performance, and is scraped easily.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The modified silicon dioxide and modified perlite are mixed at a mass ratio of 100:12-15, added to furnace, added with 6 wt.% calcium oxide and 0.1-0.3 wt.% calcium stearate, and then sealed and heated to a mixture temperature of 1620-1650 degrees C for 8-10 hours until the mixture reaches a molten state to obtain molten glass. The molten glass is sent to a molding machine, extruded, made into glass cups of different specifications, naturally cooled to hardening and then sent to an annealing furnace for annealing for 30-40 minutes to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Conditions: The silicon dioxide has a particle size of 250-300 mesh. The hexamethyldisilazane solution is prepared by dissolving hexamethyldisilazane in acetone. The perlite has particle size of 400 mesh.
DC L01 (Glass - includes chemical compositions, batch treatment, furnaces, flat glass forming, hollow-ware forming, postforming and glass/ceramics, but not lens designs, bottling, bottle-washing, closures for containers, glazing designs, glass cutting, chamfering edges, printing on glass, disposing of used glass or the production of pure sodium silicate. Chemical aspects of optical fibres (C03).)

GA 201890020K
AB    NOVELTY - Oligomeric multi center metalloporphyrin complex (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for preparing polycarbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has high product selectivity, high temperature stability in catalyzing copolymerization. The method: uses carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide copolymerization products having number average molecular weight of 86,000-218000 g/mol, and molecular weight distribution of 1.13-1.22; and produces by-product of less than 1%, even less than 0.01%; content of carbonate units in the polymer reaches to 78-99%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Oligomeric multi center metalloporphyrin complex of formula (I) is new.
   Z=sulfur substituted alkyl group of formula (II) or of formula (III);
   Rc=2-12C alkyl;
   q=0 or 1;
   Rd=1-12C alkyl;
   R=cyano substituted alkyl group of formula (IV) or 2-15C substituted carboxy;
   Rp and Rq=H, 1-12C alkyl or 2-15C carboxy;
   Ra, Rb, R1-R19=H, halo, optionally substituted aliphatic group, aryl or heteroaryl;
   m=2-50;
   dotted line=linking group e.g. carbonyl substituted alkyl of formula (V);
   A=metal complex of formula (VI);
   M=metal element;
   h=0 or 1;
   X=halo, -NO3, CH3C(=O)O-, CCl3C(=O)O-, CF3C(=O)O-, ClO4-, BF4-, BPh4-, CN, -N3, p-toluic acid group, p-toluenesulfonic acid group, O-nitrophenol oxyanion, p-nitrophenol oxyanion, m-nitrophenol oxyanion, 2,4- nitrophenol oxyanion, 3,5-nitrophenol oxyanion, 2,4,6-nitrophenol oxyanion, 3,5-dichlorophenol oxyanion, 3, 5-difluorophenol oxyanion, 3,5-bis-trifluoromethylphenol oxyanion or pentafluorophenol oxyanion; and
   n=1-16.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing polycarbonate, comprising copolymerizing carbon dioxide and epoxide under action of co-catalyst and (I) as main catalyst to obtain polycarbonate, where the co-catalyst includes quaternary ammonium salt, quaternary phosphonium salt and/or organic base, and the ratio of main catalyst to co-catalyst is 1:0.1-5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of main catalyst to epoxide is 1:2000-500000. The co-catalyst is tetraethylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium chloride, tetrabutylammonium hydrogen sulfate, bistriphenylphosphine chloride, bistriphenylphosphine bromide, bistriphenylphosphinonitroamine, 1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)decan-5-ene/4-dimethylaminopyridine and/or gamma -chloropropylmethyldimethoxysilane.

Preferred Method: The temperature of the copolymerization reaction is 20-150 degrees C, and the time of the copolymerization reaction is 0.1-12 hours.
   Preferred Definitions:
   metal element=magnesium, aluminum, zinc, chromium, manganese, iron, cobalt, titanium, palladium, nickel or ruthenium.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 4 Compounds (I) are specifically claimed which are 7-(dodecylthiocarbonothioylthio)-3-((6-hydroxyhexyloxy)carbonyl)-2,2,3-trimethylheptanoic acid-porphyrin-aluminum chloride complex (Ia), 9-(dodecylthiocarbonothioylthio)-3-((4-hydroxybutoxy)carbonyl)-2,2,3-trimethylnonanoic acid-porphyrin-aluminum chloride complex, 4-hydroxybutyl 2-(2-cyanopropan-2-yl)-2-methyl-10-(phenylthiocarbonothioylthio)decanoate-porphyrin-aluminum chloride complex and 4-cyano-11-(dodecylthiocarbonothioylthio)-5-((4-hydroxybutoxy)carbonyl)-4,5-dimethylundecanoic acid-porphyrin-cobalt-2,4-dinitrophenol complex.

TI Counterflow fire tube boiler system, has peripheral tube for connecting exhaust line through upstream end to evacuate exhaust gas produced by oxy-combustion and transfer heat from exhaust gas to working fluid and ion transport membrane.
AU HABIB M A
   BEN-MANSOUR R
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 201883254L
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1000) has an ion transport membrane (3150) for connecting an upstream end of a first inner tube with a downstream end of the first inner tube to extract oxygen from air and form oxygen depleted air. A second inner tube surrounds the first inner tube to receive oxygen from the ion transport membrane and connects a supply line (4000) to receive a mixture of fuel and carbon dioxide to react with oxygen in oxy-combustion. A peripheral tube surrounds the second inner tube and seals the second inner tube around the first inner tube at a downstream end and directs exhaust gas in countercurrent direction of flow with respect to direction of flow of the air in another supply line (5000) and the mixture of fuel and carbon dioxide in the former supply line. The peripheral tube connects an exhaust line through the upstream end to evacuate the exhaust gas produced by the oxy-combustion and transfer heat from exhaust gas to working fluid and the ion transport membrane.
   USE - Counterflow fire tube boiler system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures that portions of exhaust ducts longitudinally extend from a vessel end to another vessel end and/or transversely extend along the latter vessel end to enhance surface contact between the working fluid and the exhaust ducts through longitudinal arrangement and/or transverse arrangement so as to transfer heat from the exhaust gases to the working fluid before the exhaust gases exit the vessel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a fire tube boiler system.
   Counterflow fire tube boiler system (1000)
   Oxygen transport reactors (3000)
   Ion transport membrane (3150)
   Supply lines (4000, 5000)
   Exhaust ducts (7200)

PN CN108671933-A
TI Preparing copper(I) oxide-niobium pentoxide heterojunction photocatalyst comprises e.g. mixing niobium(V) chloride and ethanol to dissolve, transferring above solution to a steel sleeve and dissolving copper(II) chloride in distilled water.
AU ZHOU J
   ZOU X
   LI N
   GE Y
   CHEN D
   CHEN Y
   JI Z
   XU C
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 201883346D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper(I) oxide-niobium pentoxide (Cu2O-Nb2O5) heterojunction photocatalyst comprises (i) mixing and stirring niobium(V) chloride and ethanol to dissolve, (ii) adding isopropanol, and then transferring the above solution to a steel sleeve with polytetrafluoroethylene liner, (iii) placing and sealing in an oven and heating at constant temperature, cooling reactor to room temperature, filtering and washing, drying obtained product, and grinding to obtain rod-shaped Nb2O5, (iv) dissolving copper(II) chloride in distilled water, dispersing the above rod-shaped Nb2O5 in copper(II) chloride solution, and ultrasonically agitating to mix the solution to obtain mixed solution of Nb2O5-copper(II) chloride, (v) adding Nb2O5-copper(II) chloride mixed solution into glucose solution and heating under water bath, and (vi) adding sodium hydroxide solution into above solution, then washing with deionized water, and vacuum drying to obtain Cu2O-Nb2O5 catalyst.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful in preparing methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst: has simple preparation method, mild reaction condition and high stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of photocatalyst in preparing methane comprising (a) taking visible micro high pressure reactor made of PTFE lining, covering bottom and periphery with tin foil for collecting light, adding quartz wool to carry the catalyst, leaving the room for mixing with bottom, and mixing the gas thoroughly, (b) dispersing Cu2O-Nb2O5 heterojunction photocatalyst on quartz cotton, and continuously passing nitrogen gas for 15-60 minutes to remove air from the kettle, (iii) introducing carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas in a volume ratio of 1:4-1:9, and (iv) carrying out photocatalytic carbon dioxide hydrogenation reduction reaction at normal temperature in a light source of 300 W xenon lamp for 3-5 hours to obtain methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), mixing time of the niobium(V) chloride and ethanol is 5-10 minutes. In step (iii), heating temperature is 100-300 degrees C, and heating time is 10-80 hours. In step (v), water bath heating temperature is 40-60 degrees C. Preferred Components: In step (ii), ratio between isopropanol and ethanol is 2:1-5:1. In step (iv), mass ratio between distilled water and copper(II) chloride is 1:5-1:10. In step (v), concentration of glucose solution is 0.5-2 M. In step (vi), concentration of sodium hydroxide solution is 1-8 M. In step (b), mass ratio between catalyst and quartz wool is 1:3-1:6.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN US10076723-B1
TI Method for cooling food and beverage containers,            involves providing dry gas source in fluid            communication with product container and gas release            unit for releasing dry gas from dry gas source.
AU ANTHONY M M
AE ANTHONY M M (ANTH-Individual)
GA 201872902E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a product                container (20) including a product release port                with release port opening unit and containing a                product and a humidification liquid. A dry gas                source is provided in fluid communication with the                product container and gas release unit for                releasing dry gas (GS) from the dry gas source,                such that the dry gas includes a dew point below                minus 100 degrees F and is comprised of nitrogen                and carbon-dioxide. The release port opening unit                is operated to open the product release port. The                gas release unit is operated to release dry gas                from the dry gas source into the product container                upon opening of the product release port. The dry                gas is provided from the dry gas source gathers                moisture from the humidification liquid and absorbs                heat from the product as a result of humidification                of the dry gas and the humidified gas carries away                the heat.
   USE - Method for cooling food and beverage                containers.
   ADVANTAGE - The cost of manufacture is now only limited by                the containment design. The requirement of                cryogenic liquids, refrigerants, and desiccants are                eliminated by using the thermodynamic potential of                extremely dry gases such as air to remove heat from                the beverage. The use of a dry gas such as                carbon-dioxide which is already found in carbonated                beverages, definitely increases the cooling                capacity of dry gases on water. The pressure within                the actuation chamber momentarily increases and                allows a slight axial displacement of the dry gas                canister element relative to the actuating sleeve                element.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional side view                of the product container.
   Product container (20)
   Container bottom domed wall (22)
   Beverage container closure (26)
   Beverage container opening unit (28)
   Dry gas (GS)

PN WO2018162576-A1; CA3054999-A1; AU2018231412-A1; CN110267916-A; IN201917030521-A; KR2019126826-A; US2019382277-A1; EP3592702-A1; BR112019018362-A2; VN68654-A; MX2019010585-A1; US11124423-B2; EP3592702-B1
TI Producing ammonia synthesis gas involves reforming hydrocarbon feed, shifting resultant synthesis gas comprising e.g. methane, sending process condensate originating from cooling and washing synthesis gas and adding part of steam stream.
AU SPETH C H
   DAHL P J
   KR OSLASH LL JENSEN A E
   SCHJ OSLASH DT N C
   SYMRENG M
   KROLL J A E
   SCHJODT N C
   KROLL JENSEN A E
   KROLLJENSEN A E
   JENSEN A E K
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2018718990
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (P1) an ammonia synthesis gas comprises: reforming a hydrocarbon feed in a reforming section thus obtaining a synthesis gas comprising methane, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen gas and water; shifting the synthesis gas in a shift section comprising at least one shift steps in series; optionally wash the synthesis gas leaving the shift section with water; sending a process condensate originating from cooling and washing the synthesis gas leaving the shift section to a process condensate stripper, where the dissolved shift byproducts and dissolved gases are stripped out of the process condensate using steam resulting in a steam stream; and adding at least part of the steam stream from the process condensate stripper to the synthesis gas downstream the reforming section, upstream the last shift step, where the steam/carbon ratio in the reforming step and the shift steps is less than 2.6.
   USE - The process is useful for producing an ammonia synthesis gas by using ammonia plant (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is environmental friendly and provides the gas in highly reliable ammonia production; utilizes carbon dioxide removal step that results in less capacity and high replacement cost; reduces methanol and other byproducts formation by 80-99%; and utilizes low steam/carbon ratio to the ATR and in the overall process is that smaller equipment is required in the front-end due to the lower total mass flow through the plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing (P2) ammonia, where the ammonia synthesis gas is achieved by the process (P1); and
   (2) a plant arranged to carry out the processes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (P1) further comprises: a prereforming step; a carbon dioxide removal step removing carbon dioxide from the synthesis gas down to a level less than 400 vppm carbon dioxide, preferably less than or equal to 20 vppm; and a nitrogen gas wash step. The process (P1) comprises at least one additional shift step comprising at least one high temperature shift step, at least one medium temperature shift step and/or at least one low temperature shift step. The shift steps are at least one high temperature (HT) shift step, using a promoted zinc-aluminum oxide based catalyst, with possibility for cooling and/or steam addition in between. The temperature in the high temperature shift step(s) is 300-600 degrees C, preferably 320-550 degrees C. The step of reforming takes place in an autothermal reformer (ATR). The steam is optionally added to the synthesis gas before the at least one additional shift step downstream the high temperature shift step. The steam stream contains more than 90% of the methanol dissolved in process condensate, preferably more than 99% of the methanol dissolved in process condensate. Preferred Components: The promoted zinc-aluminum oxide based HT shift catalyst comprises a zinc/aluminum molar ratio of 0.5-1 and a content of alkali metal of 0.4-8 wt.% and a copper content of 0-10% based on the weight of oxidized catalyst. The steam/carbon ratio in the reforming step is 2.6-0.1, 2.4-0.1, 2-0.2, 1.5-0.3 or 1.4-0.4, preferably 1.2 or 1 or, 0.6. The space velocity in the ATR is less than 20000 Nm3 C/m3/hour, preferably less than 7000 Nm3 C/m3/hour. The ammonia process loop is an inert free loop in process (P2).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN108359595-A
TI Biological fermentation reaction device used in bio-industry, has connecting pipe which is arranged at lower end of right end surface of gas storage chamber and connecting hole is arranged in connecting pipe.
AU ZHENG X
AE ZHENG X (ZHEN-Individual)
GA 2018628776
AB    NOVELTY - The device has chemical reaction chamber (19) whose upper side of left end wall is provided with an upwardly extending second vent hole (38). The second vent hole communicates with the chemical reaction chamber and the sealing chamber (37). A second sealing valve (30) is fixed on the second vent hole. A pressure groove (39) is arranged at a center of the upper end surface of the sealing box (36). The pressurized tank communicates with the sealed cavity and the external space. A pressure maintaining valve is arranged inside the pressurized tank. A sealing thin piece is arranged inside the sealing cavity. A detachable air storage chamber (42) is arranged on a left side of the upper end surface of the machine casing (1). A connecting pipe (43) is arranged at a lower end of the right end surface of the gas storage chamber, and a connecting hole (23) is arranged in the connecting pipe, and the connecting hole communicates with the sealing cavity and the gas storage cavity.
   USE - Biological fermentation reaction device used in bio-industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The biological fermentation reaction and the chemical reaction are realized, and the chemical reaction of the carbon dioxide gas generated by the fermentation reaches the role of oxygen production, reduces the carbon dioxide emission, ensures the reuse of the energy, and is conducive to the sustainable development of resources, and thus reduces environmental pollution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic front view illustrating the entire structure of a biological fermentation reaction device.
   Machine casing (1)
   Chemical reaction chamber (19)
   Connecting hole (23)
   Second sealing valve (30)
   Sealing box (36)
   Sealing chamber (37)
   Second vent hole (38)
   Pressure groove (39)
   Air storage chamber (42)
   Connecting pipe (43)
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

IP C01B-013/02; C01D-007/00; C12M-001/02; C12M-001/107; C12M-001/34

PT P
PN US2018188700-A1; US10663337-B2
TI In-situ method for calibrating gas flow control apparatus, involves measuring pressure within gas box as process gas is provided to gas box, and determining gas map for process gas based on determined volume and measured pressure.
AU PENLEY S
   MAEDER M
AE RENO TECHNOLOGIES INC (RENO-Non-standard)
   ICHOR SYSTEMS INC (ICHO-Non-standard)
   RENO TECHNOLOGIES INC (RENO-Non-standard)
GA 201859004A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring pressure within a gas box as reference gas is provided to the gas box, where the reference gas is one of nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, argon, and helium. Volume of the gas box is determined based on the measured pressure. The reference gas is removed from the gas box. Process gas is provided to the gas box of a system for processing articles at a set of flow rates using a gas flow control apparatus, where the gas box is sealed and the a set of flow rates is one of set of set points, a linear curve, a parabolic curve, a logarithmic curve, and an exponential curve. Pressure within the gas box is measured as the process gas is provided to the gas box. A gas map for the process gas is determined based on the determined volume and the measured pressure.
   USE - In-situ method for calibrating a gas flow control apparatus for supply of a process gas such as nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, argon, helium, hydrogen, hydrogen bromide, hydrogen chloride, hydrogen fluoride, and hydrogen sulfide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables adapting semiconductor fabrication techniques to improve flow rate accuracy and yield, increase factory throughput, and reduce lost operating time to improve a calibration result. The method enables mounting a temperature sensor and a pressure transducer to the gas box to enhance flexibility in selection of sensors and does not require use of integrated sensors incorporated into one of apparatuses.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating an in-situ method for calibrating a gas flow control apparatus for supply of a process gas.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); T06 (Process and Machine Control)
MC E31-A03; E31-B03; E31-D02; E31-F04; E31-F05; E31-H03; E31-H05; E31-J; E31-L; E31-N05B; E31-N05C; E31-P06B; E31-Q; E32-A02; E35-G; J04-C; S03-E12C; T06-A05; T06-B04B

RG 1712-U; 1785-U; 1889-U; 1704-U; 1713-U; 1901-U; 1881-U; 1781-U; 1777-U; 1779-U; 1738-U; 1066-U; 1423-U; 1671-U; 1532-U; 1667-U; 1668-U; 0365-U; 1831-U
UT DIIDW:201859004A
ER

PT P
PN CN108144621-A; CN108144621-B
TI Catalyst for preparing synthesis gas for shale gas and carbon dioxide catalytic reforming, comprises primary active component nickel having, and secondary active group, and carbon-resistant auxiliary magnesium oxide.
AU WU Y
   LIU Y
   ZHA L
   GAO Y
   ZHAO W
   LIU D
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 201848211L
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises a primary active component nickel having a mass fraction of 5-10%, secondary active group having a mass fraction of 3-5%, 20-30% carbon-resistant auxiliary magnesium oxide and carrier, where mass ratio of nickel to niobium is 1-3:1, and carrier is titanium oxide, aluminum oxide or silicon oxide.
   USE - Catalyst used for preparing synthesis gas for shale gas and carbon dioxide catalytic reforming.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst which effectively clean the shale gas resource utilization, and reduces greenhouse effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing a catalyst, which involves dissolving carrier precursor in absolute ethanol in a volume ratio of 1:4-7, adding glacial acetic acid in dropwise manner with stirring to prepare a transparent solution A, and selecting carrier precursor from butyl titanate or tetraethyl orthosilicate; disolving Ni(NO3)26H2O, Ce(NO3)36H2O, and Mg(NO3)26H2O in water by a mass ratio of nickel, lanthanum, magnesium oxide of 1-3:1:5-6 , volume ratio of the control water to the carrier precursor is 6-9:1, and the pH is adjusted to 3-6, to prepare a transparent solution B; and adding solution A to solution B, stirring until a sol, allowing sealing to stand in an air atmosphere to form a gel, drying gel, grinding, calcining at 500-700 degrees C, and calcining after calcination, and sieving to obtain final product; and
   (2) a method of using catalyst, which involves using a fixed bed or a fluidized bed process to carry out shale gas and CO2 catalytic weight whole reforming temperature of 500-900 degrees C, using together with the high-temperature flue gas heat exchange heat recovery or recovery device, providing heat by the high temperature flue gas waste heat.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The main active component nickel has a mass fraction of 5% and the secondary active component rhodium has a mass fraction of 5%. Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of the glacial acetic acid to the carrier precursor is 0.5:1. The stirring speed is 300-400 rpm, the drying temperature is 100-110 degrees C for 3-4 hours. The coprecipitation is carried out by dissolving proportionally, Ni(NO3)26H2O, Ce(NO3)36H2O, Mg(NO3)26H2O and Al(NO3)39H2O in water to form a transparent solution, and adding sodium hydroxide. The precipitation is washed with water until the filtrate is neutral, the precipitate is dried, calcined at 500-700 degrees C, and sieved to obtain a desired catalyst. The calcination time is carried out for 3-4 hours. The reforming temperature is 800 degrees C.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E11-W; E31-A01; E31-P01; E34-B01; E34-C01; E35-K01; H06-A05; H06-P; J04-E04; J04-E09; J04-E11; N01-B; N02-C01; N03-A02A; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-J

TI Wireless sensor network for sensing parameter of            local environment in facilities, has set of sensor            devices for providing reporting command to selected            sensor device to update reporting condition associated            with selected sensor device.
AU BAUGH R
AE DRAYSON TECHNOLOGIES EURO LTD (DRAY-Non-standard)
   SENSYNE HEALTH GROUP LTD (SENS-Non-standard)
   SENSYNE HEALTH GROUP LTD (SENS-Non-standard)
   SENSYNE HEALTH GROUP LTD (SENS-Non-standard)
GA 2018451824
AB    NOVELTY - The network has a set of sensor devices for                reporting sensor data to a controller (100)                according to reporting condition associated with                the set of sensor devices. The controller obtains                location data indicating location of the set of                sensor devices. The controller obtains the sensor                data from each of the set of sensor devices. The                controller determines a reporting command for a                selected sensor device based on the obtained sensor                data and the corresponding location data, and                provides a reporting command to the selected sensor                device to update the condition associated with the                selected device.
   USE - Wireless sensor network for sensing parameter                of a local environment in facilities, and for                tracking location of vehicles e.g. on-motorway                vehicles such as e.g. cars, buses and trains or                off-motorway vehicles such as materials handling                vehicles and heavy plant machinery and passage of                foot traffic, occupancy of car parks or venues i.e.                concert halls. Uses include but are not limited to                sense ambient temperature, wind speed, humidity,                pressure, level of a gas e.g. carbon dioxide or                oxygen, level of contaminant e.g. carbon dioxide,                namely nitric oxide and pollen in building,                warehouse, work place and cold store.
   ADVANTAGE - The network reduces energy used per byte of                data transmission in sensor networks, thus reducing                financial cost of networks. The network is                accomplished by extending battery life and allowing                use of energy harvesting mechanism to obviate or                reduce need to rely on batteries.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for operating a sensor network.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                sensor network.
   Controller (100)
   Communication devices (104, 110)
   Area of sensor device (126)

TI Preparation of protected anode for battery,            involves providing electrochemical cell containing            cathode, anode and electrolyte, performing            discharge-charge cycle, and applying voltage to charge            electrochemical cell which is free of water.
AU SALEHI-KHOJIN A
   ASADI M
   SAYAHPOUR B
   ABBASI P
   GERARD M
AE UNIV ILLINOIS FOUND (UNII-C)
   UNIV ILLINOIS FOUND (UNII-C)
   UNIV ILLINOIS FOUND (UNII-C)
   UNIV ILLINOIS FOUND (UNII-C)
GA 2018321507
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of protected anode involves                providing an electrochemical cell comprising a                cathode comprising at least one transition metal                dichalcogenide, an anode comprising a metal, an                electrolyte, and carbon dioxide dissolved in the                electrolyte, performing a discharge-charge cycle by                discharging the electrochemical cell, and applying                a voltage across the anode and the cathode to                charge the electrochemical cell. The                electrochemical cell is free of water. One or more                chemical species formed in the discharge-charge                cycle and dissolved in the electrolyte are                deposited on the anode.
   USE - Preparation of protected anode used for                battery e.g. metal-air battery and metal-ion                battery e.g. metal-sulfur battery (all claimed) for                electric vehicles and microelectronics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces protected                anode which provides battery with excellent                performance and cycle characteristics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of protected anode involves                providing an electrochemical cell comprising a                cathode comprising at least one transition metal                dichalcogenide, an anode comprising a metal, an                electrolyte in contact with the transition metal                dichalcogenide of the cathode and the metal of the                anode, and carbon dioxide dissolved in the                electrolyte, performing a discharge-charge cycle by                discharging the electrochemical cell, and applying                a voltage across the anode and the cathode for a                required time to charge the electrochemical cell.                The electrochemical cell is free of water. One or                more chemical species formed in the                discharge-charge cycle and dissolved in the                electrolyte are deposited on the anode. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) protected anode; and
   (2) battery, which comprises the protected                anode, the cathode, and the electrolyte in contact                with the anode and optionally with the metal of the                anode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                lithium-air battery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Condition: The voltage is 1-5                  V.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal is lithium,                  magnesium, zinc, or aluminum, preferably lithium.                  The electrolyte comprises a metallic ion. The                  metallic ion is lithium ion, magnesium ion, zinc                  ion, or aluminum ion, preferably lithium ion. The                  metal of the anode and the element of the                  metallic ion are the same. The cathode comprises                  the transition metal dichalcogenide arranged on a                  porous component or a current collector. The                  porous component is porous carbon component, and                  is electroconductive. Each transition metal                  dichalcogenide is titanium of formula: TiX2 (I),                  vanadium of formula VX2 (II), chromium of                  formula: CrX2 (III), zirconium of formula: ZrX2                  (IV), niobium of formula: NbX2 (V), molybdenum of                  formula: MoX2 (VI), hafnium of formula: HfX2                  (VII), tungsten of formula: WX2 (VIII), tantalum                  of formula: TaX2 (IX), technetium of formula:                  TcX2 (X) or rhenium of formula: ReX2 (XI). The                  transition metal dichalcogenide is preferably                  titanium disulfide, titanium diselenide,                  molybdenum disulfide, molybdenum diselenide,                  tungsten disulfide or tungsten diselenide, more                  preferably molybdenum diselenide. The transition                  metal dichalcogenide is in nanoparticle form,                  nanoflake form, nanosheet form, nanoribbon form,                  or bulk form. The electrolyte comprises an ionic                  liquid. The ionic liquid comprises a cation and                  an anion. The electrolyte is free of non-ionic                  liquid organic solvents. The electrolyte                  comprises an aprotic organic solvent.X = S, Se                  and/or Te.Preferred Composition: The electrolyte                  comprises less than 5 wt.%, preferably less than                  1 wt.% water, less than 5 wt.%, preferably less                  than 2 wt.% hydrogen, less than 5 wt.%,                  preferably less than 2 wt.% oxygen, 0.005-5 M,                  preferably 0.02-0.5 M metallic ion, at least 5%,                  preferably at least 50% saturated carbon dioxide,                  at least 50 wt.%, preferably at least 90 wt.%                  ionic liquid. The total content of hydrogen and                  oxygen in the electrolyte is less than 10 wt.%.                  The material of the transition metal                  dichalcogenide-containing cathode comprises at                  least 50 wt.% transition metal dichalcogenide.                  The protected anode comprises a protective layer                  arranged on the anode. The protective layer                  comprises at least 50 atomic% lithium cobalt.                  Preferred Process: The method further involves                  performing additional discharge-charge cycles,                  removing the protected anode from the                  electrochemical cell after discharge-charge                  cycles, and configuring the battery. The total                  number of discharge-charge cycles is 2-25,                  preferably 5-15. Preferred Properties: The                  transition metal dichalcogenide nanoparticles                  have an average size of 1-1000 nm, preferably                  1-400 nm. The transition metal dichalcogenide                  nanoflakes have an average thickness of 1-100 nm,                  average dimensions along the major surface of 50                  nm-10 mu m, and an aspect ratio of at least                  5:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cation is selected                  from imidazolium, pyridinium, pyrrolidinium,                  phosphonium, ammonium, choline, sulfonium,                  prolinate and methioninate, preferably                  imidazolium cation of formula (II). The anion is                  1-6C alkyl sulfate, tosylate, methanesulfonate,                  bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide,                  hexafluorophosphate, tetrafluoroborate, triflate,                  halide, carbamate or sulfamate, preferably                  1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate.                  The solvent is ether, preferably tetraethylene                  glycol dimethyl ether, dimethoxyethane, or                  dimethyl sulfoxide.R1-R3 = H, linear 1-6C aliphatic group,                  branched 1-6C aliphatic or 1-6C cyclic                  aliphatic;R2 = H;andR1,R3 = linear or branched 1-4C alkyl.

TI Horse-collar-shaped rebreathing apparatus for removing carbon dioxide from air exhaled from user in underwater teams, has mouthpiece interface operatively coupled to mouthpiece valve assembly and delivering cooled air to user for inhalation.
AU MORAN R N
   HERNANDEZ F E
   COWGILL D E
AE MINE SURVIVAL INC (MINE-Non-standard)
GA 2018262756
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a mouthpiece interface operatively coupled to a mouthpiece valve assembly (1750) and configured for veiling a face of a user. An apparatus main body adapted to receive air at a first temperature exhaled from the user, cool the air to another temperature by dissipating heat from the scrubbed air through an outer layer (24) to the latter atmosphere and deliver the cooled air from an inhalation chamber to the mouthpiece interface by an inlet hose (106). The mouthpiece interface is adapted for delivering the cooled air to the user for inhalation.
   USE - Horse-collar-shaped rebreathing apparatus for removing carbon dioxide from air exhaled from a user in military settings e.g. underwater teams.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus has only the scrubbing material, so that the weight of the apparatus can be greatly reduced for making the breathing more natural and less labored so as to reduce user fatigue during usage of the apparatus. The apparatus is flat, light in weight and compact in size, and can be easily stored and transported. The apparatus ensures that a mouthpiece plug end can be inserted within the air opening in the mouthpiece interface such that the mouthpiece plug prevents air from entering or exiting the apparatus when the apparatus is not in use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) an apparatus for cooling air before inhalation by a user
   (2) a mouthpiece interface apparatus for exhaling and inhaling air using a rebreathing apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a rebreather apparatus with a mouthpiece comprising an oral nasal cup.

PN CN107617435-A
TI Strontium titanate-loaded titanium oxide nanotube heterojunction photocatalyst useful for photoanode-driven photoelectrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, is prepared by reacting titanium oxide nanotube array with precursor solution.
AU LI G
   CAO Y
   LI R
   ZHU X
   LI Z
   CHEN Y
AE UNIV SHANGHAI NORMAL (USNO-C)
GA 201808879G
AB    NOVELTY - Strontium titanate-loaded titanium oxide nanotube heterojunction photocatalyst is prepared by (a) adding an electrolyte, a titanium sheet as an anode and a platinum sheet as a cathode to a plastic container, applying a voltage of 15-25 V, etching and calcining to obtain titanium oxide nanotube array, (b) placing the titanium oxide nanotube array in a hydrothermal kettle containing precursor solution, putting the mixture into a drying oven and reacting, and (c) washing the reaction product with 0.1 M hydrochloric acid and calcining to obtain the photocatalyst.
   USE - Strontium titanate-loaded titanium oxide nanotube heterojunction photocatalyst is useful for photoanode-driven photoelectrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Strontium titanate-loaded titanium oxide nanotube heterojunction photocatalyst is prepared by (a) adding an electrolyte, a titanium sheet as an anode and a platinum sheet as a cathode to a plastic container, applying a voltage of 15-25 V, etching for 5-15 hours and calcining at a temperature of 350-800 degrees C and a heating speed of 3-8 degrees C/minute for 1-3 hours to obtain titanium oxide nanotube array, (b) placing the titanium oxide nanotube array in a hydrothermal kettle containing precursor solution, putting the mixture into a drying oven and reacting at 100-300 degrees C for 5-15 hours, and (c) washing the reaction product with 0.1 M hydrochloric acid and calcining at 300-800 degrees C for 3-5 hours to obtain the photocatalyst. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the method for preparing the strontium titanate-loaded titanium oxide nanotube heterojunction photocatalyst, which involves carrying out the above steps (a)-(c).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The plastic container is a beaker.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The electrolyte is a mixed solution of 30 ml of 0.2 M phosphoric acid and 30 ml of 0.4 M ammonium fluoride. The precursor solution is 0.04 M strontium hydroxide solution Preferred Conditions: In the step (a), the applied voltage is 20 V and the calcining step is carried out at a temperature of 450 degrees C and a heating speed of 5 degrees C/minute for 2 hours. In the step (b), the reacting step is carried out at 150 degrees C for 10 hours. In the step (c), the calcining step is carried out at 600 degrees C.

AU SUZUKI T
AE SANWA SHUZO KK (SANW-Non-standard)
GA 2022779581
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing effervescent refined sake involves (a) obtaining a suspension and a refined sake separately from mash, (b) putting the suspension and the sake together into a fermentation tank, and (c) performing a secondary fermentation by sealing fermentation tank and storing while gradually lowering the temperature, and generating and dissolving carbon dioxide gas while suppressing changes in liquor quality.
   USE - The method is useful for producing effervescent refined sake.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of sparkling refined sake with high quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Component: The suspension is combined with sake having 10-13% alcohol to have 2-6 %mass suspension. Preferred Method: The suspension is obtained from the mash by coarse grinding, and the sake is obtained by squeezing yabuta. The method involves rapidly lowering the temperature of secondary fermentation to a sub-cooled state below the freezing point to coagulate and settle yeast and to stop it. The secondary fermentation is performed by starting at 12 degrees C, gradually decreasing temperature, and lowering the temperature to -5 degrees C at once to stop the fermentation when a predetermined gas pressure is reached at 0-3 degrees C.

PN CN113170873-A
TI Preparation of microcapsule spice material involves respectively crushing spice to obtain spice powder, supercritical extracting to obtain spice oleoresin, wall material embedding and spray drying.
AU LIU F
   ZHAO Z
AE SICHUAN YASHI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2021882822
AB    NOVELTY - A microcapsule spice material is prepared by respectively crushing spice to obtain spice powder, supercritical extracting to obtain spice oleoresin, wall material embedding and spray drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing microcapsule spice material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can further prevent oxidation and escape of heat-sensitive substances in oleoresin prepared by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, has higher extraction rate of spice flavor substance under wall material, better solubility and no residue, and retains flavor components in spices to greater extent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The spice comprises cinnamon, pepper, aniseed, tsaoko amomum fruit and clove. The wall material powder comprises carrageenan, maltodextrin and soybean isolated protein according to mass ratio of 0.1:1:3, or carrageenan and maltodextrin according to mass ratio of 1:4. The spice oleoresin comprises pepper, aniseed, tsaoko amomum fruit, cinnamon and clove oil resin at mass ratio of 1.2:1.4:1.2:1.4:1.5:1. Preferred Method: Crushing is done to 30-40 meshes. Supercritical extraction involves adding 300 g spice powder into 1 L extraction tank, pumping into 300-500 mL of 60-100 wt.% entrainer ethanol, supercritical carbon dioxide extracting at 30-50 degrees C for 1.5-2.5 hours at pressure of 20-40 MPa and carbon dioxide flow speed of 40-50 L/hour and collecting product from separating kettle to obtain spice oleoresin. The separating kettle has temperature of 40-50 degrees C and pressure of 10-15 MPa. Drying after embedding involves dissolving wall material powder in 85 degrees C hot water at ratio of 1:4 to material-to-liquid ratio, homogenizing stirring for 1 minute at speed of 10000 revolutions/minute (rpm), cooling, adding 1.6 wt.% spice oleoresin and 5 wt.% salt, homogeneously stirring for 1 minute at speed of 14000 rpm, pressurizing at pressure of 30 MPa, homogenizing, and spray drying at air inlet temperature of 160 degrees C and air outlet temperature of 80 degrees C.

   USE - Method for producing methane or                methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce methane or                methanol that improves the selectivity for the                glycol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogenation                  catalyst comprises Ru, Ni, Fe, Co, Rh, or                  combination. Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of H_2 to                  CO_2 is from 4:1-10:1 or CO_2 is adsorbed,                  absorbed, covalently bound, or ionically bound to                  the amine, and process is performed in absence of                  CO_2 overpressure. The condensed phase is                  condensed phase solution further comprises CO_2.                  Preferred Process: The method involves preparing                  condensed phase solution by contacting gas stream                  comprising CO_2 with amine-containing solvent or                  solution to combining the epoxide, hydrogenation                  catalyst, and hydrogen with condensed phase                  solution. The amine comprises polyamine, tertiary                  amine, aminopyridine, R_1(R_2)N-L_1-NH-R_3, where                  R_1 and R_2 independently is aliphatic or                  cycloaliphatic or R_1 and R_2 together with the                  nitrogen to which they are attached form                  heterocyclyl ring, L_1 is aliphatic or                  cycloaliphatic or L_1 and R_1 together with the                  nitrogen to which they are attached form                  heterocyclic ring, and R_3 is aliphatic,                  cycloaliphatic, cycloalkylalkyl or alkoxyalkyl.                  The methodd involves separating the methane from                  amine and water, cooling condensed phase solution                  comprises methane, amine, and water to                  temperature of 20-50?oC and flowing condensed                  solution through vapor-liquid separator,                  separating the methane from the amine and the                  water. The method involves flowing methane,                  water, and amine through steam generator prior to                  separating the methane from the amine and the                  water, generating power. The condensed-phase                  amine comprises                  N-(2-ethoxyethyl)-3-morpholinopropan-1-amine                  (EEMPA), concentration of CO_2 in the                  condensed-phase amine is 25-50 mol.%. The                  hydrogenation catalyst comprises 5 wt.%                  Ru/Al_2O_3, molar ratio of H_2 to CO_2 is from                  4:1-10:1.

   ADVANTAGE - The halogenated ammonium salt compound enables efficient preparation of alkylene carbonate with high yield in a short period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A halogenated ammonium salt compound of formula (I) is new.
   R1,R2=1-10C alkyl, I, Br, Cl or F;
   R3=1-6C aryl or alkyl;and
   X=I, Br, Cl or NO3.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of alkylene carbonate, which involves reacting epoxide and carbon dioxide in presence of the compound (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The compound (I) is prepared by reacting methyl iodide and 2-(((2-(dimethylamino)ethyl)((2-hydroxy-3,5-dimethylphenyl)methyl)amino)methyl)-4,6-dimethylphenol of formula (II) in presence of acetonitrile. Preferred Process: The reaction between epoxide and carbon dioxide is carried out at 25-200 degrees C in carbon dioxide at atmospheric pressure of 0.1-100 bar optionally under action of solvent chosen from tetrahydrofuran, methylene chloride, diethyl ether, hexane and water. Preferred Components: The epoxide is chosen from ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, styrene oxide, glycidol and hexylene oxide, preferably propylene oxide. The usage amount of compound (I) is 0.001-10 mol% with respect to epoxide. The alkylene carbonate is of formula (III).R=H, C, Cl, 1-4C alkyl, alkoxy or aryloxy.

AU WANG D
   CHEN Z

AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)

GA 201817164F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of graphite by low-temperature electrochemical methods using waste flue gas comprises taking alkali metal carbonate and alkali metal sulfate as molten salt electrolyte system, inert material anode and metal cathode, feeding to electrochemical reaction device, feeding carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide industrial flue gas, heating at 675 degrees C, performing electrolysis, and electrodepositing.
   USE - Method for preparing graphite by low-temperature electrochemical methods using waste flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has improved waste utilization.

The cathode is nickel electrode, copper electrode, titanium electrode, platinum electrode, aluminum electrode, silver electrode, gold electrode, and/or platinum electrode. Preferred Method: Electrolysis is done at 675-725 degrees C.

TI System for supplying and dosing cold to thermal package used for conditioning and transporting thermosensitive products using carbon dioxide, has exhaust fan that is installed at exit of packaging room.
AU SCHMIDT A
AE TERMOTECNICA LTDA (TERM-Non-standard)
GA 202244330C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide storage tank (100) in liquefied form at a temperature of -18.6 degrees C under a pressure of 300 psi or 20 kgf/cm2. A remote transmission unit (RTU) is provided to control the storage tank. A pipe (101) vacuum insulated or polyurethane coated with non-contaminating materials to reduce pressure of vaporized nitrogen to a pressure of 2.0 to 2.5 bar is arranged at the outlet of the liquid carbon dioxide phase of the storage tank. A conveyor belt (103) for packages arranged under a dosing device (102) is provided. An electronic scale (104) is arranged on the conveyor belt of packages at the exit point of the dosing device of the dry ice. An exhaust fan (105) is installed at the exit of the packaging room. Carbon dioxide is expanded at atmospheric pressure of 1.0 kgf/cm2, and reaches a temperature of -79.9 degrees C at the outlet of the pipe, providing latent heat of 85 kcal/kg and forming snowflakes poured into the package that passes through the belt.
   USE - System for supplying and dosing cold to thermal package used for conditioning and transporting thermosensitive products using carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The low cost refrigeration system for packaging using carbon dioxide is provided, and packaging assists in the dosing and distribution of cold, storing and transporting thermosensitive products, which are susceptible to exposure of temperatures promoted by environmental changes during the storage, transport and arrival logistics chain to the final destination, providing a controllable, bacteriostatic cold environment and maintaining low temperatures inside the package suitable for maintaining the integrity of the product for long periods of time, without breaks in the cold chain. The system accompanies equipment installed in the packages, which provide online monitoring of geolocation, humidity and temperature to allow preventive action of the user avoiding possible losses due to deviations of processes. The system allows flexibility in the assembly of the carbon dioxide generating equipment, with reduction of steps in the operation in assembly. The use and handling of the packaging are facilitated by the users during the product packaging process. The cost of freight by the fact that the package is made of a low weight material is reduced. The waste of discards in the final destination is reduced, since the packaging is manufactured in EPS, 100% recyclable product. The package prevents direct contact of the user with carbon dioxide. The mechanical strength of the packaging is provided, ensuring a safe stacking of the packaging stack and optimizing storage and transport conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a thermal package for packaging and transporting thermosensitive products.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the system for supplying and dosing cold in the package.

PN CN112267317-A; WO2022088236-A1
TI Method for anhydrous dyeing of viscose fibers comprise loading viscose fiber to be dyed into viscose fiber anhydrous dyeing system in order to complete material preparation, adding viscose fiber in dye and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid.
AU GAO C
   GAO W
   WANG M
AE SHANDONG GAOMIAN INTELLIGENT FIBER DYEING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG GAOMIAN INTELLIGENT FIBER DYEING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202113548S
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises (1-1) loading the viscose fiber to be dyed into the viscose fiber anhydrous dyeing system in order to complete the material preparation, (1-2) adding the prepared viscose fiber in the dye and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid in the viscose fiber anhydrous dyeing system to perform the preliminary dyeing step to complete the initial dyeing, (1-3) repeatedly compacting the viscose fiber and rotating to loosen the material, and the repeated preliminary dyeing steps are completed to obtain the dyed viscose fiber, (1-4) alternately placing the dyed viscose fiber in a supercritical state, a high pressure state, and a low pressure state in the viscose fiber anhydrous dyeing system to undergo the anti-fading step, (1-5) repeating the above-mentioned initial dyeing steps to achieve enhanced dyeing and obtain the final firmly dyed viscose fiber and (1-6) controlling the waterless dyeing system of viscose fiber to realize the unloading of firmly dyed viscose fiber.
   USE - Method for anhydrous dyeing of viscose fibers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses carbon dioxide fluid in supercritical state to dye with dye in the water-free dyeing; ensures that the dye is fully immersed in fiber when dyeing; and improves the dyeing effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for viscose fiber anhydrous dyeing system comprising a dyeing bin, in which a material pressing rotating mechanism is installed, and the inside of the pressing material rotating mechanism is used to fill viscose fibers, both upper and lower ends of the pressing and rotating mechanism extend out of the dyeing bin movably, and supporting legs are provided at the bottom of the dyeing bin, sealed maintenance, loading and unloading integrated door is arranged on the lower outer side wall of the dyeing bin, a carbon dioxide feed pipe and a carbon dioxide discharge pipe connected to the inside of the dyeing bin are respectively provided on the side walls of the dyeing bin, number of dye inlet pipes and dye row pipes communicating with the interior of the dyeing bin are also provided, the electromagnetic valves are installed on the carbon dioxide feed pipe and the carbon dioxide discharge pipe, the dye inlet pipe and the dye discharge pipe, a pressure gauge for detecting internal pressure is installed on the dyeing bin, and an air pump is installed on one side of the dyeing bin, the air pump is in communication with the inside of the dyeing bin and is used to control the air pressure inside the dyeing bin, and a circulating pump is provided outside the dyeing bin, the outlet of the circulating pump is in communication with the carbon dioxide feed pipe, and the inlet of the circulating pump is in communication with a carbon dioxide source.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The dyeing time in the preliminary dyeing step is controlled at 0.5-1 hour. In this step, the internal environment of the viscose fiber waterless dyeing system is filled with carbon dioxide in a supercritical state and dyeing dyes. In the preliminary dyeing step, the dyeing time is controlled at 1-2 hours, and the viscose fiber in the dyeing state is periodically compacted and rotated to loosen the material. In the pressure-regulated dyeing step, the viscose fiber anhydrous dyeing system is first in a supercritical state, and the carbon dioxide supercritical fluid passed through the interior in the early stage is combined with the dye to realize the viscose fiber dyeing and the initial dyeing; in the mid-term, the internal pressure reaches a high-pressure state, and the carbon dioxide is fully liquefied. At this time, the dyed viscose fiber is quickly rotated and loosened and every 10 minutes to observe the fading of the currently dyed viscose fiber, in the later stage, the pressure is quickly reduced to a low pressure state. At this time, the carbon dioxide is in a gas state, the dyed viscose fiber is quickly rotated and loosened, and the fading situation of the currently dyed viscose fiber is observed every 10 minutes. The duration of the supercritical state is 2-2.5 hours. The duration of the medium-term internal pressure to reach a high pressure state is 0.5-1 hour. When the pressure is quickly reduced to low pressure in the later stage, the duration is 0.5-1 hour.
