PN CN108129002-A
TI Preparing mashed clarifying agent for sugar sludge involves pre-treating sugar sludge, and metering pretreated material, and transferring to suspension preheating device, and pre-heating material to critical decomposition temperature.
AU XIN S
   XIN M
AE XIN S (XINS-Individual)
   XIN M (XINM-Individual)
GA 201846447N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mashed clarifying agent involves pre-treating sugar sludge, metering pretreated material, and transferring to a suspension preheating device, pre-heating material to the critical decomposition temperature, transferring to a suspension decomposition device, so that most of the materials are decomposed by calcium carbonate to form calcium oxide and carbon dioxide. The material of the levitation decomposition device is introduced into the deep decarburization device to complete deep decarburization to generate high temperature lime, and the high temperature lime of the deep decarburization device is cooled by the fluidized suspension cooling device to obtain powdered active lime. The by-product carbon dioxide is used as the carbon dioxide gas required for the saturation process.
   USE - Method for preparing mashed clarifying agent for sugar sludge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare mashed clarifying agent in eco-friendly manner, and realizes recycling of sugar filter mud.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The prepared powdered active lime is added with water to make lime milk calcium hydroxide, and then mixed with the raw sugar solution, and then the mixture is filled with calcium oxide to form calcium carbonate particles, and the particles adsorb impurities to form a paste precipitate, which is sugar filter mud. The sugar sludge is calcined and carbon dioxide is released. The sugar sludge is first pretreated by drying and dehydrating, and then make it into a powder. The materials are stored in the raw material warehouse, metered and transferred to the suspension preheating device. The materials are preheated to the critical decomposition temperature and then transferred to the suspension decomposition device to cause calcium carbonate to occur in most of the materials at the critical decomposition temperature decomposed to generate calcium oxide and carbon dioxide The material of the levitation decomposition device is introduced into the deep decarburization device to complete deep decarburization, and the deep decarburization device is used for completely escaping carbon dioxide in the incompletely decomposed calcium cabonate. The high-temperature lime of the deep decarburization device is cooled by the fluidized suspension cooling device, and powdered active lime is obtained. The by-product carbon dioxide produced during the calcination process of the sugar sludge is used as the carbon dioxide gas required for the filling process, and is used for saturating the juice-lime milk mixture. The lime milk calcium hydroxide is prepared by adding the powdered lime fired from the sugar filter sludge, and then mixed with the raw sugar solution and transferred to the saturating tank, and then saturated. The mixture of the filling tank is filled with carbon dioxide to form calcium carbonate particles, calcium carbonate particles adsorb impurities to form a creamy precipitate, separated by filtration and solid-liquid separation, juice filtered is transferred to a deep processing workshop, and the filter residue is a sugar filter sludge which is recycled, used as a raw material for calcining powdered lime.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PN CN114620727-A
TI Absorbing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas of a            cement plant and a power plant by using carbide slag            involves putting the calcium carbide slag waste from            the calcium carbide factory into the reaction tank, and            adding water to the reaction tank to dilute the calcium            hydroxide in the waste.
AU YANG X
AE YANG X (YANG-Individual)
GA 202282978D
AB    NOVELTY - Absorbing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas of a                cement plant and a power plant by using carbide                slag involves putting the calcium carbide slag                waste from the calcium carbide factory into the                reaction tank, then adding water to the reaction                tank to dilute the calcium hydroxide in the waste,                and filtering the diluted waste to remove the solid                filter out impurities. The industrial exhaust gas                is passed into the purification tower, filtered and                purified the exhaust gas, filtered out the                particles in the exhaust gas, and cooled. The                purified exhaust gas is introduced from the bottom                of the carbonization tower by unit of                pressurization, so that the exhaust gas enters the                interior of the carbonization tower at high                pressure. The calcium hydroxide emulsion is                prepared after dilution and passed into from the                top of carbonization tower, and prepared calcium                hydroxide emulsion absorb carbon dioxide gas in                exhaust gas to form calcium carbonate                precipitate
   USE - Method for absorbing carbon dioxide in exhaust                gas of a cement plant and a power plant by using                carbide slag
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the emission of carbon                dioxide, protects the environment, and uses the                carbide slag waste to absorb carbon dioxide and has                the effect of waste utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Absorbing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas of a                cement plant and a power plant by using carbide                slag involves putting the calcium carbide slag                waste from the calcium carbide factory into the                reaction tank, then adding water to the reaction                tank to dilute the calcium hydroxide in the waste,                and filtering the diluted waste to remove the solid                filter out impurities. The industrial exhaust gas                is passed into the purification tower, filtered and                purified the exhaust gas, filtered out the                particles in the exhaust gas, and cooled. The                purified exhaust gas is introduced from the bottom                of the carbonization tower by unit of                pressurization, so that the exhaust gas enters the                interior of the carbonization tower at high                pressure. The calcium hydroxide emulsion is                prepared after dilution and passed into from the                top of carbonization tower, and prepared calcium                hydroxide emulsion absorb carbon dioxide gas in                exhaust gas to form calcium carbonate precipitate.                The generated calcium carbonate precipitate is put                into the dehydration tower, dehydrated the calcium                carbonate, and dried the calcium carbonate after                the dehydration is completed. The dried calcium                carbonate is packaged into bags and then                sold.

TI Carbon dioxide supplier used in greenhouse for growing horticultural crop, comprises combustion chamber for combusting air and fuel, and having chamber component, intake and exhaust holes, mixing space for mixing air and fuel, and catalysts.
AU JEONG P
   PARK D
   DONGWOOKPARK
AE DAONRS INC (DAON-Non-standard)
   DAONRS (DAON-Non-standard)
GA 202116500R
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide supplier (4) comprises a combustion chamber for combusting a mixture of air and fuel, an air supply unit for supplying air into the combustion chamber, and a fuel supply unit. The combustion chamber has a chamber component, an intake hole provided at an end (E1) of the chamber component and located in upstream of the flow, an exhaust hole provided at an end (E2) of the chamber component and located in downstream of the gas flow, a mixing space for mixing air and fuel, provided inside the chamber component, a catalyst (C1) positioned between the exhaust hole and mixing space, a combustion space positioned between the exhaust hole and catalyst (C1), an ignition device, a catalyst (C2) positioned between the exhaust hole and combustion space, and an exhaust space positioned between the exhaust hole and catalyst (C2). The air is supplied into the chamber component through the intake hole. The fuel is supplied into the chamber component through the intake hole.
   USE - Carbon dioxide supplier is used in greenhouse for generating carbon dioxide for growing horticultural crops.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide supplier has excellent startability, and is capable of completely combusting at a medium temperature and under ultra-lean conditions, and is highly safe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide supplier comprises a combustion chamber for combusting a mixture of air and fuel, an air supply unit for supplying air into the combustion chamber, and a fuel supply unit for supplying fuel to the combustion chamber. The combustion chamber has a chamber component, an intake hole provided at an end (E1) of the chamber component and located in an upstream of the flow, an exhaust hole provided at an end (E2) of the chamber component and located in a downstream of the gas flow, a mixing space for mixing air and fuel, provided inside the chamber component, a catalyst (C1) positioned between the exhaust hole and mixing space, a combustion space positioned between the exhaust hole and catalyst (C1), an ignition device positioned inside the combustion space, a catalyst (C2) positioned between the exhaust hole and combustion space, and an exhaust space positioned between the exhaust hole and catalyst (C2). The air is supplied into the chamber component through the intake hole. The fuel is supplied into the chamber component through the intake hole.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide supplier.
   Greenhouse (1)
   Fuel tank (2)

   Carbon dioxide supplier (4)
   Controller (5)

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The carbon dioxide supplier has a housing surrounding the combustion component. PN CN110761764-A; CN110761764-B
TI Performing liquid carbon dioxide fracturing process involves entering wellbore structure parameters, reservoir or experimental core physical and mechanical parameters, formation stress and thermodynamic parameters.
AU MU C
   LI X
   ZHANG Y
   BAI J
   LI D
   JIA J
   HE P
   KUANG D
   ZHU L
   LIANG L
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2020137248
AB    NOVELTY - Performing liquid carbon dioxide fracturing process involves entering the wellbore structure parameters, reservoir or experimental core physical and mechanical parameters, formation stress and thermodynamic parameters, fracturing operation inlet conditions and operations parameter according to the wellhead pressure, inlet temperature, carbon dioxide flow rate, density, friction coefficient and well depth and wellbore diameter, using the basic equations of carbon-di-oxide flow in the wellbore to obtain different rows according to the relationship between the temperature and pressure of the wellbore during obtained carbon-di-oxide fracturing, and the carbon-di-oxide physical property parameter model to obtain relationship between the physical properties of carbon-di-oxide in the wellbore and the depth of the well at different displacements.
   USE - Method for performing liquid carbon dioxide fracturing process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform liquid carbon dioxide fracturing process is able to comprehensively analyzes the pressurization rate and pressure profile in the wellbore, investigates the time at the bottom of the well to reach the cracking pressure at different displacements and also provides reasonable suggestions for fracturing site operations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing liquid carbon dioxide fracturing process involves entering the wellbore structure parameters, reservoir or experimental core physical and mechanical parameters, formation stress and thermodynamic parameters, fracturing operation inlet conditions and operations parameter according to the wellhead pressure, inlet temperature, carbon dioxide flow rate, density, friction coefficient and well depth and wellbore diameter, using the basic equations of carbon-di-oxide flow in the wellbore to obtain different rows according to the relationship between the temperature and pressure of the wellbore during obtained carbon-di-oxide fracturing, and the carbon-di-oxide physical property parameter model to obtain relationship between the physical properties of carbon-di-oxide in the wellbore and the depth of the well at different displacements. The relationship between the physical properties of carbon-di-oxide in obtained wellbore and the well depth, the external radial stress sigma theta r and the pressure caused by the wellbore are obtained. The tangential stress sigma theta p, the tangential stress sigma theta caused by the pore pressure, and the tangential stress T caused by the thermal effect are superimposed, and the four basic loads are superimposed to obtain the relational expression of the tangential stress sigma theta at the well wall. The minimum value theta is taken, the crack initiation pressure at the determined displacement is obtained. The liquid carbon dioxide consumption and displacement are performed fracturing construction at a pressure not less than the initiation pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves programming and calculation of each equation or model using Matlab software.

PN IN201721007597-A
TI Preparing composite catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprises e.g. providing porous anodic alumina as aluminum substrate, anodizing, impregnating reduced graphene oxide on porous anodic alumina films and depositing.
AU GAIKAR V G
   KANDY M M
AE INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (CHTE-Non-standard)
GA 2018715209
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprises: (a1) providing a porous anodic alumina (PAA) as aluminum substrate, prepared by pre-treatment involving annealing, and electro-polishing; (a2) anodization to form thin layer of aluminum on the substrate as porous anodic alumina films (PAA/Al/PAA); (a3) impregnation of reduced graphene oxide on porous anodic alumina films (PAA/Al/PAA) at elevated temperature to form reduced graphene oxide porous anodic alumina (RGO)/PAA/Al/PAA as substrate; (a4) deposition of cadmium sulfide ions on substrate of step (a3) by reacting cadmium nitrate with sodium sulfide to form adherent film; (a5) cationic and anionic adsorption cycle is followed with drying process is cycle is repeated for 10 times to obtain a homogenous and well adherent film.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing composite catalyst which is used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: does not use any toxic solvents like dimethyl sulfoxide during synthesis of cadmium sulfide; is performed on large scale with a series of photocatalytic cells and hence establishes its applicability for large scale use; and increases the mechanical strength and electrical conductivity of the substrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) composite catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprising cadmium sulfide layer/s deposited on the surface of the reduced graphene oxide which is impregnated on the porous anodic alumina as substrate;
   (2) photochemical cell for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprising end plate of acrylic sheet of and the middle plate is with a cavity, inlet and outlet is provided on two opposite ends of middle plate , the top plate has got a window where in quartz plate of is mounted which is as shown in figure 1 of the specification where exposed active area of the catalyst/reactor is as shown in figure 1 of the specification, and each photocatalytic cell is loaded with film catalyst consisting of RGO/cadmium sulfide nanorod catalyst; and
   (3) continuous photochemical reduction of carbon dioxide comprising passing moist carbon dioxide to the reactor after introduction of carbon dioxide from cylinder to water bubbler saturator, reactions are carried out using sun as the source of energy, series of photocatalytic cells are arranged in a series, compound parabola is used a line focus concentrator, samples are collected continuously using a gas sampling tube, analysis using gas chromatography technique using thermal conductivity detector and flame ionization detector.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The anodization is carried at room temperature. The impregnation of reduced graphene oxide layer is formed by evaporation of dilute reduced graphene oxide at 100 degrees C. Preferred Components: The cadmium nitrate is present at 0.01 M. The sodium sulfide is present at 0.01 M. The photocatalytic cells are at least two.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Tin(IV) oxide powder used in ink for forming            catalyst for manufacturing electrode used in system for            converting carbon dioxide into formate or formic acid,            comprises hollow spheres comprising tin(IV) oxide            particles, and having preset size.
AU NGUYEN-PHAN T
   KAUFFMAN D R
AE BATTELLE MEMORIAL INST (BATT-C)
   BATTELLE MEMORIAL INST (BATT-C)
GA 2022A66181
AB    NOVELTY - A tin(IV) oxide powder comprises 90 %mass or                more hollow spheres comprising tin(IV) oxide                particles, and having a size (diameter) of 175-225                nm, preferably 190-210 nm.
   USE - Tin(IV) oxide powder is used in ink for                forming catalyst for manufacturing electrode used                in system for converting carbon dioxide into                formate or formic acid (all claimed) which is used                as emerging fuel for fuel cell, liquid hydrogen                carrier with high volumetric capacity and biomass                upgrading applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The three-dimensional hollow nanosphere                electrocatalyst formed using the tin(IV) oxide                powder with heterogeneous size distribution enables                conversion of carbon dioxide into formate with high                current density and Faradaic efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   manufacture of tin(IV) oxide catalyst, which                involves providing a suspension of polymer                particles, combining a tin salt with the                suspension, removing the liquid from the suspension                by evaporation to form tin-coated polymer                particles, drying the tin-coated polymer particles,                and calcining the dried particles to burn out the                polymer particles to obtain hollow tin(IV) oxide                spheres;
   a catalyst ink, which comprises the tin(IV)                oxide particles dispersed in a liquid phase along                with conductive particles and binder                particles;
   an electrode, which comprises a conductive                substrate coated with the tin(IV) oxide                powder;
   manufacture of the electrode;
   a system, which comprises the electrode                arranged in a solution that is saturated with                carbon dioxide; and
   conversion of carbon dioxide into formate or                formic acid, which involves contacting a tin(IV)                oxide-coated electrode with carbon dioxide and                water, and passing an electrical current through                the electrode, where the carbon dioxide and water                react over the tin(IV) oxide catalyst to form                formate. The catalyst has a Faradaic efficiency to                formate of 50% or more, preferably 60-85% after                operating for at least 24 hours without replacing                or regenerating the electrode, measured at a                potential in the range of 0.7-1.4 V vs. reversible                hydrogen electrode, preferably 0.9-1.3 V vs.                reversible hydrogen electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: At least 90 %mass of                  the spheres have diameter within 10 nm,                  preferably 7 nm or 5 nm or 3 nm. At least 90                  %mass of the spheres have diameter of 200-220 nm.                  The hollow spheres are comprised of nanocrystals                  having a mass average diameter of 5-15 nm,                  preferably 6-9 nm, an average crystallite size of                  5-10 nm, preferably 6-8 nm measured by X-ray                  diffraction (XRD). The catalyst ink comprises 50%                  or more, preferably 80% or more tin(IV) oxide                  particles, 2% or more, preferably 3-15%                  conductive particles and 5% or more, preferably                  5-40% binder. The conductive particles comprise                  carbon black, carbon fibers, carbon or graphene                  sheets, or carbon nanotubes. The conductive                  substrate comprises a porous carbon paper.                  Preferred Properties: The hollow spheres have a                  wall thickness of 20-35 nm, preferably 25-30 nm.                  The tin(IV) oxide powder has durability of                  maintaining formate (mA.cm-2) of 35 or more,                  preferably 50 or more at 1.2 V vs. reversible                  hydrogen electrode for at least two days without                  regeneration, a double-layer capacitance of 10                  mF.cm-2, and an electrochemical surface area of                  35 cm-2or more. The catalyst has an                  electrochemical surface area of 35 cm-2or more,                  preferably 45-52 cm-2, and a Faradaic efficiency                  to formate of 50% or more, preferably 60-85%                  after operating for at least 24 hours without                  replacing or regenerating the electrode.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The suspension is an                  aqueous suspension. The polymer particles                  comprise poly(methyl methacrylate) spheres,                  polystyrene spheres, carboxylic polystyrene                  spheres, poly(n-butyl acrylate-acrylic acid)                  spheres, carbon spheres, or silica spheres. The                  binders are polymeric binders, preferably                  sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene-based                  fluoropolymer-copolymers. The liquid phase                  comprises 50% or more or mixture of                  alcohols.

PN CN114717611-A; CN217677824-U
TI Centralized collection method of carbon dioxide in            production of electrolytic aluminum, involves sealing            space between electrolyte liquid and furnace surface            insulating material, and introducing flue gas into gas            collecting flue.
AU XIONG X
AE GUIZHOU XINSENJU MINING IND CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202297128C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves passing material from a                material box (7) into a discharging pipe (2)                through a feeder. The material passes through the                discharge pipe into a flue gas pipe (1). A crust                breaking hammer rod (9) capable of moving up and                down is installed in the smoke gas pipe.                Crust-breaking and blanking process is performed to                prevent a hammer head of the crust-breaking hammer                rod from being long wrapped and blocked when                furnace surface insulating material (19) is heated                to form a compact shell surface sealing cover. A                space between an electrolyte liquid (18) and the                furnace surface insulation material is sealed. A                draft fan is mainly introduced carbon dioxide from                a lower port of the flue air pipe into the furnace                air pipe. The furnace gas pipe is entered into a                gas collecting flue. The flue is collected and                treated.
   USE - Central collecting method for carbon dioxide                in electrolytic aluminum production. Can also be                used in recycling, defluorination, dust removing                the flue gas, after desulphurization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents carbon dioxide from mixing                with air, improves the concentration of carbon                dioxide, satisfies industrial recycling                concentration requirement, reduces the carbon                dioxide emission, avoids extraction of flue gas to                take away the electrolytic heat, and provides good                heat preservation effect. The power of the smoke                exhaust fan is greatly reduced, the energy                consumption is low, and the phenomenon of blocking                material of the hammer head long bag and the lower                material hole is eliminated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                centralized collecting device for carbon dioxide in                electrolytic aluminum production.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                the centralized collecting device.

AB    NOVELTY - A methane destruction apparatus (10) includes a methane-capturing module (100) for capturing fugitive methane gas emissions, and a methane conversion module (200) for receiving captured methane from the methane-capturing module. The methane-capturing module includes a fugitive methane gas emission intake (102) connected to a captured emissions line (104) having a backpressure equal to 1-3 inches of water column of 249-746 Pa, a natural gas feed (110) for feeding natural gas into the methane-capturing module, and a drain (130) for draining liquefied methane condensed within the methane-capturing module. The methane conversion module includes a conversion pad for catalytically converting the captured methane into carbon dioxide and water, a water vapor opening for outputting the water, and a carbon dioxide opening for outputting the carbon dioxide.
   USE - Methane destruction apparatus used for capturing and converting fugitive methane gas emissions into carbon dioxide and water (claimed) in oil and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The methane destruction apparatus is capable of controlling vented and fugitive methane emissions in the oil and gas industry to prevent greenhouse gas-induced climate change, and has unique characteristic that it can operate without the need to supply power on continuous basis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for capturing and converting fugitive methane gas emissions into carbon dioxide and water, which involves: (a) capturing the fugitive methane gas emissions using the methane-capturing module by maintaining backpressure equal to 1-3 inches of water column of 249-746 Pa in captured emissions line connected to fugitive methane gas emission intake, feeding natural gas into the methane-capturing module via natural gas feed to natural gas line (112) and draining liquids condensed within the methane-capturing module; and (b) converting captured methane from the methane-capturing module using the methane conversion module by catalytically converting the captured methane into carbon dioxide and water.

PN US11242419-B1
TI Making propylene polymers and copolymers via zero carbon dioxide emissions process by converting alkanes to ethylene, propylene, and butene using renewable electric power in oxidative-coupling of methane plant and polymerizing propylene.
AU MCHANEY S C
   LAWRENCE D
   FOWLER J N
AE REXTAC LLC (REXT-Non-standard)
GA 2022211549
AB    NOVELTY - A substantially zero carbon dioxide emissions process for making propylene polymers and copolymers involves (a) converting alkanes to ethylene, propylene, and butene using renewable electric power in an oxidative-coupling of methane (OCM) plant, by (i) converting natural gas and oxygen in oxidation reactor of ethylene plant to produce oxidation reactor effluent comprising ethylene and passing oxidation reactor effluent to first fractionation train to produce ethylene streams, (ii) converting portion of first ethylene stream in dimerization unit of 2-butene plant to produce dimerization unit effluent comprising 2-butene and passing dimerization unit effluent to second fractionation train, and (iii) converting 2-butene stream and portion of second ethylene stream in metathesis reactor of propylene plant to produce metathesis reactor effluent comprising propylene and passing metathesis reactor effluent to third fractionation train to recover propylene; and (b) polymerizing propylene.
   USE - A substantially zero carbon dioxide emissions process for making propylene polymers and copolymers.
   ADVANTAGE - The integrated process results in substantially zero carbon dioxide emissions into the atmosphere while producing any type of propylene polymer or copolymer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A substantially zero carbon dioxide emissions process for making propylene polymers and copolymers involves (a) converting alkanes to ethylene, propylene, and butene using renewable electric power in an oxidative-coupling of methane (OCM) plant, by (i) converting natural gas and oxygen in oxidation reactor of ethylene plant to produce oxidation reactor effluent comprising ethylene and passing oxidation reactor effluent to first fractionation train to produce ethylene streams, (ii) converting portion of first ethylene stream in dimerization unit of 2-butene plant to produce dimerization unit effluent comprising 2-butene and passing dimerization unit effluent to second fractionation train, and (iii) converting 2-butene stream and portion of second ethylene stream in metathesis reactor of propylene plant to produce metathesis reactor effluent comprising propylene and passing metathesis reactor effluent to third fractionation train to recover propylene; and (b) polymerizing propylene and, optionally, the third ethylene stream in a polypropylene plant while applying a propylene polymerization technology, using renewable electric power, and scrubbing carbon dioxide from any fired heater or boiler stack gas(es), emitting substantially zero carbon dioxide atmospheric emissions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Processes: Natural gas comprises methane, and oxidation reactor effluent comprises unconverted methane, where the method further comprises recycling a stream of the unconverted methane from the first fractionation train to the oxidation reactor. Dimerization unit effluent comprises unconverted ethylene, where the method further comprises recycling a stream of unconverted ethylene from the second fractionation train to the dimerization unit. Metathesis reactor effluent comprises unconverted ethylene and unconverted 2-butene, where the method further comprises recycling a stream of unconverted ethylene and unconverted 2-butene from the third fractionation train to the metathesis reactor. Converting the natural gas and oxygen in the oxidation reactor to produce the oxidation reactor effluent comprising ethylene comprises contacting a catalyst in the oxidation reactor with oxygen and the natural gas at a pressure of 6-16 bar and a temperature of 600-800degrees Celsius to produce the oxidation reactor effluent, where (i) the catalyst is selected from the group consisting of a lanthanide series metal supported catalyst and an actinide series metal supported catalyst; (ii) the natural gas comprises a mixture of alkanes; and (iii) the oxidation reactor effluent comprises ethylene and carbon dioxide. Metathesis reactor effluent comprises propylene, unconverted ethylene, and unconverted 2-butene, where passing the metathesis reactor effluent to the third fractionation train to recover the propylene further comprises recovering the unconverted ethylene and unconverted 2-butene along with the propylene.Preferred Component: Dimerization unit effluent comprises 2-butene and by-products.Preferred System: Polypropylene plant further comprises hydrocarbon-fueled heaters and/or boilers, where the oxygen enhanced combustion is employed to limit nitrogen-based gas volumes.

PN CN113480248-A
TI Sealing and storing foaming concrete carbon, comprises mixing crushed solid waste with cement, stirring mixed raw material and hot water to obtain hot slurry, introducing purified flue gas generated by boiler, subjecting carbon-dioxide in cleaned flustrated air to carbonation maintenance and drying.
AU HUANG Y
   PENG S
   ZHANG S
   YANG Y
AE CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 2021C0846G
AB    NOVELTY - Sealing and storing foamed concrete carbon comprises (a) mixing the crushed solid waste with cement, (b) stirring the mixed raw material and hot water to obtain a hot slurry, (c) introducing purified flue gas generated by a boiler to the hot silt, so that the carbon dioxide in the clean flue air reacts with the hot slur to finish the cement foaming process, (d) injecting the foamed slurry after foaming into a shaping mold to foam and solidify, (e) sending the foasted brick obtained after solidifying into the carbonatation curing chamber, (f) introducing clean flustered gas into carbonating curing chamber, subjecting the carbon-dioxide in the cleaned flustrated air to carbonation maintenance and drying, and (g) using the steam produced by the boiler or the steam turbine to steam and maintain and drying the carbonated and cured foamed brick.
   USE - Method for sealing and storing foaming concrete carbon used for building wall body and heat preservation. Can also be used in building material production and construction operation, reinforcing low carbon, negative carbon technology in construction of popularization and application, to relieve carbon discharge pressure and solve climate problem of new way.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables directly introducing the clean flue gas discharged from the boiler into the concrete slurry, using aeration and bubble reaction, hardening reaction for a certain time, forming directly fixed flue gases and carbon dioxide of the foaming concrete brick, so that the cost is low to realize the collection of carbon dioxide in the flue gasses. The carbon dioxide bubble in the concrete forms good heat insulation material, for building wall construction and heat preservation. The method utilizes boiler fly ash, boiler slag, or steel slag and other solid waste of steel plant crushing and preparing powder, mixing other concrete raw material, can realize the waste utilization, and can capture the boiler or steel plant to discharge the carbon dioxide.

PN US2021298632-A1; WO2022220823-A1
TI Sensor system for measuring partial pressure of            carbon dioxide in mucosal tissue, has sensor that is            responsive to alternating electrical potential to            measure impedance of electrolyte-free liquid.
AU WINGER K R
   PIERSKALLA I T
AE EXOSTAT MEDICAL INC (EXOS-Non-standard)
   EXOSTAT MEDICAL INC (EXOS-Non-standard)
GA 2021B2036C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a sensor (10) with a C-shaped                in a cross-section sensor cover (12) defining an                opening (26) bounded on a top by an underside of                the sensor cover. A membrane body (18) is housed                within the opening, where the membrane body                comprises an amorphous fluoroplastic and forms an                enclosed chamber including a closed end situated                against a lip (24). A sensor body (14) is arranged                at an end for coupling the end to the cover.                Electrodes (16) extend from the end and are                positioned within the enclosed chamber. An                electrolyte-free liquid is contained within the                chamber and in contact with the electrodes. A                sensor arm is configured to couple with the sensor                at the end, where the end is on a plane.
   USE - Sensor system for measuring partial pressure                of carbon dioxide in a mucosal tissue of a subject                i.e. patient, during a hypoperfusion process.
   ADVANTAGE - The sensor can be placed proximate to a buccal                tissue of a subject without air gaps and without                applying pressure in excess of 25 mm Hg using a                sensor placement device and measuring partial                carbon dioxide in a buccan tissue. The sensor                system can be used on an oral mucosal tissue which                is inherently moist such that the oral mucosal                tissue can be employed sublingually with a tongue                assisting to seal off ambient exposure. The system                achieves a correct amount of flexibility so as not                to create excessive pressure and include enough                pressure to keep the sensor in contact, thus                measuring an impedance of electrolyte-free liquid.                The lip of the generally C-shaped in cross-section                sensor cover can be configured to shield the                membrane body from end-tidal carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a sensor placement device for placing a                sensor for measuring partial pressure of carbon                dioxide against a buccal tissue of a subject;
   (2) a method for determining partial pressure                of carbon dioxide in a tissue.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of a carbon                dioxide sensor.
   Sensor (10)
   Cross-section sensor cover (12)
   Sensor body (14)
   Electrodes (16)
   Membrane body (18)
   Lip (24)
   Opening (26)

PT P
PN CN113200573-A
TI Preparing hollow shell spherical cobalt tetroxide by adding cobalt solution and sodium carbonate solution to reactor, passing carbon dioxide gas, synthesizing cobalt carbonate under stirring, aging, separating, drying, sieving, calcining and collecting hollow shell spherical cobalt carbonate product.
AU CHEN X
   WANG P
   WANG F
   GUAN L
   DU P
   LI G
   YANG Q
   LIU X
AE JINCHUAN GROUP CO LTD (JCJT-C)
   LANZHOU JINCHUAN NEW MATERIAL SCI & TECH (LANZ-Non-standard)
GA 202192543W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of hollow shell spherical cobalt tetroxide, involves (a) using cobalt chloride as a raw material, preparing the cobalt solution as the solution (A), and preparing the sodium carbonate solution as the solution (B), (b) at the beginning of the synthesis reaction, adding the solutions (A) and (B) into the reactor in parallel flow, passing carbon dioxide gas into the reactor in a certain way, performing the synthesis of cobalt carbonate under stirring, and the flow of solutions (A) and (B), and strictly controlling carbon dioxide flow, reaction temperature, stirring intensity and reaction time during the synthesis process, (c) stopping adding liquid and passing carbon dioxide and aging, (d) separating and washing the solid-liquid synthesis product, (e) drying and sieving the washed synthetic product to obtain a hollow shell spherical cobalt carbonate synthetic product, (f) calcining and collecting the hollow shell spherical cobalt carbonate product, and (g) cooling.
   USE - Preparation method of hollow shell spherical cobalt tetroxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is a simple and easy-to-control production process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation method of hollow shell spherical cobalt tetroxide, involves (a) using cobalt chloride as a raw material, preparing the cobalt solution as the solution (A), and preparing the sodium carbonate solution as the solution (B), (b) at the beginning of the synthesis reaction, adding the solutions (A) and (B) into the reactor in parallel flow, passing carbon dioxide gas into the reactor in a certain way, performing the synthesis of cobalt carbonate under stirring, and the flow of solutions (A) and (B), and strictly controlling carbon dioxide flow, reaction temperature, stirring intensity and reaction time during the synthesis process, (c) after completing the synthesis reaction, stopping adding liquid and passing carbon dioxide and aging, (d) separating and washing the solid-liquid synthesis product after aging, (e) drying and sieving the washed synthetic product to obtain a hollow shell spherical cobalt carbonate synthetic product, (f) calcining and collecting the hollow shell spherical cobalt carbonate product, and (g) sending the obtained product to a rotary cooler, and cooling to obtain a hollow shell spherical cobalt tetroxide product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reactor in the step (b) is a 30 L reactor, and the introduction of carbon dioxide gas into the reactor is the introduction of gas from the bottom of the reactor. During the reaction process, the conditions includes the solution (A) flow rate at 8-10 ml/minute, the solution (B) flow rate at 12-16 ml/minute, the carbon dioxide flow rate at 1-2 l/minute, the reaction temperature at 43-47 degrees C, the stirring intensity at 240-260 rpm, and the reaction time for 16-18 hours. In step (c), the aging time is 0.5-1 hours. In step (d), the solid-liquid separation and washing device is a Buchner funnel, a suction filter pump and a suction filter bottle, and the washing material uses deionized water at 80-90 degrees C. In step (e), the device for drying the material is an oven, and the drying temperature is 80-90 degrees C, and a 200-mesh sieve is used for sieving. In step (f), the calcination conditions are calcination in a box-type atmosphere furnace, and the calcination is performed at 700-720 degrees C for 2-3 hours. In step (g), the cooling time of the rotary cooler is 5-10 minutes, and the rotation speed of the rotary cooler is 110-120 rpm. Preferred Composition: In step (a), the cobalt concentration of the cobalt solution is 50-70 g/l, and the concentration of the sodium carbonate solution is 100-120 g/l. Preferred Properties: In step (g), the physical and chemical indexes of the hollow shell spherical cobalt tetroxide product are that the laser particle size D50 is 12-14 mu m, the tap density of 1-1.2 g/cm3, the specific surface area of 0.5-1.5 m2/g, and the shape is hollow shell spherical.

TI Mass transfer enhanced carbon dioxide            electroreduction electrolytic cell, has feeding groove            which is in communication with material channel, and            opposite end is provided with discharging groove in            communication with electrolyte cavity.
AU DU Z
   XU G
   SONG P
   DENG Z
   LI B
   MAO Q
AE STATE GRID CORP CHINA (SGCC-C)
   UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
   GLOBAL ENERGY INTERCONNECTION RES INST (SGCC-C)
   GLOBAL ENERGY INTERCONNECTION RES INST (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID CORP CHINA (SGCC-C)
GA 201998451S
AB    NOVELTY - The electrolytic cell has a second insulating                surface arranged on a side of the solution flow                field plate (8) near the cathode gas diffusion                electrode (9). A flow channel running through the                first insulation surface and the second insulation                surface, the electrolyte supporting layer (7), the                cathode gas diffusion electrode and the flow                channel cooperate to form an electrolyte cavity for                the reaction of carbon dioxide and the electrolyte.                A feeding groove connected with the electrolyte                cavity is provided at one end of the electrolyte                cavity. The feeding groove is in communication with                the material channel, and the opposite end is                provided with a discharging groove in communication                with the electrolyte cavity, and the discharging                groove is in communication with the material                channel.
   USE - Mass transfer enhanced carbon dioxide                electroreduction electrolytic cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrolytic cell realizes the reactant                flowing out of the electrolyte cavity along the                flow channel. The design of the flow channel makes                the shape of the electrolyte cavity into a                serpentine shape. The reactants flow in from the                material channel and enter the electrolyte cavity                through the feed groove to participate in the                electrochemical reaction. The reactants and                products pass through the discharge groove Out of                the serpentine electrolyte cavity, enter the                material channel. The flow channel increases the                flow path of the reactants in the electrolyte                cavity, thus increasing the degree of reaction of                the reactants in the electrolyte cavity. The                reduction rate of the carbon dioxide electric                reduction ability is reduced, thus improving the                stability of the carbon dioxide electric reduction                ability in a long-term reaction process, and                ensuring the stability of the product yield. The                setting of the material channel realize the flow of                the electrolyte and the update of the electrolyte,                thus improving the stability of the long-term                operation of the carbon dioxide                electroreduction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of an                assembly structure of a mass transfer enhanced                carbon dioxide electroreduction electrolytic                cell.

PN CN114383449-A
TI Control method of actively regulated carbon dioxide heat pipe cooling system, involves obtaining condenser inlet temperature, and evaporator outlet and inlet temperature and pressure difference, and increasing temperature difference, and evaporator outlet and inlet temperature difference.
AU HAN Z
   WU H
   TONG Z
AE UNIV QINGDAO TECHNOLOGY (UQDT-C)
GA 202260209G
AB    NOVELTY - The control method of the actively regulated carbon dioxide heat pipe cooling system, involves obtaining condenser inlet temperature T1, evaporator outlet temperature T2, evaporator inlet temperature T3 and evaporator inlet and outlet pressure difference DeltaP, increasing the temperature difference DeltaT1=T1-T2, and evaporator outlet and inlet temperature difference DeltaT2=T2-T3. When the fluctuation range of DeltaP exceeds the first threshold, it is considered that the heat pipe cooling system is in a fluctuating operation state, and the opening of the first control valve is reduced by the set angle. When the fluctuation range of DeltaP does not exceed the first threshold, continue to judge the operating state of the heat pipe system according to DeltaT1 and DeltaT2, as follows if DeltaT2 greater than the second threshold, it is considered that the heat pipe cooling system exceeds its heat transfer limit, the second control valve is closed, and the liquid pump is turned on.
   USE - Control method of the actively regulated carbon dioxide heat pipe cooling system.
   ADVANTAGE - Through the first control valve and liquid pump and other equipment, according to the operating characteristics of the separated carbon dioxide heat pipe, different control methods are adopted under different loads, which fully expands the load adaptation range of the heat pipe system and ensures that the heat pipe system can perform under different loads. The normal operation is realized, the stability and reliability of the heat pipe system are improved, and the advantages of carbon dioxide working medium can be exerted.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a control flow of an actively regulated carbon dioxide heat pipe cooling system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN113769790-A
TI Preparing carboxy-functionalized ionic liquids-metalorganic frameworks composite material useful in catalyzing carbon dioxide and epoxide cycloaddition reaction, comprises e.g. reacting zirconium(IV) chloride, terephthalic acid and 1,3-bis(carboxymethyl)-1H-imidazol-3-ium chloride.
AU QIU T
   YANG C
   HUANG Z
   WANG X
   WANG Q
   YE C
   QI Z
   CHEN J
   LUO J
   LI L
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2021E9651J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carboxy-functionalized ionic liquids-metalorganic frameworks composite material, comprises (i) dissolving zirconium(IV) chloride in dimethylformamide and acetic acid solution uniformly, ultrasonicating, adding organic ligands and ionic liquid ligands, performing ultrasonication, placing in hydrothermal synthesis reactor, and reacting in oven at 110-150degrees Celsius for 24-36 hours, (ii) naturally cooling to room temperature, taking out liquid and centrifuging, dissolving precipitate in dimethylformamide for washing and centrifugation, and then dissolving precipitate in methanol for washing and centrifugation, and (iii) drying filtered product in oven at 70-110degrees Celsius to obtain final product.
   USE - The carboxy functionalized ionic liquid-metalorganic frameworks composite material is useful in catalyzing carbon dioxide and epoxide cycloaddition reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material can efficiently adsorb and convert carbon dioxide as catalyst, and exhibits excellent catalytic activity and stability in the cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) Carboxy-functionalized ionic liquids-metalorganic frameworks composite material, obtained by above method; and
   (2) Use of carboxy functionalized ionic liquids-metalorganic frameworks composite material in catalyzing carbon dioxide and epoxide cycloaddition reaction, comprising adding carboxy-functionalized ionic liquids-metalorganic framework composites and epoxide into the autoclave, charging reaction kettle with carbon dioxide, and reacting for 30-180 minutes at a temperature of 90-130degrees Celsius and rotational speed of 200-400 revolutions/minutes, placing reactor in ice-water bath and cooling to 0degrees Celsius, releasing excess gas in the kettle, and taking out composite material and product and separating, washing composites 3-5 times with dimethylformamide and methanol, and then recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic ligand is terephthalic acid. The ionic liquid ligands are 1,3-bis(carboxymethyl)-1H-imidazol-3-ium chloride or 1,3-bis(carboxymethyl)-1H-imidazol-3-ium bromide. The mole fraction of the ionic liquid ligands accounts for 33-67% of the total ligands. The molar ratio of zirconium(IV) chloride and total ligand is 0.5-2:1. The molar ratio of the addition amount of epoxide to the addition amount of carboxy-functionalized ionic liquid-metal organic frameworks composite material is 1:100-3:100.Preferred Method: The washing and centrifugation in step (ii) is carried out by washing at 25-45degrees Celsius for 2-5 hours, and repeating the washing for 3-5 times and then centrifuging. The carboxy-functionalized ionic liquids-metalorganic frameworks composite material and products are separated by simple centrifugation.

PT P
PN CN112625427-A
TI Sound insulation material is prepared by reacting molded product formed from polyoxypropylene glycol, stannous octoate, triethanolamine, silicone oil, controllable porogen, and diphenylmethane diisocyanate, and curing under carbon dioxide.
AU LI X
AE CHENGDU LUKUIPING ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202141950J
AB    NOVELTY - A sound insulation material is prepared by reacting preformed liquid, molded products, controllable porogen, and diphenylmethane diisocyanate, and curing under carbon dioxide atmosphere. The preformed liquid is prepared by mixing polyoxypropylene glycol, deionized water, stannous octoate, triethanolamine, triethylamine, and silicone oil. The molded product is prepared by pulverizing reed straws and performing molded reaction with a suspension of Trichoderma aeruginosa. The controllable porogen is prepared by mixing (N-amidino)dodecylacrylamide, polyethylene glycol lactate, p-dichlorobenzene, ferrocene, and tetrahydrofuran. The carbon dioxide atmosphere is realized by accurately controlling the carbon dioxide inflow amount of the steaming kettle.
   USE - Sound insulation material.
   ADVANTAGE - The sound insulation material has low frequency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the sound-insulating material, which involves (1) weighing and crushing the reed stalks, obtaining straw crushed material, mixing the crushed straw with the suspension of T.aeruginosa dark green and placing the mixture into the mildew solution reaction chamber, molding to obtain a molded product, (2) preparing controllable porogen by weighing (N-amidino)dodecyl acrylamide, dimeric polyethylene glycol lactate, p-dichlorobenzene, ferrocene, and tetrahydrofuran, stirring and reacting, (3) weighing polyoxypropylene glycol, deionized water, stannous octoate, triethanolamine, triethylamine, and silicone oil, and stirring and reacting to obtain a preformed liquid, (4) preparing reactants by continuously adding the molded product to the reaction kettle, stirring the mixture, adding the controllable porogen and diphenylmethane diisocyanate, and stirring and reacting to obtain the reactant, (5) pouring the reactants into a stainless steel mold, and moving the mold into the steaming kettle, introducing carbon dioxide, and foaming in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, after the heat preservation and foaming, curing, after the curing, and removing the mold.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide atmosphere is obtained by accurately introducing carbon dioxide into the steaming kettle at a rate of 20-25 mL/minute. Preferred Process: The method involves weighing and crushing the reed stalks for 20-30 minutes, obtaining straw crushed material, mixing the crushed straw with 108 cfu/mL suspension of T.aeruginosa dark green in a mass ratio of 10:1, and placing the mixture into the mildew solution reaction chamber at 35-45 degrees C and relative humidity of 60-70%, heating and moisturizing the mixture, after mildew reaction is completed for 10-12 days, taking out the molded product, preparing controllable porogen is prepared by mixing 60-70 pts. wt. (N-amidino)dodecyl acrylamide, 10-12 pts. wt. dimeric polyethylene glycol lactate, 1-2 pts. wt. p-dichlorobenzene, 2-3 pts. wt. ferrocene, and 100-120 pts. wt. tetrahydrofuran, placing the mixture into reactor, stirring and mixing at 50-60 degrees C and rotation speed of 250-350 rpm for 1-2 hours, (3) weighing 80-90 pts. wt. polyoxypropylene glycol, 15-20 pts. wt. deionized water, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. stannous octoate, 8-10 pts. wt. triethanolamine, 0.1-0.3 pt. wt. triethylamine, and 1.5-2 pts. wt. silicone oil, placing the mixture in a reactor, and stirring at 300-400 rpm for 3-5 minutes and reacting to obtain a preformed liquid, (4) preparing reactants by continuously and sparingly adding 5% molded product to the reaction kettle, stirring the mixture at 300-400 rpm for 20-30 minutes, adding controllable porogen of 10% of the mass of the preformed liquid, and diphenylmethane diisocyanate, and stirring and reacting for 3-5 minutes at a rotational speed of 600-800 rpm to obtain the reactant, (5) pouring the reactants into a stainless steel mold, and moving the mold into the steaming kettle, introducing carbon dioxide at a rate of 20-25 mL/minute, foaming for 2-3 hours in a carbon dioxide atmosphere at 50-60 degrees C, taking out the mold, after the heat preservation and foaming, curing for 24-30 hours, after the curing, and removing the mold.
DC A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.);

PT P
PN CN114345298-A
TI Preparing melamine resin-based nitrogen-doped mesoporous carbon dioxide adsorbent useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. dissolving poly(ethylene oxide)-poly(propylene oxide)-poly(ethylene oxide) triblock copolymer in water, centrifuging polymer microspheres and carbonizing.
AU LI M
   ZHANG R
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202258136R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing melamine resin-based nitrogen-doped mesoporous carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises (i) dissolving 0.6 g poly(ethylene oxide)-poly(propylene oxide)-poly(ethylene oxide) (PEO-PPO-PEO) triblock copolymer F127 in water at room temperature and adding 0.69 ml 1,3,5-trimethylbenzene (TMB) to react; (ii) placing melamine and 0.378 g formaldehyde solution 0.23 ml in 40 ml deionized water, dropping 0.05 ml sodium hydroxide, stirring at 100degrees Celsius for 0.5 hours until solution is uniform and transparent to obtain melamine resin prepolymer solution; (iii) adding F127/3,3',5,5'-tetramethyl -benzidine mixed solution to obtain mixed solution and heating; (iv) centrifuging polymer microspheres, washing, drying and grinding to powdery form, carbonizing at 600-800degrees Celsius under nitrogen to obtain nitrogen-containing mesoporous carbon; and (v) centrifuging polymer microspheres, washing, drying and grinding to powdery form, carbonizing at 600-800degrees Celsius under nitrogen atmosphere.
   USE - The adsorbent is useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has large specific surface area, high porosity and excellent properties, and exhibits a good application prospect in the field of carbon dioxide capture and separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for adsorbent prepared by the above mentioned method.

In the step (iv), the amount ratio of the hydrochloric acid and melamine substance is 0.1-0.5.Preferred Method: The nitrogen-containing mesoporous carbon dioxide adsorbent is prepared by carbonization (600degrees Celsius) after introducing F127 surfactant on the melamine resin matrix. In the step (i), reaction is carried out for 3-6 hours. In the step (iii), the heating is carried out at 60-100degrees Celsius, and the reaction is carried out for 2-3 hours. In the step (iv), the stirring is carried out for 3-6 hours.

PT P
PN CN112358623-A
TI Synthesizing zeolite imidazole ester framework            metal organic material in supercritical carbon dioxide,            by e.g. mixing cobalt-based metal ion and            2-methylimidazole in reaction column in supercritical            system and separating product and solvent.
AU JIA D
   HAN W
AE UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
GA 202118306R
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing zeolite imidazole ester framework                metal organic material in supercritical carbon                dioxide, comprises (1) using cobalt-based metal ion                as precursor and 2-methylimidazole as organic                ligand, and mixing uniformly at room temperature;                (2) placing the mixed raw materials obtained in                step (1) in the reaction column, and placing the                reaction column in the supercritical system; (3)                opening the thermostat, setting the temperature                required for the reaction, closing the inlet valve,                outlet valve, and drain valve; when the temperature                in the chamber reaches the preset temperature, open                the inlet valve, and using a carbon dioxide                pressure pump to fill the reaction column carbon                dioxide, making it reach the preset reaction                pressure, start timing and react; (4) after the                reaction, separating the product and the solvent by                releasing the pressure and cooling; and (5) taking                out the above-obtained product, washing with                organic solvent, and drying the material in a                drying box overnight.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing zeolite                imidazole ester framework metal organic material in                supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple preparation, no need to                add organic solvent, less time-consuming, short                technological process, little environmental                pollution, is environmentally friendly preparation                process, and has strong operability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                crystal material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The cobalt-based metal                  ion includes cobalt nitrate, cobalt carbonate,                  cobalt acetate, cobalt tetroxide, cobalt oxide,                  cobalt chloride, cobalt acetylacetonate(II) or                  cobalt acetylacetonate (III). In the step (1),                  the molar ratio of cobalt-based metal ion to                  2-methylimidazole is 100:1-1:100. In the step                  (5), the organic solvent includes methanol,                  ethanol, acetone or N,N-dimethylformamide, and                  the drying time is 2-48 hours. The crystal                  structure of the material is a ZIF-67 structure,                  and its microstructure is rhombic dodecahedron                  particles with a size between 150-200 nm.                  Preferred Method: In the step (3), during the                  reaction process, the required temperature for                  the reaction is 35-100 degrees C, the reaction                  pressure is 8-40 MPa, and the reaction time is                  10-1440 minutes.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce hydrogen, oxygen                and carbon monoxide that has high efficiency.

AU WANG D
   WANG K
   WANG J
   FAN S
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
   HON HAI PRECISION IND CO LTD (HONH-C)
   UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
   HON HAI PRECISION IND CO LTD (HONH-C)
   HONGFUJIN PRECISION IND SHENZHEN CO LTD (HOPR-C)
   HON HAI PRECISION IND CO LTD (HONH-C)
GA 201964456E
AB    NOVELTY - A battery electrode is manufactured by providing a carbon nanotube material comprising carbon nanotubes; placing the carbon nanotube material into a furnace containing carbon dioxide; heating the furnace to 800-950 degrees C and oxidizing the carbon nanotube material in carbon dioxide; dispersing the carbon nanotube material in 1st solution to form a carbon nanotube suspension, where 1st solution comprises 1st organic solvent and water; dispersing an active material in 2nd organic solvent to form an active material dispersion, mixing the carbon nanotube suspension with the active material dispersion to form a 2nd solution; and drying the 2nd solution after filtering.
   USE - Manufacture of battery electrode.
   ADVANTAGE - Electrolyte has a good permeability, and sufficient active sites is provided to promote a transmission of electrons and ions. The battery assembled by the composite electrode has a good cycle stability and a good rate performance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the manufacture of battery electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further comprises providing a substrate; forming a catalyst layer on the substrate; annealing the substrate with catalyst layer in air at 700-900 degrees C for 30-90 minutes; heating the substrate with catalyst layer to a temperature of 500-740 degrees C in a furnace with a protective gas; supplying a carbon source gas to the furnace for 5-30 minutes and growing a super-aligned carbon nanotube array on the substrate; and removing the substrate. The furnace is heated to temperature of 900 degrees C. Multi-walled carbon nanotube tube wall is continuously peeled off, and a diameter of the multi-walled carbon nanotube is reduced. The carbon-oxygen single bond is formed on surfaces of multi-walled carbon nanotubes after greater than or equal to 1 multi-walled carbon nanotube tube wall is peeled off. Carbon nanotubes are uniformly dispersed in the carbon nanotube suspension. The second solution is stirred through ultrasonic unit. The second solution is heat dried at 155-180 degrees C. Preferred Components: The furnace is a tube furnace or a muffle furnace. The furnace contains no other gas. The carbon nanotube material comprises multi-walled carbon nanotubes. At least one multi-walled carbon nanotube tube wall peeled off is a sheet structure. The active material is sulfur particles. The content of sulfur particles is 80 wt.%. Preferred Parameters: Thickness of the sheet structure is 1-3 nm. Length of the sheet structure is greater than or equal to 50 nm.

PT P
PN US2019194092-A1; IN201721046122-A; ZA201807242-A; RU2702895-C1; US10654773-B2; IN343879-B
TI Integrated process for catalytic conversion of            alkanes to alkenes involves converting alkane stream            from recovery section of fluidized catalytic cracking            process to respective olefins by catalytic oxidative            dehydrogenation.
AU DOOSA H B
   THAKUR R M
   NATH V V
   GUPTA K
   BHATTACHARYYA D
   MAZUMDAR S K
   RAMAKUMAR S S V
AE INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
GA 201952871Q
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated process for catalytic conversion of                alkanes to alkenes, comprises converting an alkane                stream from recovery section of fluidized catalytic                cracking (FCC) process to respective olefins by                catalytic oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH), where                the ODH process utilizes carbon dioxide from flue                gas (2) exiting from regenerator of the FCC                process.
   USE - The integrated process is useful for catalytic                conversion of alkanes to alkenes comprising                propylene, butylenes, and ethylene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: enhances the yield of propylene,                butylenes, and ethylene by utilizing carbon dioxide                from the FCC flue gas, hence reduces carbon dioxide                emissions to atmosphere; avoids the pretreatment of                the flue gas by either carbon dioxide recovery or                oxygen removal; and requires less energy, since no                additional intermittent heaters are required for                heating feed or catalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic flow diagram of                the integrated process involving FCC and catalytic                oxidative propane dehydrogenation.
   Flue gas (2)
   Regenerated catalyst (3)
   Deactivated catalyst (4)
   Cracked hydrocarbon products (6)
   Dry gas (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process comprises:                  (a) feeding cracked hydrocarbon products (6) from                  FCC riser-reactor to a main fractionator column                  to obtain a product stream comprising of dry gas                  (10), LPG, and light naphtha; (b) separating the                  product stream in a gascon section, into LPG                  comprising 3C and 4C hydrocarbons, and dry gas                  comprising hydrogen, methane, ethane, ethylene,                  and other non-condensable gases; (c) routing the                  LPG stream to propylene recovery unit (PRU) to                  separate and obtain propane, where the propane is                  used as feed stream for ODH process; (d)                  subjecting the flue gas from FCC regenerator to                  carbon dioxide separation to recover carbon                  dioxide, where the remaining flue gas is released                  to stack; (e) pre-mixing the propane feed stream                  obtained from step (c) and the recovered carbon                  dioxide from step (d) and utilizing the mixture                  as feed mixture for ODH process to obtain                  propylene, unreacted propane, ethylene, and other                  gases; and (f) re-routing the products from step                  (e) to the gascon section after separation of                  water. The process comprises the steps (a) and                  (b) as per se, (c1) routing the dry gas to                  ethylene recovery section to separate and obtain                  ethane, where the ethane is used as a feed stream                  for ODH process, the step (d) as per se, (e1)                  pre-mixing the ethane feed stream obtained from                  step (c1) and the recovered carbon dioxide from                  step (d) and utilizing the mixture as feed                  mixture for ODH process to obtain ethylene,                  unreacted ethane, and other gases, and (f)1                  re-routing the products from step (e1) to the                  gascon section after separation of water. The                  process comprises: the steps (a) and (b) as per                  se; (c2) routing the LPG stream to PRU to                  separate and obtain 4C hydrocarbon stream from                  debutanizer section; (d2) routing the 4c                  hydrocarbon stream to the 4C-separation section                  to separate and obtain 4C alkanes comprising                  isobutane and n-butane, where the isobutane or                  n-butane or their mixture is used as feed stream                  for the ODH process; (e2) performing the step (d)                  as per se; (f2) premixing the 4C feed stream                  obtained from step (d2) and the recovered carbon                  dioxide from step (e2) and utilizing the mixture                  as feed mixture for ODH process to obtain                  butylenes, unreacted butane(s), 2C, 3C, and other                  hydrocarbons; and (g2) re-routing the products                  from step (f2) to the gascon section after                  separation of water. The FCC process is fluid                  catalytic cracking, resid fluid catalytic                  cracking, high severity fluid catalytic cracking,                  or high severity propylene maximizing fluid                  catalytic cracking process. The ODH reactor is                  fixed bed, fluidized bed and/or moving bed. The                  alkane for dehydrogenation in the ODH process is                  ethane, propane and/or 4C alkanes. The                  dehydrogenation of ethane, propane and 4C alkanes                  is carried out in the ODH process, where the 2C,                  3C and 4C alkanes are separately introduced at                  different zones of a fluidized bed riser type of                  reactor, with continuous catalyst regeneration.                  The process comprises: (a3) sending the flue gas                  from the FCC regenerator to carbon dioxide                  recovery unit for carbon dioxide separation,                  where the remaining flue gas is released to                  stack; (b3) mixing the carbon dioxide gas with                  ethane from ethylene recovery section and sending                  to feed mixture to the fluidized bed riser type                  of reactor; (c3) mixing the carbon dioxide gas                  with the propane from PRU and sending feed                  mixture to the fluidized bed riser type of                  reactor; (d3) mixing the carbon dioxide gas with                  4C alkanes from 4C-separation section and sending                  the feed mixture to the fluidized bed riser type                  of reactor; (e3) introducing the feed mixtures                  from step (b3), (c3) and (d3) separately at                  different zones of the fluidized bed riser type                  of reactor to produce ethylene, propylene, and                  butylene; (f3) continuous circulation of catalyst                  from the reactor to catalyst regeneration unit;                  (g3) re-circulating the hot catalyst to the                  reactor; and (h3) re-routing the products from                  step (e3) to the gascon section after separation                  of water. The dehydrogenation of propane and                  butanes is carried out in the ODH process, where                  the dehydrogenation of ethane, propane and                  isobutane or n-butane occur simultaneously in                  separate reactors in series on a catalyst which                  circulates from the former reactor to the latter                  with subsequent continuous catalyst regeneration.                  The process comprises: the step (a3) as per se,                  (b4) mixing the carbon dioxide gas with the 3C                  hydrocarbon product from PRU and sending the                  resultant feed mixture to PDH reactor to produce                  propylene, where the feed mixture is pre-heated                  by PDH reactor effluent stream, and (c4) mixing                  the carbon dioxide gas with 4C alkanes from                  4C-separation section and sending the resultant                  feed mixture to a butane dehydrogenation (BDH)                  reactor to produce butylenes, where the feed                  mixture is pre-heated by BDH reactor effluent                  stream, (d4) continuous circulation of catalyst                  from PDH reactor to BDH reactor and then to                  catalyst regeneration unit, (e4) re-circulating                  the hot catalyst to PDH reactor, (f4) re-routing                  the products from step (b4) and step (c4) to the                  gascon section after separation of water. The                  feed mixtures comprise of at least one of                  ethane/carbon dioxide, propane/carbon dioxide,                  isobutane/carbon dioxide, and n-butane/carbon                  dioxide, where the feed mixtures optionally                  comprise of diluents comprising steam, nitrogen                  and/or helium. The alkane feed stream from the                  PRU is mixed with hot flue gas from first stage                  regenerator of resid FCC unit and the mixture is                  directly used as feed for ODH reactor to obtain a                  product stream comprising of hydrocarbons and                  non-condensable gases. The product stream is                  optionally routed to carbon monoxide recovery                  unit for separation of carbon monoxide, where the                  carbon monoxide deficient stream is recycled to                  the FCC gascon section. The heat from the flue                  gas exiting the FCC regenerator is utilized to                  pre-heat the ethane/carbon dioxide,                  propane/carbon dioxide, isobutane/carbon dioxide,                  and n-butane/carbon dioxide feed stream prior to                  sending to ODH reactor. The heat from the                  products exiting the dehydrogenation reactor is                  utilized to pre-heat the ethane/carbon dioxide,                  propane/carbon dioxide, isobutane/carbon dioxide,                  and n-butane/carbon dioxide feed stream. The                  oxygen from the flue gas stream is removed in                  oxygen separation unit and oxygen-free flue gas                  stream is mixed with the ethane, propane or                  butane feed stream prior to feeding to ODH                  reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

AE HUNAN JINJIAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019524784
AB    NOVELTY - Method of fixing a greenhouse gas by dissolving wollastonite/calcium nitrate involves adding dilute sulfuric acid to the mixed powder composed of wollastonite and calcium nitrate, and stirring at 30-90 degrees C to obtain a solid-liquid phase reaction product, adding aqueous ammonia to the solid-liquid phase reaction product and passing carbon dioxide gas at 20-50 degrees C, stirring the reaction mixture at atmospheric pressure and filtering to obtain carbon dioxide mineral carbonation fixed product as filter residue.

TI New conjugated polymer for regulating adsorption and release of carbon dioxide in vivo, and has strong absorption, strong light-to-heat conversion ability, and high conversion efficiency.
AU XING C
   LI D
   GAO D
AE UNIV HEBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHT-C)
GA 201946432G
AB    NOVELTY - Conjugated polymer (I) is new.
   USE - Conjugated polymer for regulating adsorption and release of carbon dioxide in vivo (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The conjugated polymer has strong absorption near 808 nm, strong light-to-heat conversion ability, high conversion efficiency up to 15-35%, simply controls adsorption and release of carbon dioxide by near-infrared light dynamically, and demonstrates excellent application prospect of pyrrolopyrrole type conjugated polymer in gas adsorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Conjugated polymer of general formula (I) is new.
   R1=group of general formula (II);
   R2=-N3;
   R3=group of general formula (III);
   A=a group of general formula (IV);
   x and y=1-50;
   X=C or N atom; and
   n=4-60.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing conjugated polymer; and
   (2) a method for regulating adsorption and release of carbon dioxide, which involves heating diphenylphosphine-type conjugated polymer to a different degree by controlling illumination power.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The conjugated polymer is prepared by coupling compound of formula (V) with compound of formula (VI) under action of a catalyst and a ligand to obtain diphenylphosphine type conjugated polymer. The catalyst is tris(dibenzylideneacetone)dipalladium or tetrakistriphenylphosphinepalladium. The ligand is triphenylphosphine or tris(o-methylphenyl)phosphine. The coupling reaction is performed at 90-110 degrees C for 12-24 hours in an organic solvent selected form toluene, chlorobenzene, benzene, tetrahydrofuran or chloroform. The obtained diphenylphosphine type conjugated polymer is 3-mercapto-1-propyne or alkynylated polyethyleneimine which is subjected to a click reaction to obtain the diphenylphosphine type conjugated polymer. The dual catalyst is copper (I) bromide and pentamethyldiethylenetriamine. The click reaction is performed at normal temperature for 30-40 hours in N,N'-dimethylformamide.
