PN US2018280942-A1; TW201838713-A; TW644727-B1; US10967361-B2
TI Photocatalyst which is efficient in converting            carbon dioxide gas and water vapor to hydrocarbon under            visible light, for producing solar fuel comprises            carbon doped tin sulfide.
AU CHEN K
   SHOWN I
   CHEN W
   CHEN L L
   CHENLIN L
AE ACAD SINICA (SNIC-C)
   UNIV TAIWAN NAT (UNTU-C)
   ACAD SINICA (SNIC-C)
   UNIV TAIWAN NAT (UNTU-C)
GA 201876762E
AB    NOVELTY - A photocatalyst comprises carbon doped tin                sulfide (C-SnSx, where x is 1.5-2).
   USE - A photocatalyst for producing solar fuel                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Photocatalyst is efficient in converting                carbon dioxide gas and water vapor to hydrocarbon                under visible light.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) synthesizing nanostructured photocatalyst                containing carbon doped tin disulfide which                involves mixing tin chloride pentahydrate                (SnCl4.5H2O) and 1st substance into suitable                solvent where 1st substance is L-cysteine                (C3H7NO2S) or thiourea (CH4N2S) and suitable                solvent is distilled water or polyethylene                glycol-400 (PEG-400); and allowing SnCl4.5H2O and                1st substance to react under hydrothermal or                microwave reactor to form nanostructured                photocatalyst; and
   (2) producing a solar fuel which involves                reducing gas phase carbon dioxide (CO2) or                splitting water with photocatalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide with                photocatalyst via hydrothermal process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Photocatalyst is                  mixture of tin(II) sulfide/tin(IV) sulfide or                  tin(III) sulfide/tin(IV) sulfide. Photocatalyst                  contains co-catalyst which is selected from                  metal, metal oxide, and/or metal sulfide where                  co-catalyst is deposited on carbon doped tin                  sulfide. Co-catalyst is selected from silver                  (Ag), copper (Cu), gold (Au), platinum (Pt),                  nickel (Ni), zinc (Zn), titanium oxide (TiO2),                  zinc oxide (ZnO), tungsten oxide (WO3), copper(I)                  oxide (Cu2O), copper(II) oxide (CuO), tin oxide                  (SnO2), cadmium sulfide (CdS), molybdenum sulfide                  (MoS2), zinc sulfide (ZnS), and/or nickel sulfide                  (NiS). Contents of tin (Sn), sulfur (S) and                  carbon (C) are 29.49-37.23 at.%, 55.32-57.69 at.%                  and 7.45-12.82 at.%. Photocatalyst is compound of                  formula SnS1.95C0.43 or SnS1.49C0.2. Carbon doped                  tin sulfide is carbon doped tin disulfide (C                  SnS2) which is in a size of nanometer scale, and                  photocatalyst has a sheet structure. Carbon doped                  tin disulfide has carbon content of 0.5-20%.                  Preferred Properties: Photocatalyst has                  nanostructure which is selected from sheet type,                  flower type, sphere type, and needle shape.                  Nanostructure has dimension of 3-300 nm in length                  and in diameter. Carbon doped tin disulfide has a                  size of 3-300 nm. Nanostructured photocatalyst is                  in nanoflower morphology or nanocage morphology.                  Nanostructured photocatalyst contains SnS2                  polycrystalline phases dominated by 001, 100, 101                  and 110. Photocatalyst has maximum solar fuel                  production rate of 1.6-28.6 mu mole/gcat, hour.                  Preferred Processes: Reduction of CO2 or                  splitting of water is carried out under visible                  light.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Solar fuel is                  hydrocarbon which is selected from acetaldehyde,                  methanol and/or ethanol, or hydrogen and                  oxygen.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Material useful for manufacturing cup, comprises polypropylene, nucleating agent, carbon dioxide gas and titanium dioxide.
AU TAO X
AE SICHUAN JUFU YOUSHENG NEW MATERIALS CO (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201805020Y
AB    NOVELTY - Material comprises 75-85 pts. wt. polypropylene, 3-6 pts. wt. nucleating agent, 3-9 pts. wt. carbon dioxide gas and 3-6 pts. wt. titanium dioxide.
   USE - The material is useful for manufacturing cup (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has heat preservation, heat insulation effect and processing quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing material comprising (i) placing powdered polypropylene in heating tank, heating and melting, (ii) adding melted polypropylene flows into stirring tank through the high temperature pipe, adding nucleating agent along high temperature pipe into high temperature pipe of the flow of polypropylene and stirring mixture in stirring tank, (iii) passing stirred fluid in stirring tank into foaming tank, passing carbon dioxide gas into bottom of the foaming tank, adding titanium dioxide into foaming tank, agitating, stirring, where bubble in the tank liquid level per square meter of carbon dioxide bubble burst number does not exceed twenty, and (iv) carrying out forming in foaming tank after foaming mixture flows into the cooling molding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (iii), foaming temperature in foaming tank is 150-170 degrees C. In step (ii), density of mixture in mixing tank is 0.91 g/cm3; and in step (iii), density of foamed mixture in foaming tank is 0.2-0.9 g/cm3. In step (iii), passing carbon dioxide gas creates pores in the mixture, and diameter of the pores is 0.1-0.5 mm.

PN WO2022157223-A1
TI Preparing synthesis gas involves providing gas            mixture of hydrocarbons and steam, preparing hydrogen            and oxygen stream, reforming process, providing            reforming heat for primary reforming, treating reformed            output gas, subjecting shifted gas to carbon dioxide            removal step, and mixing hydrogen stream.
AU FILIPPI E
   OSTUNI R
   PANZA S
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 202295607C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a synthesis gas, involves                (a) providing a gas mixture of hydrocarbons (1) and                steam (2), (b) preparing a hydrogen stream (21) and                an oxygen stream (20) by water electrolysis (19),                (c) a reforming process involving a primary                reforming of the gas mixture of hydrocarbons and                steam of step (a) in presence of reforming heat,                (d) providing the reforming heat for the primary                reforming of step (c), (f) treating the reformed                output gas in water gas shift sections (10)                yielding a shifted gas (11), (g) subjecting the                shifted gas to further processing including a                carbon dioxide removal step, so that the further                processing yields a carbon dioxide-depleted gas                stream (13,16), and (h) mixing at least a portion                of the hydrogen stream (21) of step (b), obtained                by water electrolysis, with at least one process                stream selected from the partially reformed gas,                the reformed output gas, the shifted gas obtained                from the water-gas shift sections, the CC-depleted                gas stream (13,16).
   USE - The method is used for preparing a synthesis                gas and revamping a front end of an ammonia plant                for the synthesis of ammonia or methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces carbon dioxide emission,                avoids installation of costly apparatus, avoids                thermal duty unbalances between primary and                secondary reformer, reduces flow rate of fuel over                the flow rate of hydrocarbons, and outlet                temperature of the steam reformer tubes, and                increases production capacity of existing ammonia                or methanol synthesis plants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing a synthesis gas comprises                (a) providing a gas mixture of hydrocarbons (1) and                steam (2), (b) preparing a hydrogen stream (21) and                an oxygen stream (20) by water electrolysis (19),                (c) a reforming process which includes a primary                reforming of the gas mixture of hydrocarbons and                steam of step (a) in presence of reforming heat and                optionally includes a secondary reforming step,                which may be autothermal reforming, of a partially                reformed gas (7) obtained from the primary                reforming, the secondary reforming being performed                in the presence of pre-heated air (6) or in the                presence of oxygen (31), the reforming process                yielding a reformed output gas (55,9) which is                obtained directly after the primary reforming                without a secondary reforming step, or after the                secondary reforming, (d) providing the reforming                heat for the primary reforming of step (c) through                a combustion reaction between a fuel stream (4) and                an oxygen-enriched air obtained by mixing air (33)                with the oxygen stream (20) obtained in step (b),                the process further comprises (f) treating the                reformed output gas in water gas shift sections                (10) yielding a shifted gas (11), (g) subjecting                the shifted gas to further processing including a                carbon dioxide removal step, so that the further                processing yields a carbon dioxide-depleted gas                stream (13,16), and (h) mixing at least a portion                of the hydrogen stream (21) of step (b), obtained                by water electrolysis, with at least one process                stream selected from the partially reformed gas,                the reformed output gas, the shifted gas obtained                from the water-gas shift sections, the CC-depleted                gas stream (13,16). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a method for revamping a front end of                an ammonia plant, involving installing a water                electrolysis section arranged to produce oxygen and                hydrogen, providing means arranged to feed the                oxygen without a compressor to the radiant                combustion portion of the reforming section and                optionally to the steam reforming portion,                providing means arranged to mix the hydrogen either                with the CO2- depleted gas stream exiting the                carbon dioxide removal section or to feed the                hydrogen to the ammonia synthesis loop via a                dedicated compressor or via a pre-existent                compressor, where the front-end being arranged to                produce an adjusted make-up gas and reformed gas                comprising carbon monoxide, hydrogen and residual                impurities, and the front-end includes reforming                section, shift conversion section, carbon dioxide                removal section and optionally a methanation                section, the reforming section comprising a steam                reforming portion, a radiant combustion portion                heated by burners and a convective portion in fluid                communication with the radiant combustion portion,                where the steam reforming portion is traversed by a                mixture of hydrocarbons that undergoes catalytic                reforming in presence of steam and reforming heat,                the radiant combustion portion is traversed by a                fuel that is combusted in presence of air providing                the reforming heat and the convective portion is                configured to recover the excessive heat generated                by the combustion/oxidation reactions between the                fuel and the air leaving the radiant portion of the                reforming section.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view for                preparing synthesis gas.
   1Hydrocarbons
   2Steam
   4Fuel stream

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The portion of the                  hydrogen stream, preferably the majority or the                  entire amount of the hydrogen, is mixed with the                  carbon dioxide-depleted gas stream. The hydrogen                  stream is added to the carbon dioxide-depleted                  gas stream before or after a methanation step of                  the carbon dioxide-depleted gas. The method                  further involves mixing a nitrogen stream with                  the reformed output gas and/or with the carbon                  dioxide depleted gas stream. The method for the                  preparation of a synthesis makeup gas for the                  synthesis of ammonia or methanol. The primary                  reforming step is carried out in a reforming                  section comprises a steam reforming portion, a                  combustion radiant portion and a convective                  portion, where the steam reforming portion                  includes the reforming catalyst and is traversed                  by the gas mixture of hydrocarbons and steam                  undergoing reforming, the combustion radiant                  portion being configured to surround the steam                  reforming portion and being traversed by the                  oxygen-enriched air stream of step (d) and the                  fuel undergoing combustions, reforming heat is                  indirectly transferred from the radiant portion                  towards the reforming portion, the convective                  portion being in fluid communication with the                  combustion radiant portion and being arranged to                  recover the excess of heat from the combusted gas                  originated by the combustion between the fuel and                  the oxygen-enriched air exiting the combustion                  radiant portion. The reforming includes secondary                  reforming, where pre-heated air fed to the                  secondary reformer section or autothermal                  reformer section is pre-heated in the convective                  portion of the primary reforming section. The                  primary reforming step is performed at a pressure                  which is not greater than the pressure of the                  oxygen produced by water electrolysis, and the                  oxygen is fed directly without compression to the                  combustion radiant portion and optionally to the                  steam reforming portion. The electrolysis of                  water is powered by renewable energy. The method                  comprises reducing the amount of the air fed to                  the fired combustion portion, and/or installing                  or modifying heat exchanger to super heat the                  steam or preheat the natural gas or the natural                  gas mixed with steam after the secondary                  reformer, and/or increasing the heat generated in                  the radiant combustion portion of the reforming                  section, and/or increasing the heat recovered in                  the convective portion of the reforming                  section.
   EXAMPLE - The example section describes the general                preparation of synthesis of gas that does not                support the claims.

TI Synthesis of modified vegetable oil fatty acid            methyl ester includes mixing non conjugated vegetable            oil fatty acid methyl ester, unsaturated diamine and            biocatalyst, recovering carbon dioxide and filtering            residue to remove biocatalyst.
AU PENG S
   LI L
   CAO C
   CHENG J
   LI X
   WANG Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP DALIAN RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202064801X
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of modified vegetable oil fatty acid                methyl ester comprises mixing non conjugated                vegetable oil fatty acid methyl ester, unsaturated                diamine and biocatalyst, reacting under                supercritical carbon dioxide, recovering carbon                dioxide, filtering residue to remove biocatalyst                and performing pressure reducing                distillation.
   USE - The method is for synthesis of modified                vegetable oil fatty acid methyl ester.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves lubricity of low sulfur                diesel oil. The ester has good lubricity, low pour                point, small dosage and detergent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The non conjugated                  vegetable oil fatty acid methyl ester is corn oil                  fatty acid methyl ester, cottonseed oil fatty                  acid methyl ester, rapeseed oil fatty acid methyl                  ester, flaxseed oil fatty acid methyl ester,                  soybean oil fatty acid methyl ester, peanut oil                  fatty acid methyl ester and/or castor oil fatty                  acid methyl ester. The unsaturated diamine is                  1,4-diamino-2-butene, 1,4-diamino-1-butene,                  1,5-diamino-1-pentene, 1,5-diamino-2-pentene,                  1,6-diamino-1-hexene, 1,6-diamino-2-hexene,                  1,6-diamino-3-hexene,                  1,3-diamino-2-methyl-1-propene,                  1,4-diamino-2-methyl-2-butene and/or                  1,5-diamino-4-methyl-2-pentene. The molar ratio                  of unsaturated diamine to non conjugated                  vegetable oil fatty acid methyl ester is                  0.5:1-3:1, preferably 0.8:1-2:1. The biocatalyst                  is linoleic acid isomerase. The dosage of                  biocatalyst is 0.5-2% of the mass of vegetable                  oil fatty acid methyl ester.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

TI Reducing emission of methane and/or carbon dioxide            from manure during its storage, by filling storage tank            with manure, adding composition comprising calcium            cyanamide and contacting composition with manure.
AU WINKLER S
   EBERL M
   ERL S
   SANS J
   BEZLER J
   ABEL M
   EARL S
AE ALZCHEM TROSTBERG GMBH (ALZC-Non-standard)
   ALZCHEM TROSTBERG GMBH (ALZC-Non-standard)
   ALZCHEM TROSTBERG GMBH (ALZC-Non-standard)
   ALZCHEM TROSTBERG GMBH (ALZC-Non-standard)
GA 202039687T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for reducing the emission of methane                and/or carbon dioxide from manure during its                storage, involves (a) providing a storage tank for                the manure with a volume X, (b) filling the storage                tank with 5 vol.% manure of the volume X of the                storage tank, and (c) adding a composition                comprising calcium cyanamide to the storage tank                and contacting the composition with the                manure.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing the emission                of methane and/or carbon dioxide from manure during                its storage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves adding                  the composition before, during or after first                  filling with manure. The method further involves                  adding the composition (i) once after or during                  the first addition of a first portion of manure                  to the storage tank, or (b) in portions after                  each partial filling of the storage tank, or (c)                  once after or during the complete filling of the                  storage tank with manure. The method further                  involves adding 0.5-10 kg per one m3 composition                  to the manure. Preferred Device: The storage tank                  for the manure is an open storage tank or a                  closed storage tank, and the storage temperature                  of the manure is 0-60 degrees C. Preferred                  Composition: The composition comprises 10-100                  wt.% calcium cyanamide and the composition is                  added to the manure as a solid or as a                  suspension. The composition is used as                  fermentation stop or fermentation inhibitor of                  anaerobic fermentation of manure and/or as an                  inhibitor of microbial degradation of organic                  substrates in manure. The composition comprises                  (A) 25-95 wt.%, preferably 35-55 wt.% calcium                  cyanamide, (B) 5-40 wt.%, preferably 15-35 wt.%                  at least one compound chosen from magnesium                  carbonate, magnesium hydrogen carbonate,                  magnesium oxide, magnesium hydroxide, calcium                  carbonate, calcium hydrogen carbonate, calcium                  oxide and calcium hydroxide, or their mixtures,                  (C) up to 20 wt.%, 1-20 wt.% at least one nitrate                  chosen from sodium nitrate, potassium nitrate,                  magnesium nitrate and calcium nitrate, or                  mixtures, and (D) up to 15 wt.%, preferably 1-15                  wt.% free carbon, coal or graphite, and (E) up to                  10 wt.% water.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. PT P
PN CN109675556-A
TI Cerium oxide supported ruthenium catalyst for catalytic combustion of propane to water and carbon dioxide at lower temperatures, is supported by rod-shaped cerium oxide, and active component is noble metal ruthenium.
AU ZHAN W
   WANG Z
   MIN Z
   GUO Y
   WANG L
   WANG Y
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 201940789C
AB    NOVELTY - A ruthenium catalyst is supported by rod-shaped cerium oxide. The noble metal ruthenium is active component. The catalyst is capable of fully catalyzing the oxidation of propane to water and carbon dioxide at lower temperatures.
   USE - Cerium oxide supported ruthenium catalyst for catalytic combustion of propane to water and carbon dioxide at lower temperatures (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst ensuring increased stability, reduced by-products and high activity is prepared by simple process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation of rod-shaped cerium oxide carrier involves (a) dissolving lanthanum source and alkali source in certain amount of deionized water, mixing thoroughly and stirring for certain period of time, (b) transferring the mixed solution to crystallization vessel, and crystallizing at certain temperature for certain period of time to obtain a rod-shaped cerium oxide precursor, (c) centrifuging the resulting solid product several times, drying at 50-180 degrees C in oven for 2-48 hours and calcining for 2-6 hours. The sedimentation precipitation method for preparing cerium oxide supported ruthenium catalyst involves (i) mixing certain amount of rod-shaped cerium oxide powder, deionized water and ruthenium chloride aqueous solution in a three-necked flask and stirring at room temperature for certain period, (ii) under constant stirring at room temperature, dripping freshly prepared sodium hydroxide solution to the mixture and controlling the end point pH of 7-10, (iii) stirring for 2-6 hours, centrifuging and washing the solid sample, (iv) placing the obtained sample in oven at 50-100 degrees C for 2-48 hours, and calcining at 300-500 degrees C for 1-5 hours under hydrogen atmosphere. Preferred Components: The cerium source is cerium nitrate hexahydrate, cesium chloride hexahydrate, preferably cerium nitrate hexahydrate. The alkali source is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxides, preferably sodium hydroxide. Preferred Composition: The content of noble metal ruthenium to the total amount of supported ruthenium oxide is 0.5-5 %mass, preferably 2 %mass. Preferred Conditions: The reaction is carried out at 190 degrees C for 60 hours, and the catalyst is not deactivated.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))
MC J04-E04A; Q73-A01; Q73-A05; Q73-U20

TI Vitamin sugar-free bubble water beverage comprises vitamin, sugarless sweetener, sea salt and food essence.
AU NIE Y
   LIN Z
AE HEYTEA SHENZHEN ENTERPRISE MANAGEMENT CO LTD (HEYT-Non-standard)
GA 2022446103
AB    NOVELTY - Vitamin sugar-free bubble water beverage comprises vitamin, sugarless sweetener, sea salt and food essence.
   USE - Used as vitamin sugar-free bubble water beverage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing vitamin sugar-free bubble water beverage comprising (1) adding vitamin, sugar-free sweetener, sea salt and food essence and optionally stabilizing agent, preservative, acidity regulator, concentrated juice of solution, and (2) charging the solution in the step (1) with carbon dioxide, so that the gas content is 3.5 times or more of the volume of the solution, preferably 3.5-4.5 times.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The beverage further comprises one or more of stabilizer, preservative, acidity regulator. The vitamin comprises one or more of vitamin C, vitamin B, preferably vitamin C, vitamin B6, nicotinamide, more preferably vitamin C, vitamin B6 and niacinamide. The gas content of the beverage is 3.5 times or more than the volume of the beverage, preferably 3.5-4.5 times. The sugarfree sweetener is erythritol, sucralose, stevioside, aspartame, xylitol, maltitol, neotame, sodium cyclamate, acesulfame potassium and saccharin, preferably, the sugar-free sweetener is erythritol, and sucralose or stevioside, where relative to the total weight of the beverage, the content of erythritol is 0.1-10%, the content of sucralose or stevioside is 0.001-0.1 %. The food essence is sucrose taste essence and fruit flavor essence. The content of the sea salt is 0.01-0.1%, preferably 0.02-0.05 %. The vitamin fruity bubble water beverage comprising vitamin sugar-free bubble water beverage and concentrated juice, where the concentrated juice is concentrated apple juice, concentrated peach juice, concentrated orange juice, concentrated pear juice, concentrated grape juice, concentrated strawberry juice, concentrated cherry juice, concentrating the pitaya juice, concentrated hami melon juice, concentrated banana juice, concentrated pawpaw juice, concentrated lemon juice, concentrated mango juice, concentrated Chinese goosebeery juice, concentrated pineapple juice, concentrated watermelon juice, concentrated litchi juice, concentrated pomelo juice, concentrated passion fruit juice and concentrated litchi juice. The vitamin and the food essence are added under the condition of not damaging the chemical structure and activity thereof, the condition is selected from one or more of temperature, stirring, illumination, pH and so on. The food essence is added after all other components of the beverage.Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises preparing vitamin sugar-free bubble water beverage comprising (a) adding erythritol and optionally concentrated juice into the water at 50-90degrees Celsius, stirring, cooling to obtain the first material liquid, (b) adding vitamin, sucralose or stevioside to 4-40degrees Celsius of water, sea salt, stabilizing agent, preservative, acidity regulator, fully dissolving to obtain the second batch liquid, (c) mixing the first material liquid and the second material liquid, then adding food essence, mixing uniformly to obtain the concentrated liquid, (d) mixing the concentrated solution with the degassed water and then cooling; (e) inflating the product of step (d) with carbon dioxide such that the gas content is 3.5 times or more of the volume of the product, preferably 3.5-4.5 times. In the method vitamin and the food essence are added under the condition of not damaging the chemical structure and activity, the condition is temperature, stirring, illumination, pH and so on. The food essence is added after all other components of the beverage.

PN IN202041015594-A; IN352067-B
TI Kairomone canister useful for controlling pest, preferably mosquitoes in domestic and outdoor purposes, comprises kairomone lures.
AU MAYURI P K
   SEKARAN G R
   GOKUL R S
AE MGH LABS PVT LTD (MGHP-Non-standard)
GA 202047972C
AB    NOVELTY - Kairomone canister comprises two kairomone lures, where first lure is ethyl vinyl acetate (EVA) biodegradable resin coated with non-toxic proportions of active components and second lure is carbon dioxide generating bio-resin, where the active components are chosen from uric acid, lactic acid, propionic acid, octanol, phenyl acetaldehyde and hexanoic acid.
   USE - The kairomone canister is useful for controlling pest, preferably mosquitoes in domestic and outdoor purposes.
   ADVANTAGE - The kairomone canister is safer for humans and pets, non-toxic, and cost-effective, and does not contain any unpleasant odor, attracts and captures mosquitoes, and works independent of power supply.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The 2/3rd of the canister is filled with EVA resin coated with active components and 1/3rd of the canister is filled with carbon dioxide generating bio-resin. The two kairomone lures, EVA resin coated with active components and carbon dioxide generating bio-resin are separated by a thin filter paper. The thickness of the coating of active components on EVA resin is 0.5-2 mm. The thickness of pre-treated activated carbon coating is 0.1-10 mm. The pre-treated activated carbon layer forms a carbon dioxide zone, where rapid exchange and absorption of carbon dioxide occurs. The kairomone lures are released to the surroundings by the contact of air currents. The canister has a height of 20-50 mm and diameter of 35-75 mm. The kairomone canister has slits on sides and top of the canister, facilitating the release of kairomone lures. The canister is of different shapes chosen from spherical, vertical cylindrical, flat cubical or cuboidal.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition of active compounds coated on EVA bio-resin comprises (a) 5-15 vol.% propionic acid, (b) 10-30 vol.% lactic acid, (c) 30-50 vol.% octanol, (d) 5-15 vol.% hexanoic acid, (e) 5-15 vol.% phenylacetaldehyde, and (f) 5-15 vol.% uric acid. Preferred Component: The carbon dioxide generating bio-resin is EVA resin coated with pre-treated activated carbon.

   USE - Laminated body.
   ADVANTAGE - The laminated body is superior in carbon dioxide reduction effect over laminates made of unrecycled polyethylene terephthalate, and excellent in sanitation property.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The vapor-deposited layer is a metal-deposited layer, or transparent vapor-deposited layer. The barrier layer further includes a gas barrier coating film provided on a surface of the transparent deposition layer. The adhesive layer has a cured product of a polyester polyol and an aliphatic isocyanate compound. The polyester polyol has two or more hydroxyl groups as a functional group in one molecule. The isocyanate compound has two or more isocyanate groups in one molecule as a functional group. The polyester polyol is a polycondensate of an ortho-oriented polyvalent carboxylic acid or its anhydride and a polyhydric alcohol. The polyester polyol is represented by formula (I).R1, R2 and R3=hydrogen, or formula (II).The polyester polyol has an isocyanuric ring. The content of the isophthalic acid is 0.5 mol.% or more and 5.0 mol.% or less with respect to all dicarboxylic acid units constituting the polyethylene terephthalate. The intrinsic viscosity of the polyethylene terephthalate is 0.58-0.80 deciliters/g.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of alkyl salicylate involves                carrying out neutralization reaction of alkyl                salicylic acid, solvent, accelerator, neutral oil,                alkali metal and/or alkaline-earth metal oxide and                water, adding accelerating agent (A) and                accelerating agent (B), introducing carbon dioxide,                and carrying out carbonation reaction.
   USE - Preparation of alkyl salicylate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently provides alkyl                salicylate having excellent compatibility with                sulfonate, detergency, oxidation resistance, acid                neutrality, dispersion properties, abrasion                resistance and low-temperature properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The alkyl salicylic acid                  is prepared by subjecting alpha -olefin and                  salicylic acid and/or salicylate to alkylation                  reaction under the action of a catalyst. The                  catalyst is obtained by mixing ionic liquid,                  polyphosphoric acid and organic acid salt at                  30-100 degrees C for 1-12 hours. Preferred                  Composition: The alpha -olefin is 10-30C alpha                  -olefin. The salicylate is 1-4C salicylate. The                  molar ratio of salicylic acid and/or salicylate                  and alpha -olefin is 1-2:1. The ionic liquid is                  alkyl imidazolium tetrafluoroborate, alkyl                  imidazole trifluoromethanesulfonate,                  alkylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate, alkyl                  imidazolium hydrosulfide, alkylpyridine                  tetrafluoroborate, alkyl pyridine trifluoromethyl                  sulfonate, alkyl pyridine hexafluorophosphate                  and/or alkylpyridine hexahydrochloride.                  Polyphosphoric acid is phosphoric acid,                  pyrophosphoric acid and/or tripolyphosphate. The                  organic acid salt is metal salt of organic                  carboxylic acid and/or metal salt of organic                  sulfonic acid. The organic carboxylic acid is                  2-18C fatty acid. The organic sulfonic acid is                  6-18C sulfonic acid or 6-18C benzenesulfonic                  acid. The metal is alkali metal and/or                  alkaline-earth metal. The mass ratio of ionic                  liquid, polyphosphoric acid and organic acid salt                  is 100:5-30:5-20. The addition amount of catalyst                  is 1-100 %mass with respect to total amount of                  alpha -olefin, and salicylic acid and/or                  salicylate. The accelerating agent (A) is 4-10C                  aliphatic primary amine, aqueous ammonia and/or                  calcium chloride. The accelerating agent (B) is                  polymethacrylate and/or polyacrylate. Alkyl                  salicylate is prepared using 100 pts. wt. alkyl                  salicylic acid, 20-200 pts. wt. accelerator,                  200-800 pts. wt. solvent, 50-300 pts. wt. neutral                  oil, 15-150 pts. wt. alkali metal and/or                  alkaline-earth metal oxide, 10-50 pts. wt. water,                  2-50 pts. wt. accelerating agent (A) and 1-50                  pts. wt. accelerating agent (B). The amount of                  carbon dioxide introduced is 60-140% of the                  theoretical value.

PN KR2022109017-A
TI Preparing catalyst for dry reforming of methane            used e.g. for producing syn gas in dry reforming            process comprises providing perovskite crystal            structure material comprising first transition metal            and second transition metal, and depositing.
AU GUNTAE K
   SEONG A
   JOO S
AE UNIST ULSAN NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (ULSA-C)
GA 2022A0615U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst comprises (a) providing a                perovskite crystal structure material comprising a                first transition metal and a second transition                metal; and (b) depositing a precursor comprising a                third transition metal on the perovskite crystal                structure material, where portion of the third                transition metal enters into the perovskite crystal                structure by topotatic ion exchange with the second                transition metal and at least a portion of the                second transition metal elutes to form binary alloy                particles of the third transition metal and the                second transition metal on the surface of the                perovskite crystal structure material.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for producing syn gas                in dry reforming process using methane and carbon                dioxide, or as material for an anode in a solid                oxide fuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high catalytic activity and                catalyst stability, prevents carbon accumulation,                increases performance and stability of catalyst;                has high catalyst stability and activity; solves                the carbon coking phenomenon, which is a major                problem in the dry reforming reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a catalyst for dry reforming of methane                produced by above method; and
   (2) a solid oxide fuel cell, comprising an air                electrode disposed to face the anode and an                electrolyte between the anode and the cathode,                where the anode comprises a catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (b), the                  precursor is an organic precursor including a                  third transition metal, and (c) oxidizing the                  deposited organic precursor. In step (d),                  reducing the perovskite crystal structure                  material on which the precursor is deposited. In                  step (b), the deposition is performed by atomic                  layer deposition (ALD). The atomic layer                  deposition (ALD) is performed two or more times.                  The third transition metal is used in an amount                  of 0.015 mol or less based on 1 mol of the                  perovskite crystal structure material. The third                  transition metal has a lower absolute value of                  co-segregation energy than the second transition                  metal. The third transition metal is iron, and                  the second transition metal is nickel, cobalt, or                  manganese. The perovskite crystal structure                  material of step (a) has an ABO3 structure, where                  A/B greater than 1 due to lack of A site. The                  perovskite crystal structure material of step (a)                  is represented formula R(a)E(b)T1(x)T(1-x)O-(3-                  delta ) (I). In Formula (I), R is an element from                  lanthanide group, and E is an element from the                  alkaline earth metal group, T and T' are                  different elements from transition elements and                  represent a first transition metal and a second                  transition metal, respectively, O is oxygen, a                  and b is a number that is greater than 0 and less                  than 1, respectively, and is a number satisfying                  0 greater than a+b less than 1 and a greater than                  b, x is a number greater than 0 and less than 1,                  delta is a positive number of 0 or 1 or less, and                  is a value for making the compound of Formula (I)                  electrically neutral. T and T' are each                  independently nickel, cobalt, iron, copper,                  manganese, titanium, ruthenium, rhodium,                  palladium, iridium, platinum, gold, and silver,                  where the third transition metal has an absolute                  value of cavitation energy compared to T (first                  transition metal). Higher and lower than the T'                  (second transition metal). T (first transition                  metal) is titanium or chromium, and T' (second                  transition metal) is nickel, cobalt or manganese.                  The perovskite crystal structure material is                  represented by the following formula                  La(0.6)Sr(0.2)T1(x)T(1-x)O(3- delta ) (II). In                  Formula (II), T, T', x, and delta are as defined                  in Formula (I) above. The binary alloy particles                  have a size of 20-50 nm.
   EXAMPLE - Lanthanum(III) nitrate hexahydrate, Strontium                nitrate and Nickel nitrate hexahydrate were                dissolved in distilled water in stoichiometric                amounts. Separately, Titanium isopropoxide was                dissolved in ethanol, followed by addition of                ethylene glycol/citric acid as a complexing agent.                All the above solutions were mixed together and                stirred overnight. The powder was obtained by                burning at 300 degrees C or higher on a heating                plate. The powder was calcined at 600 degrees C for                4 hours to remove residual organic matter.                La(0.6)Sr(0.2)Ti(0.85)Ni(0.15)O(2.95) (abbreviated                as LSTN) was prepared. The prepared LSTN was placed                in an atomic layer deposition (ALD) chamber to                create a vacuum atmosphere. The precursor Ferrocene                chamber was heated to 160 degrees C, a temperature                at which it can be vaporized, to generate a vapor                pressure of Ferrocene. The LSTN of the ALD chamber                was heated to 350 degrees C, the passage connected                to the ALD chamber and the vacuum tube was closed,                and the heated and vaporized Ferrocene in the                precursor ferrocene chamber was supplied to the ALD                chamber and reacted with LTSN. After that, the ALD                chamber was evacuated to remove the excess                precursor and the substrate was oxidized by                exposing it to air for 300 seconds. The above                process corresponds to 1 cycle, and 10 times, that                is, 10 cycles of atomic layer deposition of iron                oxide on LSTN is LSTN10C-Fe, and 20 cycles is                LSTN-20C-Fe. After deposition via ALD, all samples                were reduced in hydrogen atmosphere to obtain                catalyst.

   LIU L
AE UNIV WUCHANG TECHNOLOGY (UYWU-Non-standard)
GA 2022A3330D
AB    NOVELTY - Lightweight interior wall sound insulation                material comprises diamine monomer, tetracarboxylic                dianhydride monomer, modified carbon nanotube and                solvent.
   USE - The lightweight sound insulation material is                useful in a building energy-saving field. Can also                be used in a lath wall, shale hollow brick, gypsum                board wall and a brick wall.
   ADVANTAGE - The lightweight sound insulation material has                waterproof effect, high flame retardancy, excellent                mechanical, sound insulation and thermal insulation                properties, and broad application prospects in the                field of building energy conservation; and greatly                reduces the volume shrinkage rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for preparing a lightweight interior wall                sound insulation material comprising (1) mixing and                stirring N-methylpyrrolidone, 4,4'-diaminodiphenyl                ether and 3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic                dianhydride, and then adding modified carbon                nanotubes and reacting to obtain the reaction                solution, (2) mixing and stirring the reaction                solution with triethylamine and acetic anhydride,                and then immediately pouring into the mold to form                polyimide gel, (3) aging the polyimide gel,                immersing in a mixed solution of acetone and water,                and performing solvent replacement treatment on the                aging gel to obtain a semifinished product, and (4)                cleaning the semifinished product with liquid                carbon dioxide to obtain a lightweight interior                wall sound insulation material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lightweight                  interior wall sound insulation material includes                  4,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether,                  3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride,                  modified carbon nanotubes and N-                  methylpyrrolidone. The mass ratio of                  N-methylpyrrolidone, 4,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether                  and 3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride                  is 23-23.2:3:1.5-1.6. The modified carbon                  nanotubes are added in an amount of 0.05-0.2% of                  the total weight of 4,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether,                  3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride and                  N-methylpyrrolidone. The modifier is                  octadecylamine. The mass ratio of triethylamine,                  acetic anhydride and 4,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether                  in step (3) is 1.07-1.08:1.08-1.09:3. In step                  (4), the volume ratio of acetone and water in the                  mixed solution of acetone and water is                  50:50-70:30.Preferred Method: The modified carbon                  nanotube is prepared by (S1) adding the calcined                  multiwalled carbon nanotubes to hydrochloric acid                  and stirring, ultrasonically processing,                  filtering, washing and drying to obtain purified                  multiwalled carbon nanotubes, (S2) adding the                  purified multiwalled carbon nanotubes into nitric                  acid, ultrasonically processing, heating,                  reacting, filtering, washing and drying, to                  obtain carboxylated carbon nanotubes, (S3)                  refluxing the carboxylated carbon nanotubes with                  excess thionyl chloride, and then removing the                  residual thionyl chloride to obtain carbonyl                  chloride carbon nanotubes, (S4) adding the                  carbonyl chloride nanotubes to                  N-methylpyrrolidone for ultrasonic treatment to                  obtain a suspension, (S5) adding the modifier to                  N-methylpyrrolidone, and then adding to the                  suspension, stirring, centrifuging and vacuum                  drying to obtain modified carbon nanotubes. The                  solvent replacement treatment in step (4)                  includes performing solvent replacement treatment                  on the aged gel with a mixed solution of acetone                  and water with 1-3 times the weight of every 8-12                  hours for a total of 3-5 times.

AU LEETAEWON
AE KOREA CIVIL ENG & BUILDING TECHNOLOGY (KOCT-C)
GA 202280876E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves analyzing first fire information based on a first condition when fire is occurred. An intake passage (20) and an exhaust passage (40) are blocked. Fire extinguishing gas is supplied to an inner side of a building. Second fire information is analyzed based on a second condition, where the first and second fire information include rate of change over time of carbon dioxide concentration, temperature, smoke concentration and oxygen concentration. The intake passage and the exhaust passage are blocked when the first fire information satisfies the first condition. The first fire information is received when the first fire information does not satisfy the first condition.
   USE - Method for controlling, detecting and suppressing fire generated in a building.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables quickly and accurately determining whether fire is occurred and extinguished based on different fire information and determination conditions, so that fire control can be effectively performed. The method enables introducing an effective suffocation extinguishing system based on quick and accurate determination, and minimizing construction of a separate facility.

The step (b) involves adding 1 ml HEK293TS suspended cells to each well of the 24-well plate. The step (c) involves adding different amounts of pre-diluted virus solution to each well and adding the virus diluent, so that the volume of virus solution in each well is the same.

PN CN114212784-A
TI Preparing graphite for friction material involves performing physical desulfurization of raw coal, drying, crushing, sieving, graphitization, intercalation oxidation, and mixing graphite and pyridinium chromate and oxalic acid.
AU XU S
   ZHAO J
   WANG P
   HAN X
   ZANG Y
   ZANG W
AE TANGSHAN KIMWAN CARBON CO LTD (TANG-Non-standard)
GA 202246919A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphite involves crushing and screening raw coal, and performing desulfurization treatment by using jigging selection. The desulfurization treatment is carried out on raw coal subjected to physical desulfurization by using 1-2 moles/liter (mol/L) sodium hydroxide, dried at 100-150degrees Celsius until the water content is lower than 5%, and crushed and sieved by 100-mesh sieve. The temperature is raised to 1800-2000degrees Celsius under the protection of inert gas, heat is preserved for 240-360 hours, and graphitization treatment is performed. The activation process is carried out by adopting carbon dioxide gas, where activation temperature is 500-600degrees Celsius, and reaction time is 6-8 hours. The activated graphite, pyridinium chromate and oxalic acid are mixed, and intercalation oxidation reaction is carried out to obtain expandable graphite.
   USE - Method for preparing graphite used as lubricating component for friction material e.g. iron base, copper base and iron-copper base powder metallurgy friction material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides friction material with good mechanical performance and wear resistance. The obtained friction material has excellent wear loss and indirectly improves the utilization rate of raw coal.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a method for preparing graphite for friction material. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw coal is selected from lignite, bituminous coal, sub-bituminous coal, coking coal or anthracite. The inert gas is nitrogen.Preferred Conditions: The jigging selection is performed by using a screen-side air chamber double-vibration jigging machine. The activated graphite, pyridinium chromate and oxalic acid are fed in the ratio of 1:0.2:5.Preferred Process: The raw coal processed is soaked in 1-2 mol/L sodium hydroxide solution, and is irradiated with 400-500 watts (W) microwave for 1-2 minutes, where total soaking time is 20-30 minutes.

PN CN114149973-A
TI Composition useful for inducing H22 cell to express calcium net protein comprises mushroom acidic polysaccharide and potassium chloride.
AU WANG C
   ZHAO Z
   GAO S
   LIU Z
   HU G
   LI X
   ZHENG Y
   LI Y
   YANG X
   HAN X
AE UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 202241227X
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises Lentinus edodes acidic polysaccharide and potassium chloride.
   USE - The composition is useful for inducing H22 cell to express calcium net protein.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: uses Lentinus edodes acidic polysaccharide as inducer to induce the H22 cell to generate inducing protein through the synergistic effect of phased pH adjustment and K-ion cell regulation; prepares calcium net protein with high expression amount with low reagent cost; and ensures industrial preparation and application of calcium net protein.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for inducing H22 cell to generate calcium net protein by using the composition comprising (a) adding H22 cells into complete culture medium to prepare cell suspension, then adding mushroom acidic polysaccharide, adjusting the pH of complete culture medium to 7.3-7.5, transferring the complete culture medium in carbon dioxide incubator to culture for 10-14 hours, adjusting the pH of the complete culture medium to 6.4-6.6, adding potassium chloride aqueous solution, continuously culturing for 2-3 hours, adjusting the pH of the complete culture medium to 7.3-7.5, continuously culturing for 20-25 hours to obtain co-culture, and (b) freeze-thawing the co-culture, centrifuging, taking the supernatant, adding ammonium sulfate into the supernatant for salting out, centrifuging, taking the precipitate and dissolving in physiological saline, dialyzing and removing salt, concentrating, purifying the concentrated solution to obtain the calcium net protein.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of the Lentinus edodes acidic polysaccharide comprises dissolving the lentinan in sodium hydroxide solution, allowing to stand for 30-50 minutes at 50-70degrees Celsius, centrifuging, taking the supernatant, adjusting the pH to 6.8-7.2 and freeze-drying. In the step (b), the co-culture is repeatedly frozen and thawed 4-6 times, where the freeze-thawing includes first freezing the co-cultures at -15 to -25degrees Celsius for 2-3 hours, and then thawing at 30-37degrees Celsius. The purifying process in the step (b) comprises adding the concentrated solution into anion exchange chromatography column, eluting with eluent composed of 8-12 mmol/l tris-hydrochloride buffer solution, 0.15-0.25 mol/l sodium chloride, 0.8-1.2 mmol/l ethylene glycol bis(2-aminoethyl ether) tetraacetic acid and 0.8-1.2 mmol/l 2-hydrazinoethanol, collecting the target component and drying to obtain the calcium net protein.Preferred Components: The mass volume ratio of lentinan and sodium hydroxide solution is 1:35-45, where the unit of mass is gram, and the unit of volume is ml. The concentration of the Lentinus edodes acidic polysaccharide after adding the complete culture medium in the step (a) is 100-170 microg/ml. In the step (a), the concentration of potassium chloride in the co-culture is 0.1-0.5 microg/ml. The anion exchange chromatography column is Diethylaminoethylene (DEAE) Sephader A-50 (Weak ion exchanger).
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

TI Photoelectric methane production biocatalyst            useful in photoelectrochemical energy conversion and            producing methane, comprises microbial core, bimetallic            intermediate layer coated on surface of microbial core            and nano-semiconductor shell deposited on side of            bimetallic intermediate layer.
AU ZHOU S
   WANG C
   YE J
AE UNIV FUJIAN AGRIC & FORESTRY (UYFN-C)
GA 202236373S
AB    NOVELTY - Photoelectric methane production biocatalyst                comprises microbial core, where the microorganism                is methanogen, bimetallic intermediate layer, which                is coated on the surface of the microbial core and                nano-semiconductor shell, where the                nano-semiconductor is deposited on the side of the                bimetallic intermediate layer away from the                microbial core.
   USE - The photoelectric methane production                biocatalyst is useful in photoelectrochemical                energy conversion and producing methane                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The biocatalyst has sandwich-structure, and                high universality, can be driven by ubiquitous                sunlight and operate normally under natural                environment conditions, overcomes the shortcomings                of low interface electron transfer efficiency,                insufficient photoelectron conversion efficiency,                and low quantum efficiency existing in traditional                bio-photoelectric systems and its maximum quantum                efficiency reaches more than 13%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) method (M1) for preparing the sandwich                structure photoelectric methane production                biocatalyst, involves adding bimetallic                intermediate layer to the microbial core solution,                then adding the nano-semiconductor shell and                reacting in the dark; and
   (2) method (M2) for producing methane,                involves uniformly mixing the photoelectric methane                production biocatalyst with a carbon-free medium,                adding 0.1-0.7 wt.% cysteine, and performing carbon                dioxide reduction methane production under                illumination of a light source.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Microorganisms: The methanogen                  comprises at least one of Methanosarcina barkeri,                  Methanobacterium formicicum, Methanosaeta                  harundinacea, Methanobrevibacter smithii,                  M.ruminantium, and Methanospirillum                  hungatei.Preferred Components: The bimetallic                  intermediate layer comprises nickel-copper alloy,                  iron-copper alloy and manganese-nickel alloy. The                  nano-semiconductor shell is made of cadmium                  sulfide, titanium dioxide and zinc sulfide. The                  microbial core, bimetallic intermediate layer                  solution and semiconductor shell are in a mass                  ratio of 1-20:1-30:1-60. The microbial core has                  effective viable count of 1-3x106/mg. The                  inorganic medium comprises trace elements,                  selenite solution, magnesium chloride, calcium                  chloride, ammonium chloride, potassium chloride                  and sodium bicarbonate. Preferred Method: In the                  method (M2), the light sources include natural                  light sources and artificial light sources, where                  the visible light is at least one of sunlight,                  LED lamps, xenon lamps and mercury lamps.

AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of low-nitrogen crude steel involves melting directly reduced iron and/or scrap in a melting furnace with arc resistance heating to a metallic melt (23) and a slag, removing the metallic melt from the melting furnace and charging into a converter, refining the metallic melt in the converter to form liquid crude steel and tapping the liquid crude steel with nitrogen content of at most 50 ppm, preferably at most 30 ppm.
   USE - Manufacture of low-nitrogen crude steel used for forming ULC steel,preferably IF steel, preferably non-grain-oriented electrical steel strip (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces low-nitrogen crude steel in which the carbon dioxide-emission is reduced and as many existing units as possible can be reused in order to keep the investment low when changing the technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of ULC steel;
   (2) unit for manufacturing low-nitrogen crude steel;
   (3) method of retrofitting existing low-nitrogen crude steelmaking unit; and
   (4) method of retrofitting existing unit for the production of ULC steel.

   Furnace vessel (15)
   Refractory material (17)
   Interior space (19)
   Electrodes (21)
   Metallic melt (23)
   Slag layer (25)
   Cover (29)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metallic melt immediately before refining has ratio of carbon content to nitrogen content of at least 20, preferably at least 100. The iron content of the slag in the melting furnace is less than 30 wt.%. The metallic melt comprises 1-5% carbon, 450 ppm or more nitrogen, 0-50 ppm optionally oxygen, 100-1500 ppm optionally phosphorus, 0-1500 ppm sulfur, 0-1.5% optionally silicon, 0-0.5% optionally manganese.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The tapped liquid crude steel comprises 600 ppm or more carbon, at most 50 ppm, preferably at most 30 ppm nitrogen, 300-2300 oxygen, 0-400 ppm optionally phosphorus, 0-1500 ppm optionally sulfur, 0-300 ppm optionally silicon and 0-0.4 %mass optionally manganese.

PN CN113980338-A
TI Thermoplastic polyurethane foam particle used in automotive interiors, beds, seats, and shoe materials, comprises thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, hyperbranched polyimide, and supramolecular foaming improver containing cyclic structure.
AU HUANG Q
   SUN P
   ZHANG J
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 202223023U
AB    NOVELTY - Thermoplastic polyurethane foam particle comprises 100 parts mass thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, 0.2-5 parts mass, preferably 0.5-2 parts mass hyperbranched polyimide, and 0.01-1 part mass, preferably 0.05-0.5 part mass supramolecular foaming improver containing cyclic structure.
   USE - The thermoplastic polyurethane foamed particle is used in automotive interiors, beds, seats, and shoe materials, and in preparation of sole materials and automobile interiors (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The thermoplastic polyurethane foam particle has excellent low-temperature bending resistance and has the characteristics of low density, high resilience and excellent mechanical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the thermoplastic polyurethane foamed particle comprising (a) mixing thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, hyperbranched polyimide, the supramolecular foaming improver containing cyclic structure uniformly, extruding and pelletizing to obtain thermoplastic polyurethane blended gold particles, and (b) adding the thermoplastic polyurethane blended gold particles of step (a) into the autoclave, and feeding supercritical carbon dioxide into it, immersing the thermoplastic polyurethane blended gold particles and foaming it to prepare the finished product; and
   (2) an application of the thermoplastic polyurethane foamed particles in the fields of automotive interiors, beds, seats, and shoe materials, especially suitable for the preparation of sole materials and automotive interiors.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer is chosen from polyether polyurethane, polyester polyurethane, polycarbonate polyurethane, and polylactone polyurethane, preferably polyether polyurethanes and/or polyester polyurethanes.Preferred Method: When the thermoplastic polyurethane foam particles are used to prepare the sole material, the following methods can be used to prepare filling thermoplastic polyurethane foam particles into the sole mold, using 110-150degrees Celsius water vapor to compress 5-15% along the thickness direction of the mold to form the particles, drying for 4-10 hours at 60-90degrees Celsius of ovens, placing 1-4 hours at room temperature to obtain foamed sole products.Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the pre-reaction, temperature is 0-35 degrees Celsius, preferably 10-30 degrees Celsius for 2-12 hours, preferably 4-10 hours. In step (b), the reaction, temperature is 80-150 degrees Celsius, preferably 100-120 degrees Celsius, for 1-6 hours, preferably 2-5 hours. In step (a), the extrusion granulation, temperature is 160-250 degrees Celsius, preferably 180-230 degrees Celsius. The temperature of the dipping and foaming is 10-50degrees Celsius lower than the melting point (Tm) of the thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, usually 60-90degrees Celsius, and soaking and foaming for 1-30 minutes.Preferred Properties: The molecular weight of the hyperbranched polyimide is 2000-30000(D), preferably 2500-20000(D). The shore hardness of the thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer is 60-90 A, preferably 80-90A. In step (b), in the immersion foaming process, the pressure in the autoclave is 30-200 bar, preferably 50-180 bar.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hyperbranched polyimide is prepared by reacting organic polyamine, aliphatic diamine and/or aromatic dianhydride. The organic polyamine is an organic polyamine containing 3-4 aminophenyl structures, 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenoxy)benzene, 1,3,5-tris (4-aminophenyl)benzene, 5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-aminophenyl)porphyrin, or N,N,N',N'-tetrakis(p-aminophenyl)p-phenylenediamine. The aliphatic diamine is a 2-20C straight-chain aliphatic diamine, preferably 4-12C straight-chain aliphatic diamine, hexamethylenediamine, or butanediamine. The aromatic dianhydride is an aromatic dianhydride containing a phthalic anhydride structure, more preferably 3,3',4,4'-diphenyl ether tetracarboxylic dianhydride, bisphenol A type diether dianhydride, 3 ,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride, 3,3',4,4'-benzophenone tetracarboxylic dianhydride, or 1,2,4,5-pyromellitic dianhydride, preferably 3,3',4,4'-diphenyl ether tetracarboxylic dianhydride, and bisphenol A diether dianhydride. In step (a), the molar ratio of the total amount of the organic polyamine and aliphatic diamine to the aromatic dianhydride is 1-1.02:1. The molar ratio of the aliphatic diamine to the organic polyamine is 1:0.001-0.053, preferably 1:0.002-0.004. In step (a), the organic solvent is chosen from N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, diethylene glycol monomethylbase ether, p-cresol, o-cresol, or m-cresol, preferably N,N-dimethylformamide and/or dimethylsulfoxide, the amount of the organic solvent is 2.3-11.5 times, preferably 3.1-8.8 times, the total mass of the organic polyamine, aliphatic diamine and aromatic dianhydride. In step (b), the catalyst is chosen from acetic anhydride and/or triethylamine. The amount of the catalyst is 0.1-2 mol% of the total molar amount of organic polyamine, aliphatic diamine, and aromatic dianhydride in step (a), preferably 0.2-1.5 mol%. The supramolecular foaming improver containing cyclic structure is chosen from crown ether, cyclodextrin, calixarene and calixcarbazole, preferably beta-cyclodextrin, gamma-cyclodextrin, 15-crown-5, 18-crown-6, or their combinations.

PN CN113603908-A
TI Preparing degradable and environmentally friendly plastic masterbatch useful in field of polymer material comprises e.g. carrying out sealed reaction of polybutylene adipate terephthalate, polyglycolic acid, polyisocyanate prepolymer, inorganic filler and plasticizer, extruding and granulating.
AU LUO D
   WU C
AE WU C (WUCC-Individual)
GA 2021D0912E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a plastic master batch comprises (i) adding polybutylene adipate terephthalate, polyglycolic acid, polyisocyanate prepolymer, inorganic filler and plasticizer into a reaction kettle, introducing nitrogen, setting the nitrogen pressure at 0.5-3 MPa and temperature at 160-200degrees Celsius, the stirring speed at 60-120 rotations/minute (rpm), carrying out sealed reaction for 1 hour, adding photosensitive additive and coupling agent and continuously reacting for 3-6 hours to obtain an intermediate, (ii) extruding and granulating the intermediate obtained in the resultant step by using a double-screw extruder, controlling extrusion temperature at 150-180degrees Celsius and cooling to room temperature, where the plastic masterbatch comprises 30-50 pts. wt. polybutylene adipate, 40-60 pts. wt. polyglycolic acid, 1-5 pts. wt. polyisocyanate prepolymer, 5-15 pts. wt. inorganic filler, 5-10 pts. wt. plasticizer, 1-5 pts. wt. photosensitive additive and 2-8 pts. wt. coupling agent.
   USE - The plastic masterbatch is useful in field of polymer material.
   ADVANTAGE - The masterbatch: is degradable and environmentally friendly; can be changed into micromolecules from a macromolecular chain in a short time; and can be degraded into water, carbon dioxide and inorganic matters.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The polybutylene adipate-terephthalate is prepared by graft copolymerization of citric acid, heating polybutylene adipate-terephthalate to a molten state, sequentially adding citric acid and an initiator when the temperature is controlled to be 130-150degrees Celsius, stirring at the speed of 40-60 rpm for 20-30 minutes and naturally cooling to room temperature. The polyisocyanate prepolymer is prepared by heating polyester polyol to 70-80degrees Celsius, sequentially adding isocyanate, a chain extender and a catalyst and continuously reacting for 6-8 hours under the protection of nitrogen, where the mass ratio of the polyester polyol, isocyanate, chain extender and catalyst in a mass ratio of 40-60:30-50:3-5:1-3.Preferred Components: The inorganic filler comprises nano calcium carbonate, nano montmorillonite and/or nano magnesium oxide. The photosensitive additive comprises aryl triazole, orange yellow, nanometre titanium dioxide and/or nanometre zinc oxide. The coupling agent comprises phthalate ester coupling agent, amino silane coupling agent and/or silane coupling agent. The plasticizer comprises polyethylene glycol, dimethyl phthalate and/or tributyl citrate. The initiator used in the citric acid graft copolymerized polybutylene adipate-terephthalate is benzoyl peroxide or azobisisobutyronitrile. In the preparation of the polyisocyanate prepolymer, the polyester polyol comprises polycarbonate diol, adipic acid polyester diol and/or ethylene glycol. The isocyanate comprises toluene diisocyanate, diphenylmethane diisocyanate and/or dicyclohexylmethane diisocyanate. The chain extender comprises glycerol, trimethylolpropane, diethylene glycol, ethylenediamine and/or diethyl ether. The catalyst is an amine catalyst, N, N-dimethylcyclohexylamine, triethylamine, N'-diethylpiperazine, triethanolamine and/or pyridine.

GA 2021C5509B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves loading copper oxide on the gas diffusion layer of the gas diffusion electrode electrolytic cell. The silver/silver chloride and the electrode saturated with potassium chloride solution is used as the reference electrode. A counter electrode and an electrolyte are set, and electrochemical cyclic voltammetric electrolysis is performed on the copper oxide in a three-electrode system. The potential of the electrochemical cyclic voltammetry is -0.6V-2.0V, and the scanning speed is 25-75mV/s. The number of cycles is 50-200, and a copper catalyst is prepared, after the gas diffusion layer is taken out and dried.
   USE - Method for preparing copper catalyst (claimed) used in electrochemical reduction carbon dioxide technology. Uses include but are not limited to biological reduction, photoreduction, thermal reduction and electroreduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy, and provides copper catalyst with excellent catalytic activity and current density, and reduced energy consumption. The method provides good expansibility, which is good for cost control and efficiency guarantee in scale production, and effectively solves the problem that the existing copper catalyst has low selectivity, and the technical problem of serious hydrogen evolution side reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   1. a copper catalyst;
   and
   2. an application of the copper catalyst.

AU PARK H J
   BYEON J
   JUNG T S
AE ECOTT CO LTD (ECOT-Non-standard)
GA 2021A4378H
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating sprouted barley in plant factory, comprises (i) preparing barley seeds on a seed tray; (ii) supplying moisture to the barley seeds positioned on the tray using water having a pH of 6-7; (iii) germinating the moisture-supplied barley seeds by dark treatment and red light emitting diode (LED) light treatment; (iv) cultivating the germinated barley by applying a blue LED light treatment and supplying a stimulant; and (v) harvesting barley after 1-3 days of performing the LED light treatment and supplying of stimulant.
   USE - The sprouted barley is useful as a material in health functional foods and medicines for preventing and treating alcoholic fatty liver.
   ADVANTAGE - The sprout barley cultivated has increased content of functional secondary metabolites e.g. saponarin, lutonarin and isovitexin, excellent quality and antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-cancer functions, and is suitable for industrial applicability. The method can improve the growth and yield by adjusting the pH of the water supplied to the sprouted barley; and is performed in plant factory enables planned and continuous production of crops regardless of the season by artificially controlling environmental conditions e.g. light, temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide, and culture medium in a facility that is blocked from the outside.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), moisture is supplied using a spray-on cultivation stand. The light cycle during the LED light treatment in the steps (iii) and (iv) is 8-16 hours in light and 8-16 hours in dark on a daily basis. The white LED light treatment can be additionally performed before the blue LED light treatment in the step (iv). In the step (iv), stimulant comprises at least one of methyl jasmonate and geraniol, where the stimulant supplied is 250-500 parts/million (ppm) at a rate of 100-150 ml/tray (25x 55 cm).
   ACTIVITY - Hepatotropic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Sprouted barley was grown by supplying moisture using water having a pH of 6.5 (dark conditions for germination, white light for growth).

   USE - Method for simultaneously detecting 7 kinds of acrylic acid and acrylic ester monomer in plastic product.

AE TIANDICI DONGGUAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)

   (B) introducing solid powder into carbon                dioxide at room temperature, maintaining pressure                at 6-9 megaPascal for 5-10 minutes to obtain a                processed powder;
   (C) adding tridecyl trimellitate to the                processed powder and ultrasonicating for 3-5                minutes; and
   (D) adding the mixed solution, sonicating for                2-3 minutes, homogenizing at high pressure for 3-5                times to remove insoluble materials, pre-freezing                at -18 degrees C for 12 hours and drying under                vacuum to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the                  treated powder to tridecyl trimellitate is                  1:0.7-0.95. The mass ratio of the treated powder                  to the mixed liquid is 1:1. The frequency of the                  ultrasound is 20-25 kiloHertz and the power is                  100-400W. The pressure of the high-pressure                  homogenization is 80-120 megaPascal. Preferred                  Components: The mixed liquid comprises                  glycyrrhizic acid, caffeoyl amino acid ethyl                  ester and water, and the glycyrrhizic acid,                  caffeoyl amino acid ethyl ester with water in the                  mass ratio of 1:0.2-0.3:1.
