PN CN112931305-A
TI Green breeding based on circulating water breeding aquatic product comprises pumping water in breeding pool into a filtering pool, filtering, degassing with a degassing film, performing oxygenation, and heating or cooling in cold-warm pool.
AU YU X
   XU Y
AE QIYUAN FISHERY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (QIYU-Non-standard)
GA 202171009H
AB    NOVELTY - Green breeding based on circulating water breeding aquatic product comprises monitoring the water quality environment in a breeding pool, detecting pH value in water source, adjusting the pH value of pool water in the breeding pool, adding proper pH value adjusting agent, pumping the water in breeding pool into a filtering pool for filtering, removing the impurity, discharging the impurity into a sedimentation tank, collecting the water in the filter tank, degassing by a degassing film, where the filtered water is removed by degassing film to remove the harmful gas (e.g. ammonia nitrogen, carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide) in the water, discharging the harmful gas into the sedimentation tank, performing oxygenation treatment, processing the degassed water by air flotation pump, passing oxygen-enriched water into a cold-warm pool, heating or cooling, adjusting the water temperature suitable for growth of aquatic product; and passing the processed water into breeding pool again.
   USE - The method is useful for green breeding based on circulating water breeding aquatic product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes filtering pool, degassing film, air flotation pump and cold and warm pool to process the environment of the breeding water thus improves the environment of the breeding pool, and achieves the purpose of recycling water resource.

PT P
PN KR2021054746-A; KR2264268-B1
TI Manufacture of pillow cover used in hotel, involves preparing pulp fabric by mixing pine needle extract, lyocell fiber powder and calcium carbonate filler with wood pulp dissociation solution, and cutting pulp fabric into pillow cover shape.
AU KIM Y A
   LEE T J
   JOO B M
   YOUNG K D
   PARK J S
   KIM C G
AE UNIV IND & ACADEMIC COOP IN CHUNGNAM NAT (UCHU-C)
GA 2021535038
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of environmentally-friendly pillow cover involves (i) preparing a wood pulp dissociation solution by beating and dissociating the wood pulp, (ii) obtaining a pine needle extract, (iii) obtaining lyocell fiber powder, (iv) preparing a post hybrid calcium carbonate (PHCC) filler, (v) preparing a pulp fabric by mixing the obtained pine needle extract and lyocell fiber powder and the prepared PHCC filler with the wood pulp dissociation solution, and (vi) cutting the manufactured pulp fabric into a pillow cover shape.
   USE - Manufacture of environmentally-friendly pillow cover. Uses include but are not limited to hotel, motel, hospital, and nursing home.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces environmentally-friendly pillow cover which provides comfort and various health benefits to users by using the antibacterial, anti-inflammatory and atopic prevention effects of pine needle extract and the soft texture of lyocell fibers, helps users' health and sleep through health benefits and soft texture, prevents the generation of carbon dioxide generated when manufacturing the pillow cover by the addition of the post hybrid calcium carbonate filler, uses vegetable materials that can be easily decomposed in nature and does not pollute the environment, and manufactures the pillow cover for a single use for hygiene and can be replaced after use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The wood pulp contains 80 pts. wt. hardwood wood pulp and 20 pts. wt. softwood wood pulp. Preferred Property: The powdered eucalyptus lyocell fiber powder has particle size of 10 mu m or less. Preferred Process: The step (ii) involves washing the pine needles collected from pine trees, removing moisture from the washed pine needles using a dryer, and obtaining a pine needle extract containing the active ingredients terpene and alginic acid components using a steamer using the dried pine needles. The step (iii) involves dissolving the wood pulp obtained from the eucalyptus tree by immersing in an amine oxide solution, evaporating moisture by heating the solution in which the wood pulp is dissolved, drying the solidified wood pulp by evaporation of moisture, and producing eucalyptus lyocell fiber powder by pulverizing the dried solidified wood pulp using a grinder. The step (iv) involves preparing grounded calcium carbonate (GCC), preparing a linear aggregate by adding calcium oxide to grounded calcium carbonate (GCC), adjusting the PH to be 7 by injecting carbon dioxide into the prepared linear aggregate, and preparing PHCC filler by stirring the line aggregate with the pH adjusted in a stirrer. The step (v) involves mixing 80-90 pts. wt. wood pulp dissociation solution, 5-10 pts. wt. pine needle extract, 10-50 pts. wt. lyocell fiber powder, and 2-5 pts. wt. PHCC filler, and stirring for a certain period of time, removing water from the stirred mixture and compressing to obtain paper, removing moisture from the obtained paper using a press roller and drying in a dryer to obtain a pulp fabric.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A84 (Household and office fittings - including carpets and carbon paper.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).); P27 (Shop, household, furnishings (A47F, G, H).)

   USE - The culture medium is useful for breast cancer organoids.
   ADVANTAGE - The culture medium: can realize the rapid growth of breast cancer organoids, which can be cultured stably for a long time and are regular and uniform in size; and can preserve the heterogeneity of patient's tumor tissue in vitro.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the special culture medium comprising adding specific additive factors to the basic culture medium, and mixing uniformly;
   (2) 3 dimensional (3D) culturing of breast cancer organoids comprising (a) pre-treating the separated breast cancer tissue, digesting and filtering to obtain cell precipitate, and (b) lysing the cell precipitate, then mixing with matrigel glue, inoculating, solidifying the mixed glue, then adding culture medium, culturing at 37 degrees C for 7-14 days in 5% carbon dioxide concentration, and replacing the culture medium for every 2-3 days to obtain product; and

The basic culture medium comprises serum-free culture medium. The serum-free culture medium is Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's medium/F12 (1:1) reduced serum medium.

Two ends of the air inlet channel are                arranged in a chamber. The blower is started. PT P
PN CN112590315-A
TI Preparing barrier composite film useful for refrigerated packaging, comprises e.g. printing ink layer on one side of polyester film layer; and coating adhesive on surface of polyethylene film layer.
AU LV J
AE FUJIAN PENGDA PACKAGING MATERIALS CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2021370592
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing barrier composite film comprises (1) respectively preparing antibacterial porous polyethylene (PE) sealing film, PE film layer, polyester film layer and polyvinyl chloride (PVC) resin wet material; (2) printing ink layer on one side of polyester film layer; (3) coating the adhesive on surface of PE film layer, using composite roller and composite pressure roller to compound the polyester film layer obtained in step (2) on PE film layer; curing, using carbon dioxide laser with wavelength of 10.25-10.6 mu m to burn through polyester film layer to form ventilation groove and produce waterproof-grade micropores at corresponding position of PE film layer; and (4) filling the PVC resin wet material into ventilation groove on the polyester film layer obtained in step (2), coating the adhesive on surface of the antibacterial porous PE sealing film, using composite roller and composite pressure roller to compound on surface of polyester film layer away from PE film layer, and curing.
   USE - The barrier composite film is useful for refrigerated packaging (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The barrier composite film: can reduces internal and external moisture and gas convection in a low-temperature freezing state, has good barrier performance, and can permeates steam in microwave heating or high-temperature cooking state, and at the same time meets the requirements of antibacterial performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The antibacterial porous PE sealing film is made of linear low-density polyethylene, polyethylene oxide-polypropylene oxide-polyethylene oxide triblock copolymer and antibacterial additive, mixing and melting and extruding into sheet, mixed solvent of ethanol and water removes the polyethylene oxide-polypropylene oxide-polyethylene oxide triblock copolymer, and finally biaxial stretching; where the mass ratio of linear low-density polyethylene, polyethylene oxide-polypropylene oxide-polyethylene oxide triblock copolymer and antibacterial additives is 10:1.5-3:1-2. The PE film layer is made of linear low-density polyethylene, metallocene linear low-density polyethylene, epsilon -caprolactone/polyether/propylene oxide terpolymer, inorganic filler and slip agent, mixing and melting and extruding into sheet, the mass ratio of linear low-density polyethylene, metallocene linear low density polyethylene, epsilon -caprolactone/polyether/propylene oxide terpolymer, inorganic filler and slip agent is 10:3-5:1.5-3:2-4:0.5-1. The PVC resin wet material adopts a micro-suspension polymerization process, which is specifically comprises first adding water, emulsifier SDS, and cetyl alcohol into the polymerization kettle, stirring to prepare the dispersion, adding the initiator, filling nitrogen to check air tightness and replacing air, introducing vinyl chloride monomer, where the mass ratio of vinyl chloride monomer, water, emulsifier SDS and cetyl alcohol is 100:150-200:0.5-1.0:1-2, high-speed mixing and swelling, heating to 50-60 degrees C for polymerization, when the pressure in the polymerization kettle drops to 5 MPa, terminating the reaction and releasing unreacted vinyl chloride monomer, collecting the mixed liquid in the kettle and dehydrating to a water content of 20-30% to obtain a wet PVC resin material. Preferred Components: The inorganic filler is titanium dioxide or nano-calcium carbonate with a particle size of 10-100nm. The slip agent is silicone oil. The average particle diameter of primary particles of the PVC resin wet material is 0.8-2 mu m. The layer thickness ratio of the PE film layer to the polyester film layer is 10:3-6.

Testing.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A28 (Condensation polymers in general.); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); ; G03 (Adhesives - excluding dispensers. Polymeric adhesives are also classified in Section A (C09H, J).); P73 (Layered products (B32).)

PN KR2021029948-A
TI Preparing fermented silk (Yeobisu) by ripening process, using stevia and finely grinding coarse silk plant in mixer, sterilizing, adding oxygen, placing in bottle and going through enzyme fermentation process, aging, and filtering.
AU LEE Y J
AE LEE Y J (LEEY-Individual)
GA 202127990D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fermented silk (Yeobisu) involves ripening process, where harvest time is twice a year in July and October, or in July and October, until the skin is peeled off by hand; 1st aging process using stevia by finely grinding coarse silk plant in a mixer and then sterilizing with hot water at 90-120 degrees C; mixing uniformly so that oxygen can be added smoothly, but no foreign substances are allowed to enter; placing in a bottle and going through enzyme fermentation process; the fermentation period is about 10-15 days; after aging, filtering the remnants of the fermentation broth before overloading and alcoholizing enzyme; and storing at refrigeration temperature. The fermented silk is sold in glass bottles by mixing fermentation liquid of well-fermented silk plant and natural carbonated water (carbon dioxide).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fermented silk (Yeobisu) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fermentation is smooth without adding sugar. The taste and aroma are good, thus the overall preference of consumers is high. The fermented silk is a safe food that catches the attention of consumers and is in the spotlight according to the tastes of modern people. Above all, it is considered as a healthy food.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The fermented silk can be processed, manufactured, and sold only. If the fermentation broth is alcoholized due to overloading of enzymes in the fermentation broth, it can be prepared and sold as vinegar by adding acetic acid bacteria.

PN CN112442288-A
TI Preparing nano-calcium carbonate comprises e.g. aging refined lime milk, adding milk of lime into crystal form control agent, heating nano calcium carbonate suspension, adding surface treatment agent and dehydrating and packaging.
AU WEN Q
   ZHU Y
   LI R
   YANG A
   WU L
   ZHAN Q
AE GUANGXI HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202124426V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-calcium carbonate comprises (i) aging refined lime milk for 24-48 hours, and then adjusting specific gravity of the aged lime milk to 1.055-1.075 for use, (ii) adding above-mentioned milk of lime into crystal form control agent composed of sulfuric acid and sulfate, turn on the stirring, and mixing evenly, adding mixed gas containing carbon dioxide for carbonation reaction, when pH of reaction system is 7, stop ventilation to terminate the reaction to obtain nano calcium carbonate suspension, (iii) heating the nano calcium carbonate suspension, adding surface treatment agent compounded with higher fatty acid sodium, vegetable gum, and monoalkyl ether phosphatidic acid potassium salt, where addition amount is 3-4% of dry amount of calcium carbonate in suspension, carrying out surface modification under emulsifier, and (iv) dehydrating modified nano calcium carbonate suspension by a filter press, drying, crushing, sieving, and packaging to obtain nano calcium carbonate.
   USE - The nano-calcium carbonate is useful for filling automobile weld seam glue (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano calcium carbonate prepared solves the problems of low thixotropic and low yield value of calcium carbonate filled automobile weld seam glue, has excellent stability and reproducibility, solves performance fluctuation of the automobile weld glue caused by the calcium carbonate filler.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The higher fatty acid sodium in the surface treatment agent accounts for 60-80% of the total surface treatment agent; vegetable glue accounts for 15-35% of the total surface treatment agent content; monoalkyl ether phosphatidic acid potassium salt total surface treatment agent content 2-5%. The vegetable gum is sesbania gum, guar gum, and Coumadin gum. The addition amount of the crystal form control agent is 0.5-1% of the dry basis mass of the nano calcium carbonate. Preferred Method: The preparation method of the higher fatty acid sodium comprises performing saponification reaction of palmitic acid, stearic acid, oleic acid, linolenic acid, arachidic acid, behenic acid and sodium hydroxide to obtain water-soluble saponification liquid, where saponification temperature is 85-100 degrees C, and the amount of sodium hydroxide is 15% of the fatty acid. The carbonation reaction control temperature is 18-25 degrees C, the concentration of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 25-35%, and the stirring speed is 100-150 revolutions/minute. The surface modification is carried out by stirring for 30-60 minutes at a rotation speed of 300-500 revolutions/minute. The drying is performed at a temperature of 100-120 degrees C for 24-48 hours.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); G01 (Inorganic pigments and non-fibrous fillers (C09C).); G03 (Adhesives - excluding dispensers. Polymeric adhesives are also classified in Section A (C09H, J).)
MC E05-A02; E05-G09C; E10-C04H; E10-C04K; E11-F11; E31-U01; E34-D03A; G01-A01; G01-B01; G01-B03; G03-B02D2

PN CN112410222-A; CN112410222-B
TI Multi-chamber bubble column bioreactor useful for culturing animal cell, has reactor with several chambers provided with gas distributor and valve port alternately opened or closed to make liquid level alternately rise or fall.
AU LI X
   CHEN J
   DU G
   XU S
   LIU H
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 2021272070
AB    NOVELTY - Bioreactor has a reactor, where the reactor is divided into several chambers, the upper portion of each chamber is closed to communicate with the bottom, the lower portion of each chamber is respectively provided with at least one gas distributor, and the top of each chamber is respectively provided with at least one valve port. At least one of the several chambers are provided with gas on the top. Each gas distributor separately inputs gas into each chamber. The valve ports are alternately opened or closed to make the liquid level in each chamber alternately rise or fall. The reactor has two chambers separated by partitions or cylinders. The gas is at least one of air, oxygen, nitrogen and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The multi-chamber bubble column bioreactor is useful for culturing animal cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The bioreactor realizes mixing without stirring, overcomes the problems of too long mixing time and too narrow operable liquid level range when operating with low gas velocity and large bubbles, cultures cell until concentration reaches more than 106 cells/ml, maintains a better mixing effect by using lower gas flow and larger bubbles and is applied to large-scale cultivation of shear-sensitive animal cells.

PN CN112409456-A; CN112409456-B
TI New polypeptide, useful for promoting the secretion of cytokines from adipose stem cells.
AU ZHANG H
   LI N
   YU R
AE BEIJING XINSONG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   SICHUAN SHENGMAIYUAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202126478L
AB    NOVELTY - Polypeptide comprising fully defined sequence of 19 amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 1), as given in the specification, is new.
   USE - The polypeptide is useful for promoting the secretion of cytokines from adipose stem cells. The cytokine is useful in preparing medicine for repairing skin (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polypeptide: has good healing effect on diabetic skin wound repair; can quickly exert a curative effect; and has excellent application value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) promoting the secretion of cytokines from adipose stem cells comprising taking adipose stem cells, inoculating cells in T25 culture flasks, culturing with complete medium containing Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium-HG/F12 medium and 10% fetal bovine serum, aspirating the original medium and washing twice with phosphate buffered saline after the cell concentration reaches 80%, then adding Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium-HG/F12 medium and 10% fetal bovine serum complete medium with a final concentration of 50 ng/ml peptide to each bottle at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide, and 5% oxygen, continuing culturing for 36 hours, then changing half of the same fresh medium, collecting the changed medium for cell metabolism, culturing for 36 hours, collecting the culture medium, and combining the half of the previously replaced cell metabolism medium, where the high-content cytokine prepared; and
   (2) use of cytokine in preparing medicine for repairing skin; and
   (3) preparing cosmetics for repairing skin comprising using cytokines.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises centrifuging the harvested medium, then passing the supernatant through hollow fiber filter membrane for circulating filtration, pumping to tangential flow ultrafiltration membrane bag for circulating filtration and concentration, filtering the concentrated solution through 0.22 mu m filter membrane, sterilizing, dispensing into vials, placing in 80 degrees C refrigerator for quick freezing, adding the vial in the capping vacuum freeze dryer, covering the floating lid, starting the freeze dryer, setting the vacuum to 1.05 mbar, and drying for 24 hours, capping the vial under vacuum, then taking out the capped and vial, and storing at -20 degrees C. Preferred Components: The medicine further comprises other second substances that can promote skin damage repair. The medicine is a liquid preparation or solid preparation.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.

PN CN112371953-A
TI Manufacture of double-liquid alloy box-free hammer head by mixing and stirring quartz sand and sodium silicate, pouring sand plate, drying, plastering fixing adhesive, clamping, pouring alloy steel casting on mold and solidifying.
AU HUANG Y
AE HUANG Y (HUAN-Individual)
GA 2021202981
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of double-liquid alloy box-free hammer head comprises selecting sand plate, preparing mold, mixing and stirring 1 ton of 50-100 mesh quartz sand and 70 kg of sodium silicate at 35 degrees C for 10 minutes, pouring sand plate to make mold, drying mold by carbon dioxide, plastering a fixing adhesive on connection position of two corresponding molds, clamping fixing mold by a clamp, pouring alloy steel casting on mold, stopping casting after boundary is reached, solidifying, pouring from high chrome alloy pouring port, stopping pouring when riser is reached, cooling and knocking mold.
   USE - The method is for manufacturing double-liquid alloy box-free hammer head.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can reduce the number of workers; the joint of the manufactured hammer head is stable; there is no condition of alloy joint breaking; removing the sand box; and it avoids the problem that the speed is slow and easy to fire in the casting process. The cast hammer head is less than the parts; and the fire water utilization degree is greatly improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of mold structure for manufacturing double-liquid alloy box-free hammer head.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing multi-metal oxidation state coexisting nanopowder involves preparing binary or ternary zirconium-based amorphous alloy powders prepared by gas atomization method through direct combustion process and high-energy ball milling process.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing multi-metal oxidation state coexisting nanopowder, which is useful for chemical catalysis including high-efficiency catalytic degradation reaction, photoelectric catalytic carbon dioxide to methanol reaction, and biomass catalytic conversion reaction (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively control the precursor components to control the relative content of various oxides formed to meet the requirement of different chemical reactions; and provides nanopowder, which has uniform particle size, and high stability, catalytic activity and selectivity, does not agglomerate easily, and is not easy to be deactivated as a catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing multi-metal oxidation state coexisting nanopowder involves preparing binary or ternary zirconium-based amorphous alloy powders prepared by gas atomization method through direct combustion process and high-energy ball milling process. The binary zirconium-based amorphous alloy is of formula: ZrxM100-x (I). The ternary zirconium-based amorphous alloy is of formula: ZraCuyM'100-a-b (II).
   M = Fe or Co;
   M' = Fe, Co, Ni or Pd;
   x = 55-80;
   a = 55-75;and
   b = 15-35.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves preparing alloys of different components into amorphous powders by gas atomization method at powder spraying gas flow rate of 0.35-0.65 m3/hour, preferably 0.4-0.6 m3/hour, carrying out combustion reaction in an air atmosphere, then grinding the oxidation products obtained from the combustion to l200-mesh, and performing high-energy ball milling using a planetary ball mill at 200-450 rpm, preferably 280-320 rpm for 1-10 hours, preferably 2-5 hours according to ball-to-material ratio of 10:1-50:1, preferably 15:1-25:1 to obtain the final mixed powder. Preferred Components: Each alloy raw material with a purity greater than 99.5% is selected and formulated according to the designed atomic percentage of the chemical composition for gas atomization to prepare amorphous powder.

PN CN112167626-A
TI Sea buckthorn sleep aid food comprises sea buckthorn leaves, hops, sea buckthorn seed coats, wild jujube seeds, lilies, peanut leaves, ground fungus and Poria cocos.
AU LIU Z
   ZHANG X
   WEI X
AE INNER MONGOLIA GUGUOQIQI SEABERRY BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 202107329L
AB    NOVELTY - Sea buckthorn sleep aid food comprises sea buckthorn leaves, hops, sea buckthorn seed coats, wild jujube seeds, lilies, peanut leaves, ground fungus and Poria cocos.
   USE - Sea buckthorn sleep aid food.
   ADVANTAGE - The sea buckthorn sleep aid food improves overall immunity, focuses on increasing content of sleep aid active ingredients in enzymatic hydrolysis and fermentation degradation, and enhances effect of product to help sleep and calm nerves.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing sea buckthorn sleep aid food, which involves:
   (A) mixing and cooking sea-buckthorn leaf, hops, sea-buckthorn seed coat, wild jujube kernels, lily and peanut leaves to obtain sea-buckthorn leaf hop mixture;
   (B) placing sea-buckthorn leaf hop mixture in sealed container under aseptic conditions, incubating sea-buckthorn leaf hop mixture with carbon dioxide (CO2) gas in sealed container to obtain sea-buckthorn leaf hop mixed clinker;
   (C) adding medium components to sea buckthorn leaf hop mixed clinker, inoculating ground bark fungus in first medium, cultivating in light and dark conditions every day for 18-22 days to obtain culture mixture; and
   (D) adding culture mixture to culture medium to obtain second culture medium, inoculating Poria cocos in second medium and cultivating until uniform of hyphae to obtain mixture of mycelium and second medium which is prepared sea buckthorn sleep aid food.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The sea buckthorn sleep aid food comprises potato, honey and pine cone powder. The medium components comprises sodium nitrate, potassium phosphate, magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, calcium chloride dihydrate and ferric citrate. The culture substrate comprises corn flour, bran, calcium sulfate, potassium dihydrogen phosphate, magnesium sulfate and vitamin B1. Preferred Compositions: The sea buckthorn sleep aid food comprises 25-35 wt.% seabuckthorn leaf powder, 15-25 wt.% hop pollen, 10-20 wt.% seabuckthorn seed coat powder, 12-18 wt.% jujube seed powder, 8-12 wt.% lily powder and 8-10 wt.% peanut leaf powder. The light intensity is 40-60 micromole/m2/second. Preferred Process: The pine cones, pine branches and pine sticks are added to second medium. The potato is added to honey, and boiled with water to obtain potato honey leaching water. The pine cone powder is added and boiled to make pine cone potato honey leaching water. The pine cone potato honey soaked water is mixed with sea-buckthorn leaves, hops, sea-buckthorn seed coat, jujube kernels, lily and peanut leaves, cooked thoroughly, cultured under light conditions for 12-14 hours and under no light and dark conditions for 10-12 hours. The mixture of hyphae and second medium is dried, crushed, and sieved to obtain seabuckthorn sleep aid food.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN111905749-A
TI Metal oxide/graphene composite catalyst includes metal oxide as copper-zinc-aluminum mixed metal oxide, and is uniformly dispersed on reduced graphene oxide in layered hydrotalcite structure.
AU LUO J
   CHEN X
   SUN C
   FENG M
   JIANG C
   HE M
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 2020B39356
AB    NOVELTY - Metal oxide/graphene composite catalyst includes metal oxide as copper-zinc-aluminum mixed metal oxide, and is uniformly dispersed on the reduced graphene oxide in a layered hydrotalcite structure.
   USE - Metal oxide/graphene composite catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal oxide/graphene composite catalyst can effectively solve the problems of easy deactivation of the existing catalyst, low carbon dioxide conversion rate and low formaldehyde selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing metal oxide/graphene composite catalyst, which involves:
   (A) adding freeze-dried graphene oxide to a mixed solution of ethanol and water to obtain solution A after ultrasonic treatment;
   (B) dissolving copper (II) nitrate trihydrate, zinc nitrate hexahydrate and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate in water to obtain solution B;
   (C) dissolving sedum hydroxide and sodium carbonate in water to obtain solution C;
   (D) adding solution B and solution C to solution A;
   (E) transferring the resulting mixed solution into the reaction vessel under sealing and heating;
   (F) cooling the reaction vessel to room temperature;
   (G) filtering the resulting solution by using absolute ethanol and water to obtain composite of copper-zinc-aluminum hydrotalcite and reduced graphene oxide;
   (H) roasting the obtained composite in a reaction vessel under protection of inert gas; and
   (I) naturally cooling at room temperature to obtain the composite of copper-zinc-aluminum mixed metal oxide and reduced graphene oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The composite of the copper-zinc-aluminum mixed metal oxide and reduced graphene oxide is ground, pressed, crushed, and sieved into 20-40 mesh particles.

PN CN111905747-A
TI Iron-based Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst used            for catalyzing Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction, has            active phase that is non-carbide, and active phase of            catalyst is iron metal compound.
AU QING M
   YANG Y
   LI L
   WAN H
   WANG H
   LIU S
   WANG J
   WEN X
   BAI Y
   LI Y
AE ZHONGKE SYNTHETIC OIL INNER MONGOLIA CO (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 2020B6029K
AB    NOVELTY - Iron-based Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst                has an active phase which is non-carbide, and the                active phase of the catalyst is iron metal compound                of formula: FexM (I), where M is boron, nitrogen or                silicon, and x is 2-4.
   USE - The catalyst is used for catalyzing the                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst obtained after reduction does not                form carbides, greatly suppresses the generation of                carbon deposits during the reaction process, and                significantly improves the operational stability of                the catalyst. The catalyst avoids the phase                transition between carbides during the reaction,                reduces the chemical abrasion of the catalyst,                improves the strength of the catalyst, and                significantly improves the stability of the                catalyst. The product selectivity of the catalyst                can be effectively adjusted, and the product has                low selectivity of low carbon hydrocarbons such as                methane in the product, and low selectivity of                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the iron-based Fischer-Tropsch                synthesis catalyst, involving (a) reducing the                iron-based Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst,                where the iron element in the iron-based                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst exists in the                form of iron oxide, and (b) reducing the iron oxide                to the compound (I) to obtain the iron-based                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reduction adopts any                  one of (a1) firstly using hydrogen for reduction,                  and then using other reducing gases for                  continuously reduction, (b1) using hydrogen and                  the other reducing gas for reduction, and (c1)                  using the other reducing gas for reduction, where                  the reducing gas includes at least one of borane,                  ammonia and silane. Preferred Conditions: In                  method (a1), the conditions for reduction with                  hydrogen include temperature of 200-400 degrees                  C, temperature rise rate of 5-10 degrees C/hour,                  pressure of 0.1-3 MPa, preferably 1 MPa, the                  space velocity of 500-5000 hours-1, the treatment                  time of 10-36 hours, and the cycle ratio of 0-10.                  In method (a1), the conditions for reduction                  using the other reducing gases include                  temperature of 230-400 degrees C, pressure of                  0.1-2 MPa, space velocity of 500-5000 hours-1,                  treatment time of 20-72 hours, and cycle ratio of                  0-8. In method (b1), The reduction conditions                  include the temperature of 230-400 degrees C, the                  heating rate of 5-10 degrees C/hour, the pressure                  of 0.1-2 MPa, the space velocity of 500-5000                  hours-1, the treatment time of 20-72 hours, and                  the cycle ratio of 0-8. The reduction conditions                  in the method (c1) include the temperature of                  230-400 degrees C, the heating rate of 5-10                  degrees C/hour, the pressure of 0.1-2 MPa, the                  space velocity of 500-5000 hours-1, the treatment                  time of 20-72 hours, and the cycle ratio of 0-8.                  The reduction is carried out in a Fischer-Tropsch                  synthesis reactor, and the Fischer-Tropsch                  synthesis reactor is a fixed bed reactor.                  Preferred Composition: In method (b1), the molar                  ratio of the hydrogen to the other reducing gas                  is 100:(1-100). The iron-based Fischer-Tropsch                  synthesis catalyst consists of iron element,                  structural additive, carrier and electronic                  additive, where the mass ratio of the iron                  element, structural additive, carrier and                  electronic additive is                  100:(0.5-20):(5-40):(0.2-10), and the mass of the                  iron element is calculated as the mass of iron                  oxide. Preferred Components: The structure                  additive is at least one of copper, zirconium,                  lanthanum, yttrium, cerium, chromium, zinc,                  manganese and molybdenum. The carrier is silicon                  dioxide and/or aluminum oxide. The electronic                  additive is at least one of lithium, sodium,                  potassium, ruthenium and caesium.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC H04-E05; H04-F02E; H04-F05; H04-F06

TI Newborn baby commemoration board as souvenir figurine, has childbirth reports, or gifts, sole or palm of newborn is photographed and applied to flat wood plate as digital image, and name and date of birth can be written on margin of plate.
AU MORISAKI K
AE MORISAKI K (MORI-Individual)
GA 2020A48960
AB    NOVELTY - Newborn baby commemoration board has childbirth reports such as infants and kindergarten, or gifts in return for celebration of birth, sole or palm of the newborn which is photographed with a camera and the image is transferred to a personal computer, which is transferred and modified into digital image to resemble the sole and palm of the newborn along the contours of the sole and palm of the hand, based on the modified image, the contour shape of the sole and palm of the newborn baby is applied to a flat wood plate with a thickness of 2-40 mm within a range of 50-300 mm in length and 50-300 mm in width by a carbon dioxide type two-dimensional laser. The contour shape of the sole and palm of the hand can be taken out, and the name, photograph, date of birth, weight and height of the newborn baby at the time of delivery can be written on the surface of the margin of the flat plate.

The laser cutter that cuts the contour shape of the sole and palm of newborn baby is a carbon dioxide type two-dimensional laser that obtains continuous waves in the infrared region or high-power pulse waves using gaseous carbon dioxide as a medium, where the cutting width is 10-100 mu m and the cutting depth is 2-40 mm by processing using energy in a wavelength band of 12 mu m. The photograph of newborn baby at birth on the surface of the margin of the flat plate material uses UV printing using white ink or digging with a laser cutter. The name, date of birth, weight, and height are indicated by laser engraving, cutting, or UV printing.

TI Method for removing volatile organic compounds in factory in environment-friendly manner, comprises using high-concentration of oxidizing microorganism with high activity to transform and degrade gas after condensing.
AU WANG D
   WAN Y
   HAN X
AE NANJING FENGQINGYANG HEALTH TECHNOLOGY (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020A0624B
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises: pre-processing factory tail gas into a pre-processing device; introducing the pre-treated tail gas into an acidification tank for acidification treatment; introducing the acidified tail gas into the alkalization treatment device to carry out alkalization treatment; introducing the tail gas after alkalization treatment into the electrolysis device; separating the active ion of organic matter in the waste gas by using a high-pressure pulse corona discharge; introducing the separated tail gas into the thermal oxidation reactor fir gradually heating, so that the residual organic active ion in the tail gas is oxidized to generate carbon dioxide and water; condensing the volatilizable organic matter in the hot tail gas after thermal oxidization treatment; using high-concentration of oxidizing microorganism with high activity to transform and degrade gas after condensing; and discharging clean tail gas after degradation process is discharged into the outside air.
   USE - The method is useful for removing volatile organic compounds in a factory in an environment-friendly manner.
   ADVANTAGE - The method includes pre-treatment, acidification, alkalization, plasma separation, thermal oxidation, condensation, degradation, and discharge steps, which improves treatment efficiency of the factory waste gas, and can effectively remove factory volatile organic compounds without secondary pollution, and promote the improvement of factory volatile organic compounds emission.

PT P
PN CN111689939-A
TI Extracting anthocyanidin from pitaya peel comprises e.g. crushing pitaya peel into fine powder, extracting using complex enzyme, ethanol and water and vacuum recycling ethanol condensate and adding water to precipitate to remove impurity.
AU DAI L
   PAN C
   LIANG S
   HUANG C
AE NANNING QUHE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANN-Non-standard)
GA 202095367K
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting anthocyanidin from pitaya peel comprises (i) crushing the raw material into fine powder of 30-70 meshes, (ii) extracting using complex enzyme, 35% ethanol and water for three times, the extraction temperature is 30 degrees C and the extraction time is 20 minutes, 30 minutes and 40 minutes, (iii) vacuum recycling ethanol condensate and adding water to precipitate to remove impurity, (iv) separating de-impurity supernatant by membrane, (v) extracting and separating the concentrated solution by supercritical carbon dioxide at 40 degrees C under a pressure of 20 MPa, (vi) removing impurity of the separated material, then spraying and drying the extract, (vii) carrying out inclusion with hydroxypropyl- beta cyclodextrin in a mass ratio of 1:4, the mass fraction of dextrin is 30% and the inclusion temperature is 20 degrees C, (viii) drying air inlet temperature at 150 degrees C, the feeding rate is 485 ml/hour, and (ix) finally checking, packaging and storing.
   USE - The anthocyanidin is useful in additive as medicine, food and feed (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes membrane separation technology, supercritical extraction separation technology, low temperature separation technology and hydroxypropyl beta -cyclodextrin inclusion technology which produces pitaya peel extract powder with excellent stability and high purity, excellent water solubility and stability; and does not easily change color.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises combining complex enzyme, 35% ethanol and water and then carrying out extraction. The vacuum degree during vacuum concentration is -0.065 to -0.082 MPa. The membrane separation temperature is 36 degrees C and the pressure is 0.08 MPa. The method comprises separating the supercritical separation extracts at 37 degrees C under a pressure of 18 MPa. During spray drying: the air inlet temperature is less than 150 degrees C, and the feed rate is 485 ml/hour. The method comprises carrying out inclusion with hydroxypropyl- beta cyclodextrin in a mass ratio of 1:4, the mass fraction of dextrin is 30%, and the inclusion temperature is 20 degrees C.

PN CN111686303-A
TI Preparation method for allogeneic or heterogeneous tendon and tendon complex, involves trimming surface of trimmed tendon and draining water and vacuum-pack through packaging bag, and each layer of packaging bags are sealed by vacuum sealer.
AU LUO J
   CHEN J
   BAI Y
   ZHOU X
   CHEN W
   HU K
   HOU L
AE SHANGHAI YAPENG BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202095445A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining allogeneic or heterogeneous tendon and tendon complex material. The blood stains on the surface of the tendon are removed with purified water. The supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction equipment is turn on, and place the cleaned tendons in the supercritical CO2 extraction equipment for extraction. The 0.02%-0.05% of sodium lauryl sulfate is added in the proportion for decellularization treatment and oil washing. The shaking and washing time is 3-6 hours, and the purified water is replaced every 0.5-2h. The tendon is trimmed after shaking and washing. The surface of the trimmed tendon is trimmed and draining the water and vacuum-pack through three-layer packaging bags under aseptic conditions, and each layer of packaging bags are sealed by a vacuum sealer. The packaged tendon is irradiated and sterilized again at a dose of 10-30kGy.
   USE - Preparation method for allogeneic or heterogeneous tendon and tendon complex.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has a convenient processing, high efficiency, environmental protection and safety. The effect of existing irradiation sterilization on tendon tissue is avoided.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical view of the residues before and after processing of the allogeneic or heterogeneous tendon and bone tendon complex.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The entrainer is one of absolute ethanol, peracetic acid or hydrogen peroxide.

TI Hydraulically driven variable valve timing mechanism, has high-pressure oil chamber with overflow valve and oil drain pipe to discharge oil during high oil pressure, and sliding block matching with oil leaking hole to stabilize pressure.
AU SUN C
   LI L
   LIU G
AE WEIFANG VOCATIONAL COLLEGE (WEIF-Non-standard)
GA 202089529R
AB    NOVELTY - The mechanism has a high-pressure system, a low-pressure system and an actuator connected with an electronic control unit (ECU). The high-pressure system is provided with a high-pressure common oil chamber that is connected with a high-pressure oil pump. The high-pressure oil pump is connected with a hydraulic oil tank. The high-pressure common oil chamber is provided with a pressure sensor, an overflow valve, and an oil drain pipe is connected with the hydraulic oil tank such that when the oil pressure is high, the oil is discharged in time. The low pressure system includes a pressure stabilizing spring and a pressure limiting sliding block such that the sliding block and an oil leaking hole are matched to ensure stable pressure in the low pressure system.
   USE - Hydraulically driven variable valve timing mechanism.
   ADVANTAGE - The mechanism conveniently controls discharging amount and speed of hydraulic oil after experiment, reduces fuel consumption, carbon dioxide emission and hydrocarbon emission of a preheating stage by a hydraulic driving variable valve timing mechanism body, and minimizes nitrogen oxygen compound discharge by about 60 percentage.

TI Producing alcohol-free or low-alcohol beer comprises e.g. producing mash from malt grist and brewing water, lautering, separating hot trub, cooling, adding yeast, fermenting, cooking intermediate beer and discharging clarified green beer.
AU PFISTER S
AE PFISTER S (PFIS-Individual)
GA 202074273J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing an alcohol-free or low-alcohol beer, comprises: (a) producing a mash from malt grist and brewing water; (b) lautering of the mash and removal of the lautered wort; (c) cooking the wort without adding hops or hop products; (d) separating the hot trub from the wort and discharging the clarified cast wort; (e) cooling the wort; (f) adding yeast to the removed wort and fermenting the wort up to a predetermined final degree of fermentation; (g) cooking the intermediate beer product obtained in step (f) with the addition of hops or hop products and evaporation of the alcohol formed in step (f) until a predetermined original wort level is reached; (h) separating the hot trub from the hop wort obtained in step (g) and discharging the clarified green beer; (i) cooling the green beer obtained in step (h); (j) adding yeast to the cooled green beer and storing the green beer for a specified period of time, and (k) carbonizing the green beer with carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing an alcohol-free or low-alcohol beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be carried out in simple, cost efficient manner, and in small breweries.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for beer.

TI Metal-nitrogen-carbon material used as            electrocatalyst e.g. for oxygen reduction comprises            porous carbon material as carrier loaded with metal            nanoparticles, metal carbide and/or metal nitride, and            comprising micropores and mesopores.
AU WANG S
   XU X
   SUN G
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202058104B
AB    NOVELTY - Metal-nitrogen-carbon material comprises                porous carbon material as carrier, which is loaded                with metal nanoparticles, metal carbide and/or                metal nitride. The carbon material has specific                surface of not less than 200 cm2/g, preferably                500-800 cm2/g. It comprises micropores having pore                diameter of 1-2 nm and pore volume of 0.1-0.3                cm3/g, and mesopores having pore diameter of 5-50                nm and pore volume of 0.15-0.4 cm3/g.
   USE - The metal-nitrogen-carbon material is used as                electrocatalyst for oxygen reduction and oxygen                evolution, and carbon dioxide reduction reaction                (all claimed), useful in the field of energy                storage and conversion.
   ADVANTAGE - Compared with the prior art, the preparation                does not need liquid solvent, is green,                environment-friendly, simple, and easy to scale up                for mass production, and has quick and uniform                microwave reaction and easily obtained raw                materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the metal-nitrogen-carbon material                comprising preparation of precursor by mixing zinc                compound, imidazole salt and active metal salt                solid powder, carrying out ball milling, and                subjecting the obtained powder to microwave                reaction, subjecting the precursor to high                temperature carbonization treatment under an inert                atmosphere, and washing away the residual zinc                compound with an acid solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The zinc compound is one                  or two of zinc oxide, zinc sulfide and zinc                  selenide. The metal salt is iron salt, cobalt                  salt and/or nickel salt. The weight ratio of zinc                  compound and metal salt is 10:1-100:1. The inert                  atmosphere is nitrogen, argon gas, nitrogen gas,                  and/or argon mixed gas. The acid solution is                  hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid and/or nitric                  acid. It has a concentration of 0.5-2                  mol/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The active metal in the                  metal-nitrogen-carbon material is iron, cobalt                  and/or nickel. It is at 0.1-5 mass%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The                  metal-nitrogen-carbon material is doped with                  nitrogen (N) elements in the form of pyridine N,                  pyrrole N, graphitized N and/or oxidized N at                  5-15 mass%. Preferred Method: The imidazole salt                  is imidazole, 2-methylimidazole and/or                  benzimidazole. The ratio of the zinc compound and                  imidazole salt is 1:1-1:4. The ball material                  ratio of the ball milling step is 2:1-20:1. The                  ball milling is conducted at 100-400                  revolutions/minute (rpm) for 1-24 hours. The                  microwave reaction is conducted at 100-2000 W for                  5-30 minutes. The high temperature carbonization                  comprises raising the temperature from room                  temperature to 900-1100 degrees C at 2-5 degrees                  C/minute, keeping the temperature for 0.5-3                  hours, and reducing the temperature to room                  temperature at 1-10 degrees C/minute.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   (2) constructing medium temperature asphalt composition, comprising (a) producing the moderate temperature asphalt mixture by mixing raw materials, and (b) laying the medium-temperature asphalt mixture and compacting at 80-150 degrees C.

The medium-temperature asphalt mixture has softening point of 70-100 degrees C, dynamic stability of greater than or equal to 800 times/mm. Preferred Components: The adhesive resin is a mixture of resin, rosin, 5C petroleum resin, 9C petroleum resin, dicyclopentadiene resin and/or their modified products of maleic anhydrides.

PN CN111011820-A
TI Purification of low-sodium salt and alkaline water using prepared brine by preparing concentrated brine, cooling, crystallizing, centrifuging to obtain crude low sodium salt, and removing impurities to obtain refined low sodium salt.
AU HUANG W
   ZHOU S
   JIANG S
   ZHOU J
   LIU Y
   LI Y
AE SHANDONG SUSHA ECOLOGICAL DEV CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202036179G
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of low-sodium salt and alkaline water using prepared brine comprises subjecting underground brine to settling aeration, filtering, removing carbon dioxide, fine filtering, preheating to evaporate, filtering preheated brine using greater than or equal to 0.2 mu m filter, adding to evaporation platform, and evaporating to obtain concentrated brine with 20-30 Baume degree; cooling, crystallizing, centrifuging to obtain crude low sodium salt and mother liquor; condensing steam generated in evaporation process to obtain condensed water; removing impurities from crude low sodium salt to obtain refined low sodium salt; passing condensed water through reverse osmosis to obtain primary target water, adjusting pH to 6.5-7.5 and conductivity to less than or equal to 10 mu S/cm, adding mother liquor, stirring until conductivity reaches 20-30 mu S/cm and total hardness is less than or equal to 50 ppm, electrolyzing until pH value is 8-9.5 and total hardness is 1-50 ppm, filtering, and UV ray sterilizing.
   USE - The method is for purification of low-sodium salt and alkaline water using prepared brine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can obviously increase the content of calcium and magnesium, reduces sodium chloride content, and produces alkaline water that has low hardness and sweet taste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The underground brine contains 0.1-0.4 wt.% calcium, 0.1-0.5 wt.% magnesium, 1-3 wt.% sodium, 0.01-0.1 wt.% potassium, 1-2.5 wt.% chloride ion and 0.2-1 wt.% sulfate; and has concentration of 4-6 Baume degree, electric conductivity of 4000-6000 mu S/cm and salt content of 8000-12000 mg/L. The condensed water has pH of 6.5-7.5 and electrical conductivity of less than or equal to 150 mu S/cm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The filtration includes sand filtration and activated carbon filtration. The sand filtration is done by adding sand filter to sand filter tank filled with 2-8 mesh quartz sand, and filtering at speed of 10-15 m3/hour. The activated carbon filtration is done by filtering brine using 10-20 mesh coconut shell activated carbon at speed of 10-15 m3/hour. The carbon dioxide removal is done by adding filtered brine into carbon remover, and reacting until carbon dioxide concentration is less than 5 ppm. The method further comprises secondary settling step before fine filtration. The secondary settling is done by adding carbon dioxide-free brine into settling tank, and settling for 1-3 days. The preheating is done at 55-65 degrees C using 80-100 degrees C steam. The evaporation is done through three-stage evaporation at 150-200 degrees C and at steam pressure of 0.3-0.6 MPa for 2-4 hours.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
MC E11-N; E11-Q02C; E31-N04C; E31-N05C; E31-P03
IP A23L-027/40; C01D-003/06

PT P
PN DE202019004441-U1
TI Gas combustion engine used for a motor vehicle, comprises an arrangement of a working piston without any intermediate pieces, such as connecting rods takes place in the center instead of on a crankshaft on a so-called piston rod.
AU 
AE WOLFF R (WOLF-Individual)
GA 202019018G
AB    NOVELTY - The gas combustion engine comprises an arrangement of a working piston without any intermediate pieces, such as connecting rods takes place in the center instead of on a crankshaft on a so-called piston rod. The working piston on the piston rod does its work in a cylinder tube. The materials used are gas, oil, temperature and acid resistant to avoid possible material aggressiveness, such as with bio gas operation. The piston has a combustion chamber on both sides. The piston is turned so that it does not need piston and oil rings. The power is tapped by two hydraulic pressure pumps operated by the piston rod. The combustion air supply is supplied via a compressor controlled by sensors. A rotary valve is installed instead of valves for the combustion air supply.
   USE - Gas combustion engine used for a motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The exhaust gas pollutant reductions are ensured due to the reduced consumption with half less nitrogen oxides, carbon monoxides, carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide, nitrous oxide or hydrocarbon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a gas combustion engine.
   Upper hydraulic pump (3)
   Hydraulic pressure output (4)
   Compressor (5)
   Central exhaust duct (9)
   Cylinder head (14)

PT P
PN WO2020028450-A1; CA3107755-A1; KR2021040084-A; CN112672974-A; EP3830021-A1; US2021299749-A1; JP2021533259-W; EP3830021-A4
TI Preparing tetrahexahedra nanoparticles for use as oxidative catalyst by heating particles comprising first metal comprising platinum, palladium, rhodium, nickel, and/or cobalt in the presence of second metal e.g., antimony and/or tellurium.
AU MIRKIN C A
   HUANG L
   LIN H
AE UNIV NORTHWESTERN (NOUN-C)
   UNIV NORTHWEST (UYNO-Non-standard)
   UNIV NORTHWESTERN (NOUN-C)
   UNIV NORTHWESTERN (NOUN-C)
GA 202011023A
AB    NOVELTY - Tetrahexahedra (THH) nanoparticles are prepared by heating particles comprising 1st metal in the presence of 2nd metal at 500-1300 degrees C for 0.5-12 hours to form THH nanoparticles. The 1st metal comprises platinum (Pt), palladium (Pd), rhodium (Rh), nickel (Ni), and/or cobalt (Co); and the 2nd metal comprises antimony (Sb), bismuth (Bi), lead (Pb), and/or tellurium (Te).
   USE - Preparation of tetrahexahedra (THH) nanoparticles for use as oxidative catalyst for oxidation of formic acid to carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can provide a one-step, ligand-free, generalizable process that can synthesize catalytically active THH nanoparticles on an industrial scale and can recycle non-THH shaped nanoparticles, such as waste catalysts in the industry, and form catalytically active THH nanoparticles. The resulting particles exhibit irregular shapes, similar to the random shapes observed with waste catalysts, establishing a model system for testing the shape-repairing capability of the alloying/dealloying process. Unlike conventional high-index facet nanoparticle synthesis methods, the method is remarkably easy to employ, scalable, and effective for industrial production. Additionally, because deactivated structures that have lost their high-index facets can be recycled by the method, the method is particularly attractive for catalyst recovery and reactivation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: THH nanoparticles comprise high-index facets. THH nanoparticles comprise (210) facets, (310) facets, and/or vicinal plane. Preferred Components: The 1st metal is bimetallic selected from PtNi, PtCo, PtCu, PdPt, PdAu, PdNi, PdCo, PdCu, RhPt, RhCo, and/or RhNi. Gas comprises argon, nitrogen, helium, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and/or carbon dioxide. Particles comprising the 1st metal are incorporated onto a support. Support comprises silica, titania, ceria, alumina, zirconia, niobium oxide, zinc oxide, iron oxide, and/or vanadium oxide. Support is conductive. Support comprises carbon black, graphene, graphite, carbon nanotube, carbon fiber, and/or tungsten carbide. The 1st metal comprises Pt, Pd, and/or Rh, preferably platinum. Particles comprising the 1st metal are non-THH particles. The 2nd metal comprises Sb. The 2nd metal comprises Bi. Preferred Processes: The method is performed in a reactor, where the 2nd metal is oriented upstream of the particles, and carried to the particles via a gas flow. Particles are formed by decomposition and/or reduction of a salt of the 1st metal, or particles are formed from a metal alloy of the 1st metal. The 2nd metal is formed by decomposition and/or reduction of a salt of the 2nd metal. The method is performed in the absence of an organic ligand.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); M22 (Casting; powder metallurgy - including foundry moulding, moulding machines, patterns, moulds, cores and metal casting (B22).); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).)

TI Remote patient monitoring method, involves compressing data packets to fit physiological information in voice over internet protocol (VoIP) bandwidth, and transmitting compressed data packets to remote device using communication medium.
AU RANGI S P S
AE CLARITY MEDICAL PVT LTD (CLAR-Non-standard)
GA 202014178N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves acquiring physiological information of a patient, converting the acquired physiological information into the data packets, compressing the data packets to fit physiological information in voice over internet protocol (VoIP) bandwidth, and transmitting the compressed data packets to a remote device using a communication medium. The physiological information comprises physiological parameters and high quality waveforms. The physiological information includes non-invasive blood pressure (NIBP), invasive blood pressure (IBP), heart rate (ECG/EKG), amount of carbon dioxide (CO2) in exhaled air (capnography/ETCo2), respiratory rate (RESP), body temperature (TEMP), and saturation of peripheral oxygen (SpO2).
   USE - Remote patient monitoring method.
   ADVANTAGE - The user selects and view the details of the patients in real-time. The user accesses the decompressed information using an online web portal or a web page or using any internet enabled device. Transmits data packets containing physiological information efficiently by reducing packet drop rate, signal latency, and overall operational and functional cost required for data transmission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for remote patient monitoring.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic block diagram of the monitoring patients in a medical environment.

PN CN110624595-A
TI Calcium indium sulfide/titanium carbide photocatalytic complex material comprises photocatalytic complex titanium carbide distributed around calcium indium sulfide flower-shaped microspheres.
AU ZHUGE Z
   LIU X
   CHEN T
   GONG Y
   LI C
   NIU L
AE UNIV CHINA JILIANG (UYJA-C)
GA 202002642W
AB    NOVELTY - Calcium indium sulfide/titanium carbide photocatalytic complex material comprises photocatalytic complex titanium carbide distributed around calcium indium sulfide flower-shaped microspheres, the titanium carbide accordion-shaped structure composed of a two-dimensional layer stack, the calcium indium sulfide is flower-shaped microsphere structure self-assembled from nanosheets.
   USE - The photocatalytic complex material is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide and degradation of antibiotics (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, economical, and suitable for large-scale production; and has mild experimental conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing calcium indium sulfide/titanium carbide photocatalytic complex comprising (i) adding titanium carbide to hydrofluoric acid (HF acid), and magnetically stirring and etching at room temperature, and centrifuging and drying to obtain an accordion-shaped titanium carbide material, (ii) dissolving the calcium source, the indium source and the obtained titanium carbide in deionized water and dispersing by ultrasound to obtain a homogeneous mixed solution A, (iii) dissolving the sulfur source in deionized water and stir with ultrasound to obtain solution B, and (iv) adding the solution b dropwise to the solution a, and evenly mixing the mixture to obtain a mixed solution C and transferring the mixed solution C to a hydrothermal reaction kettle, reacting at a constant temperature of 100-180 degrees c for 12-24 hours, centrifuging, washing and vacuum drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of to calcium indium sulfide in this material is 0.5-5%. In the step (i), 20 ml of HF acid with a mass fraction of 40% is required per 1 g of Ti3A1C2. The molar ratio of the calcium source, indium source, and sulfur source is 1:2:4. The calcium source comprises calcium nitrate and/or calcium chloride, and the calcium source concentration in the solution A is 0.05-0.2 mol/l. The indium source comprises indium nitrate and/or indium chloride, and the concentration of the indium source in the solution A is 0.1-0.4 mol/l. The sulfur source comprises thioacetamide, thiourea, and/or sulfur powder, and the concentration of the sulfur source in the solution B is 0.2-0.8 mol/l.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AU XU N
   WANG C
   LU H
   HUANG X
   FENG L
   WANG X
AE HUNAN EDIBLE FUNGI RES INST (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019A38482
AB    NOVELTY - Processing Lepista sordida comprises taking Lepista sordida and auxiliary materials, cutting into cube-shaped granular materials, performing preliminary crushing treatment using high-shear mixer for 20-30 minutes at speed of 4000-4500 rotations/minutes and feeding at 25-35 L/H, (2) mixing the material obtained after preliminary crushing with water at a mass ratio of 1: (0.8-1.5), homogenizing using homogenizer for (15-20) minutes and material flow rate is (100-600) L/H, (3) carrying out last 5 minutes of the homogenization process, using the online inflator to fill the homogenized material with carbon dioxide gas to obtain mushroom puree in slurry form with solid content of more than 35%, the solids include soluble solids and insoluble solids, the insoluble solid content is less than 10%, the auxiliary material includes fungus or plant-based medicinal material, the mass of the auxiliary material is 0-10% the fresh weight of Lepista sordida.
   USE - The processed Lepista sordida is useful for preparing special dietary food or health products (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The processed Lepista sordida is good in color, improves appetite of the eaters, has smooth taste and good market prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for mushroom paste prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The auxiliary material comprises auxiliary fungus, nourishing food and medicinal materials, or jackfruit seeds with translucent hard seed coat. The auxiliary fungus includes Agaricus blazei, Cordyceps militaris, Coprinus comatus, Morchella, white jade mushroom, Hypsizygus tessellates or Hypsizygus marmoreus. The nourishing food-medicine material comprises rhizoma gastrodiae, Angelica, medlar, lily or Astragalus. Preferred Method: The jackfruit seeds with translucent hard seed coat are prepared by immersing jackfruit seeds in 5-10% brine for 2-3 hours, cooking for 15-30 minutes. The Lepista sordida is defective Lepista sordida. The filling quantity of the carbon dioxide is less than 5% of the homogenizing material volume. The method further comprises adding 0.1-0.5% salt in mushroom puree.

AU MUNOZ MORATE E
AE LAB QUIVER SA DE CV (QUIV-Non-standard)
GA 202141684C
AB    NOVELTY - Fertilizer compound comprises 5.0-30 wt.% copper acetate, 0.10-0.5 wt.% copper carbonate, 0.25-1.0 wt.% acetic acid, 0.0-2.0 wt.% copper chloride, and water to complete 100%.
   USE - Fertilizer compound used for increasing copper content in plants (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fertilizer enables to provide copper to soil and plants, which is produced by high efficiency reaction system, has wide field of activity, attacks good number of different fungi and good persistence in short term, remains active much longer, improves absorption of copper in vegetables, provides maximum crop yield with minimal environmental impact, allows reducing dose of fertilizer units which provides copper and is incorporated into soil through fertilization, sprinkler irrigation or drip irrigation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for obtaining fertilizer, which involves:
   (A) mixing copper carbonate, and water with continuous stirring;
   (B) adding acetic acid (CH3COOH) to previous mixture in form of drops with continuous stirring, and cause reaction until pH between 4.6-4.8 obtained, preferably 4.7;
   (C) extracting carbon dioxide (CO2) formed by reaction between magnesium carbonate, and acetic acid;
   (D) decanting obtained mixture by reaction for 24 hours, or centrifuge;
   (E) collecting obtain solution from copper acetate from insoluble portion of mixture; and
   (F) reusing decanted product in next operation of reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: The fertilizer compound comprises 15.0 wt.% copper acetate, 0.5 wt.% copper carbonate, 0.5 wt.% acetic acid, and 84 wt.% water. Preferred Conditions: The solution with pH in range of 4.6-4.8, preferably 4.7. The temperature varies between 80-90 degrees C. The nutritive action in application of 250-750 mL in 100000 L (100 m3). The fungicidal bactericidal-fungicidal action in foliar application is 1-3 mL in 11 L. The fungicidal bactericidal-fungicidal action of fertilizer is applied by irrigation 500-2000 mL per hectare. Preferred Method: The method for obtaining fertilizer involves cooling extracted solution to room temperature, where total CO2 produced in reaction is extracted and stored in pressure containers, where vinegar is replaced in place of acetic acid.

AU GOMES ESTEVES DA SILVA J
   DA COSTA PINTO DA SILVA L
   RODRIGUEZ BORGES J E
   NUNEZ MONTENEGRO A

GA 2019937862
AB    NOVELTY - A chemiluminescent imidazopyrazinone-based                photosensitizer compound (I) with available singlet                and triplet excited states, is new.
   USE - The chemiluminescent imidazopyrazinone-based                photosensitizer compound is useful in photodynamic                therapy of cancer, cancer theranostics or                diagnostic and photocatalytic reactions (all                claimed). Uses include but are not limited to                air/water purification, fuel production from                wastewater or renewable feedstock, synthesis of                fine chemicals, water splitting and carbon dioxide                reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The new photosensitizer compound has excellent                selectivity and light-emitting property, and is                prepared efficiently and economically by simple                method. The chemiluminescent photosensitizer can                emit light and induce cytotoxicity at the same                time, thus allows for real-time cancer                imaging/diagnostics irrespective of the size and                localization of the tumor in the body. The                chemiluminescent photosensitizer can be used as                photocatalyst without use of energy consuming and                potentially-expensive light source.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A chemiluminescent imidazopyrazinone-based                photosensitizer compound of formula (I), and its                salt, tautomer and stereoisomer, with available                singlet and triplet excited states, are new.
   R1 = aryl or heteroaryl (both optionally                substituted), H, alkyl, amino or electron donating                group;
   R2 = H or alkyl;
   R3 = aryl or heteroaryl (both optionally                substituted) or electron withdrawing                group;and
   R4 = aryl or heteroaryl (both optionally                substituted), halo or electron withdrawing                group.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of compound (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): Compound (I) is                  obtained by dissolving deprotected pyrazine-type                  compound of formula (II) in a solvent with                  ketone-type compound of formula (III) and placing                  under argon atmosphere, adding a concentrated                  reagent, heating and stirring the mixture,                  cooling to 20-25 degrees C, evaporating solvent                  under reduced pressure, and washing the residue                  with ethyl acetate and ether.Y'1 = protecting group, preferably                  t-butyldimethylsilyl, trimethylsilyl,                  triethylsilyl, triisopropylsilyl, phenylsulfonyl                  or toluenesulfonyl;R5 = methyl, ethyl, propyl, butyl or                  isopropyl;andor R6+R7 = 2-4C alkylene.Preferred Composition: The reagent is an                  acid catalyst(s) chosen from hydrochloric acid,                  hydrobromic acid, hydroiodic acid, hydrofluoric                  acid, perchloric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric                  acid, nitric acid and acetic acid. The solvent is                  dioxane, tetrahydrofuran, toluene, acetonitrile,                  acetone, N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl                  sulfoxide, ether, methanol, ethanol and/or water.                  Preferred Condition: The reaction of compounds                  (II,III) is carried out at 80-180 degrees C for                  4-8 hours.
   Preferred Definitions:
   aryl = phenyl, naphthyl, styryl or                benzophenanthryl;
   heteroaryl = thienyl, thiazolyl, thiophenyl,                furyl or pyrazolyl;
   optionally substituted aryl = phenyl, naphthyl                or hydroxyphenyl;and
   optionally substituted heteroaryl =                indolylpyrazinyl or fullerene.

PT P
PN CN110302745-A
TI Preparation of smoke removing agent involves preparing diatomaceous balls containing atomized nitric acid, and mixture containing nicotine adsorbent, mixing resin powder, mixture, tourmaline, titanium dioxide, and binder, and granulating.
AU XIE X
AE GUANGZHOU CHITIAN DAILY CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201985934P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of smoke removing agent involves drying diatomaceous earth, calcining, immersing in hydrochloric acid solution for 0.5-2 hours, immersing the acid-immersed diatomaceous earth in sodium hydroxide solution for 10-30 minutes to obtain modified diatomaceous earth, wetting the modified diatomaceous earth using water, heating at 60-90 degrees C in the microwave, stirring for 10-30 minutes, rinsing using atomized nitric acid, washing, drying to obtain microporous modified diatomaceous balls, dispersing nicotine adsorbent and tar decomposing agent in water, adding microporous modified diatomaceous balls, stirring uniformly to prepare mixture, mixing 80-120 pts. wt. water-absorbent resin powder, 10-30 pts. wt. mixture, 3-10 pts. wt. tourmaline, 3-10 pts. wt. nano titanium dioxide, and 5-15 pts. wt. binder, kneading, extruding, granulating at 130-250 degrees C to obtain finished product, and packing the finished products in oil-paper bags.
   USE - Preparation of smoke removing agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The smoke removing agent effectively absorbs harmful chemicals in cigarette smoke, and prevents the redistribution of harmful chemicals into the air. The tourmaline, nano-titanium dioxide, and nicotine adsorbent in the smoke removing agent react with harmful chemicals to oxidize and decompose the harmful chemicals into water molecules and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The calcination treatment of diatomaceous earth is carried out at 800-1000 degrees C for 1-3 hours. Preferred Composition: The concentration of the hydrochloric acid solution is 1-2 mol/L, and sodium hydroxide solution is 1-2 mol/L. The weight ratio of the modified diatomaceous earth to water is 5-10:1. The weight ratio of nicotine adsorbent to microporous modified diatomaceous sphere is 0.5-3:100. The weight ratio of tar decomposition agent to microporous modified diatomaceous sphere is 0.5-3:100. Preferred Components: The nicotine adsorbent is graphene oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The tar decomposition agent is one or more of sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, sodium secondary alkyl sulfonate, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, and secondary alcohol polyoxyethylene ether. The water absorbent resin powder is chosen from one or more of starch xanthate grafted acrylate water absorbing resin, starch grafted acrylic/acrylamide water absorbing resin, crosslinked hydroxyethyl cellulose grafted acrylamide polymer, and 2-acrylamido-2-methylpropanesulfonic acid grafted polyvinyl alcohol water absorbing resin. The binder is polyurethane adhesive.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids.
