PT P
PN IN321577-B
TI Catalyst composite used for forming lower hydrocarbons and hydrocarbon oxygenates, comprises base catalyst comprising strontium titanate, and modifying agent chosen from nitrogen, sulfur, ferric oxide, magnesium oxide, and aluminum oxide.
AU VELU J
   KONDA K R
   BALASUBRAMANIAN V
   KANAPARTHI R
   PEDDY V C R
   NETTEM V C
   GANDHAM S G
AE HINDUSTAN PETROLEUM CORP (HIND-Non-standard)
   INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY MADRAS (IITM-C)
GA 2019876264
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst composite comprises strontium titanate as a base catalyst, and 0.5-15 %(weight/weight) at least one modifying agent chosen from nitrogen, sulfur, ferric oxide, magnesium oxide, and/or aluminum oxide, incorporated into the base catalyst.
   USE - Catalyst composite used for forming lower hydrocarbons and hydrocarbon oxygenates (claimed) such as methane, methanol, ethane, ethanol, acetone and formaldehyde, and free hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst composite has synergistic effect due to co-doping of modifying agent, and provides lower hydrocarbon having high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of catalyst composite, which involves mixing a source of strontium, at least one source of modifying agent, methanol, ethylene glycol, and citric acid to obtain a reaction mixture, heating the reaction mixture to obtain polymerized complex gel, and pyrolyzing and calcining the polymerized catalyst to obtain catalyst composite; and
   (2) production of lower hydrocarbons and hydrocarbon oxygenates, which involves suspending a catalyst composite comprising strontium titanate as a base catalyst and at least one modifying agent incorporated into the base catalyst in alkaline solution comprising solution of sodium hydroxide in water to obtain mixture (M1), passing carbon dioxide through the mixture (M1) to obtain mixture (M2) having a pH of 8-12, irradiating the mixture (M2) with an electromagnetic radiation having wavelength of 300-700.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The source of titanium includes titanium (IV) isopropoxide, and the source of strontium includes strontium nitrate. Preferred Composition: The composite catalyst comprises strontium titanate in the form of SrTiO3, Sr3Ti2O7 or Sr4Ti3O10 as a base catalyst. The modifying agent contains 0.5-15 %(weight/weight) nitrogen, 0.5-15 %(weight/weight) sulfur and 0.5-15 %(weight/weight) ferric oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The source of modifying agent is chosen from urea, thiourea, dimethyl urea, mercaptan, thiomalic acid, ferric oxide, ferric nitrate, and/or ferric acetate. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of methanol, ethylene glycol and citric acid is 2:1:0.5. Preferred Process: The reaction mixture is heated at 130 degrees C for 20 hours to obtain the polymerized complex gel, the polymerized complex gel is pyrolyzed at a temperature of 350 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain pyrolyzed gel, and the pyrolyzed gel is calcined at 900 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain catalyst composite. Preferred Apparatus: The irradiation is performed in all-glass thermostatic photocatalyst reactor provided with quartz window.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI New nickel-base complex used e.g. for preparing adsorbing material, is prepared by dissolving 2-amino(1,1:3,1-terphenyl)-4,4,5-tricarboxylic acid ligand and nickel nitrate hexahydrate in solvent water, placing in glass bottle and storing.
AU SUN D
   ZHANG K
   WANG Y
   WANG X
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 201991935U
AB    NOVELTY - A nickel-base complex (I) is new.
   USE - The nickel-base complex is used for preparing adsorbing material, antibacterial material, catalytic material, photo-electromagnetic material and medicine carrier and fluorescent detection and gas adsorption separation field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A nickel-base complex of formula ((Ni'1.5(attca)(H2O)3))n (I) is new. The compound (I) belongs to orthorhombic system, Pmmb space group and has cell parameter of long shaft angle of a=34.854 (2) Angstrom , b=26.440 (2) Angstrom , c=6.3950 (3) Angstrom , alpha =90 degrees , beta =90 degrees , gamma =90 degrees C and cell volume of V=5893 (7) Angstrom 3 and Z=8.
   n=even number greater than 1;
   Ni'=divalent nickel ion; and
   attca=deprotonated 2-amino(1,1:3,1-terphenyl)-4,4,5-tricarboxylic acid ligand.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the compound (I);
   (2) fluorescent detection comprising dissolving compound (I) as fluorescent material in solvent to be detected, detecting fluorescence intensity by spectrometer, and achieving fluorescence detection; and
   (3) gas adsorption separation comprising adsorption testing compound (I) on light hydrocarbons comprising carbon dioxide, methane, ethane, ethylene, acetylene, propane and propene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): Compound (I) is prepared by dissolving 2-amino(1,1:3,1-terphenyl)-4,4,5-tricarboxylic acid ligand and nickel nitrate hexahydrate in solvent to obtain solution, placing in glass bottle, and storing at 90-110 degrees C for 35-37 hours. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of 2-amino(1,1:3,1-terphenyl)-4,4,5-tricarboxylic acid ligand to nickel nitrate hexahydrate is 1:9.5-11.5. The concentration of ligand in solution is 0.006-0.008 mmol/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent comprises N,N-dimethylformamide and water at volume ratio of 1:1.

TI Implantable oxygen nanosensor for detecting oxygen levels alone and/or in combination with physiologic read-outs including calcium, comprises oxygen sensor comprising working electrode, and optionally nanofiber material surrounding sensor.
AU ALLEN M
   CUNNINGHAM Z Z
   FALK M J
   ZOLKIPLI-CUNNINGHAM Z
AE CHILDRENS HOSPITAL PHILADELPHIA (CHHP-C)

   CHILDRENS HOSPITAL PHILADELPHIA (CHHP-C)
   ALLEN M (ALLE-Individual)
GA 201980754V
AB    NOVELTY - An implantable oxygen nanosensor comprises oxygen sensor comprising a working electrode, a reference electrode, a counter electrode, an inner electrolyte cell, and oxygen-permeable membrane bounding one portion of the inner electrolyte cell, and optionally nanofiber material surrounding the oxygen sensor.
   USE - Implantable oxygen nanosensor for detecting oxygen levels alone and/or in combination with physiologic read-outs including calcium, potassium, sodium, chloride, pH, bicarbonate, carbon dioxide, hydrogen peroxide, temperature, lactate, pyruvate, NAD, adenosine triphosphate, adenosine diphosphate, adenosine monophosphate, ammonia, acetoacetate, beta hydroxybutyrate, or emitted light, for safety assessments of exercise performance, capacity, and training, safety assessments in post-concussion syndrome, military training or injuries, diagnosing primary (genetic based) mitochondrial disease and secondary mitochondrial disease, monitoring in vivo mitochondrial respiratory capacity, predicting and assessing organ failure at a pre-critical or critical level, where clinical trial outcome measure to assess mitochondrial disease or dysfunction natural history and progression (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sensor detects mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation dysfunction with harness repeatable, and less invasive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for measuring oxygen level in a tissue in-vivo comprising implanting the implantable oxygen nanosensor into a muscle tissue of a subject, and assessing oxygen level as a function of the conversion of oxygen into water and resulting current generated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxygen sensor is a Clark-type oxygen sensor comprising a working electrode, a reference electrode, a counter electrode, an inner electrolyte cell, reaction chamber, and oxygen-permeable membrane separating the inner electrolyte cell and the reaction chamber. The working electrode is a platinum electrode or a silver electrode. The nanosensor comprise materials that are biodegradable, such as wires that are biodegradable.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxygen-permeable membrane is Teflon (RTM: PTFE) membrane, and permselective membrane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The subject is a non-human animal or human. The subject is suspected of or diagnosed as suffering from disease or disorder. The disease or disorder is a myopathy, such as mitochondrial myopathy. The subject is acutely ill, such as not exclusively from trauma, surgery, infection, sepsis, stroke, heart attack, hemorrhage or shock. The subject is in the general population and seeking to optimize exercise training regimen, and to evaluate mitochondrial effects of their medication. The subject is in the general population and seeking to evaluate mitochondrial effects of lifestyle choices, including diet, medications, exercise, drug use, tobacco exposure, environmental toxin exposure, and chemical exposure. The subject is an athlete or participating in athletic training, and a military recruit or element. The subject has a family element with mitochondrial disease or dysfunction. The disease or disorder is a primary or secondary mitochondrial disorder without known myopathic features. Preferred Method: The step of implanting is into the subjects forearm muscle or gluteal muscle. The step of implanting nanosensor into the subjects brain, heart, liver, intestines, pancreas, urinary bladder, uterus, kidney, adrenal gland, thyroid gland, bone, cartilage, joint, or eye. The subject is being medically monitored for resuscitation purposes. The subject is being evaluated on the sports field or for medical purposes for post-concussion mitochondrial pathology.

PN CA2994131-A1
TI Process for improving performance of wood for use in products e.g. railway ties, involves allowing resin to cure for bonding woods xylem or fiber structures to seal against weathering, microbial, and insect infestation that leads to rot.
AU SMITH L A
AE SMITH L A (SMIT-Individual)
GA 2019717744
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves performing first thermal-vacuum drying in a special kiln that preserves the woods natural porosity. The dried wood is then loaded into an autoclave where it is submerged in- and impregnated with a solution of resin dissolved in super critical carbon dioxide. The resulting solution has extremely low viscosity and is able to penetrate deeply into the wood. Multiple timed pressure changes in the autoclave maximizes the saturation by the solution. The surplus solution is then drained and the resin is allowed to cure for bonding the woods xylem or fiber structures together for strength and to seal against weathering and against microbial and insect infestation that leads to rot. (Incomplete specification, abstract based on available information published by the patent office).
   USE - Process for improving performance of wood for use in products such as railway ties posts, and fence staves.
   ADVANTAGE - The resin has bonded and sealed all the wood fiber together to make a high performance wood with increased strength and resistance to splintering, and sealed against weathering, rot, infestation, and resistant to electric phenomena both dielectric and conductive, upon curing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the process for improving performance of wood.

   (B) subjecting acrylonitrile fiber raw material under the inert gas atmosphere condition of 800-1500 degrees C for carbonization treatment;
   (C) subjecting acrylonitrile carbon fiber material after carbonization treatment at 800-1100 degrees C, mixing water vapor and carbon dioxide in atmosphere condition for activation treatment to obtain activated carbon fiber cloth; and
   (D) cutting active carbon fiber cloth and terylene cloth into mask sheet shape and then pasting into facial mask patch to obtain final product.

   HOU L
   CHEN Z
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN TECHNOLOGY (UYGL-C)
GA 201958502M
AB    NOVELTY - Polyarylene ether ketone and multi-wall carbon nanotube gas separation composite film comprises polyarylene ether ketone and multi-walled carbon nanotubes, and polyarylene ether ketone is represented by (I).
   USE - Polyarylene ether ketone and multi-wall carbon nanotube gas separation composite film.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyarylene ether ketone and multi-wall carbon nanotube gas separation composite film has good permeability, selecting separation performance, wide application prospect and improves permeation rate of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polyarylene ether ketone and multi-wall carbon nanotube gas separation composite film comprises polyarylene ether ketone and multi-walled carbon nanotubes, and polyarylene ether ketone is represented by formula (I).
   m-n=1.
   . An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing polyarylene ether ketone and multi-wall carbon nanotube gas separation composite film, which involves:
   (A) adding bisphenol hydrazine, 4,4'-difluorobenzophenone, bisphenol A and 4-aminophenyl hydroquinone to reaction vessel to obtain polyarylene ether ketone;
   (B) dissolving polyaryl ether ketone obtained to obtain polymer solution;
   (C) dissolving multi-walled carbon nanotubes and ultrasonicizing to obtain multi-walled carbon nanotube solution; and
   (D) mixing polymer solution and multi-wall carbon nanotube solution, stirring to form film, paved film is bubbling in methanol to obtain polyarylene ether ketone and multi-wall carbon nanotube gas separation composite film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The dehydrating agent is toluene. The forming agent is potassium carbonate. The solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, sulfolane, N-methylpyrrolidone or dimethyl sulfoxide. Preferred Conditions: The ratio of polyarylene ether ketone to multi-wall carbon nanotube is 1:(0.02-0.06). The polyarylene ether ketone and multi-walled carbon nanotube gas separation composite film has thickness of 40-70 micrometer. The specific viscosity of polyaryl ether ketone is 0.55-0.69g/dL. Preferred Process: The method further involves adding a mol of 4-aminophenyl hydroquinone, b mol bisphenol AF monomer, c mol 4,4'-difluorobenzophenone, d mol bisphenol hydrazine in reaction vessel, mixing, add salting agent, watering agent and solvent, reflux with water for 3-5 hours at 120-140 degrees C, let off watering agent, raise temperature to 170-195 degrees C, and distill off remaining with water agent, continue to react for 6-20 hours to obtain polyaryl ether ketone sulfone, where a is equal to 0.001-1.0, b is 0.001-1.0, c is equal to 0.001-1.0, d is equal to 0.001-1.0, and sum of a, b, d is 1.

PT P
PN CN109686987-A; CN109686987-B
TI Method for preparing carbon/nitrogen-loaded titanium dioxide nanotube matrix for use in microbial fuel cell, involves pre-treating titanium sheet, followed by and treating carbon and nitrogen-containing titanium dioxide nano matrix.
AU YUAN H
   WANG L
   DENG L
   CHEN Y
   QIAN X
AE GUANGZHOU ENERGY INST CONVERSION CHINESE (CAEC-C)
GA 2019409323
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon/nitrogen-loaded titanium dioxide nanotube matrix preparing method involves pre-treating a titanium sheet, using the pretreated titanium sheet as an anode and a copper sheet as a cathode, anodizing at a steady-state potential in a hydrofluoric acid electrolyte having a concentration of 0.5 wt%, washing the anodized titanium sheet with water, drying and annealing at 450-550 degrees C for 0.5-1.5 hours to prepare a titanium dioxide nano matrix, immersing the titanium dioxide nano matrix in a lysine solution of 0.5-1.5 mol/L and drying, reacting hydrothermally the titanium dioxide nano matrix at 110-130 degrees C for 10-15 hours to realize a semi-carbonization of lysine to obtain a carbon dioxide and nitrogen-containing titanium dioxide nano matrix and treating the carbon and nitrogen-containing titanium dioxide nano matrix by pyrolyzing at 850-950 degrees C for 1.5-2.5 hours in an nitrogen atmosphere to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon/nitrogen-loaded titanium dioxide nanotube matrix for use in microbial fuel cell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing carbon/nitrogen-loaded titanium dioxide nanotube matrix with increased maximum output power density of a microbial fuel cell.

PN CN109545579-A
TI Method for preparing pore controllable electrode            based on photocurable printing, involves taking out            sample, washing electrode with dilute acid, and drying            electrode with deionized water to get porous            electrode.
AU ZHANG L
   WANG P
   LI D
   ZHANG H
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 201931694E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves designing the structure of                the porous electrode and the micropores. The                photosensitive resin is selected and harmonized as                a printing material. The support is added, sliced                and three dimensionally printed to obtain the resin                electrode structure, and then cured and dried. The                structure of the resin is pyrolyzed and printed at                equal weight loss rate, in the inert gas                environment. The pyrolysis electrode and activator                are mixed into a nickel crucible. The deionized                water is added to make the activator cover the                surface of the electrode. The nickel crucibles is                dried and activated at high temperature in the                nitrogen environment of tubular furnaces. The                sample is taken and the electrode is washed with                dilute acid, and dried with deionized water to get                the porous electrode. The activator is selected                from potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, zinc                chloride, potassium hydrogen carbonate, potassium                carbonate, carbon dioxide, or water.
   USE - Method for preparing pore controllable                electrode based on photocurable printing.
   ADVANTAGE - The three dimensional printing process has a                simple process and a good structure. The pore                parameters of the porous electrode can be                independently regulated to regulate the                electrocatalytic performance of the material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of an                electron micrograph showing the shape and size of                different pores.

TI Follicle-activating preparation for preparing human ovarian cortex culture for non-diagnostic treatment purposes, comprises phosphoinositide 3-kinase/rotein kinase B/mammalian target of rapamycin pathway activator and/or Phosphatase.
AU GU J
   GU S
   LAN D
AE MULTIPOTENT STEM CELL REGENERATIVE MEDIC (MULT-Non-standard)
GA 201924858A
AB    NOVELTY - Follicle-activating preparation comprises phosphoinositide 3-kinase/rotein kinase B/mammalian target of rapamycin (P13K/Akt/mTOR) pathway activator and/or Phosphatase and tensin homolog (PTEN) inhibitor SF1670, where concentration of P13K/Akt/mTOR pathway activator is a 5-15 mu M statin, and PTEN inhibitor is 20-50 mu M SF1670.
   USE - Follicle-activating preparation for preparing human ovarian cortex culture for non-diagnostic treatment purposes.
   ADVANTAGE - The follicle-activating preparation has good clinical effect on follicle development and selection in vitro activators and used in new technology application field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of applying ovarian cortex culture fluid for non-diagnostic treatment purposes, which involves:
   (A) taking human ovarian tissue in 10mL-15mL cell culture medium, separating the ovarian cortex and cutting into small pieces of 1mm3;
   (B) mixing 1 mL of solution I with reagent I and/or reagent II to obtain 10 mL tissue culture solution; and
   (C) culturing the human ovarian cortex in a 24-well plate containing a suspension network, adding 400-3000 mu L tissue culture solution under the small mesh, and culturing for 2 hours at 37 degrees C, under 5 vol.% carbon dioxide and after 2 hours, completing tissue culture, which is used for subsequent operations.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The statin is lovastatin, pravastatin or atorvastatin. The human ovarian cortex culture solution comprises P13K/Akt/mTOR pathway activator statin and/or a PTEN inhibitor SF1670, and base culture solution. The basal culture solution is MESAM cell culture solution, which is added with 0.23 mM sodium pyruvate and 10 wt.% human serum albumin. The human ovarian cortex culture solution comprises reagent composition composition solution I, basal culture solution, reagent I, P13K/Akt/mTOR pathway activator, 5-15 mu M statin. Preferred Conditions: The dose of 30mL basic culture solution is separately pre-packaged with reagent II with PTEN inhibitor, 20-50 mu M SF1670, and pre-packaged according to the dose of 30mL basic culture solution.

PN CN109400197-A
TI Wearable ceramic composite material comprises yttrium-stabilized zirconium oxide, wurtzite type boron nitride, silicon carbide, copper fiber, cobalt oxide, carbon black, polyethylene elastomer, alumina fiber, binder and auxiliary material.
AU WU B
   LI L
AE UNIV FOSHAN (UYFS-C)
GA 2019226908
AB    NOVELTY - Wearable ceramic composite material comprises 20-80 pts. wt. yttrium-stabilized zirconium oxide, 20-60 pts. wt. wurtzite type boron nitride, 12-30 pts. wt. silicon carbide, 5-20 pts. wt. copper fiber, 2-5 pts. wt. cobalt oxide, 2-10 pts. wt. carbon black, 3-10 pts. wt. polyethylene elastomer, 3-10 pts. wt. alumina fiber, 4-8 pts. wt. magnesium borate crystal whisker, 5-30 pts. wt. binder and 2-10 pts. wt. auxiliary material.
   USE - Wearable ceramic composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The wearable ceramic composite material increases efficiency and quality of each component, and effectively improves wear resistance of composite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a wearable ceramic composite material, which involves:
   (A) mixing and ball milling yttria-stabilized zirconium oxide, wurtzite-type boron nitride, silicon carbide, and cobalt oxide by ball milling to obtain slurry, and introducing a carbon dioxide gas into slurry to obtain first mixture;
   (B) ball-milling auxiliary material, carbon black, alumina fiber, copper fiber, magnesium borate whisker and binder to obtain second mixture; and
   (C) stirring molten polyethylene elastomer with first mixture and second mixture to obtain a blank, pressing blank to obtain a green body, and subjecting obtained blank to debinding and sintering to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Compositions: The auxiliary material comprises 30-80 wt.% diatomite, 10-60 wt.% kaolin, and 0-10 wt.% calcium carbide based on the total mass of the auxiliary material. The binder comprises 20-60 wt.% polyvinyl alcohol, 10-50 wt.% sodium carboxymethylcellulose, and 5-40 wt.% water based on total mass of binder. The yttrium-stabilized zirconium oxide contains 4-6 mol.% yttrium. Preferred Conditions: The polyethylene elastomer has a molecular weight of from 25000-32000. The method involves ball-milling yttria-stabilized zirconia, wurtzite-type boron nitride, silicon carbide, and cobalt oxide by using anhydrous ethanol as a ball milling medium for 10-20 hours, adding 0.4-0.6 times of total mass of deionized water to obtain slurry, and introducing carbon dioxide gas into slurry at 3-8MPa for 5-10 hours to obtain first mixture. The pressing time is not less than 30 hours. The debinding method is carried out in a nitrogen atmosphere. The sintering is carried out by heating obtained blank to 1800-1900 degrees C for 2-8 hours, and slowly dropping temperature to 1200-1400 degrees C for 2-4 hours.

GA 201919738E
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a wave emission source for                emitting radiation having a frequency spectrum in                millimeter range. A lithographically fabricated                high-Q resonator is coupled to the wave emission                source, where the resonator comprises a cavity                formed in a semiconductor substrate, the a                characteristic dimension of the cavity is for about                one half of a wavelength in the frequency spectrum                of the wave emission source, and the cavity is                dimensioned to define a resonant structure (1) for                frequencies in the frequency spectrum of the wave                emission source.

   ADVANTAGE - The device adopts high quality nature of                cavities for providing long absorption path length                within gas sample, while keeping overall dimensions                compact in cost-effective manner.    a method for sensing gases

TI Preparing low-carbon mixed gas hydrate anti-explosive agent involves preparing a hydrating agent by adding inorganic salt to solvent, where solvent is water or dry water, inorganic salt is silica, potassium phosphate or metal halide.
AU WANG L
   XU Y
   LI Z
   ZUO N
   LIANG P
   CHEN M
   LIU Y
AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
GA 2018A51585
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low-carbon mixed gas hydrate anti-explosive agent involves preparing a hydrating agent by adding an 0.0010-1 moles/L inorganic salt to a solvent, where solvent is water or dry water, inorganic salt is silica, potassium phosphate or metal halide ABn, where A is sodium, magnesium, potassium or calcium, B is fluorine, chlorine, bromine or indium, n is 1, 2. 85-95 vol.% inert mixed gas is mixed with 5-15 vol.% carbon dioxide, nitrogen gas and argon gas mixed, nitrogen and argon are mixed, inert mixed gas is introduced into the hydrating agent at 4-10 megaPascals, at (-10) to 8 degrees C for 100-300 minutes to form mixed gas hydrate. The mixed gas hydrate is formed by the reaction is pulverized to a hydrate powder having a particle diameter of less than 1.0 mm at (-10) degrees C or lower.
   USE - Method for preparing low-carbon mixed gas hydrate anti-explosive agent.
   ADVANTAGE - Preparing low-carbon mixed gas hydrate anti-explosive agent in simple, easy, convenient, environment-friendly and cost effective manner which has good explosion-proof effect and effectively reduced the explosion pressure wave propagation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The hydrating agent is prepared by adding 0.05 moles/L inorganic salt silica to water, inert mixed gas is prepared, where inert mixed gas comprises 10% carbon dioxide, nitrogen gas and argon gas mixture, and nitrogen gas and argon gas, volume ratio is 1:1. The inert mixed gas is charged into the hydrating agent to make the pressure reach 7 megaPascals, and the reaction gas is formed at 3 degrees C for 150 minutes to form a mixed gas hydrate, and the mixed gas formed by the reaction is formed. The hydrate is pulverized to a hydrate powder having a particle diameter of less than 1.0 mm at (-10) degrees C or lower. The hydrating agent is prepared by adding 0.01 moles/L sodium chloride inorganic salt to dry water to make sodium chloride. The inert mixed gas is charged into the hydrating agent, the pressure is up to 6 megaPascals, and the reaction gas is formed at 1 degrees C for 160 minutes to form a mixed gas hydrate, and the mixed gas hydrate is formed by pulverizing to a hydrate powder having a particle diameter of less than 1.0 mm at 10 degrees C or lower.

PN CN108997599-A
TI High strength cross-linked alkaline anion exchange            composite film in electrochemical energy device,            comprises water-soluble polyvinyl alcohol and            water-soluble polymer containing ammonium groups, where            crosslinking agent is glutaraldehyde.
AU QIAO J
   WANG M
   WEI Y
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 2018A3571V
AB    NOVELTY - High strength cross-linked alkaline anion                exchange composite film comprises water-soluble                polyvinyl alcohol and water-soluble polymer                containing quaternary ammonium groups.
   USE - High strength cross-linked alkaline anion                exchange composite film in electrochemical energy                device such as hydrogen/oxygen fuel cell, direct                methanol fuel cell, metal-air battery, carbon                dioxide electrochemical reduction and                supercapacitors (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The high strength cross-linked alkaline anion                exchange composite film is easy to produce                commercially, has high mechanical strength, good                conductivity, mild reaction condition, short                reaction time, and strong operability, and prepared                in simple, eco-friendly and cost effective                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing high strength cross-linked alkaline                anion exchange composite film, which                involves:
   (A) dissolving water-soluble polyvinyl alcohol                containing hydroxyl in de-ionized water, dissolving                water-soluble polymer, stirring at 70-100 degrees C                to obtain transparent and uniform solution A,                dissolving quaternary ammonium groups in de-ionized                water, and stirring to obtain uniform mixed                solution B, mixing solution A and solution B and                pouring into plastic culture dish, and naturally                drying to obtain polymer film; and
   (B) peeling polymer film from dish, and                thermally processing physical cross-linking,                chemical cross-linking and finally dipping in                potassium hydroxide solution for ion                exchange.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The water-soluble                  polymer containing quaternary ammonium group is                  guar gum hydroxypropyl trimethyl ammonium                  chloride, dextran hydroxypropyltrimonium chloride                  or dimethyl diallyl ammonium chloride. The                  crosslinking agent is glutaraldehyde,                  2-thiophenecarboxaldehyde,                  7-methoxy-3,7-dimethyloctanal,                  4-hydroxybenzaldehyde, 3,4-dichlorobenzaldehyde,                  2,6-dimethyl benzaldehyde and                  5-methyl-2-thiophenecarboxaldehyde. Preferred                  Condition: The physical cross-linking temperature                  is 100-180 degrees C. The cross-linking time is                  40 minutes-3 hour. The chemical crosslinking time                  is 30 minutes-12 hours. The concentratio of                  potassium hydroxide is 1-8 mole/L.

TI Preparing nano-zinc oxide comprises crushing zinc ore, mixing with e.g. nutrient solution, filtering, mixing with e.g. sulfuric acid, ultrasonically vibrating, adding additive, freeze-drying, ball milling with e.g. ethanol and calcining.
AU HU G
   FU P
AE HU G (HUGG-Individual)
GA 201891925J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-zinc oxide comprises (a) taking zinc ore into a crusher for crushing, collecting the powder, mixing the powder, a mixture and a nutrient solution in a mass ratio of 4-7:2-4:8-10, mixing, adjusting the pH and allowing to stand, (b) mixing the mixture with a mixed solution in a mass ratio of 2-4:12-15, filtering, collecting the filtrate, adjusting the pH to 5.5-6 to obtain a processing solution, (c) mixing the processing solution, zinc powder and sulfuric acid solution in a mass ratio of 5-8:1:6, stirring, ultrasonically vibrating, placing and allowing to stand at 28-35 degrees C, adding the additive and heating, and (d) heating, freeze-drying, collecting the freeze-dried product, ball milling the freeze-dried product, auxiliary agent and ethanol solution in a mass ratio of 8-13:2:5, calcining, collecting the milled calcined product, washing and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano-zinc oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses carbon dioxide and zinc to form zinc oxide, which can control the diameter of the formed nano zinc oxide; uses gas, which increases the specific surface area of zinc oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mixture in the step (a) is formed by mixing humus and bolbostematis in a mass ratio of 6:1-3. The step (a) comprises allowing to stand at an air humidity of 55-60% at 28-35 degrees C. The auxiliary agent in the step (d) is formed by mixing ammonium bicarbonate and urea in a mass ratio of 5:1 to 3. Preferred Components: In the step (b): the mixed solution is formed by mixing polyethylene glycol and glucose solution in a mass ratio of 1:7-9.    USE - The method is useful for preparing nickel                ion-modified covalent organic framework                material.

In the step (ii), the                  solvent used for the centrifugal washing is                  acetonitrile and the number of centrifugal                  washing is 3-5 times. In the step (ii), the                  vacuum drying process parameter is: a drying                  temperature of 70 degrees C and a drying time of                  12 hours.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The small molecule compound is glycerol or polyethylene glycol. The transient film has an elastic modulus of 0.05-100 MPa and a stretching ratio of 50-300%. The modified cellulose is cellulose having a degree of hydroxy substitution of 0.6-1.8 and the modified cellulose has a viscosity value of 200-5000 mPa.s. Preferred Method: In step (i) after the modified cellulose is dissolved in deionized water the solution in which the modified cellulose is dissolved is further stirred in a water bath, where the water bath temperature is 0-100 degrees C, the stirring speed is 500-1000 rotations/minute and the stirring time is 2-4 hours. In step (ii) after adding a small molecule compound to the modified cellulose solution the solution for adding the small molecule compound is also stirred in a water bath, where the temperature of the water bath is 0-100 degrees C, the stirring speed is 500-1000 rotations/minute, and the stirring time is 2-4 hours. In step (iii) after the watch glass is placed in a dry box, the temperature of the drying oven is controlled to be 10-200 degrees C and dried for 1-72 hours. In step (iii) the mixed solution is subjected to bubble removal treatment before being poured into the watch glass and the process of removing bubbles includes (a) removing bubbles in the mixed solution by using ultrasound, where the ultrasonic power is 20-100 W and the ultrasonic duration is 0.5-1 hour and a mixed solution in which bubbles are first removed is obtained; (b) placing the mixed solution for initial bubble removal in an environment at a temperature of 10-40 degrees C for 24-72 hours to obtain mixed solution for removing bubbles.

   USE - Organic aerogel is used as acoustic material,                and thermal-insulating material for storage of                cryogens (claimed), aircraft, pipeline, tanker and                maritime ships, car battery housing, under hood                liner, lamp, cold packaging of tank and box, jacket                and footwear, tent, construction material, etc. Can                also be used as adsorption agent for oil spill                cleaning, and shock-absorbing medium for safety and                protective equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - The organic aerogel has excellent                thermal-insulation property and mechanical                property, and low thermal conductivity and density,                and high oil absorption rate, and is dust-free and                lightweight.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacture of organic aerogel, which involves                dissolving the cyclic ether compound into the                solvent and adding the amine compound and mixing,                optionally adding the catalyst and mixing,                transferring the mixture to a sealed mold, heating                or maintaining the solution in order to form a gel,                washing the gel with a solvent, optionally adding a                silylation agent to the wet gel, washing the gel                with acetone, drying the gel by supercritical                drying or ambient drying, and optionally recycling                carbon dioxide from supercritical drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amine compound is                  an aliphatic amine compound, a cycloaliphatic                  amine compound, an aromatic amine compound, or an                  oligomeric polyamine compound, preferably an                  aliphatic amine, and is chosen from compounds of                  formulae (I)-(VIII). The cyclic ether compound is                  an epoxy compound having functionality of 2-10,                  preferably 3-4, and is chosen from 28 compounds,                  e.g. compounds of formulae (IX)-(XIII). The ratio                  of functional groups in the amine compound and                  cyclic ether compound is 8:1, preferably 1:1. The                  organic aerogel has solid content of 5-40 wt.%,                  preferably 7-25 wt.%. The solvent is a polar                  solvent, preferably chosen from acetone,                  chloroform, dimethyl sulfoxide,                  dimethylacetamide, dimethylformamide,                  1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinone, acetonitrile,                  acetophenone, polypropylene carbonate, and water.                  The catalyst is chosen from alkyl amine, tertiary                  amine, hydroxyl-containing compound, imidazole                  compound, and aza compound, preferably chosen                  from 2,4,6-tris(dimethylaminomethyl)phenol,                  2-ethyl-4-methylimidazole, triethanolamine,                  dimethylbenzylamine,                  8-diazabycyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene, and                  1,4-diazabicyclo(2.2.2)octane.R1 = 1-30C alkyl, 3-30C cycloalkyl, aryl,                  7-30C alkylaryl, 3-30C heterocycloalkyl, or 1-30C                  heteroalkyl (all optionally substituted);n,m = 1-30, preferably 1-4;R2 = -O-, -S-, -C(O)-, -S(O)2-, -S(PO3)-, or                  1-30C alkyl, 3-30C cycloalkyl, 3-30C                  heterocycloalkyl, or 1-30C heteroalkyl (all                  optionally substituted);X1-X3 = H, halo, alkoxy, or linear or                  branched 1-6C alkyl;R3 = -Si(OCzH2z+i)3;z = 1-6;p = n;R4 = linear or branched 1-6C alkyl;R5 = -O-, -S-, -C(O)-, -S(O)2-, -S(PO3)-, or                  1-30C alkyl, aryl, 3-30C cycloalkyl, 3-30C                  heterocycloalkyl, 7-30C alkylaryl, or 1-30C                  heteroalkyl (all optionally                  substituted);andR6 = R1.Preferred Properties: The amine compound has                  at least one primary amine functionality, and                  total amine functionality of 2-10, preferably                  2-4. Preferred Condition: Formation of gel is                  carried out at room temperature to 180 degrees C,                  preferably 30-80 degrees C.

PN BR102018001873-A2
TI System for transforming automotive vehicle i.e. electric car, into hydraulic powered vehicle, has cooling module connected to gearbox, and control panel arranged with temperature gage, pressure gage, oil meter and vehicle steering module.
AU DOS SANTOS S R
AE DOS SANTOS S R (DSAN-Individual)
GA 2018950227
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a hydraulic pump (4) connected to a tank (8), and an oil distributor provided with driving coils (9), which are connected to a gearbox (11) and a cooling module (12). The oil distributor is connected to an overflow valve (13), a clutch (14), a throttle (16) and a control panel (17), where the cooling module is connected to the gearbox and the overflow valve. The control panel comprises a dial, a temperature gage, a pressure gage, an oil meter and a vehicle steering module. A battery (1) is connected to a CPU (2).
   USE - System for transforming an automotive vehicle i.e. electric car, into a hydraulic powered vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces emission of carbon dioxide gas responsible for global warming and waste of energy. The overflow valve is set with an oil-mounting twist lock, which transforms oil pressure into rotary motion, thus driving gears of the vehicle in effective manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of a system for transforming an automotive vehicle into a hydraulic powered vehicle.

   Gearbox (11)
   Cooling module (12)
   Overflow valve (13)
   Clutch (14)

PT P
PN CN108567980-A; CN108567980-B
TI Releasing photo dynamically induced carbon            monoxide comprises e.g. carrying out controlled release            of carbon under illumination of near-infrared light by            mixing a near-infrared light-responsive type            photosensitizer.
AU CAI X
   WU L
   GU Z
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 201878673N
AB    NOVELTY - Releasing photo dynamically induced carbon                monoxide comprises carrying out controlled release                of carbon under the illumination of near-infrared                light by mixing a near-infrared light-responsive                type photosensitizer which can be used for                photodynamic therapy and a carbon monoxide                releasing molecule CORM-401.
   USE - The method is useful for improving anti-tumor                effect.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes the entry of the carbon                monoxide-controlled delivery system into the cell,                disintegrates nano gel under the action of                intracellular glutathione, releases CORM-401 and                apply near-infrared light activation simultaneously                to produce singlet oxygen and carbon dioxide for                photodynamic therapy and carbon mono oxide gas                treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) carbon monoxide controllable delivery                system; and
   (2) preparing carbon monoxide controllable                delivery system, comprising (i) covalently coupling                a three-generation lysine dendrimer G3-Lys with a                polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxane POSS as a core                to a photosensitizercerium (VI) to obtain a                conjugate G3- cerium (VI), (ii) cross-linking                G3-cerium (VI) with a cross-linking agent                3,3'-dithiodipropionate di(N-hydroxysuccinimide)                ester into a nanogel G3DSP-Ce6 delivery system, and                (iii) adding CORM-401 into the G3DSP-cerium (VI)                solution slowly by means of physical load, stirring                until mixing evenly, dialyzing off the free                CORM-401 and freeze drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photosensitizer is                  cerium (VI), cerium (IV),                  2-(1-hexyloxyethyl)-2-devinyl pyropheophorbide-                  alpha (HPPH) or aluminum phthalocyanine                  tetrasulfonate(AlPcS4). The molar ratio of the                  photosensitizer cerium (VI) and carbon monoxide                  releasing molecule CORM-401 is 0.05-1:1,                  preferably 0.1-1:1. The molar ratio of G3-cerium                  (VI) and DSP is 1:2-1:4, where particle size of                  the nano gel product G3DSP-cerium (VI) is below                  200 nm. The mass of CORM-401 in step (iii)                  accounts for 10-30 wt.% of the total mass of                  CORM-401 and G3DSP-Ce6. Preferred Method: The                  method comprises controlling release of carbon                  monoxide out under the irradiation of                  near-infrared light by mixing the photosensitizer                  cerium (VI) and the carbon monoxide releasing                  molecule CORM-401. The step (i) comprises                  dissolving cerium (VI) and                  N,N'-dicyclohexylcarbodiimide and                  N-hydroxysuccinimide in dimethyl formamide under                  the protection of nitrogen, reacting at room                  temperature overnight to activate carboxy groups,                  removing dicyclohexylurea by filtering, adding                  obtained carboxylated cerium (VI) slowly drop                  wise into the G3-Lys solution in the dark,                  dialyzing resulting solution successively                  dialyzed against dimethyl formamide and water,                  and lyophilizing to obtain a conjugate G3-cerium                  (VI). The step (ii) comprises dissolving                  G3-cerium (VI) in dimethyl formamide at room                  temperature, slowing adding dimethyl formamide                  solution containing DSP drop wise into the                  solution of G3-cerium (VI) by stirring, reacting                  completely, dialyzing against dimethyl formamide                  and water in turn, and lyophilizing to obtain a                  nanogel product G3DSP-cerium (VI). The step (iii)                  comprises dissolving G3DSP- cerium (VI) and                  CORM-401 in phosphate-buffered saline solution                  with pH 7.4, adding CORM-401 into G3DSP-cerium                  (VI) solution slowly under ultrasonic stirring at                  room temperature overnight, mixing                  CORM-G3DSP-cerium (VI) in water, dialyzing to                  remove free CORM-401 and freeze-drying. The                  molecular weight cut off during dialysis in each                  step is 1000 Da.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The carbon monoxide                  controllable delivery system has a particle size                  below 200 nm. The delivery system comprises a                  near-infrared light-responsive type and                  photosensitizer is used for photodynamic therapy,                  a carbon monoxide release molecule CORM-401, and                  a carrier that supports or binds photosensitizer                  and the carbon monoxide release molecule                  CORM-401. The carbon monoxide controllable                  delivery system is a CORMG3DSP- cerium (VI) nano                  gel delivery system comprising a nano gel                  G3DSP-cerium (VI) and a carbon monoxide release                  molecule CORM-401 supported in a nanogel G3DSP-                  cerium (VI) cavity, Nano gel G3DSP- cerium (VI)                  is a three-generation lysine dendrimer G3-Lys                  with polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxane POSS as                  the core and covalently coupled with                  photosensitizer cerium (VI) to obtain conjugate                  G3-Ce6 cross linker nano gel formed by cross                  linking of 3,3'-dithiodipropionate                  bis(N-hydroxysuccinimide) ester.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

PT P
PN CN108525694-A; CN108525694-B
TI Preparation of composite photocatalyst by baking            melamine, dispersing graphitic carbon nitride in            isopropanol, ultrasonically stripping, drying,            dispersing with silicon carbide in water, adding            platinum sol, evaporating and roasting.
AU ZHENG Y
   ZHANG X
   DU Z
   LI Q
   SUN D
   HUANG J
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 201874257G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of composite photocatalyst                comprises baking melamine at 550-650 degrees C for                3-5 hours, grinding solid into powder, marking as                graphitic carbon nitride (g-C3N4)-bulk, dispersing                in isopropanol, ultrasonically stripping, removing                bulk precipitate, centrifugally washing remaining                g-C3N4-bulk with deionized water for 1-3 times,                drying in oven at 40-60?OC for 8-12 hours, grinding                sample, marking as g-C3N4-exfoliated, carbon                dioxide stripping, marking as g-C3N4-nanosheet,                adding aromatic camphor extract liquid as reducing                agent in sodium hydroxide solution, adding                chloroplatinic acid hexahydrate solution as metal                precursor, stirring at 80-90 degrees C for 1-2                hours, preparing platinum sol, dispersing                g-C3N4-nanosheet and silicon carbide in deionized                water, mixing, stirring at room temperature for                1.5-2.5 hours, slowly adding platinum sol while                stirring, completely evaporating water to obtain                solid mixture, and roasting at 300-400 degrees C                for 3-5 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing composite                photocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is green and                environment-friendly, can increase force between                molecular platinum nanoparticles, g-C3N4 and                silicon carbide, increases chemical and physical                stability and photocatalytic activity, and has                photolysis hydrogen production rate of 595.3-725.6                mu mol/hour/g, and quantum efficiency of                2.76-3.36%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Dispersing of g-C3N4-bulk                  in isopropanol is done for 1-3 hours, and then                  statically placed for 10-30 seconds. Carbon                  dioxide stripping is done for 10-30 minutes. The                  mass ratio of platinum to g-C3N4-nanosheet is                  1:100. The mass ratio of silicon carbide to                  g-C3N4-nanosheet is 1:20. After dispersing                  g-C3N4-nanosheet and silicon carbide in deionized                  water, the solution is ultrasonically treated for                  15-30 minutes, mixed and ultrasonic treated again                  for 10-30 minutes. Evaporation is done at 80-90                  degrees C in oil bath while stirring.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI UV light catalytic exhaust gas purifier, has insulating board fixedly connected with power master switch, and box body shell fixedly equipped with lamp frame, where lamp frame is provided with UV beam.
AU LIANG Y
AE JIANGMEN LILONG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201871085R
AB    NOVELTY - An ultraviolet light catalytic exhaust gas purifier claimed by the utility model, comprising a box shell, a box and a UV lamp tube and box shell right side is provided with an air inlet, the left side is provided with an exhaust port; air inlet and air outlet outside is used for connecting exhaust pipe flange; the box comprises a box body set on the facade of the shell box door frame, the door frame is equipped with an electric box door; the box door is provided with a door lock, the door is provided with the switch button; the box door is provided with an insulating plate, the insulating plate is provided with a plurality of ballast. the insulating plate is set with power supply main switch; the cavity of the tank body shell is provided with equidistant arrangement of lamp frame, the lamp frame is provided with a UV lamp tube, each UV lamp will emit high energy high ozone UV ultraviolet beam, the organic waste gas and malodorous gas enters the purifier when passing through UV ultraviolet source beam area, instantly generates photolysis reaction into carbon dioxide, water and other low molecular compound, to be discharged after multi-stage purification.

PT P
PN US2018193331-A1; US10226457-B2
TI Prevention or treatment of pain comprises administering to patient an opioid agonist to provide analgesic effects and sub-analgesic amount of buprenorphine to prevent or treat adverse pharmacodynamic response.
AU HUMMEL M
   KYLE D J
   WHITESIDE G
AE PURDUE PHARMA LP (PURP-C)
   PURDUE PHARMA LP (PURP-C)
GA 201859508X
AB    NOVELTY - Prevention or treatment of pain comprises administering to patient an opioid agonist to provide analgesic effects and sub-analgesic amount of buprenorphine to prevent or treat adverse pharmacodynamic response induced by administration of the opioid agonist. The sub-analgesic amount of buprenorphine does not affect the analgesic effects of opioid agonist. The opioid agonist is formulated for controlled release.
   USE - Method of preventing or treating pain. A study was conducted by using male Sprague-Dawley rats as test subject, administering 0.0005-1 mg/kg buprenorphine free base or 25% hydroxyl propyl-beta-cyclodextrin to test subjects, administering 10 mg/kg morphine sulfate or saline 1 hour later, administering 1 ml/100 grams charcoal meal 0.5 hour later, euthanizing test subjects with carbon dioxide and removing GI tract from stomach to cecum, recording length of small intestine and distance (cm) to leading edge of charcoal, analyzing data using one-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) with Bonferroni's multiple comparisons test. The results demonstrate that buprenorphine when administered prior to morphine prevents the morphine induced retardation of GI transit. This effect was dose dependent with greater magnitude of effect observed with increasing dose.
   ADVANTAGE - The method treats or prevents opioid-induced bowel dysfunction (e.g. opioid-induced constipation) or other opioid induced adverse pharmacodynamic responses.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The buprenorphine comprises buprenorphine free base, salts, solvates and polymorphs and controlled release dosage form comprises from about 10 mg to about 160 mg of oxycodone hydrochloride.
   ACTIVITY - Analgesic; Laxative.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration is oral, transdermal, sublingual, buccal, gingival, rectal, subcutaneous or intramuscular (claimed). No dosage details given.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B02 (Fused ring heterocyclics.)
MC B04-A04; B12-M10; B14-C01; B14-E09; B14-E10C; B14-L01; B14-S18

TI Vertical torsion spring carbon-dioxide laser light guiding arm, has nesting mechanism fixed with first light guiding arm, and second light guiding arm whose corner wall is fixed with third light guiding arm.
AU SUN L
AE CHANGCHUN CHANGGUANG HENGCHANG LASER TEC (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201854883D
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the field of optics machine technology, and claims a vertical torsion spring CO2 laser light guiding arm, comprising a first light guiding arm, said first light guide arm is in L shape, the bottom end of the first light guide arm is fixedly connected with a connecting seat; one side of said first light guide arm upper end is fixedly connected with a first connector, the first connector far away from one side of the first light guiding arm is rotatably connected with a second light guiding arm, said second light guide arm is in Z shape, the second light guiding arm upper end far away from the one side of the first connector is fixedly connected with the second connector. the second connector far away from one side of the second light guiding arm is rotatably connected with a connecting pipe. the vertical torsion spring CO2 laser light guide arm by setting the embedding mechanism and convenient disassembly of the mounting plate, the mounting plate is provided with two bumps, which can limit the position of the laser reflector sheet, the laser reflector sheet is adhered on the mounting plate, when the mirror is damaged, it is convenient to replace the reflector, strong practicability and worth to popularize.

PT P
PN DE102017127388-A1; US2018156550-A1; JP2018091480-A; CN108146028-A; CN108146028-B; JP6998504-B2
TI Flame-retardant thermal insulating material used in e.g. electronic device, comprises silica xerogel, carbon material and nonwoven fibers that are set to hold silica xerogel and carbon material.
AU OIKAWA K
   TOYOTA K
   SAKATANI S
   KOHMYOHJI D
   OOSHIRO T
   WADA T
   KOMYOJI D
   SUSUMU W
AE PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)
   PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)
   PANASONIC IP MANAGEMENT CO LTD (MATU-C)
GA 201845666E
AB    NOVELTY - The thermal insulating material (108) comprises a silica xerogel (115), a carbon material (114) and nonwoven fibers (116) that are set to hold the silica xerogel and the carbon material. The heating time of the thermal insulating material is set less than 15 seconds and a maximum heat release rate of less than 15 kW/m in a cone-calorimeter-based exothermic test. The carbon material is reacted with oxygen in the atmosphere at a temperature at which the silica xerogel thermally decomposes or at a temperature higher than the temperature reacted to produce carbon dioxide.
   USE - Flame-retardant thermal insulating material used in device (claimed) such as electronic device, and automotive and industrial equipment devices.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat insulating materials are combined with high thermal insulating properties that provide effective thermal insulation and flame retardance so as to prevent the spread of fire. Since heat insulating materials are provided with lower thermal conductivities, sufficient heat insulating effects is obtained even in narrower spaces in electronic devices, automotive and industrial equipment devices. Thus, heat to be transferred from heat-generating components is allowed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the sectional views of flame-retardant heat insulating materials.
   One-component compound layer (101)
   Flame-retardant thermal insulating material (108)
   Carbon material (114)
   Silica xerogel (115)
   Nonwoven fiber (116)

PT P
PN CN107983300-A; CN107983300-B
TI Manganese dioxide-modified biochar composite            material used in treatment of heavy metal wastewater,            comprises manganese dioxide and biochar.
AU LIANG J
   LI X
   YU Z
   ZENG G
   LIU J
   LUO Y
   YANG Z
   QIAN Y
   FANG Y
AE UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 201836708J
AB    NOVELTY - A manganese dioxide-modified biochar composite                material comprises manganese dioxide and biochar,                and is prepared by loading generated manganese                dioxide on the biochar through a neutralization                reaction between permanganate and manganese element                in divalent manganese salt.
   USE - The manganese dioxide-modified biochar                composite material is useful in the treatment of                heavy metal wastewater including lead-containing                heavy metal wastewater or cadmium-containing heavy                metal wastewater (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The manganese dioxide-modified biochar                composite material: has excellent adsorption                capacity; and can be prepared using cheap raw                materials with high yield and without any toxicity                in a simple and economical manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing the manganese                dioxide-modified biochar composite material, which                involves adding the divalent manganese salt to a                mixed solution containing biochar and permanganate                to perform a neutralization reaction to obtain the                product; and
   (2) use of manganese dioxide-modified biochar                composite material prepared by preparation method                in the treatment of heavy metal wastewater, where                the application method involves mixing the                manganese dioxide-modified biochar composite                material with heavy metal wastewater for                oscillation adsorption reaction to complete the                treatment of the heavy metal wastewater, where the                addition amount of manganese dioxide-modified                biochar composite material is 0.2-4 g of the carbon                dioxide-modified charcoal composite material added                to each liter of heavy metal wastewater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  divalent manganese salt, biochar and permanganate                  in the mixed solution is                  (2.45-9.80):15:(1.06-4.22). The initial                  concentration of lead in the lead-containing                  heavy metal wastewater is 0.5-1500 mg/l. The                  initial concentration of cadmium in the                  cadmium-containing heavy metal wastewater is                  0.5-250 mg/l. Preferred Method: The preparation                  method of biochar involves: (1) combining                  livestock manure with wood chips, and carrying                  out aerobic composting to obtain a mixture; and                  (2) calcining the mixture obtained in step (1) to                  obtain the product. The calcining step in step                  (2) is performed under a protective atmosphere of                  nitrogen. The neutralization reaction is carried                  out under stirring conditions. The preparation                  method further involves, after the neutralization                  reaction is completed, heating the manganese                  dioxide-modified biochar composite material                  suspension obtained by the neutralization                  reaction at 70-90 degrees C, maintaining the                  temperature for 30-60 minutes, washing to neutral                  and drying. Preferred Condition: The aerobic                  composting in step (1) is carried out for 56-92                  days. The calcining step in step (2) is carried                  out at a heating rate of 5-15 degrees C/minute to                  400-700 degrees C for 1.5-2.5 hours. The                  oscillation adsorption reaction is carried out at                  a temperature of 25-45 degrees C and a speed of                  150-180 rpm for 10-14 hours. Preferred                  Properties: The heavy metal wastewater has a                  pH-value of 2-8 and an ionic strength of 0.01-0.1                  mole/l.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.)
MC D04-A01F2; D04-A01F3; D04-A01J; D04-B05A; D04-D; E11-Q02B; E31-N04C; E35-D; E35-J; E35-S

GA 2018183383
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano-silica composite material involves magnetically stirring reactive nitrogen species and methyl acrylate in presence of methanol as solvent, washing, filtering, drying, obtaining half dried silica-anchored poly(amidoamine) (PAMAM) dendrimers, magnetically stirring half-dried product and ethylenediamine in presence of methanol as solvent, obtaining silica-anchored PAMAM dendrimers, adding 12-lactam, dendritic silica, water and phosphoric acid into polymerization tank, reacting, cooling the material using water and pelletizing.

GA 201988761D
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating grapes comprises (i) adopting hand-held grape detection devices, arranging a temperature sensor, a humidity sensor, an illumination sensor, placing the temperature sensor on 10-15 cm below the ground of the grape plantation, placing the humidity sensor on 10-15 cm below the ground of the grape plantation, placing the light sensor at 500-80 cm above the vines in the vineyard, synchronously setting up multiple assisted monitoring sensors, distributing in the grape plantation field, and comprehensively tests the temperature, humidity and illuminance, (ii) connecting the sensors through the network, and arrange the temperature control device, the humidity control device, and the illuminance control device, (iii) placing the remote monitoring device in the monitoring room and setting up the display to facilitate observation by personnel, (iv) controlling the temperature, humidity and illumination.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating grapes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high quality and high yield grapes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating grapes comprises (i) adopting hand-held grape detection devices, arranging a temperature sensor, a humidity sensor, an illumination sensor, placing the temperature sensor on 10-15 cm below the ground of the grape plantation, placing the humidity sensor on 10-15 cm below the ground of the grape plantation, placing the light sensor at 500-80 cm above the vines in the vineyard, synchronously setting up multiple assisted monitoring sensors, distributing in the grape plantation field, and comprehensively tests the temperature, humidity and illuminance, (ii) connecting the sensors through the network, and arrange the temperature control device, the humidity control device, and the illuminance control device, (iii) placing the remote monitoring device in the monitoring room and setting up the display to facilitate observation by personnel, (iv) controlling the temperature of the temperature control device when the temperature sensor detects that the temperature is not in the proper range, controlling the humidity control device to perform drip irrigation when the humidity sensor detects that the humidity is not in the proper range, controlling the illumination control device to be shaded when the illumination sensor detects that the illumination is strong and controlling the illumination control device to perform a fill operation when the illumination sensor detects that the illumination is weak, where the hand-held grape detecting device comprises a detecting probe, a mechanical connecting portion, a cylinder, a display device, a hand-held portion and a communication connecting portion. The detecting probe is fixedly coupled to one end of the cylinder through the mechanical connecting portion. The display device is disposed on the column. The hand-held portion is sleeved on the column. The communication portion is disposed at the other end of the column. The detecting probe includes an ethanol gas sensor, a carbon dioxide gas sensor, a signal processing circuit, a sugar monitoring sensor, an image capturing device, and an image processing device. The communication connection portion includes a signal receiving unit and a signal transmission unit. The hand-held grape detecting device further includes an illumination sensor, a temperature sensor, and a humidity sensor. The illumination sensor is disposed on a grape planting field, the temperature sensor and the humidity sensor are disposed in a soil of a grape planting field, and the hand-held grape detecting device further comprises a central processing device, a wireless transmission device, and a remote monitoring device. The central processing device and the wireless transmission device are disposed in the column. where the temperature sensor is used to detect a temperature signal of the soil of the grape plantation field. The humidity sensor is used to detect the humidity signal of the soil of the grape plantation field. The light sensor is configured to detect an illumination signal of the grape plantation field, and the temperature signal, the humidity signal, and the illumination signal are transmitted to the central processing device through the signal receiving unit. The central processing unit converts the received temperature signal into a temperature value. The central processing unit converts the received humidity signal into a humidity value. The central processing unit converts the received illumination signal into an illumination value. The ethanol gas sensor is used for detecting an ethanol concentration signal emitted by the grape. An output of the ethanol gas sensor is coupled to an input of the central processing unit. The carbon dioxide gas sensor is configured to detect a carbon dioxide concentration signal emitted by the grape. An output end of the carbon dioxide gas sensor is connected to an input end of the signal processing circuit. An output of the signal processing circuit is coupled to an input of the central processing unit. The signal processing circuit performs signal processing on the received carbon dioxide concentration signal and transmits the signal to the central processing device. The sugar monitoring sensor is configured to detect a sugar signal in the grape. An output of the sugar monitoring sensor is coupled to an input of the central processing unit. The image acquisition device is configured to collect image information of grapes. An output end of the image capture device is coupled to an input end of the image processing device. An output of the image processing device is coupled to an input of the central processing device. The image processing device transmits the received grape image information to the central processing device. The central processing unit converts the received ethanol concentration signal into an ethanol concentration value. The central processing unit converts the received carbon dioxide concentration signal into a carbon dioxide concentration value. The central processing unit converts the received sugar signal into a sugar value. The central processing unit transmits the temperature value, the humidity value, the illumination value, the ethanol concentration value, the carbon dioxide concentration value, the sugar value, and the image information of the grape to the display device for display by the signal transmission unit. The central processing device transmits temperature value, humidity value, illumination value, ethanol concentration value, carbon dioxide concentration value, sugar value, and image information of the grape to the remote monitoring device through the wireless transmission device.

   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, has obvious economic and                social benefits. In the industrial production                process is not capable of avoiding the crushing                process, through the cylindrical sieve can enter                into lithium iron phosphate powder containing                aluminum debris screen, in the baking process of                de-bonding agent using cheap reducing gas hydrogen                and carbon dioxide protective gas to realize the                operation atmosphere of absolute anti-oxidant,                ensuring that the aluminum and bivalent iron will                not be oxidized, so that the aluminum will not                pulverization phenomenon. The specific gravity of                carbon dioxide is greater than air in the actual                industrial roasting process, it will not easily                leave the roasting furnace, so it can greatly save                the consumption amount of the protective gas,                reduce the operation cost of removing the adhesive                agent calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing gas is                  hydrogen, carobon monoxide, methane, ethyne, or                  ethene. The electrode sheet is lithium iron                  phosphate anode sheet, lithium iron phosphate                  negative plate, or lithium cobalt oxide cathode                  sheet.

PN RU2777082-C1
TI METHOD FOR OBTAINING MAGNESIUM OXIDE FROM NATURAL            BRINES AND SIMULTANEOUSLY EXTRACTED WATERS OF OIL            FIELDS.
AU BUSLAEV E S
   ZVEZDIN E I
   SHAIDULLIN F F
AE TATNEFT SHASHIN PUBLIC STOCK CO (TATN-C)
   TATNEFT SCI TECH CENT CO LTD (TATN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B33302
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the production of                magnesium oxide from natural brines and                simultaneously extracted reservoir waters of oil                fields for the purpose of their subsequent                injection into the layers of oil fields to maintain                reservoir pressure. The brine and the produced                reservoir waters are purified from oil and iron                ions, after which they are treated with lime milk                to form magnesium hydroxide. Carbonization of a                suspension of magnesium hydroxide is carried out to                obtain a solution of magnesium bicarbonate and a                solid precipitate. The separated magnesium                bicarbonate solution is heated to a boil to form                magnesium carbonate, which is separated and                calcined to produce magnesium oxide. Moreover, the                pH value of the mentioned initial brines and waters                is preliminarily determined, as well as their pH                value after filtration of magnesium hydroxide.                After the separation of magnesium hydroxide, the                mentioned brines and waters are re-carbonized with                carbon dioxide contained in the gases after                calcination of magnesia with carbon dioxide, or                with flue gases that are formed during fuel                combustion. Carbonation is continued until the pH                value of the brine and the associated produced                reservoir waters reaches the pH value of the                original natural brine. The resulting brine is                filtered from the precipitated calcium carbonate                precipitate and sent for injection into the layers                of oil fields to maintain reservoir pressure.
   USE - Oil industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Method makes it possible to obtain magnesium                oxide with a purity of at least 98% using lime of                any quality on the basis of simultaneously                extracted waters of oil fields, as well as natural                brines with minimal emissions of carbon dioxide                into the atmosphere.
1 cl, 2 tbl, 1 ex, 2 dwg

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-loaded single-atom                carbon-based catalyst involves placing special                crystal plane exposed zinc oxide, 2-methylimidazole                and metal-organic precursor in a closed reaction                chamber of a tube furnace in a molar ratio of                1:0.8:0.01-1:3.6:0.05, introducing argon gas as a                carrier gas at flow rate of 5-15 mL/minute, heating                the furnace at rate of 2-10 &#8451;/minute to                400&#8451;, reacting for 0.25-2 hours, heating                the furnace at rate of 2-10 &#8451;/minute to                800-1000&#8451;, and reacting for 0.25-2 hours.                The placement positions of zinc oxide,                2-methylimidazole and metal-organic precursor are                set based on the flow direction of carrier gas, and                are provided from upwind direction to downwind                direction based on the order of metal-organic                precursor, 2-methylimidazole and zinc oxide. The single metal atom contained in the                catalyst is iron atom or nickel atom.

PN CN113980684-A
TI Preparing saline-alkali soil remediation agent using waste incineration fly ash useful in planting crops, comprises e.g. preparing secondary carbonated phosphorus-loaded filter residue, mixing with water to obtain mixed slurry, exposing carbon dioxide gas, irradiating with low-temperature plasma.
AU XU J
   ZHANG S
   SONG D
   ZHOU L
   HUANG T
AE CHANGSHU INST TECHNOLOGY (CHGS-C)
GA 202224694U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing saline-alkali soil remediation agent by waste incineration fly ash, comprises (i) mixing sodium phosphate and waste incineration fly ash to phosphorus-mixed fly ash, (ii) mixing phosphorus-mixed fly ash with water, stirring for 0.5-1.5 hours, and filtering to obtain filter residue which is primary phosphorus-loaded filter residue, (iii) mixing the phosphogypsum and primary phosphorus-loaded filter residue to obtain phosphogypsum-mixed filter residue, (iv) mixing the phosphogypsum-mixed filter residue with water, then exposing carbon dioxide gas until the pH of the slurry is 5-9, and then filtering to obtain secondary carbonated phosphorus-loaded filter residue, (v) mixing the secondary carbonated phosphorus-loaded filter residue with water to obtain mixed slurry, then exposing carbon dioxide gas and irradiating low-temperature plasma for 0.5-4.5 hours at the same time, filtering, drying the obtained filter residue, and grinding into powder to obtain final product.
   USE - The saline-alkali soil remediation agent is useful in planting crops (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The saline-alkali soil remediation agent: improves the soil quality of saline-alkali soil, and realizes 135.37 % of relative root growth ratio and 125.52 % of dry weight variation of species.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for saline-alkali soil remediation agent by waste incineration fly ash obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The mass ratio between sodium phosphate and waste incineration fly ash in step (i) is 5-25:100. In step (ii), the liquid-solid ratio of water and phosphorus-doped fly ash is 1-3:1 ml/g. In step (iii), the mass ratio between phosphogypsum and primary phosphorus-loaded filter residue is 15-55:100. In step (iv), the water and phosphogypsum are mixed with the filtration residue in liquid-solid ratio of 1-3:1 ml/g. In step (v), the liquid-solid ratio of water and the secondary carbonated phosphorus-loaded filter residue is 1-3:1 ml/g.Preferred Method: In step (v), the action voltage of low-temperature plasma discharge is 5-75 kV.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
