PN GB2596809-A; WO2022008882-A1
TI Producing metal phosphide nanoparticles or quantum            dots e.g. indium phosphide for quantum-dot LED display            and lamp, and diode laser, involves forming quantum            dots cores by reacting precursor of metal component            with phosphide precursor.
AU WANG Y
   GREEN M A
AE KINGS COLLEGE LONDON (UNLO-C)
GA 2022074322
AB    NOVELTY - Producing metal phosphide nanoparticles or                quantum dots, preferably aluminum phosphide, indium                phosphide, gallium phosphide, zinc phosphide,                cadmium phosphide, manganese phosphide, iron                phosphide, carbon dioxide phosphide and/or nickel                phosphide, involves forming quantum dots cores by                reacting a precursor of a metal component with a                phosphide precursor, which is phosphine carboxamide                or a phosphine compound of formula (H2PCONR1R2)                (I).
   USE - Method for producing metal phosphide                nanoparticles or quantum dots, preferably aluminium                phosphide, indium phosphide, gallium phosphide,                zinc phosphide, cadmium phosphide, manganese                phosphide, iron phosphide, carbon dioxide phosphide                and/or nickel phosphide, for quantum-dot (QD)-LED                display, QD-LED lamp, diode laser, image sensor,                solar cell, photovoltaic device and another                articles (claimed) in a variety of biological                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, safe and efficient for                producing metal phosphide nanoparticles or quantum                dots by utilizing a phosphorus precursor that is                non-volatile and air-stable, and reducing the need                for specialist glassware and scale up equipment,                thus cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing metal phosphide nanoparticles or                quantum dots, preferably aluminium phosphide,                indium phosphide, gallium phosphide, zinc                phosphide, cadmium phosphide, manganese phosphide,                iron phosphide, carbon dioxide phosphide and/or                nickel phosphide, involves forming quantum dots                cores by reacting a precursor of a metal component                with a phosphide precursor, which is phosphine                carboxamide or a phosphine compound of formula                (H2PCONR1R2) (I).
   R1=H or organic residue; and
   R2=1-4C alkyl, preferably methyl or                cycloalkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal component is                  indium optionally together with an alloy-forming                  component. The cores are formed of indium                  phosphide or indium zinc phosphate.Preferred Method: The core-forming reaction                  is carried out in a solution in an inert                  atmosphere at 200-300&#176;C. The method                  further comprises forming a shell, or inner and                  outer shells, on the core.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solution comprises                  12-20C alpha-olefin such as 1-octadecene, and a                  capping agent which is a long chain alpha-amine.                  The amine is hexadecylamine. The shell is made of                  zinc sulfide and is formed by thermal                  decomposition of a dialkyldithiocarbamate in a                  solvent.
   EXAMPLE - 0.075 g Indium chloride, 0.43 g zinc stearate,                0.105 g phosphine carboxamide, 1.5 g                hexadecylamine, and 20 ml 1-octadecene were added                into a three-necked flask under a nitrogen                atmosphere. The reagents were degassed at                120&#176;C for 180 minutes, then the flask was                back-filled with nitrogen. The flask was heated up                to 220&#176;C and left for 30 minutes, during                which time the solution turned from clear red to                clear saffron orange. 0.3 ml Pre-prepared 0.1 M                ZDEC (361.93 mg zinc diethyldithiocarbamate/10 ml                1-octadecene) was added into indium zinc phosphide                solution, then heated up rapidly to 300&#176;C                for 10 minutes under the nitrogen atmosphere to                synthesis indium zinc phosphide/zinc sulfide                quantum dots.

TI Energy optimization dispatching method for island multi-energy complementary electric-pneumatic coupling system, involves constructing objective function with maximizing revenue of dispatching system as optimization objective.
AU LI B
   LI X
   WANG J
   LI J
AE UNIV NORTHEAST DIANLI (UNEE-C)
GA 202185126H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves building a wind-light-diesel-gas-storage island multi-energy complementary electric-gas coupling system. The portion of the electricity generated by photovoltaic and diesel generators, and diesel generators is passed into the electrolyzer, and portion of the hydrogen produced by the electrolysis of water in the electrolyzer and the carbon dioxide captured by carbon capture are passed into the methane reactor to produce methane. An electrical-pneumatic coupling network system model is established. The carbon dioxide involved in the Methanation reaction comes from carbon capture and direct air capture technology. The hydrogen is reacted with carbon dioxide in methane reactor to produce methane. An objective function is constructed with maximizing the revenue of the dispatching system as the optimization objective. The operating maintenance cost of a V-tank is a coefficient of maintenance cost for operation of a hydrogen storage tank.
   USE - Energy optimization dispatching method for island multi-energy complementary electric-pneumatic coupling system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can realize island multi-energy complementary electric-gas energy coupling scheduling process in a scientific, stable, safe, efficient, economical and environment-friendly manner, eliminate distributed energy output redundancy, reduce wind removing rate and light abandoning ratio of the target, improve distributed energy permeability, reduce diesel generator permeability and avoid living gas problem.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram illustrating the energy optimization dispatching method for island multi-energy complementary electric-pneumatic coupling system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN112961360-A; CN112961360-B
TI Preparation method of polyangular cage siloxane supercritical carbon dioxide thickener, involves reacting monohydrogen-terminated siloxane in cyclohexane solution of cage-shaped stereocyclosiloxane at specific temperature.
AU WANG Y
   LI Q
   LIU B
   LIANG L
   TANG L
   GONG J
   ZHANG C
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 202168750J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves reacting a hydroxy double-terminated siloxane with sodium silicon alkoxide to obtain double-terminated sodium-terminated siloxane and reacting double-ended sodium-terminated siloxane with hydrogen-containing chlorosilane to prepare monohydrogen-terminated siloxane. The monohydrogen-terminated siloxane is reacted in a cyclohexane solution of a cage-shaped stereocyclosiloxane at a temperature of 35 degrees C to 90 degrees C to prepare a polyangular cage-shaped siloxane polymer. The organic solvent used is toluene, cyclohexane, n-hexane, benzene, ethanol, methanol, petroleum ether and acetone. The hydrogen-containing chlorosilane is dimethylmonohydrochlorosilane, monomethyldihydrochlorosilane, trihydrochlorosilane, pentamethylmonohydrochlorodisilane and tetramethyldihydrochlorodisilane.
   USE - Preparation method of polyangular cage siloxane supercritical carbon dioxide thickener.
   ADVANTAGE - The reaction substrate involved in the preparation process is low in cost, convenient to purchase, and the side chain of the hydroxyl-terminated siloxane is adjusted. The preparation process of the siloxane polymer has simple steps, easy synthesis conditions, no special equipment, and high reactant safety. The method has no other reaction wastes and toxic and harmful substances are produced. The prepared siloxane polymer thickener has stable physical properties under normal temperature and pressure, and is stored in airtight at low temperature for a long time. The product has good compatibility with various cosolvents. The increase of branch chains in the polyangular cage siloxane supercritical carbon dioxide thickener improves the miscibility and thickening ability of siloxane and reduces the amount of cosolvent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an application of the polyangular cage siloxane polymer prepared by the method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a hydrogen nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) spectrum of a sample of the polyangular cage siloxane polymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The caged stereocyclosiloxane is octamethylcyclotetrasiloxane, tetramethyltetravinylcyclotetrasiloxane, tetramethyltetrahydrocyclotetrasiloxane, and decamethylcyclopentasiloxane. The catalyst is sodium chloroplatinite, chloroplatinic acid, ammonium chloroplatinate, sodium hexachloroplatinate hexahydrate, ammonium hexachlororuthenate and trimethyl vanadium acetate.

A reduction tank (140) is used for spraying an ammonia brine supplied from an ammonia dissolving tank to the carbon dioxide fume of an exhaust gas introduced from a ship engine through a spray nozzle to convert the ammonia brine into a diesel fume to remove carbon dioxide fume. AB    NOVELTY - Powder of composite oxide comprises cerium                element and zirconium element, where 10-100                nm-interval pore volume after heat-treating in                atmosphere at 1000 degrees C for three hours is                greater than or equal to 0.35 ml/g, and measuring                carbon dioxide desorption amount after                heat-treating by temperature rise desorption method                of greater than or equal to 80 mu mol/g.

   (3) a method for preparing the powder,                involving (a) preparing first cake from slurry                obtained by coprecipitation method from raw                material solution comprising cerium salt and                zirconium salt, (b) treating obtained first cake                with alcohol-containing liquid to obtain second                cake having alcohol concentration of greater than                or equal to 60 vol.%, and (c) baking obtained                second cake at 600-900 degrees C.

TI Reducing electrode consumption of electric furnace involves brushing paint on cooled electrode, drying, changing paint, starting smelting, adding lime into electric furnace, melting waste steel material, and adding carbon powder.
AU CHEN L
   WANG J
   ZHOU W
   WU G
AE PANGANG GROUP RES INST CO LTD (ANSH-C)
GA 2020C6492W
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises brushing paint on cooled electrode, drying, changing brushing paint, smelting, adding 18-25 kg/t steel into electric furnace, adding 45 t waste steel material to furnace, electrifying, melting, adding 20-30 kg/t steel carbon powder and 5-15 kg/t steel slag agent, blowing oxygen at a rate of 10-30 m3/t steel, oxygen pressure of 0.8-1.2 MPa, adding 1/3 silicon carbide when blowing, adding 1.5-2.2 kg/t steel, blowing to 1/2, adding 3.6 kg/t carbon powder, controlling voltage of melting period to 510-539 V, current to 32352-880 A, controlling voltage of oxidized heating period at 461-484 V, current at 32352-880 A bottom blowing gas in smelting process, blowing carbon dioxide at a flow control of 0.06-0.25 Nm3 /t, melting under carbon dioxide at a rate of 0.1-0.35 Nm3 /t, oxidative heating using carbon dioxide and argon in a ratio of 3:1 at a flow rate of 0.2-0.45 Nm3 /t, controlling alkalinity of steel slag in slag making process at 2.8-3.2 at 1650-1680 degrees C.
   USE - Method for reducing electrode consumption in electric furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The final electric furnace electrode consumption amount is reduced to 3.5 kg/t steel by the current 8 kg/t steel, which reduces the electric furnace electrode consumption and saves the production cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The coating composition comprises 30-50 wt.% aluminum oxide, 15-20 wt.% chromium oxide, 5-15 wt.% cobalt oxide, 25-35 wt.% iron oxide, 5-10 wt.% magnesium oxide and 5-10 wt.% tungsten oxide and remaining amount of adhesive. The waste steel material composition comprises more than 90 wt.% iron, less than 0.050 wt.% sulfur, less than 0.050 wt.% phosphorus and remaining amount of at least one of silicon, manganese, nickel and chromium. The slag melting agent comprises 33-43 wt.% calcium oxide, 6-14 wt.% magnesium oxide, 3-7 wt.% calcium fluoride, 5-6 wt.% aluminum oxide and 30-40 wt.% ignition loss. The active lime is active lime containing 85-90 wt.% calcium oxide.

TI Negative ion antibacterial textile finishing liquid comprises silver nitrate, tourmaline, zinc oxide, ethanol, organic antibacterial agent, dispersant, carbon dioxide, and deionized water.
AU WEI Z
   WEI D
   WEI R
   WEI X
   YU Y
AE GUILIN XINZHU NATURAL BIOLOGICAL MATERIA (GUIL-Non-standard)
GA 2020B2977F
AB    NOVELTY - A negative ion antibacterial textile finishing liquid comprises 5-7 pts. wt. silver nitrate, 12-15 pts. wt. tourmaline, 10-14 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 50-80 pts. wt. ethanol, 15-25 pts. wt. organic antibacterial agent, 20-30 pts. wt. dispersant, carbon dioxide, and deionized water.
   USE - Negative ion antibacterial textile finishing liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The negative ion antibacterial textile finishing liquid has excellent mildew-proof property, UV resistance, and far infrared property, and increases air permeability, sweat absorption, comfort, and hand feel of fabric.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the antibacterial textile finishing liquid, which involves preparing 0.01-1 mol/L silver nitrate aqueous solution, stirring and dispersing tourmaline particles in the silver nitrate aqueous solution such that the concentration of tourmaline particles in the final solution is 5-40 wt.%, washing, and drying to obtain tourmaline loaded with silver, adding deionized water to zinc oxide in a beaker, heating while stirring continuously until zinc oxide is completely dissolved, stopping stirring, cooling the solution to room temperature, filtering to obtain solution (A), introducing carbon dioxide gas into solution (A) in a flask, heating the flask to 80-90 degrees C, crystallizing, crushing the crystals, and calcining to obtain zinc oxide nanopowder, preparing 1-99 vol.% ethanol aqueous solution, mixing with tourmaline particles loaded with silver and zinc oxide nanopowder such that the weight ratio of zinc oxide nanopowder and tourmaline particles is 1:1.5-1:2.2, adding a dispersant and the ethanol aqueous solution, stirring for 30 minutes, and adding an organic antibacterial agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of zinc oxide and deionized water is 1:5. Preferred Properties: The ion average particle diameter of the negative ion antibacterial textile finishing liquid is 116 plus minus 6 nm, the solid content is 30%, and the pH is 8.5 plus minus 0.5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic antibacterial agent is lupinol palmitate. The dispersant is dodecyldiphenyl ether sulfonate salt.

PN JP2020159032-A
TI Crack repair method of reinforced concrete structure in heavy snow area, involves making free lime in crack and carbon dioxide of micro-bubble to react, generating calcium carbonate and filling crack.
AU SATO S
   INOUE F
AE TS PLANNING KK (TSPL-Non-standard)
GA 202095979E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a bubble generation process of generating a micro-bubble containing a carbon dioxide in a solution. The calcium hydroxide solution generation process is provided for adding a calcium hydroxide in the solution after the bubble generation process, and generating a calcium hydroxide solution. The solution injection process is configured to inject the calcium hydroxide solution containing the micro-bubble into a crack (C1) of a concrete structure (C). The free lime in the crack and the carbon dioxide of the micro-bubble are made to react, a calcium carbonate is generated, and the crack is filled. The micro-bubble is an ultra-fine bubble. The cement-type fillers such as an ultrafine particle blast furnace slag, are injected into the crack, after the solution injection process. The epoxy resin or a foaming urethane resin is injected into the crack after the crack is configured to dry after the solution injection process.
   USE - Crack repair method of reinforced concrete structure in heavy snow area.
   ADVANTAGE - The fine crack generated inside the concrete structure filled with a calcium carbonate is repaired, and the penetration of the water and oxygen into the concrete structure of a deterioration factor is prevented more reliably. The solidifying of the crack is achieved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the boring process of the crack repair method of a concrete structure.
   Boring machine (1)
   Guide mechanism (2)
   Drive motor (10)
   Concrete structure (C)
   Crack (C1)

AE NANTONG ZHUORUI LASER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 202071901H

PN CN111392913-A; CN212151905-U
TI System for advanced treatment of PVC centrifugal mother liquor waste liquid, has full-chamber anion bed filled with anion resin to deeply remove anions and convert other anions into hydrogen and oxygen root ions.
AU SU Z
   WANG X
   ZHENG T
AE RIGHTLEDER BEIJING ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOL (RIGH-Non-standard)
GA 202066717V
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an ozone catalytic oxidation device (3) that is used to catalyze and oxidize the organic matter in the waste liquid of PVC centrifugal mother liquor to convert it into carbon dioxide and water and obtain the first liquid. A primary decarburization device, connected to the ozone catalytic oxidation device, used to remove carbon dioxide generated by the ozone catalytic oxidation device in the first liquid, and obtain a second liquid. A secondary decarburization device (11), connected to the full-cell anode, for removing carbon dioxide in the fourth liquid and obtaining a fifth liquid. The full-chamber anion bed is connected to the secondary decarburization device. The inside of the full-chamber anion bed is filled with anion resin to deeply remove the anions in the fifth liquid and convert other anions in the fifth liquid into hydrogen and oxygen root ions and get pure water.
   USE - System for advanced treatment of PVC centrifugal mother liquor waste liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is used as polymerized pure water, and the water recovery rate reaches more than 95% which saves energy and reduces operating costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for advanced treatment of PVC centrifugal mother liquor waste liquid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system for advanced treatment of PVC centrifugal mother liquor waste liquid. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PT P
PN CN111068705-A; CN111068705-B
TI Supported precursor for Fischer-Tropsch synthesis            reaction catalyst, comprises carrier which is manganese            oxide molecular sieve, active metal oxide comprising            metal of specific group, and additive metal oxide            containing alkaline earth metal.
AU JIN C
   WU Y
   ZHANG R
   HOU C
   XIA G
   SUN X
   YAN Z
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP RES INST (SNPC-C)
GA 202038124D
AB    NOVELTY - A supported catalyst precursor comprises a                carrier which is a manganese oxide molecular sieve,                and an active metal oxide comprising group VIII                metal, and an additive metal oxide comprising group                IIB metal, alkaline earth metal and group IB                metals, supported on the carrier.
   USE - Supported precursor is used for producing                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction catalyst for                producing alpha -olefin (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The supported catalyst precursor can increase                the utilization rate of carbon dioxide and methane                in production of alpha -olefin, and reduces                greenhouse gas emission and system energy                consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of supported catalyst                precursor, which involves supporting the active                metal and additive metal on the carrier, drying and                calcining; and
   (2) production of alpha -olefin, which                involves contacting methane with a steam to perform                steam reforming reaction to obtain a steam to be                reformed into a gas, contacting methane and carbon                dioxide to perform a dry reforming reaction to                obtain a dry integrated gas, mixing at least one                portion of the steam and at least one portion of                the dry integrated gas to prepare a Fischer-Tropsch                synthesis reaction feed, reducing and activating                the supported catalyst precursor to obtain a                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction catalyst,                contacting the reaction feed with the catalyst to                perform Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction to                obtain a product stream, and separating alpha                -olefin, methane and carbon dioxide from the                product stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The active metal is                  preferably iron. The additive metal is preferably                  zinc. The supported catalyst precursor comprises                  3-70 wt.%, preferably 8-30 wt.% active metal                  oxide, 1-60 wt.%, preferably 2-50 wt.% additive                  metal oxide, and 12-94 wt.%, preferably 35-91                  wt.% carrier. Preferred Process: Supporting of                  active metal and additive metal on carrier is                  performed by immersion method e.g. equal-volume                  immersion method or saturated immersion method,                  or co-precipitation method. The immersion method                  is performed by dissolving a soluble salt of the                  active metal and additive metal which are nitrate                  or hydrochloride at a molar ratio of 1:0.2-5,                  preferably 1:0.3-3 in a solvent to obtain an                  impregnating solution, and immersing the carrier                  in the impregnating solution by one-time                  impregnation or step impregnation at 10-80                  degrees C, preferably 20-60 degrees C for 0.1-3                  hours, preferably 0.5-1 hour. Preferred                  Conditions: Drying of the supported carrier is                  performed at 80-350 degrees C, preferably 100-300                  degrees C for 1-24 hours, preferably 2-12 hours.                  Calcining is performed at 250-900 degrees C,                  preferably 350-800 degrees C for 0.5-12 hours,                  preferably 2-6 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Production of alpha                  -olefin further involves recycling the separated                  methane for steam reforming reaction and/or dry                  reforming reaction, and recycling the separated                  carbon dioxide for dry reforming reaction.                  Preferred Conditions: Steam reforming reaction is                  performed in a fixed bed reactor at 700-950                  degrees C, preferably 800-900 degrees C at a                  reaction pressure of 0.1-5 MPa, preferably 1-3                  MPa. Dry reforming reaction is performed in a                  fixed bed reactor at 600-800 degrees C,                  preferably 650-750 degrees C and a reaction                  pressure of 0.1-5 MPa, preferably 1-3 MPa.                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction is performed                  in a fixed bed reactor at 200-380 degrees C,                  preferably 250-350 degrees C and a reaction                  pressure of 0.8-3 MPa, preferably 1-2.8 MPa.                  Reduction activation is performed under a                  hydrogen atmosphere at 100-800 degrees C,                  preferably 250-500 degrees C for 0.5-72 hours,                  preferably 2-24 hours. The hydrogen pressure is                  0.1-4 MPa, preferably 0.1-2 MPa. Preferred                  Composition: The molar ratio of methane and steam                  is 1:0.5-4. The molar ratio of methane and carbon                  dioxide is 1:0.5-5. The molar ratio of hydrogen                  and carbon monoxide is 0.4-3:1, preferably                  1.5-2.5:1. Preferred Property: The                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed has a gas                  hourly volumetric space velocity of                  2000-50000/hour, preferably                  10000-30000/hour.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC E10-J02D1; E11-E02; E34-A; E34-B01; E34-C01; E34-D01; E34-D03C; E34-D03D; E34-E03; E35-A; E35-B; E35-C01; E35-D; E35-E; E35-U01; E35-V; E35-W; E35-X; H04-E04; H04-E05; H04-F02E; H04-F03; H04-F05; H04-F06; N01-B; N02-A01; N02-B01; N02-C01; N02-D01; N02-E03; N02-E04; N03-F01; N03-F02; N06-C05; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-C02

PN JP2020061957-A
TI Carbon dioxide supply device for supplying carbon dioxide to cultivation room has control unit, which stops combustion in combustion unit when temperature detected by at least one of temperature sensors exceeds first specified temperature.
AU HIROYASU M
   KIDOWAKI A
   YAMAJI H
   HASEGAWA K
   KODA T
AE NORITSU KK (NOTS-C)
GA 202032577N
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide (CO2) supply device has a vaporization unit (440) that heats and vaporizes liquid fuel, flame holes (482) arranged around the vaporization unit, and a flow path (464) in which a mixture of vaporized fuel and combustion air flows into the flame holes. A combustion unit (450) forms a flame in the flame hole by jetting an air-fuel mixture from the flame hole. An air supply unit (420) supplies combustion air to the vaporization unit and the combustion unit. A gas supply unit supplies combustion gas containing CO2 generated by combustion in the combustion unit into the cultivation chamber. A control unit controls the combustion unit and the air supply unit, and stops combustion in the combustion unit when the temperature detected by at least one of the vaporization unit temperature sensor (520) and a combustion unit temperature sensor (530) exceeds the first specified temperature.
   USE - CO2 supply device for supplying CO2 to a cultivation room such as a greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - Provides a CO2 supply device in which soot is less likely to occur in the cultivation room.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a longitudinal cross-sectional view of the combustion device cut at the central position and a longitudinal cross-sectional view of the combustor cut at the position of the flow hole.
   Air supply unit (420)
   Vaporization unit (440)
   Combustion unit (450)
   Flow path (464)
   Flame holes (482)
   Vaporization unit temperature sensor (520)
   Combustion unit temperature sensor (530)

PT P
PN GB2578155-A; WO2020078959-A1; GB2578155-B; CN112888848-A; EP3867514-A1
TI Method for controlling vehicle engine, involves            comparing result with reference curve to determine            measure of how far parameter averages obtained from            reference curve and adjusting control of engine systems            dependent on result.
AU PARMENTIER M
   SCHMITT J
AE DELPHI AUTOMOTIVE SYSTEMS LUXEMBOURG SA (DELP-C)
   DELPHI AUTOMOTIVE SYSTEMS LUXEMBOURG SA (DELP-C)
GA 202031682F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining two engine                operational parameters selected from the following                parameters carbon dioxide (CO2) output, power                output and nitrogen dioxide (NOx) output. The value                of parameter is averaged during a driving window. A                nominal reference curve relating two of the                following engine parameter is provided. The result                is compared with reference curve to determine a                measure of how far the parameter averages obtained                from the reference curve. The control of engine                system dependent on the result is adjusted. The                nominal reference curve represents an averaged                relationship between two parameters under multiple                driving conditions. The driving window comprises a                pre-determined driving distance or a predetermined                time window.
   USE - Method for controlling vehicle engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The gain in selective catalytic reduction                (SCR) NOx conversion performance is achieved by                thermal management to the cost of CO2 or by urea                over dosing to the cost of increased urea                consumption or increased nitrogen trihydride (NH3)                emissions. An LNT is regenerated at a higher                frequency to improve NOx conversion, to the cost of                increased CO2 and particulate matter (PM)                emissions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for controlling vehicle engine.
   Step for accumulating modeled carbon dioxide                and nitrogen dioxide (S2)
   Step for looking up nitrogen dioxide and                carbon dioxide (S4)
   Step for dividing accumulated nitrogen                dioxide by normal nitrogen dioxide (S5)
   Step for filtering ratio to calculate                severity index (S6)
   Step for modifying exhaust gas recirculation                rate function of severity index (S8)

PN CN110965071-A; CN110965071-B
TI Preparing metal catalyst for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, involves polishng zinc flakes with alumina powder successively, and then ultrasonically cleaning zinc flakes with ultrapure water and ethanol.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHANG T
   QIU Y
   LI X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202028928B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a metal catalyst involves polishng the zinc flakes with 30-100 mu m grade and 30-500nm grade alumina powder successively, and ultrasonically cleaning zinc flakes with ultra pure water and ethanol, and drying under protection of inert gas. The washed zinc flakes are put in the reaction kettle, added bismuth nitrate aqueous solution with a concentration of 0.01-2 millimole, and stand at room temperature for 0.1-5 hours. The concentration of Bi(NO3)3 aqueous solution is 0.1-0.8 millimole, and naturally cooled to room temperature after reacting at 160-200 degrees C for 2-24 hours in reaction kettle. The zinc flake is taken out and washed with ultrapure water, put it in oven, and dried it at 60-150 degrees C for 1-12 hours. The dried zinc flakes are put in saturated salt solutions in carbon di-oxide or inert atmosphere, and reduced it at (-)1.2-(-)2 volt vs SCE voltage for 0.5-4 hours. The ultrasonic cleaning of reduced zinc sheet and drying are performed under the protection of inert gas.
   USE - Method for preparing metal catalyst for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare metal catalyst which is helps to provides a simple and economical method in cost-effective manner. It has the characteristics of adjustable surface structure size and high specific surface area, and has higher catalytic activity, used for carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction, and also has excellent selectivity and catalytic activity for target products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The thickness of the zinc sheet is 0.01-2 mm, preferably 0.1-1 mm. The area is 1-100 square centimetre, preferably 5-20 square centimetre. The zinc flakes are completely submerged in the solution. Preferred Components: The cation in the saturated salt solution is sodium ion or/and potassium ion, and the anion is one or more of chloride ion, sulfate ion, carbonate ion, Bicarbonate, phosphate ion, and nitrate ion. The inert atmosphere is one or more of nitrogen, argon, and helium. The resulting catalyst is attached to the outer surface of the zinc flakes. The catalyst is one or two types of nano-flakes or nano-particles, and its elemental composition is two elements, zinc and bismuth. Preferred Compositions: The molar content of the bismuth element in the catalyst is 0.01-40 wt.%, preferably 1-20 wt.%.

AE YABASHI KOGYO KK (YABA-Non-standard)
   YABASHI IND CO LTD (YABA-Non-standard)
GA 202010313C
AB    NOVELTY - The plant cultivation system (10) has a lime                kiln (20) as a supply source of waste gas. A                distribution unit (50) distributes the waste gas                from the lime kiln to a cultivation room (11). The                waste gas discharged from the lime kiln by the                distribution unit has carbon-dioxide-gas                concentration of 10-50 vol.% and nitrogen oxide                concentration of 10-40 ppm. The sulfur-oxide                concentration of the waste gas discharged from the                lime kiln by the distribution unit is 10 ppm or                less. The distribution unit has resin piping which                distributes the waste gas. The ratio of the length                of the resin piping that occupies for the full                length of the distribution unit is 80% or                more.
   USE - Plant cultivation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The denitrification apparatus is omitted from                the flow system of waste gas. The mixed gas of                carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide suitable for                plant cultivation is supplied safely, at low cost,                and easily. Since the vertical furnace has a small                heat loss ratio and easily increases the thermal                efficiency, the temperature for firing the                limestone does not need to be high because the                thermal efficiency is high. Therefore, the increase                in thermal nitric oxide in the exhaust gas due to a                high firing temperature is suppressed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                plant cultivation system.
   Plant cultivation system (10)
   Cultivation room (11)
   Lime kiln (20)
   Distribution unit (50)

PN CN107892320-A
TI Preparation of light calcium carbonate used e.g. for medicine by heating shell powder and water with acetic acid, refrigerating, roasting, adding water, carbonizing with porous titanium dioxide catalyst and carbon dioxide gas, and drying.
AU HUANG H
AE LIUZHOU JIHUI MACHINE DESIGN CO LTD (LIUZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018288857
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of light calcium carbonate comprises adding shell powder and deionized water in reactor, adding acetic acid, heating, filtering, washing shell powder residue, quickly refrigerating, and obtaining pretreated shell powder; placing shell powder into muffle furnace, heating, and roasting to obtain calcium oxide powder; and adding calcium oxide powder and deionized water into carbonization reactor, placing bag with porous titanium dioxide catalyst into deionized water, sonicating, carbonizing with carbon dioxide gas, reacting to get calcium carbonate crystals, and hot air drying.
   USE - Method for preparing light calcium carbonate used for medicine and building decoration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses raw materials with wide sources, high purity and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of light calcium carbonate comprises adding 200 pts. wt. shell powder and 500 pts. wt. deionized water in reactor, adding 2 pts. wt. acetic acid, heating to 45-50 degrees C, filtering, washing shell powder residue, quickly refrigerating at 5 degrees C, and obtaining pretreated shell powder; placing shell powder into muffle furnace, quickly heating to 700-1000 degrees C at 50-65 degrees C/minute for 2 hours, and roasting to obtain calcium oxide powder; and adding 100 pts. wt. calcium oxide powder and 5000 pts. wt. deionized water into carbonization reactor, placing bag with porous titanium dioxide catalyst into deionized water, sonicating at 1000-1500 W and 40-50 degrees C, carbonizing with carbon dioxide gas, keeping pressure of reaction system at 2-4 MPa, reacting for 45 minutes to get calcium carbonate crystals, and hot air drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The porous titania catalyst is prepared by adding 100 pts. wt. ethanol solution, 1 pt. wt. surfactant and 2 pts. wt. ferric sulfate in reactor, starting stirring device, stirring at 200-500 revolutions/minute, adding 10 pts. wt. tetrabutyl titanate at 2 mL/minute, reacting for 2-3 hours, vacuum distilling, washing precipitates with absolute ethanol, collecting precipitate, rinsing with purified water for 25 minute, keeping flushing strength at 30-50 L/m2.second, placing rinsed precipitate in muffle furnace, and calcining. The hot air drying is done at 115-130 degrees C for 6-10 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The shell powder is oyster shell powder, or snail shell powder.

PT P
PN KR1831971-B1
TI Producing complex calcium carbonate, comprises e.g. crushing, combusting limestone, recovering coal ash, preparing mixture by adding water, forming calcium carbonate by injecting flue gas into mixture, and recovering calcium carbonate.
AU WHAN A J
AE KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
GA 2018190390
AB    NOVELTY - Producing complex calcium carbonate, comprises (a) crushing limestone while introducing into a power plant combustion chamber, (b) combusting by introducing limestone into a combustion chamber, recovering coal ash, and preparing a mixture by adding water according to the content of the added limestone, (c) forming calcium carbonate by injecting flue gas of the power plant into the obtained mixture under pressure of 10-50 bar, and (d) recovering calcium carbonate by performing solid-liquid separation of the mixture. The flue gas contains carbon dioxide and sulfur oxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing complex calcium carbonate using coal ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases carbonation efficiency through rapid carbonization by generating carbonate, produces carbonate that solidify carbon dioxide, uses low-grade coal-based ash, and recycles coal ash.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing complex calcium carbonate, comprises (a) crushing limestone while introducing into a power plant combustion chamber, (b) combusting by introducing limestone into a combustion chamber, recovering coal ash, and preparing a mixture by adding water according to the content of the added limestone, (c) forming calcium carbonate by injecting flue gas of the power plant into the obtained mixture under pressure of 10-50 bar, and (d) recovering calcium carbonate by performing solid-liquid separation of the mixture. The flue gas contains carbon dioxide and sulfur oxide. The desulfurization step is carried out by introducing the flue gas at a molar ratio of calcium and sulfur of 1:1-1.2 with respect to sulfur of the sulfur oxides in the flue gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises crushing the limestone into average diameter of 5-20 mm. The step (b) comprises missing limestone and water in a molar ratio of 1:1-2 to form the mixture in the form of slurry. The step (c) comprises injecting flue gas while maintaining the temperature to 30-60 degrees C, and stopping the injection of the flue gas when the pH of the mixture is less than or equal to 6.5. The solid-liquid separation is carried out under pressure of 20-500 mmHg.
   EXAMPLE - Limestone crushed into 20 mm was added into lignite, combusted at 900 degrees C, collected, 56 g limestone was added into 36 ml water to prepare a slurry, then introduced into a pressure vessel containing 1000 ml water, flue gas was added at 30 degrees C under pressure of 30 bar, and carbonized while stirring at 600 revolutions/minute. The flue gas supply was terminated when the pH reached to pH 6.5. Then solid and water were from the mixture separated under pressure of 20-500 mmHg, and the recovered solid was dried to obtain complex calcium carbonate.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E11-Q01B; E11-Q02B; E31-F01A; E31-N05C; E34-D03A

PN CN114988977-A
TI Catalyzing hydrogenation of carbon monoxide/carbon            dioxide to prepare olefins, involves mixing metal            carbides catalysts with hydrophobic aids, passing            hydrogen to reduce catalyst, at preset conditions,            continuously feeding hydrogen and carbon monoxide,            forming gas phase and liquid phase products.
AU FANG W
   XIAO F
   WANG L
   WANG C
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2022B83045
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyzing hydrogenation of carbon                monoxide/carbon dioxide, in the form of physical                mixed hydrophobic aid, involves: (1) providing                metal carbides as catalysts and activated carbon,                graphite or organic polymers as hydrophobic aids,                followed by controlling mass ratio of catalyst and                hydrophobic auxiliary agent, to 0.1- 10, then                mixing catalyst and hydrophobic auxiliary agent and                granulating, or mixing and granulating the catalyst                and the hydrophobic auxiliary agent, separately and                then packing into a fixed bed reactor; (2) passing                hydrogen into the reactor, followed by reducing the                catalyst, at 300&#176;C, under pressure 0.1                millipascals (MPa); (3) continuously feeding                hydrogen and CO as reaction gases into the reactor,                followed by controlling the molar ratio of the                gases to 1- 4, reaction temperature to 200-                450&#8451;, and reaction pressure to 0.1-6.0                MPa, to form gas phase products containing light                olefins and methane, and liquid phase products                containing 5-10C alpha-olefin.
   USE - Method is used for catalyzing hydrogenation of                carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide to prepare                olefins.
   ADVANTAGE - The new catalyst system used in the process,                enables to remove the water molecules generated                during the reaction process, from the surface of                the active site of catalyst, to ensure that the                catalytic performance is fully exerted. The                catalyst having high reaction conversion rate and                high selectivity, enables to control the methane                selectivity, at a lower level.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal carbide used                  in step 1 of the process used for catalyzing                  hydrogenation of carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide,                  is iron carbide, cobalt carbide and/or molybdenum                  carbide modified by metal elements namely lithium                  (Li), sodium (Na), potassium (K), rubidium (Rb),                  cesium (Cs), manganese (Mn), chromium (Cr),                  vanadium (V), zirconium (Zr), zinc (Zn), calcium                  (Ca), magnesium (Mg) and/or barium (Ba).The auxiliary agent used, is CO2, nitrogen                  (N2), argon (Ar) and/or helium (He).Preferred Composition: The amount of the                  metal carbide used in the process, is 0.1-30                  wt.%. The amount of CO2 used, is 0-72 vol.% and                  the amount of nitrogen used, is 0-20 vol.%. The                  amount of argon used, is 0-20 vol.% and the                  amount of helium used, is 0-20 vol.%.Preferred Method: The metal element used for                  modifying the metal carbide during the process,                  is added into the metal carbide by equal volume                  impregnation, excess impregnation or                  coprecipitation method.Preferred Conditions: The particle size of                  the catalyst and the hydrophobic auxiliary agent                  is controlled to 0.15-1.7 mm during the                  granulation and forming process in step (1). The                  amount of hydrogen gas used, in step (2) of the                  process, is controlled to 20 ml/minute. The                  reduction treatment process is performed for 5                  hours. The reaction space velocity of the                  reactor, in step (3) of the process, is                  1000-108000 ml/hour/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The organic polymer                  used in step 1 of the process used for catalyzing                  hydrogenation of carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide,                  is acrylonitrile butadiene styrene (ABS) resin,                  polytetrafluoroethylene, polyamide, polystyrene,                  polydivinylbenzene, polytrienylbenzene and/or                  their copolymers.

TI Preserving figs comprises e.g. picking fresh figs, sterilizing figs to be kept fresh by ozone, pre-cooling treatment, modified atmosphere packaging, filling with mixed gas of carbon dioxide, oxygen and nitrogen sealed package,and keeping fresh.
AU WANG W
   WANG X
AE XINJIANG SENRUIYAZHONG FRESH PRESERVATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021D49556
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving figs comprises (i) using fruit quality control, picking and selecting fresh figs that are intact, undamaged, and mature, as figs to be kept fresh, (ii) disinfection, sterilizing the figs to be kept fresh by ozone, (iii) pre-cooling treatment, and preferably-cooling the sterilized figs, (iv) modified atmosphere packaging, placing the pre-cooled figs into the modified atmosphere packaging box, after the gas replacement, filling with a mixed gas of carbon dioxide, oxygen and nitrogen sealed package, where the volume ratio of carbon dioxide, oxygen and nitrogen is 3: 4:93,and (v) keeping fresh in a high-voltage electrostatic field, placing modified atmosphere packaging box in a high-voltage electrostatic field with a temperature of -1degrees Celsius to 1degrees Celsius nd a voltage of 1200-1900 v or a voltage of 2300-2900 v for preservation.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving figs.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: greatly reduces the respiratory intensity and self-consumption of figs; inhibits the production of the ripening hormone ethylene; reduces the occurrence of diseases; delays the aging process of figs; and solves the loss of water and self-consumption of figs during storage, the condition of wilting, discoloration, yellowing, and rot, the freshness period can reach 92-130 days, which greatly extends the storage period and shelf life of figs compared with the existing preservation technology; and is environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (i), when picking, wear thread gloves to hold the figs lightly and then use scissors or a paper knife to cut the fruit stalks. The length of the fruit stalks remaining on the figs after picking is 0.5-0.8 cm. In the step (ii), the figs to be preserved are sterilized in ozone with a concentration of 10-25 mg/l for 1-2 hours. In the step (iii), the temperature of the pre-cooling treatment is -1degrees Celsius to 1degrees Celsius, and the time of the pre-cooling treatment is 22-24 hours. The temperature of the pre-cooling treatment is -1degrees Celsius to 1degrees Celsius, and the time of the pre-cooling treatment is 22-24 hours. In the step (iv), placing 1-3 figs in each modified atmosphere packaging box. In the step (v), the input voltage is 220plus minus22v. The modified atmosphere packaging box is placed in a high-voltage electrostatic field with a voltage of 1800v for preservation.

   USE - The method is useful for preparing substituted tetrahydro-1H-thieno(3,4-d)imidazol-2(3H)-one compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and easy to carry out in one tank; has high product yield and selectivity, and one-pot process; and produces less by-products.

The reaction is carried out under ultrasonic radiation.
   R1, R2 = low alkyl, aryl or acyl including methyl, ethyl, propyl, butyl, phenyl, benzyl, formyl, acetyl (optionally substituted by at least one substituents), or H;
   X, X' = halo, preferably Cl or Br; and
   n = 1-6, preferably 2, 3 or 4.

(II) is (3aS,6aR)-1,3-diacetyldihydro-1H-thieno(3,4-d)imidazole-2,4(6H,6aH)-dione, (3aS,6aR)-1,3-dibenzyldihydro-1H-thieno(3,4-d)imidazole-2,4(6H,6aH)-dione, (3aS,6aR)-1-acetyldihydro-1H-thieno(3,4-d)imidazole-2,4(6H,6aH)-dione, (3aS,6aR)-1-benzyldihydro-1H-thieno(3,4-d)imidazole-2,4(6H,6aH)-dione, (3aS,6aR)-3-acetyl-1-benzyldihydro-1H-thieno(3,4-d)imidazole-2,4(6H,6aH)-dione or (3aS,6aR)-dihydro-1H-thieno(3,4-d)imidazole-2,4(6H,6aH)-dione. The solvent is toluene, ether, tetrahydrofuran or 2-methyltetrahydrofuran. The acid is Bronsted acid including hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid or p-toluenesulfonic acid; or solid acid including Amberlyst type acidic ion exchange resin, preferably amber 15, 16 or 36. The method is performed in a batch or continuous manner.

PN CN112808291-A; CN112808291-B
TI Preparing cobalt-zinc/carbon-nitrogen catalyst            useful in synthesis of methanol by hydrogenation of            carbon dioxide comprises e.g. dissolving cobalt nitrate            hexahydrate and 2-methyl imidazole in methanol, adding            methanol solution, stirring.
AU LIU B
   FANG T
   HE Y
AE UNIV SOUTH CENT NATIONALITIES (USOC-C)
GA 202158504H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cobalt-zinc/carbon-nitrogen                catalyst, comprises (1) dissolving the cobalt                nitrate hexahydrate and 2-methyl imidazole in                methanol, quickly adding the cobalt nitrate                methanol solution into the 2-methyl imidazole                methanol solution, stirring at room temperature                until the mixed solution becomes purple from black                gradually, (2) dissolving the zinc nitrate                hexahydrate and 2-methyl imidazole, methanol,                quickly adding 2-methyl imidazole methanol solution                in the purple solution, quickly adding the zinc                nitrate methanol solution, stirring at room                temperature for 30-60 minutes, allowing to stand to                obtain the purple black solution, centrifuging,                washing, drying to obtain zeolitic imidazolate                frameworks -67/ zeolitic imidazolate frameworks -X,                and (3) calcining the zeolitic imidazolate                frameworks-67/ zeolitic imidazolate frameworks -8-X                under the inert gas atmosphere of 600-800 degrees C                for 4-6 hours to obtain cobalt-zinc/carbon-nitrogen                catalyst.
   USE - The cobalt-zinc/carbon-nitrogen catalyst is                useful in the synthesis of methanol by                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cobalt-zinc/carbon-nitrogen catalyst: has                higher carbon dioxide conversion rate and methanol                selectivity under mild condition; has high activity                under mild condition, stability, and has good                industrial application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing indium-cobalt-zinc/carbon-nitrogen                catalyst comprising adding the indium nitrate                aqueous solution dropwise to the zeolitic                imidazolate frameworks-67/ zeolitic imidazolate                frameworks -8-X, rotary steaming in a water bath,                after drying, calcining in an inert atmosphere at                600-800 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain                indium-cobalt-zinc/carbon-nitrogen catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  mass ratio of the cobalt nitrate hexahydrate,                  2-methylimidazole, zinc nitrate hexahydrate, and                  2-methylimidazole is 4-5: 9-10: 2-20: 2-20. The                  zeolitic imidazolate frameworks-67/ zeolitic                  imidazolate frameworks -8-X,                  cobalt-zinc/carbon-nitrogen catalyst is a rhombic                  dodecahedron structure. Preferred Method: In the                  step (3), placing the zeolitic imidazolate                  frameworks- 67/ zeolitic imidazolate                  frameworks-8-X in a tube furnace, heating to                  600-800 degrees C at 10 degrees C/minute under                  nitrogen or argon atmosphere, calcining at 800                  degrees C for 4-6 hours at 600-800 degrees C to                  obtain cobalt-zinc/carbon-nitrogen catalyst. The                  indium nitrate aqueous solution is prepared by                  dissolving indium nitrate hydrate in deionized                  water, and the mass ratio of the indium nitrate                  hydrate to zeolitic imidazolate frameworks-67/                  zeolitic imidazolate frameworks -8-X is 0.4: 1-2.                  In the specific process of calcining comprises                  heating to 600-800 degrees C, at the rate of 10                  degrees C /minute, calcining for 4-6 hours at                  600-800 degrees C to obtain                  indium-cobalt-zinc/carbon-nitrogen                  catalyst.

PN CN112517019-A
TI Methanation catalyst with titanium dioxide aerogel as carrier useful in carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide methanation reaction, comprises nickel, auxiliary agent and carrier titanium dioxide aerogel.
AU YU B
   YU M
   ZHAO R
   MEI C
   LI C
AE DATANG INT CHEM TECHNOLOGY RES INST LTD (DATA-Non-standard)
GA 202131794R
AB    NOVELTY - Methanation catalyst with titanium dioxide aerogel as a carrier comprises active component nickel which accounts for 3-60 wt.% of the total weight of the catalyst based on the elemental metal, auxiliary agent accounts for 2-30 wt.% of the total weight of the catalyst based on the oxide and the remainder of the carrier titanium dioxide aerogel. The auxiliary agent comprises at least one of lanthanum oxide, ceric oxide, praseodymium oxide or samarium oxide.
   USE - The methanation catalyst with titanium dioxide aerogel as carrier is useful in carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide methanation reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: can improve the low-temperature activity; has good thermal stability; and is suitable for carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide methanation reaction process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing a methanation catalyst with titanium dioxide aerogel as a carrier, comprising (i) mixing the precursor of the active component and the precursor of the auxiliary agent and dissolving in absolute ethanol to prepare an alcohol solution; (ii) adding tetrabutyl titanate drop wise into the alcohol solution prepared in step (i) under ultrasonic conditions, and mixing uniformly to prepare a sol; (iii) adjusting the pH value to 1-5 with hydrochloric acid, adjusting the pH value to 6-10 with aqueous ammonia after allowing to stand, washing with absolute ethanol after allowing to stand, aging, supercritical drying and roasting to obtain oxidized aerogel methanation catalyst; and (iv) reducing the oxidized aerogel methanation catalyst in hydrogen or hydrogen/nitrogen mixed gas to obtain a pre-reduced catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (iv), the hydrogen content in the hydrogen/nitrogen mixed gas in step (4) is greater than or equal to 30% by volume.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the process of preparing the alcohol solution in step (i), relative to 1000 g of absolute ethanol, the amount of the precursor of the active component is 20-60 g based on the elemental nickel and the amount of the precursor of the auxiliary agent is 10-25 g in terms of oxide. The molar ratio of the tetrabutyl titanate added in step (ii) to the absolute ethanol used in step (i) is 1:5-30, preferably 1:10-25. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the temperature for preparing the alcohol solution is 30-80 degrees C. In the step (iii), the roasting temperature is 600-850 degrees C. In the step (iv), the method of cooling is 500-650 degrees C. In the step (iv), the reduction conditions the volumetric space velocity of the reducing gas is 800-4000/hour, heating to 500-600 degrees C at a rate of 0.5-1 degrees C/min, and maintaining at this temperature for 4-8 hours.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN WO2020179745-A1; CA3130076-A1; CN113474287-A; EP3936478-A1; BR112021016013-A2; KR2021133223-A; JP2021504089-X; US2022135410-A1
TI Active carbon useful in automobile canister, comprises Brunauer, Emmett and Teller calculated from adsorption isotherm of carbon dioxide.
AU NISHITA M
   HITOMI M
   YAMADA T
   NISHIDA M
AE KURARAY CO LTD (KURS-C)
   KURARAY CO LTD (KURS-C)
   KURARAY CO LTD (KURS-C)
GA 202089786L
AB    NOVELTY - Active carbon comprises 1250-1800 m2/g Brunauer, Emmett and Teller (BET) specific area ratio (A) calculated from the adsorption isotherm of carbon dioxide, and 0.640 or lower ratio of the pore volume (B) ml/g of pores with 0.4-0.7 nm diameter to the pore volume (C) ml/g of pores with 0.7-1.1 nm diameter, which is obtained by subjecting the adsorption/desorption isotherm of carbon dioxide to grand canonical Monte Carlo simulation.
   USE - The active carbon is useful in automobile canister (claimed) capable of adsorbing and desorbing transpiration fuel to prevent the transpiration fuel from being released from the fuel tank of the vehicle to the outside.
   ADVANTAGE - The active carbon adsorbs or collects vaporized fuel from the fuel tank while the vehicle is stopped, and freshly sucks in the adsorbed vaporized fuel during operation, has high adsorption performance, excellent desorption performance, and realizes the desired butane desorption performance in addition to the desired butane adsorption performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing the activated carbon involving (i) mixing sawdust and phosphoric acid compounds, (ii) heating the obtained mixture and reducing the mass of phosphoric acid and sawdust in the mixture by 5-20 %mass based on the total mass of phosphoric acid and sawdust in dry state, (iii) molding mixture having reduced mass, (iv) heating and oxidizing the obtained molded product in the presence of oxygen, (v) heating the oxidized molded product in an atmosphere of an inert gas or gas that does not react with the oxidized molded product at the heating temperature, and (vi) washing the fired molded product with water and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The relationship between the BET specific surface area (A), pore volume (B) and pore volume (C) is given in the formula of: (A)x (B)/(C) less than or equal to 1050. The pore volume of (B) is 0.135 ml/g or less. The pore volume (D) at a pore diameter of 0.6-0.7 nm obtained by performing grand canonical Monte Carlo simulation on the adsorption isotherm of carbon dioxide is 0.050 ml/g or less. Preferred Components: The activated carbon used is in the form of pellets comprising phosphoric acid-activated activated carbon. Preferred Method: The method involves kneading the mixture obtained in the step (i) at the same time as heating in step (ii), in an independent step between step (ii) and step (iii), or just before molding in step (iii).

PT P
PN CN110864578-A; CN212458065-U
TI Efficient heat exchange channel with variable cross-section airfoil for supercritical carbon dioxide printed circuit heat exchanger (PCHE), has photochemical etching which is set to form certain depth of channels and form fluid channel.
AU LI H
   YANG Y
   ZHANG L
   WU J
   WU S
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 202020868L
AB    NOVELTY - The variable cross-section has a main portion which is provided for forming a unit case from an airfoil profile. The size of the unit case is gradually increased along the flow direction by a certain proportion coefficient. A shrinkable or expanded variable flow cross-sectional area arrangement is formed for providing the flow of hot and cold fluids, by gradually changing while keeping the shape unchanged. The upper and lower portions of the airfoil-shaped unit case are respectively provided with upper and lower border areas. The photochemical etching is provided to etch the plates except for the airfoil unit case and the upper and lower frame areas to form a certain depth of channels, forming a fluid channel.
   USE - Efficient heat exchange channel with variable cross-section airfoil for supercritical carbon dioxide PCHE.
   ADVANTAGE - The development of the thermal boundary layer is suppressed by adopting the existing airfoil profile. The increase in flow resistance caused by enhanced heat transfer is reduced. The comprehensive flow and heat transfer performance of the heat exchange channel are improved. The expansion or contraction type of variable flow cross-sectional area arrangement is adopted, according to the different changes in the density or velocity of the hot and cold fluids along the flow direction, so that the fluid flow near the same velocity in the flow direction is achieved and the flow resistance is reduced. The comprehensive heat transfer performance is achieved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the efficient variable-profile airfoil for supercritical carbon dioxide PCHE. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN DE102018119775-A1
TI Intubation device used for measuring carbon dioxide content in lungs, has electrical element that is arranged at end region such that electrically conductive cable is running through inner tube and is connected to electrical element.
AU ALSALMAN M
AE ALSALMAN M (ALSA-Individual)
GA 202014489Y
AB    NOVELTY - The intubation device (100) has an air guide tube (101), and an inner tube (102) that is arranged partially within the air guide tube. A thread is arranged partially within the inner tube and an electrical element (103). The air guide tube has a curvature. The thread is fastened to the air guide tube in a first end region of the air guide tube. The electrical element is arranged at the first end region or protrudes from the first end region. An electrically conductive cable is running through the inner tube and is electrically connected to the electrical element.
   USE - Intubation device used for measuring carbon dioxide content in lungs.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide content in lungs can be measured precisely. The intubation can be performed without laryngoscopy and bronchoscopy using camera. The guidance of the thread and the cable can be arranged within the inner tube such that the risk that components of the cable or the thread are introduced into the lungs by the air flowing into the lungs can be reduced. The air guide tube can be easily connected to a ventilation device without the thread having to be observed or causing leaks. The air guide tube is easily inserted into the trachea. The radius of curvature can be reduced by exerting the tensile force.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an intubation device.
   Intubation device (100)
   Air guide tube (101)
   Inner tube (102)
   Electrical element (103)

PN KR2075297-B1
TI Moving-type mushrooms cultivating system, has            control element for sensing temperature of inner side            of cultivation chamber to operate cold and hot air fan            to manage temperature and exhaust carbon dioxide in            inner side of cultivation chamber.
AU KIM D
AE GLINKOREA CO LTD (GLIN-Non-standard)
GA 202014652F
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a suction tube (41) located at                a side of a carrier (20) to suck air from a lower                layer. A circulation fan (42) is located at a side                of the suction tube to drive and absorb air. An air                circulation element (40) is provided with a                discharge pipe (43) for discharging air of the                lower layer to an upper portion of the carrier. The                air circulation element is formed with multiple                through-holes (41a) to suck lower air of a                cultivation chamber. A control element is turned-on                in the cultivation chamber, and senses temperature                of inner side of the cultivation chamber to operate                cold and hot air fan to manage temperature and                exhaust carbon dioxide in the inner side of the                cultivation chamber.
   USE - Moving-type mushrooms cultivating                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can improve quality and                productivity of mushrooms, and reduces mushrooms                defect rate and significant temperature difference                between upper and lower layers of the cultivation                chamber so as to provide require temperature on a                location of each mushroom in the cultivation                chamber, thus improving cultivation growth of the                mushrooms. The control element can appropriately                control and manage concentration, temperature and                humidity of carbon dioxide in the cultivation                chamber so as to generate pleasant cultivation                environment of the mushrooms to-be-concentrated in                a limited space, thus facilitating management of                the mushroom cultivation system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a                carrier.
   Carrier (20)
   Air circulation element (40)
   Suction tube (41)
   Through-holes (41a)
   Circulation fan (42)
   Discharge pipe (43)

PT P
PN WO2020028606-A1; US2020062900-A1; US11180609-B2
TI Polymer composition used to determine relative content of biobased carbon present in polycarbonates and measure and improve the sustainability profile of polymers, comprises polymer chains having substituted hydrocarbon containing units.
AU FARMER J J
AE SAUDI ARAMCO TECHNOLOGIES CO (SAOI-C)
   FARMER J J (FARM-Individual)
   SAUDI ARAMCO TECHNOLOGIES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202011021G
AB    NOVELTY - Polymer composition comprises polymer chains having substituted hydrocarbon containing units (I).
   USE - The composition is useful for: determining relative content of biobased carbon present in polycarbonates (claimed); and measuring and improving the sustainability profile of polymers.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has desirable sustainability profiles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polymer composition comprises polymer chains having substituted hydrocarbon containing units of formula (-O-C(R4)(R3)-C(R1)(R2)-O-C(O)-O-) (I), where the radiocarbon content of carbon atoms present in -O-C(R4)(R3)-C(R1)(R2)- is lower than the radiocarbon content of carbon atoms present in -O-C(O)-O-.
   Either R1-R4 = polymer chain including -H, optionally substituted group including 1-30C aliphatic or 6-14C aryl, 3-12-membered heterocyclyl or 5-12-membered heteroaryl; or
   R1-R4 (together with intervening atoms) = at least one optionally substituted 3-12-membered rings, optionally containing at least one heteroatom.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing (M1) the composition, comprising fermenting carbohydrate derived from biomass to produce biobased ethanol, converting the biobased ethanol to a biobased epoxide and copolymerizing carbon dioxide sequestered from a fossil carbon source with the biobased epoxide to produce the composition; and
   (2) determining (M2) relative content of biobased carbon present in polycarbonates, comprising (a) treating the composition to liberate a cyclic carbonate of formula (II), (b) treating (II) to liberate carbon atoms labeled a as carbon dioxide, and carbon atoms labeled b as an epoxide of formula (III), and (c) measuring the radiocarbon content of the carbon dioxide comprising carbon atoms labeled a and the epoxide comprising carbon atoms labeled b.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises: polymer chains characterized in that the carbon atoms present in -O-C(R4)(R3)-C(R1)(R2)- have a percent Modern Carbon (pMC) value measured according to ASTM D6866-16 (Method B) that is lower than a pMC value of the carbon atoms present in -O-C(O)-O-; aliphatic polycarbonates, where the aliphatic polycarbonates are on average in the composition, the percentage of carbonate linkages is greater than or equal to 90%; polyether carbonates; and block copolymers comprising at least one block comprising aliphatic polycarbonate or polyether carbonate, and polyethers, polyesters, polyolefins, polyamides and/or polyimides. The composition has a number average molecular weight of greater than 20000 g/mol or less than 20000 g/mol, which is measured by gel permeation chromatography. Preferred Method: The method (M2) further comprises: separating the carbon dioxide from the epoxide, where the radiocarbon content is measured according to ASTM D6866-16 (Method B); and using the measured radiocarbon content to confirm that the carbon atoms at the positions labeled a are derived from a fossil carbon source by having pMC value of less than 1, preferably less than 0.03, measured according to ASTM D6866-16 (Method B); and using the measured radiocarbon content to confirm that the carbon atoms at the positions labeled b are derived from a biobased feedstock by having pMC value of at least 95, preferably at least 100, measured according to ASTM D6866-16 (Method B). Preferred Components: The carbon atoms present in -O-C(R4)(R3)-C(R1)(R2)- have a pMC value of less than 50, preferably less than 1 or 0, measured according to ASTM D6866-16 (Method B). The carbon atoms present in -O-C(O)-O- have a pMC value of greater than 75, preferably greater than 105 or 100, measured according to ASTM D6866-16 (Method B). The % biobased carbon is greater than 40%, 50%, 60%, 70%, 80% or 90%. The % biobased carbon is less than 80%, 70%, 60%, 50%, 40%, 30%, 20% or 10%. The % biobased carbon is 40-80%, preferably 60-80%. The biobased epoxides are ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epoxides derived from natural oils, epoxides derived from fatty acids, epoxides derived from fatty alcohols and/or epoxides derived from terpenes. The biobased epoxides are ethylene oxide, propylene oxide or limonene oxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110589862-A; CN110589862-B
TI Preparation of nano-scale calcium carbonate by multi-stage tandem method involves feeding solid suspension, discharging calcium carbonate precipitation suspension, supplying carbon dioxide gas and adjusting operating conditions.
AU WU Q
   XUAN S
   LI W
   CHEN G
   HONG J
AE JIANDE HUAMING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A76129
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano-scale calcium carbonate by multi-stage tandem method involves using three reactors in series, continuously feeding solid suspension of each reactor, discharging calcium carbonate precipitation suspension from last reactor, supplying carbon dioxide gas raw materials to reactors of each stage, feeding calcium hydroxide raw material fed from first-stage reactor or from first-stage reactor and second-stage reactor, where last reactor consists of two rotating reactors, one of which accepts effluent from upper reactor for carbonization operation and other reactor performs operations of degassing calcium carbonate suspension, adjusting and controlling operating conditions of reactors at all levels based on technical indicators of crystal form, morphology and particle size of target nano-scale calcium carbonate products.
   USE - Preparation of nano-scale calcium carbonate by multi-stage tandem method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of nano-scale calcium carbonate by multi-stage tandem method involves using three reactors in series, where liquid phase is continuous phase, continuously feeding solid suspension of each reactor, discharging calcium carbonate precipitation suspension from last reactor, supplying carbon dioxide gas raw materials to reactors of each stage, feeding calcium hydroxide raw material fed from first-stage reactor or from first-stage reactor and second-stage reactor, where last reactor consists of two rotating reactors, one of which accepts effluent from upper reactor for carbonization operation and other reactor performs operations of degassing and discharging calcium carbonate suspension, adjusting and controlling operating conditions of reactors at all levels based on technical indicators of crystal form, morphology and particle size of target nano-scale calcium carbonate products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The reactor used is any one of stirred carbonization kettle, bubble column, and circulating jet carbonization kettle. The upper portion of the first-stage reactor is provided with an overflow port, the supply point of the calcium hydroxide raw material is set in the region with the greatest mixing intensity, and the supply of carbon dioxide passes from the bottom of the reactor to the reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide gas has a carbon dioxide volume concentration of 25-100 vol.%. The carbon dioxide gas has carbon dioxide volume concentration of 25-48 vol.%. Preferred Component: The carbonic acid gas is a kiln gas collected by calcination of lime. Preferred Property: The average particle diameter of the nano-scale precipitated calcium carbonate is 35-90 nm. Preferred Condition: The reaction in first-stage reactor with three-stage series and operating volume V1 is carried out at average residence time ( tau 1) of 25-35 minutes, operating temperature (T1) of 17-21 degrees C, feed concentration (C0) of 0.5-0.8 mol/L, Q1/A1 of 0.5-1, where Q1 is the first stage reactor carbon dioxide supply rate in mol carbon dioxide/minute, and A1 is the calcium hydroxide supply rate in mol calcium hydroxide/minute. A is calculated using the formula: C0x V1/ tau 1. The reaction in second-stage reactor with operating volume V2 is carried out at V2/V1 of 2-10, operating temperature (T2) of 40-55 degrees C, A2/A1 of 0.1-2 and Q2/A2 of 1.4-2.7, where Q2 is the second stage reactor carbon dioxide supply rate in mol carbon dioxide/minute, and A2 is the calcium hydroxide supply rate in mol calcium hydroxide/minute. The third-stage shift operation with operating volume V3 is carried out in two reactors (A) and (B) at V3 less than or equal to V2, operating temperature (T3) of 75-82 degrees C, Q3/V3 of 0.008-0.01 mol/(minute.L), where Q3 is the carbon dioxide supply rate in third stage reaction in mol carbon dioxide/minute. Feeding and carbonization time is calculating using the formula: 10+ tau 2, where tau 2 is average residence time in second-stage reactor. Degassing and discharging is carried out for 20 minutes and the total operating time of a single reactor is calculated using the formula: 2 tau 2 minutes. The nitrogen adsorption BET specific surface area of 18-35 m2/g.

TI Ventilation system of convergence total-heat exchanger, has second lower space portion which is equipped in bottom side of second portion of upper space and ejects indoor air to outdoor and supplies indoor air to first lower space portion.
AU LOCK O C
AE LOCK O C (LOCK-Individual)
GA 201925510B
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has an air channel determining unit (150) which includes an outside air feed through (121) that changes the flow path of air flowing into feed through and an indoor air incoming space (150a) for supplying the indoor air to air cleaner (140). An outdoor dust sensor measures the dust concentration of outside air and an indoor dust sensor measures dust concentration of indoor air. A carbon dioxide sensor measures indoor carbon dioxide concentration and a main controller changes fluid path of air according to density value of carbon dioxide and dust measured. An outside air exhaust plenum (150b) includes second upper space portion that ejects the indoor air to the outdoor. A second lower space portion is equipped in bottom side of second portion of upper space and ejects indoor air to the outdoor and supplies the indoor air to first lower space portion.
   USE - Ventilation system of convergence total-heat exchanger.
   ADVANTAGE - The indoor air is purely ventilated, by ventilation system. The administration and management cost are saved. The burden of the pre-filter of total-heat exchanger is reduced and the difficulty of maintenance and repair is reduced.
