TI Device for measuring concentration of e.g. carbon            dioxide, in e.g. exhaled breath, of subject, has flow            chamber comprising first channel to receive reference            sample including known concentration of component and            second channel.
AU KERTSER M
AE ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
   ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
   ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
GA 201919655N
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a flow chamber (132) comprising                a first channel to receive a reference sample i.e.                ambient air, including a known concentration of a                component, and a second channel to receive a target                sample, including an unknown concentration of the                component. A pump (130) i.e. dual-chamber                piezoelectric pump, pumps the reference sample and                the target sample at a same volume flow rate                through the first and second channels,                respectively. A first thermal mass flowmeter (140)                measures thermal conductivity of the reference                sample. A second thermal mass flow meter (150)                measures thermal conductivity of the target                sample.
   USE - Device for measuring concentration of a                component i.e. carbon dioxide, in a target sample                i.e. exhaled breath, of a subject by a                microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) device (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device utilizes a high-frequency thermal                mass flow meter to enable continuous monitoring of                carbon dioxide concentration in subject's exhaled                breath, thus providing efficient evaluation of a                subject's respiratory status.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a method for measuring concentration of a                component in a target sample of a subject
   (2) a computer-readable medium comprising a                set of instructions for measuring concentration of                a component in a target sample of a subject.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                thermal mass flow measurement system for measuring                concentration of a component in a target sample of                a subject.
   Inlets (110, 120)
   Pump (130)
   Chambers (132, 142)
   Thermal mass flow meters (140, 150)
   Display (170)

AU MALEEV I

GA 201916859U
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus comprises a measurement chamber,                which is configured to retain a substance (216),                where an entrance window (210) is mounted on a side                of the measurement chamber. An exit window (212) is                mounted on an opposite side of the measurement                chamber. The exit window being parallel with the                entrance window. A light source (202) is configured                to generate an incident light beam (220). The                incident light beam passes through the entrance                window, the measurement chamber and the exit window                to form an output light beam. A detector (218) is                positioned under the exit window. The detector is                configured to collect the output light beam passing                through the exit window and generate measurement                data. The substance includes carbon dioxide in                gaseous state, liquid state, or supercritical                state. The entrance window and the exit window are                made of borosilicate glass.
   USE - Apparatus for in-situ phase                determination.
   ADVANTAGE - Ensuring to accurately determine the phase of                carbon dioxide in a cleaning chamber to facilitate                the cleaning process, while performing the                measurement in real time, and in a cost-effective                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for in-situ phase determination.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                dual-pass type optical sensor for determining the                phases and conditions of supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid.

   Detector (218)

PT P
PN CN109111060-A
TI Treatment system for mineral processing wastewater, has scum suction separation devices that is installed at top of reactor where slag discharge port is arranged on right side of scum suction separation device.
AU TU R
AE TU R (TURR-Individual)
GA 201903962H
AB    NOVELTY - The treatment system has a beneficiation wastewater containing helium ions that enters a collecting well (1) through the waste water pipeline. The outlet of the collecting well is connected to a coarse grid (2) through the waste water pipeline. A hollow carbon dioxide aeration reverse agitating screw surrounds a set of fine-hole unidirectional filters on the outside. A set of scum suction separation devices is installed at the top of a reactor (5). A slag discharge port is arranged on the right side of the scum suction separation device. The effective volume of the carbon dioxide vortex convection slag separation reactor is 245m3, its forward stirring blade working voltage is 380V. The rotation speed is 270 rev/min, carbon dioxide vortex convection slag separation reactor, the driving motor operating voltage is 380V, rated power is 4.2kW, the rotational speed is 360 rev/min, and the pore size of the pore unidirectional filter is 0.8mm.
   USE - Treatment system for mineral processing wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses pure physical effects as a unit of purification thus eliminating the risk of introducing more harmful chemical pollutants. The advanced technical route of the system achieves the ideal purification effect, and the operation and maintenance cost is low which is conducive to widespread application.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the treatment system for mineral processing wastewater. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Collecting well (1)
   Coar grid (2)
   Prime sedimentation tank (3)
   PH adjustment tank (4)
   Reactor (5)

PN CN108948316-A; CN108948316-B
TI Silicone-modified polyurethane emulsion useful in preparing wood lacquer to prepare wood furniture comprises e.g. carbon dioxide copolymer polyol, hydroxy silicone oil, aromatic diisocyanate, small molecular chain extender, and solvent.
AU DING H
   LI H
   LIANG M
AE GUANGZHOU KANGDIFU ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECT (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A05195
AB    NOVELTY - Silicone-modified polyurethane emulsion comprises 80-120 pts. wt. carbon dioxide copolymer polyol, 2-20 pts. wt. hydroxy silicone oil, 20-60 pts. wt. aromatic diisocyanate, 0.1-5 pts. wt. small molecular chain extender, 2-10 pts. wt. hydrophilic chain extender, 1-10 pts. wt. salt-forming agent, and 150-250 pts. wt. solvent.
   USE - The emulsion is useful in preparing wood lacquer to prepare wood furniture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The emulsion and coating film: has high mechanical strength and strong water resistance and chemical resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the silicone-modified polyurethane emulsion comprising (a) dissolving the carbon dioxide copolymer polyol, hydroxy silicone oil, and hydrophilic chain extender, mixing, adding the aromatic diisocyanate at 30-60 degrees C, and stirring uniformly, (b) stirring at 75-95 degrees C for 3-5 hours, (c) further cooling to 50-75 degrees C, adding the small molecular chain extender, stirring uniformly, and (d) adding the solvent, and dispersing to obtain the silicone modified polyurethane emulsion;
   (2) wood lacquer comprises 30-80 pts. wt. above prepared silicone-modified polyurethane emulsion, 5- 50 pts. wt. an aqueous acrylic emulsion, 0.5-20 pts. wt. additive and 10-30 pts. wt. water; and
   (3) preparing wood lacquer, comprising preparing the silicone-modified polyurethane emulsion and mixing the silicone-modified polyurethane emulsion, aqueous acrylic emulsion, and additive with the water, and uniformly dispersing to obtain the coating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aromatic diisocyanate comprises at least one of toluene diisocyanate or diphenylmethane diisocyanate. The carbon dioxide copolymer polyol has a number average molecular weight of 1000-8000 g/mol, the hydroxy functionality is 2-6, and the mol fraction of intra-molecular carbonate group is 0.25-0.45. The aqueous acrylic emulsion has a glass transition temperature of 40-45 degrees C. The additive comprises 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. antifoaming agent, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. bactericide, 0.2-1 pts. wt. wetting agent, 0.2-1 pts. wt. leveling agent, 0.1-0.8 pts. wt. pH adjuster, 0.2-2 pts. wt. film-forming auxiliary, 0.2-4 pts. wt. thickener, and 0-10 pts. wt. coloring agent.

PT P
PN CN108642099-A
TI Manufacturing high-calcium animal amino acid involves placing stacked waste seafood shell fish and meatin in sealed reaction kettle, adding water, acetic acid, and adjusting pH value for meat and shell separation in separating process.
AU WANG X
   CHANG L
   CHANG Z
   YAO Y
   YAN L
   NI T
   WANG Z
AE WEIHAI CHANGQING MARINE SCI & TECHNOLOGY (WEIH-Non-standard)
   RONGCHENG KAIPU BIOLOGICAL ENG CO LTD (RONG-Non-standard)
GA 201884645V
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing high-calcium animal amino acid involves placing stacked waste seafood shell fish and meat in sealed reaction kettle, adding water, acetic acid, adjusting pH value for meat and shell separation in separating process, generating carbon dioxide gas, generating calcium acetate solution and shell fishmeat floating on solution. The generated carbon dioxide gas is absorbed by sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide solution to generate sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate. The shellfish meat and calcium acetate solution is separated in the reaction kettle. The shellfish meat is placed into enzymolysis tank. The sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide solution is mixed after absorbing carbon dioxide gas with calcium acetate solution, and reacted to form precipitate.
   USE - Method for manufacturing high-calcium animal amino acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to manufacture high-calcium animal amino acid that has high content calcium ion, high value amino acid liquid and high quality calcium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The precipitate is calcium carbonate. The solution is sodium acetate or potassium acetate. The sodium acetate or potassium acetate solution generated is pumped into the enzymolysis tank, and pH is adjusted to 8-10. The 1-2% alkali protease is added, and temperature is raised to 45-65 degrees C and stirred. The enzymolysis is carried out for 6-12 hours, grease is decomposed in meat in the process under alkaline condition into glycerol and fatty acid salt. The temperature is raised to 80 degrees C, stirring for 10-30 minutes and inactivated, and cooled to obtain high-calcium animal amino acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amount of water is 1/3-1/2 wt.% of raw material. The amount of acetic acid is 1-3 wt.% of the water. Preferred Process: The appropriate amount of alkali is added to sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide solution. The high-calcium animal amino acid solution generated is placed into powder by concentrating and spray drying process. Preferred Components: The alkaline protease is calpain.

PN CN108562701-A
TI Method for calculating concentration of methyl bromide, involves determining volume fraction of dry air as function of concentration of methyl bromide.
AU WANG Y
   MA C
   WANG G
   LI T
AE HAOCHEN HONGMING BEIJING TECHNOLOGY CO (HAOC-Non-standard)
GA 201876943E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (101) a thermal conductivity of the mixed gas. The components of the mixed gas include carbon dioxide, dry air, water vapor, and methyl bromide. The thermal conductivity of each component in the mixed gas is obtained (102). The concentration of the carbon dioxide and the humidity of the water vapor is obtained (103). A volume fraction of the dry air is determined (104) as a function of the concentration of the methyl bromide according to the concentration and the humidity. The concentration of the methyl bromide is calculated (105) based on the thermal conductivity of the mixed gas, the thermal conductivity of the respective components, the concentration, the humidity, and the functional relationship.
   USE - Method for calculating the concentration of methyl bromide.
   ADVANTAGE - The dosage is accurately controlled, and the fumigation cost is saved. The method reduces the waste of the medicine, reduces the pollution and protect the environment and is convenient for the supervision portion to supervise the fumigation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a methyl bromide concentration detection system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the method for calculating the methyl bromide concentration. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for obtaining thermal conductivity of the mixed gas (101)
   Step for obtaining thermal conductivity of each component in the mixed gas (102)
   Step for obtaining concentration of the carbon dioxide and the humidity of the water vapor (103)
   Step for determining volume fraction of the dry air as a function of the concentration of the methyl bromide (104)
   Step for calculating concentration of the methyl bromide (105)

PN CN108325528-A; CN108325528-B
TI Cobalt/graphene composite catalyst used for e.g.            electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, is            prepared by adding nitrate solution to monolithic            graphite oxide mixture, adding sodium borohydride,            centrifuging, washing, drying, calcining.
AU HAO Q
   CAI L
   XIA X
   LEI W
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 201861218K
AB    NOVELTY - A cobalt/graphene composite catalyst is                prepared by (S1) adding graphite oxide to solvent                (a), ultrasonically dispersing to form uniformly                dispersed monolithic graphite oxide mixture, (S2)                dissolving cobalt nitrate hexahydrate in solvent                (b) to form uniform nitrate solution, (S3) adding                nitrate solution to monolithic graphite oxide                mixture, continuously stirring to form mixed                solution (1) composed of graphite oxide with                concentration of 1.25-1.7 mg/mL and nitrate                solution with concentration of 3.6-5.0 mg/mL, (S4)                dripping sodium hydroxide in mixed solution (1) to                adjust pH of the solution to 11, continuously                stirring to obtain mixed solution (2), (S5) adding                sodium borohydride to mixed solution (2) and                continuously stirring to obtain mixed solution (3),                (S6) reacting mixed solution (3) at 80-180 degrees                C for 2-5 hours, (S7) centrifuging, washing, drying                and calcining at 450-800 degrees C for 2 hours to                obtain final product.
   USE - Cobalt/graphene composite catalyst used for                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide and                reduction of hydrocarbon fuels.
   ADVANTAGE - The cobalt/graphene composite catalyst has                high reducing power, high stability and current                density of 16.2 mA/cm2.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following: preparation of cobalt/graphene composite                catalyst; and #application of cobalt/graphene                composite catalyst, which involves pretreating the                catalyst, placing in 0.1 M sodium bicarbonate                electrolyte, introducing nitrogen gas into the                electrolyte for 15 minutes and passing carbon                dioxide for 15 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  cobalt nitrate hexahydrate to graphite oxide is                  1:3. The molar ratio of sodium borohydride to                  cobalt nitrate hexahydrate is 0.1-0.8. The                  solvent (b) is deionized water. Preferred                  Process: The catalyst pretreatment method is                  carried out by adding catalyst to isopropanol,                  deionized water and 5 wt.% Nafion (RTM:                  sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based                  fluoropolymer-copolymer), ultrasonically                  dispersing to form uniformly mixed electrode                  coating and dripping electrode coating on the                  surface of glassy carbon electrode.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The solvent (a) is                  selected from anhydrous ethanol or deionized                  water.

PT P
PN CN107889883-A
TI Preserving bamboo shoots, by soaking bamboo shoots in ice-water mixture, agitating, adding preservative, soaking bamboo shoots in filming agent, adding oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas mixture and packaging product.
AU LIAO Y
AE CHENGDU XINGFANG NIUWA FOOD CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201828938D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preserving bamboo shoots, involves peeling bamboo shoots, washing bamboo shoots, soaking the bamboo shoots in 7-9 times volume ice-water mixture, agitating the mixture for 5 minutes, cooling the bamboo shoot to 3 degrees C for half an hour, adding preservative to the mixture, dissolving 10-45 g chitosan sugar in 1 l 2% citric acid solution to obtain filming agent, soaking the bamboo shoots in filming agent for 10-25 seconds, adding oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas mixture, and packaging the product in food-grade aluminum foil pouches.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving bamboo shoots (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preserving bamboo shoots, involves peeling bamboo shoots, washing bamboo shoots, soaking the bamboo shoots in 7-9 times volume ice-water mixture, agitating the mixture for 5 minutes, cooling the bamboo shoot to 3 degrees C for half an hour, adding preservative to the mixture, where the preservative is sodium dehydroacetate or alcohol, and the amount of sodium dehydroacetate is 0.01-1% and the amount of alcohol is 0.001-0.5%, dissolving 10-45 g chitosan sugar in 1 l 2% citric acid solution to obtain filming agent, soaking the bamboo shoots in filming agent for 10-25 seconds, adding oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas mixture in a volume ratio of 1.5:8.5-2:3, and packaging the product in food-grade aluminum foil pouches.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method further involves storing the bamboo shoot after packaging at 1-4 degrees C.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC A08-S02; A10-E09; A11-B05A; A11-B05D; A12-B; A12-W09; D03-A04
IP A23B-007/16; A23B-007/148
